desc:
This upright variety has crisp, bright-red, finely-cut oak-leaf leaves.

This variety was developed by Franck Morton of Wild Garden Seeds in Oregon. It is the result of a cross between "Salad Bowl" and "Rouge d'Hiver".

name: Antigua
desc: This vigorous, fast-growing variety produces magnificent white and lavender variegated fruits, 20 to 30 cm long. They contain white flesh with a tender texture and mild flavor.

name: Antigua
desc: This highly productive Guatemalan variety produces impressive, elongated, fleshy fruits nearly 20 cm long. They ripen from green to bright red and offer a sweet flavor.

name: Antohi Romanian
desc: This ancient, vigorous variety from Romania produces an abundance of conical fruit, ripening from pale yellow to red, with a very tasty flavour.

name: Ao Daimaru
desc: This variety, originally from Japan, produces globe-shaped fruit with a flattened base, light green in color. The flesh is dense and becomes creamy when cooked, revealing a rich flavor.

name: Aoyu
desc:
This 40 cm-high heirloom variety produces an abundance of pods containing delicious green seeds. They are eaten whole, like snow peas.

In Japan, the seeds are traditionally eaten unripe, simply after a brief boiling.

name: Apache Red Sugarcane
desc: This old-fashioned variety, originally from Arizona, can easily reach 2 m in height, and offers magnificent, loose, red panicles. The sweet stem is eaten as a delicacy when the seeds are ripe.

name: Api Rouge
desc: This potato-leaf variety produces clusters of 3 to 4 small, round fruits weighing around 40 g, with juicy, sweet, fragrant flesh.

name: Quinoas Appelewa
desc: This variety is a selection of Adaptive Seeds, for its earliness, from a population of the seed company Bountiful Garden. Seeds are mainly white. Panicles are beige-gold and sometimes pink-red.

name: Apple
desc: This variety produces pointed, heart-shaped fruit, first green, then red. The flesh is very juicy, with a rich, apple-like flavor.

name: Apple Green
desc: This compact variety produces an abundance of oval, pale-green fruit. The thin skin encloses tender white flesh with a very mild flavor.

name: California poppies Apricot Chiffon
desc: This astonishing California poppy offers sublime semi-double flowers, with more or less pleated petals and soft shades of orange, peach and pink. It has won a Garden Merit Award from the Royal Horticultural Society.

name: Aquadulce
desc: This old variety, around 70 cm tall, produces numerous semi-erect pods up to 25 cm long. Each pod contains 7 to 9 light-green seeds, which turn brown when ripe.

name: Arancino
desc:
This Italian cantaloupe offers lightly ridged fruits, 15 to 20 cm in diameter, weighing from 900 g to almost 2 kg. Their orange flesh has a firm texture and a highly aromatic flavor.
It's an ancient variety from Sicily, whose name means "little orang

name: African Daisy Arctotis White
desc: Originally from South Africa, this ornamental plant produces sumptuous, daisy-like flowers with white petals and a blue-gray heart surrounded by a yellow border. Magnificent in flower bouquets!

name: Argus
desc: This traditional variety of extra-fine string bean is productive and disease-resistant, producing an abundance of fine green pods, totally free of string, around 18 cm long and with excellent eating qualities.

name: Aromatto
desc: This highly ornamental variety, close to the "Cinnamon" type, produces purple stems and beautiful dark purple inflorescences, as well as green and purple variegated serrated leaves with a spicy fragrance.

name: Arwen
desc:
Arwen is a highly productive mini bell pepper, 7 to 8 cm long. Its fruit ripens from green to dark red, passing through yellow, and offers delicious thick flesh with a fruity taste and few seeds. Perfect for snacks and appetizers!

This variety can

name: Ashe County Pimiento
desc: This 30 cm-high variety produces an abundance of round, flattened red fruits 10 cm in diameter. Their flesh is thick, tasty and sweet.

name: Ashwagandha
desc:
This species is one of the most renowned plants in Ayurvedic pharmacopoeia. It can be grown as a perennial in mild climates. It reaches up to 1 m in height and produces greenish flowers and round, orange fruits.

Its roots are used as a sedative, t

name: Astrakom
desc: This variety, one of the best adapted to cool climates, is ideally suited to container or pot growing thanks to its small size and earliness. It produces a large number of elongated, deep purple fruits.

name: Aswad
desc: Native to Iraq, this ancient variety produces large, pear-shaped, dark purple eggplants. Perfectly adapted to hot, arid climates, its dry flesh is ideal for frying or baking.

name: Attraction
desc: This very old Czech variety, of excellent quality, produces large, round apples with full, smooth, blond-green leaves. Particularly recommended for cooler regions.

name: of Milan Aubervilliers
desc: This very old, early variety produces, on a fairly short stalk, a slightly flattened apple weighing 2 to 4 kg, with strongly blistered, blue-green leaves. They have a tender texture and good flavor.

name: Augspurger
desc: This vigorous variety, slow to set seed, offers a multitude of delicate, wavy, blond-green leaves, tightly packed and forming heartless apples.

name: Millets Auksès
desc: This variety produces long, cylindrical, yellow inflorescences. It can be used before maturity to make bouquets.  When fully ripe, the seeds are easy to harvest.

name: Aunt Mary's Paste
desc: This old variety produces clusters of 3 to 4 elongated fruits weighing from 120 to 180 g, suitable for making sauces. Their flesh has a mild flavor.

name: Physalis Aunt Molly
desc: This ancient variety offers an abundance of small, firm-fleshed fruits with a flavor similar to that of a mandarin orange. Left in their calyx and stored in a dry, well-ventilated place, they keep for several weeks.

name: Aunt Ruby's German Cherry
desc:
This vigorous variety produces an abundance of clusters of 3 to 5 "beef-flesh" type fruits, iridescent green with red. They can vary widely in color, shape and flavor.

An abundant supply of water following a drought can cause the fruit to burst.

name: Aunt Ruby's German Green
desc: This vigorous variety produces the largest "beef-flesh" fruits of all green tomatoes. They are light green in color, slightly tinged with yellow-pink, weigh from 200 to 400 g and have a tendency to burst.

name: Aurora
desc: This very early Siberian variety, one of the most popular, produces bunches of 3 to 4 round, bright-red fruits weighing 60 to 90 g with dense, firm flesh.

name: Aussie
desc: This vigorous, disease-resistant variety produces an abundance of large, fleshy, ribbed "beef-flesh" fruits. Their pinkish flesh reveals a rich, sweet and slightly acidic flavor.

name: Australian Butter
desc: This old Australian variety produces large, flattened, ribbed fruits with light orange skin. Their firm, fragrant orange flesh, with a very small seed cavity, offers a flavor much appreciated in soups.

name: Australian yellow
desc: This productive, vigorous, heat-tolerant variety boasts very wavy leaves of a surprisingly bright, almost icy yellow-green.

name: Autumn and Winter Blue
desc:
This variety produces conical, deep purple, rabe-like roots, 15 to 20 cm long, with white, slightly piquant, firm, crunchy flesh. They have excellent keeping qualities.

This variety can also be sown in February/March for spring harvests.

name: Azoychka Russian
desc: This vigorous variety produces an abundance of clusters of 3 to 5 large, flattened, "beefsteak"-type fruits, weighing 200 to 500 g. Their melting, juicy orange-yellow flesh has a lemony flavor.

name: turnip Azur Star
desc: This very early variety, not very sensitive to bolting, produces magnificent, slightly flattened, blue-purple rabe. Their tender white flesh has a nutty flavor.

name: Azure
desc: This extremely prolific variety, less than 1 m high, produces clusters of 7 to 9 "large plum" type fruits, with thick skin and dense flesh. Ideal for preserves and sauces.

name: Ba-Ye-Qi
desc: This very early-maturing variety, grown for its seeds, produces panicles with large red grains. They are used to make flour and can also be cooked like rice or quinoa.

name: Pepo squash Baby Boo
desc:
Baby Boo squash is a highly decorative variety that produces numerous small, creamy-white, flattened, ribbed fruits that turn yellow when fully ripe. The flesh is also white, dense and sweet, with a chestnut-like flavor.

The fruit can be stored fo

name: Pepo squash Baby Pam
desc: This variety produces uniform, spherical fruits about 10 cm in diameter, weighing 1 to 2 kg. Their lightly ribbed orange skin encloses a sweet, dry flesh that's ideal for pastry-making and creative workshops!

name: Chickpeas Badil
desc: This particularly productive kabuli variety produces large, beige seeds with a high protein content. Hardy and disease-tolerant, it can be grown in both Mediterranean and continental zones.

name: Ballon
desc:
This old variety produces one of the most voluminous apples with an enormous core and excellent flavor. Its hardiness means it can be sown in autumn.

This variety adapts to a wide range of growing conditions, but is slow to bolt. Also known as "Ro

name: Heart pea Balloon Vine
desc: This climbing variety can reach 2 to 3 m in height. It has small white flowers and beautiful pea-sized black seeds with a white heart-shaped spot. These give the plant its name "Cardiospermum" ("heart seeds"). Inedible, they are highly decorative.

name: Bambino
desc: This dwarf, early Italian variety grows to no more than 40 cm in height. It produces a multitude of dark purple, rounded mini-aubergines, from 2.5 to 4 cm in diameter. Ideal for small spaces or pot cultivation!

name: Banana
desc: This ancient variety produces very elongated banana-shaped fruits that can reach 60 cm in length and weigh up to 4 kg. Their salmon-colored flesh has a firm texture and a sweet flavor reminiscent of ripe bananas.

name: Banana Early Sweet
desc: This old-fashioned, compact variety is adapted to cold regions or short seasons and offers conical, horn-shaped fruit, ripening from bright yellow to red, juicy and fruity in flavor.

name: Banana Legs
desc: This variety, up to 1 m tall, produces an abundance of elongated fruits weighing 45 to 70 g, resembling small, light-yellow bananas, sometimes with light-green stripes.

name: Moschata squash Banat
desc: This variety, similar to the "Musquée de Provence", has smaller, flattened, ribbed fruits weighing 2 to 3 kg. They contain a deliciously crunchy, fruity orange flesh! Ideal for small families.

name: Bangladeshi Long
desc: This magnificent, highly productive variety, originally from Bangladesh, produces beautiful, cylindrical, purple-grey fruits with dark purple stripes, devoid of any bitterness.

name: Baquieu
desc:
This ancient variety, one of the earliest and hardiest, produces a dense apple with green leaves tinged with red. They offer a crunchy texture and mild flavor.

This variety adapts to a wide range of growing conditions.

name: Barbaniaka
desc: This productive, shatter-resistant variety, originally from Hungary, has long clusters of small, round fruits weighing around 15g. Their delicious flesh reveals an intense, sweet flavor.

name: Barbentane
desc: This very old, hardy variety, with vigorous growth, is suited to regions with cool summers. It produces 8 to 10 cylindrical, 15 to 20 cm long, shiny dark purple fruits. They contain delicious, firm flesh with a sweet, fragrant flavor.

name: Basket Vee
desc: This variety is adapted to cool climates, is not very sensitive to verticillium and bursting, and produces light-red, slightly ribbed fruit with excellent shelf life and firm, sweet-tart flesh.

name: Bastidon
desc: On compact plants, Bastidon peppers produce large, elongated fruits with 3 or 4 lobes. They ripen from green to brilliant red, revealing thick, deliciously sweet flesh. Perfect for stuffed, grilled or marinated peppers!

name: Beams Yellow Pear
desc: This variety produces an abundance of small, pear-shaped fruits, 3 to 4 cm long and weighing 15 to 20g. They are not very sensitive to bursting.

name: Bear Claw
desc: This ancient variety has a long, consistent production of large, pinkish-red, variably shaped, "beef-flesh" fruits. They can weigh up to 700 g and offer dense flesh with exceptional flavor.

name: / Kales Bear Necessities
desc: This very cold-hardy variety produces fine, curly leaves that range in color from green to purple. They have a crunchy texture and excellent flavor.

name: Beautiful Arlesian
desc: This old, vigorous variety produces abundant bunches of smooth, round, firm-fleshed fruit that are not very sensitive to bursting. The foliage is reminiscent of potatoes.

name: Mallow Beauty
desc: This annual variety of the Malvaceae family produces very large pink and white flowers 7 to 10 cm in diameter on beautiful, vigorous plants.

name: Beauty Mark
desc: This variety, developed by Tom Wagner, offers medium-sized, variegated fruit. Their fragrant flesh reveals citrus notes. They can vary in color, shape and flavor.

name: Beaver Dam
desc: This old-fashioned, compact variety produces large, heart-shaped, elongated, pointed fruits that ripen from green to dark red. The flesh is renowned for its sweet, slightly spicy flavor.

name: Bedouin
desc:
This vigorous variety, even in cool climates, produces reddish-black, pear-shaped fruits weighing 120 to 180 g. It can reach a height of almost 2 metres, and its foliage is reminiscent of potatoes.
This black tomato variety originated in Russia and w

name: Belle de Lorraine
desc: This old French variety is very productive and quickly produces round, fully red fruit weighing 70-150 g, with firm, juicy, slightly acid flesh.

name: Pepo squash Bennings Green Patty Pan
desc: This variety produces up to 15 attractive, star-shaped, greenish-white fruits with thick, firm flesh. The young fruits, 5 cm in diameter, can be eaten raw or cooked, and have an artichoke-like flavor.

name: Berettina Piacentina
desc: This variety produces round, noticeably flattened, ribbed, green-grey fruits. They can weigh over 5 kg and have thick, orange flesh with good flavor, ideal for purées and soups.

name: Berlicum
desc: This variety produces long, heartless, deep-red roots. Their firm flesh has a pleasantly sweet flavor. It prefers loose soil and can be harvested until the first frost.

name: Better Devil
desc: This variety, particularly suited to early spring cultivation, is descended from "Devil's Tongue" and produces a more compact rosette. Its elongated red leaves offer a delicious crunchy texture.

name: Beurre de Rocquencourt
desc: This old, early variety, with black seeds, produces an abundance of long, tender, yellow, fleshy pods, without parchment, that are particularly tasty.

name: Zucchinis Bianca di Trieste
desc:
This old-fashioned variety produces rather short, pot-bellied, white "gourd-like" fruits. Their thick flesh offers a highly appreciated flavor.

WHAT ARE THE CHARACTERISTICS OF THE "BLANC DE TRIESTE" ZUCCHINI?

The "Blanche de Tr

name: Bianca Oval
desc:
This small variety produces ovoid fruits, pure white then yellow when fully ripe, best eaten when they reach 8 cm in length. They are rich in seeds, tender and very mild-tasting.
Reaching a height of 80 cm, this variety is perfectly suited to pot cul

name: Bianca Rotonda Sfumata Di Rosa
desc:
This old, highly productive variety produces round, white fruit with a hint of purple. Their firm flesh offers an appreciated flavor, without bitterness.

This variety is suited to a wide range of growing conditions.

name: Birdsfoot trefoil Birdsfoot Trefoil
desc: This deep-rooted, multi-year green manure fixes nitrogen and is particularly suited to calcareous soils. It flowers beautifully in yellow, is resistant to both cold and drought, and is fast-growing.

name: Biscia Rossa
desc: This very old variety, slow to set seed, has tender, tasty oak-leaf foliage with a lacy appearance and a beautiful bronze-green color.

name: Black
desc: This 60-80 cm-high variety boasts elegant single to double flowers of deep, intense violet above abundant gray foliage.

name: Black Aisberg
desc: This very old variety offers an abundance of "beef-flesh" type fruits, lightly ribbed, weighing 120 to 250 g. Their dark red-brown flesh reveals a very fruity, spicy flavor.

name: Black Amber Cane
desc:
This very precocious old variety reaches 3 m in height and produces black beans. Its canes are used to make molasses.

 

The leaf sheaths of Sorghum bicolor, rich in anthocyanin, are used as a natural red dye. This use is widespread in Africa.

name: Black And Brown Boar
desc:
This variety, with its dense foliage, produces an abundance of reddish-brown, cordate fruit with bright metallic green stripes. The flesh is dark, thick and very juicy, with a rich, black-tomato-like flavor.
This variety was developed by Brad Gates,

name: Black Aztec
desc:
This traditional variety produces beautiful ears 17 to 20 cm long, containing incredible dark purple to black seeds when ripe.

They can be eaten fresh, roasted, or dried and ground.

name: Zucchinis Black Beauty
desc:
This old variety offers an abundance of elongated fruit, best eaten before they reach over 15 cm in length. Their dark green skin encloses sweet flesh.

WHAT ARE THE 

WHAT ARE THE CHARACTERISTICS OF THE GREEN MILAN / BLACK BEAUTY ZUCCHINI?

name: Black Beauty
desc:
This variety produces fruit tinted dark indigo by the sun's action of anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants. They weigh from 150 to 300 g and contain thick, bright-red flesh with a flavor that intensifies after picking.

 

Brad, its creator, consi

name: Lentil Black Beluga
desc:
This variety, ideal for poor, chalky soils, takes its name from the caviar-like appearance of its seeds. It produces small, round, shiny black lentils that are particularly rich in protein.

Drought-resistant, this legume can be grown both for its

name: Black Centiflor Hypertress
desc:
This variety offers hundreds of flowers and therefore hundreds of small, black fruits, in huge, impressive clusters, whose flavor is much appreciated.
This variety of black cherry tomato is a spontaneous mutation selected by Graham Jenkins-Belohorska

name: Black Cherry
desc:
This productive variety produces bunches of dark purple to black cherry tomatoes weighing around 20 g over a long period. Their sweet flavor is much appreciated.
This variety has a vigorous growth habit.

name: Black Ethiopian
desc: This variety produces bunches of 7 to 12 plum-shaped fruits weighing 50 to 150 g, very dark reddish-brown in color. The flavor is sweet and rich.

name: Black From Tula
desc: This variety, with its abundant foliage, produces beautiful "beef-flesh" fruits weighing 300 to 400 g, with a dark red color and dark green shoulders. The intense reddish-brown flesh has a very sweet, rich flavor.

name: Black Mauri
desc: This variety produces an abundance of "black plum"-type fruit, weighing 50 to 70 g, with juicy, smoky-tart flesh. It can produce up to 5 kg!

name: Black Pear
desc: This highly productive variety produces an abundance of dark-red, pear-shaped fruits weighing 120-150 g. Their dense, juicy flesh is very dark reddish-brown, with a sweet, rich flavor.

name: Black Pearl
desc: This vigorous variety, 70 to 90 cm high, produces pods containing 3 to 4 black seeds. They have a tender texture and good flavor.

name: Black Pineapple
desc: This variety offers large, ribbed fruits, easily exceeding 500 g, with incredible colors ranging from green to very dark red. Their dense, marbled flesh has a sweet, spicy flavor.

name: Black Plum
desc: On large plants, this variety produces abundant clusters of dark-red, oval-shaped fruit weighing around 60 g. They contain very dark red-brown flesh and are not very sensitive to bursting.

name: Black Prince
desc: This highly ornamental variety has green and black leaves and purple flowers. It produces an abundance of conical fruits, about 5 cm long, ripening from black to red.

name: Black Roma
desc:
During the summer, this variety produces an abundance of dark red/mahogany/black fruits weighing around 90 g, with firm, non-juicy flesh. The fruits keep well and tend to detach easily. The plant can reach a height of two meters.
This is a variety pr

name: Black Round
desc: This ancient variety produces round roots, about 8 cm in diameter, with very crunchy white flesh and a strong flavor. They keep very well, even withstanding light frosts.

name: Black Sea Man
desc: This Russian variety offers round, ribbed fruit at the top, brownish-black with green stripes on the shoulders, weighing 150 to 300 g. They contain dense, juicy flesh, with a dark green gel around the seeds when fully ripe.

name: Black Shadow
desc:
This variety produces beautiful gold and red tomatoes, tinted indigo in the sun due to the action of anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants. The juicy fruits, weighing between 100 and 200g, are picked when the neck turns black.
This is a new indigo vari

name: Black Spanish Long
desc: This ancient variety produces roots up to 20 cm long, 9 cm in diameter and weighing up to 500 g. The white flesh has a crunchy texture and strong flavor.

name: Black Star
desc: This variety offers rounded, lightly ribbed fruits weighing 300 to 500 g. They are brownish-black with a dark green gel around the seeds when fully ripe. The flavor is very sweet, rich and fruity.

name: Black Tail Mountain
desc:
This productive, particularly early variety produces perfectly round fruit weighing 3 to 5 kg. Their dark-green skin, streaked with lighter shades, encloses orange-red flesh that is juicy, fragrant and fine-textured.

Suitable for both cool and hot

name: Black Zebra
desc: This variety produces beautiful, round, red-brown fruit with green and black stripes. Eaten when fully ripe, the juicy flesh delivers all the power of its rich, spicy flavor.

name: Blanc d'Astarac
desc: This drought-adapted variety from the southwest of France produces ears with white grains that are very rich in protein. They can be used as semolina or flour, or as animal feed.

name: Blanc Dur d'Hiver
desc: This turnip has conical, half-long roots with very sweet white flesh. Hardy and productive, it is suitable for late autumn sowing and has exceptional resistance to cold. Perfect for winter cultivation!

name: Blanc Petit de Paris
desc: This hardy, traditional French variety produces very short, cylindrical, creamy-white fruit. They are harvested regularly, when young and before they reach 5 cm in length.

name: Blauhilde
desc: This variety produces an abundance of cylindrical pods, around 25 cm long, which are blue-purple and turn green when cooked. The seeds are light-brown in color.

name: Blonde Boar
desc:
This highly productive variety produces 60-120 g fruits, pale yellow-white with green stripes.
Developed by Brad Gates of Wild Boar Farms in California.

name: Blonde de Berlaimont
desc: This old French variety is very hardy and produces a large apple surrounded by slightly wavy green leaves colored brown, offering a crunchy texture and mild flavor.

name: Blonde de Paris
desc:
This variety produces beautiful, firm apples with cut, blistered green leaves, a crisp texture and mild flavor.

Can be grown in spring and summer.

name: Blonde de Saint Étienne
desc: This old, rather late spring variety produces a large apple with delicious light-green, slightly blistered leaves. It is very resistant to bolting.

name: Blonde du Châtelet
desc: The apple is medium-sized. Leaves are thick and greenish-blond. Growth: 60 days.

name: Blonde Maraichère
desc: This very old French variety, slow to bolt, produces a tall, elongated rosette with delicious, crunchy, blond-green leaves.

name: Blonde Slow to Rise
desc:
Compact apple. Spatulate, long-stalked, pale-green leaves. Very good resistance to summer heat.

 This very old variety, originally from France, is described in Vilmorin-Andrieux's "Les Plantes Potagères". It is also known as "Romaine blanche d'été

name: Blondköpfchen
desc:
This bushy variety offers an abundance of clusters of 20 to 30 oval, yellow fruits. Their firm flesh, with a sweet, mild flavor, gives the sensation of bursting in the mouth.

Too much water after a drought can cause the fruit to burst.

name: Blondy White
desc:
This variety, less than 60 cm tall, is adapted to relatively cool climates. It produces pale-green fruits with a pentagonal cross-section that can be eaten raw or cooked before they reach 8 cm.

The fruits contain a mucilaginous substance that thic

name: Blood Red
desc: This early, hardy old variety produces dark-red, almost purplish, half-long roots. Their firm, orange-yellow flesh has a fragrant flavor.

name: Blue Ballet
desc:
Blue Ballet squash is a runner variety with blue-gray "Hubbard blue" spinning top-shaped fruits up to 20 cm in diameter and weighing up to 2.5 kg. The smooth skin encloses bright orange, sweet flesh.

Description of the Blue Ballet squash

Blue B

name: Blue Beauty
desc: This variety, which is not very sensitive to bursting, produces flattened "beef-flesh" type fruits, red with a very large blue-violet collar colored by the sun through the action of anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants.

name: Blue Beech
desc: This variety, well adapted to cool climates, produces beautiful, elongated "Andean horn" fruits with a sweet, rich flavor. Highly appreciated for preserves and sauces, their dense flesh contains few seeds.

name: Blue Berries
desc: This variety produces small, round, red fruits that turn violet-blue in the sun due to the action of anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants.

name: Blue Chocolate
desc: This vigorous variety, not very sensitive to shattering, offers an abundance of round, "beef-flesh" type fruits, chocolate brown tinted indigo by the sun through the action of anthocyanins, with firm, sweet flesh.

name: Blue Dawg
desc: This vigorous variety, with dense foliage, produces an abundance of 60-90 g fruits with intense red flesh. They turn indigo in the sun thanks to their anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants.

name: Starflower Blue Flowers
desc:
This melliferous species, with hairy stems and foliage, produces an abundance of blue flowers. The leaves and flowers, raw or cooked, can be eaten as a condiment or dried as an infusion.

The appearance of stains on the sachets is due to the oil co

name: Blue Gold
desc: This vigorous variety produces an abundance of sweet, fruity, golden-yellow fruit, tinted indigo by the sun through the action of anthocyanins. The flesh is red with yellow stripes, and the fruit is not very sensitive to bursting, with excellent shel

name: Blue Green Zebra
desc:
This variety offers round, sometimes ribbed, green-striped fruit with intense blue-purple shoulders and soft, rich flesh. They are high in anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants.
This is a recent variety - developed by Tom Wagner - which can display a w

name: Blue Hubbard
desc: This variety of pumpkin has 2 to 4 elongated, slightly ribbed fruits, weighing from 6 to 12 kg, with a blue-gray, warty skin. Their flesh is yellow, dry and moderately sweet, and they keep for 6 to 12 months.

name: Blue Pitts
desc: This variety produces small berries 2 to 3 cm in diameter, with a red skin tinted intense indigo when exposed to the sun, and very rich in anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants.

name: Blue Tears
desc:
This magnificent, vigorous variety, with its bluish leaves and stems, produces mahogany-red fruits 2 to 3 cm in diameter, colored indigo by the sun through the action of anthocyanins, with delicious sweet, juicy flesh.

Blue Tears, a recent variety d

name: Blush
desc: This exceptional variety produces beautiful clusters of elongated fruits, weighing 30 to 50 g and with excellent shelf life, with a pearly yellow, orange and red color. Their flesh reveals a particularly sweet tropical flavor when ripe.

name: Blushed Butter Cos
desc: This variety, close to the "Blonde maraîchère", produces crunchy, wavy, pinkish-green leaves forming an erect, elongated head.

name: Blushed Butter Oak
desc:
This cold-hardy variety produces wavy, broadly lobed foliage, similar to "Oak Leaves", with a bronze-tinged green surrounding a tender heart. Its leaves offer a juicy texture and delicate flavor.

It was developed by Frank Morton from crosses between

name: Boar's Hoof
desc: This vigorous-growing variety, with leaves around 15 cm long, produces plum- or pear-shaped, pink-red, sometimes purple fruit with a fresh flavor.

name: Boldog Hungarian Spice
desc: This ancient variety produces elongated, 2-lobed fruits, ripening from green to dark red, with a thin skin. The lower part of the fruit, dried and ground, makes a sweet, mild paprika. When fully ripe, they can be slightly strong.

name: Bonnet d'Évêque
desc: This variety produces "Rocotillo"-type fruit with an atypical shape reminiscent of a bishop's cap. They ripen from green to red and have a very fruity flavor.

name: Common Bean Borlotto
desc: This old Italian variety produces an abundance of buff-yellow pods spotted with red, 10 to 15 cm long and containing 5 to 6 delicious white seeds streaked with reddish-purple.

name: Bosque Blue
desc:
The Bosque Blue indigo blue tomato is a variety offering an abundance of 60-90 g fruits that turn indigo thanks to the presence of anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants. Their fresh flavor is highly appreciated, with a hint of vanilla.

Characteristi

name: Bosque Green Cherry
desc:
This variety produces an abundance of green fruit, turning green-yellow when fully ripe. They measure 1.5 cm to 2 cm in diameter and have a very pleasant, sweet flavor.
It is a new variety bred by Lee Goodwin in New Mexico from crosses with, among ot

name: Zucchinis Bou Tozzina
desc:
This ancient variety produces cylindrical fruits up to 25 cm long, weighing up to 500 g. Their light-green skin encloses firm, sweet, fruity flesh.

WHAT ARE THE 

CHARACTERISTICS OF THE BOU TOZZINA ZUCCHINI?

Bou Tozzina" zucchini, a member of

name: Boxer Rebellion
desc:
This compact, unstabilized variety is ideal for growing in pots or containers. It produces an abundance of elongated, orange-red variegated fruit, finely striped with green and gold, weighing around 60 g.

This recent creation by Tom Wagner is a cr

name: Brad's Atomic Grape
desc:
This dazzling variety, vigorous and productive, offers delicately sweet fruit, more or less elongated and pointed, mottled with green and purple before turning reddish-brown marbled with indigo when fully ripe.

 

Resistant to cracking and shatt

name: / Kales Branchu du Poitou
desc: This very old variety, close to the "Cavalier rouge" variety, produces a stem that divides into numerous branches bearing curly, elongated leaves.

name: Brandywine Black
desc: This vigorous variety produces 350-500 g "beef-flesh" fruits in the classic Brandywine shape, round to oval. Their very dark red-brown flesh offers an excellent, sweet, rich flavor.

name: Brandywine Off The Vine
desc: This very hardy variety produces fleshy, "beef-flesh" type fruits weighing 400 to 500 g, red with a slight orange tint at the base. The flesh is firm and juicy. The foliage is reminiscent of potatoes.

name: Brandywine Pink
desc:
This variety is one of the most popular. Its production is medium-sized and spread out. The fruit is flattened and "beef-fleshed", with a pinkish-red color. Their juicy flesh has a very fine flavor.

name: Brandywine Sudduth's Strain
desc:
This variety offers rounded, slightly flattened "beef-flesh" fruits weighing up to 1 kg. They are sensitive to bursting. Their fragrance is intense and their flavor highly appreciated.
This variety can reach 3 m in height, and its abundant potato-lik

name: Bitter eggplants Brazilian Oval Orange
desc: This rare and highly ornamental variety, with dense foliage, produces small oval fruits that turn from green to bright orange, almost red when fully ripe. Eaten young, they are delicious grilled or fried.

name: Brilliant
desc: This hardy, vigorous variety produces an abundance of thick-textured, fleshy white ribs and dark-green, flavorful leaves.

name: Brilliant
desc: This variety of oriental poppy offers large, hairy, cut leaves in shades of gray, and large, single flowers with crinkled petals in a beautiful red on hardy, perennial plants.

name: Bronze Arrowhead
desc: This easy-to-grow variety, awarded a U.S. prize in 1947, produces beautiful green and bronze foliage with a firm texture and delicious flavor.

name: Bronze Mignonnette
desc:
This old-fashioned variety has a rosette of very loose, crunchy, embossed green and bronze leaves with a delicious flavor. It is adapted to very hot climates and is slow to bolt.

The peripheral leaves are cut off, one by one, as needed. New ones d

name: Bronze Orange
desc:
This fairly compact variety grows to no more than 1.2 m in height, with up to 5 ears of delicious orange-bronze seeds. They can be eaten fresh, in the milky stage, or when fully ripe, in the form of flour.

It was developed by Alan Kapuler (Mushroom)

name: Broomstick
desc:
This variety can reach up to 2 m in height, with long golden inflorescences topped with edible bronze seeds. Its panicles are traditionally used to make brooms.

 

The anthocyanin-rich sheaths of Sorghum bicolor leaves are used as a natural red

name: Brown Cherry
desc: This highly productive variety produces red-brown, oval-shaped bunches of 8 to 16 cherry tomatoes. They have a very mild flavor.

name: Brown Flesh
desc: This unstabilized variety has dark fruits mottled with green and gold, in a variety of shapes. Most fruits are flattened, with three irregular lobes and few seeds. They are perfect for stuffing. Some fruits have a more rounded shape.

name: Brown Goldring
desc:
This variety offers a tight, tangy apple with a juicy, crisp texture. Brown leaves envelop the golden apple with its pirogue-shaped leaves.

This lettuce is high in vitamin C.

name: Brune de Gascogne
desc: This old variety from Gascony, very hardy and cold-resistant, produces a fairly loose apple with green-brown leaves, firm texture and excellent flavor.

name: Brune Percheronne
desc: This old French variety, hardy and vigorous, produces a beautiful, tight apple surrounded by smooth, green leaves veiled in pink.

name: Brunswick
desc:
This very old late variety, with a short stem, produces a tender green apple up to 30 cm wide, completely flattened on top and with a crunchy texture. Ideal for sauerkraut.

This variety, also known as "Early Flat Dutch", "de Boston" and "Early Dru

name: Buckbee's Beefsteak
desc: This variety produces small "beef-flesh" type fruits, very tasty and sometimes twisted in shape. Plants can reach over 2 m in height.

name: Budai Torpe
desc:
This old bushy variety grows to no more than 1 m tall. It produces small, dense, slightly flattened, dark-red fruits with thick, juicy flesh and a sweet flavor with a hint of acidity.
This is one of the first tomatoes in the garden.

name: Quinoas Buffy
desc:
This variety, the result of a cross between "Oro de Valle" and a Washington State University gene pool, offers panicles with golden grains.

This joint breeding effort by Frank Morton and Washington State University aims to develop productive, vigo

name: Buigh Annane
desc: This variety, developed by Tom Wagner, produces firm, round orange fruits weighing from 100 to 120 g. The flesh is sweet and juicy.

name: Bulgarian Carrot
desc: This ancient variety, originally from Bulgaria where it's called "Shipkas", offers an abundance of crunchy, fruity-flavored fruit. They grow in clusters, are carrot-like in shape and color, and ripen from green to fluorescent orange.

name: Bulgarian Triumph
desc:
This old variety has vigorous growth and well-developed, weeping foliage. It produces an abundance of clusters of 5 to 6 round fruits, with juicy flesh and a sweet, tangy flavor.

Fruits contain few seeds.

name: Bull Nose
desc: This very old, vigorous variety produces green, scarlet-red fruits that are cubic and 8 to 10 cm long when ripe. Their thick, crunchy flesh has a very sweet flavor.

name: Bull's Blood
desc: This very old, hardy variety produces ample, purple-red foliage, eaten like chard ribs, as well as a round, slightly flattened, dark-red root.

name: Buran
desc: This old Polish variety produces three-lobed peppers, green then red when ripe, around 7 cm wide and 10 cm long. Their flesh is thick and sweet, and they can be eaten green or red.

name: Moschata squash Burpee's Butterbush
desc: This original butternut variety, with its bushy habit, is ideal for small gardens. Early in the season, it produces fruits weighing 1 to 2 kg, with a deliciously rich, sweet flavor and excellent shelf life.

name: Bushel
desc:
This old variety has very large, light-green fruits, slightly pear-shaped.

The gourds can be dried in a dry, ventilated place, and can be painted, sculpted or transformed into various objects.

name: Buttercrunch
desc: This old variety, resistant to bolting and drought, produces compact apples, 10 to 12 cm in diameter, with a broad yellow-white core surrounded by thick, dark-green leaves with a reddish tinge.

name: Buttercup Bush
desc: This bushy variety produces fruit with bright orange, dense, non-fibrous flesh and a sweet flavor. Fruit can be stored for up to 6 months.

name: Butterflay
desc: This very hardy, fast-growing, late-harvesting variety has rosettes of rounded, light-green leaves. It can be grown all year round.

name: Sweet Pea Butterfly
desc:
This old, vigorous annual enjoys a very sunny position. It grows up to 3 m tall and offers an abundance of clusters of very fragrant pink flowers from summer to early autumn.

Remove spent flowers regularly to prolong flowering.

name: Moschata squash Butternut Brûlée
desc:
This variety offers tiny butternut-like fruits, about 15 cm long and weighing 500 g. Their green skin turns ochre when ripe, and their fine, yellow-orange flesh has a rich, nutty flavor. Earlier than Honey Nut, it also keeps better.

This variety,

name: Moschata squash Butternut Nutterbutter
desc: This butternut variety, selected for its earliness and resistance to mildew, produces fruits around 17 cm long and weighing 1 to 1.8 kg, with a delicious nutty flavor.

name: Moschata squash Butternut Ponca
desc: This vigorous variety, earlier than the "Waltham", produces 4 to 8 bottle-shaped fruits, up to 40 cm long and 1.5 kg. The flesh is very fine, with an exquisite hazelnut flavor. The fruit keeps for over a year.

name: Button
desc: This ornamental variety has clusters of red or white flowers and produces pods of black seeds, or purple speckled with black, or brown speckled with white.

name: Cab Yellow
desc: This hardy, compact variety grows to a height of just 80 cm. It produces an abundance of firm, rounded fruit, from 50 to 250 g, with a highly appreciated flavor.

name: Calabash Red
desc: This very old variety has flattened, strongly ribbed fruit with an unharmonious shape. They have a very thin skin and a sweet, delicious flavor.

name: Calabrese Natalino
desc: This Italian variety, renowned for its earliness, produces a beautiful, compact central apple 15 to 20 cm in diameter. Numerous side shoots then form until autumn. They're perfect steamed, velvety or au gratin!

name: Calabrian caviar
desc:
The Calabrian bell pepper, an Italian must-have, offers an abundance of conical, medium-sized fruits, erect to the sky and bright red when ripe. Prized for their exceptional, unique flavor, they can be eaten raw or cooked, dried or pickled.

This c

name: California Wonder Orange
desc: This hardy variety produces beautiful square fruits with 3 or 4 lobes, 10 to 15 cm long, green then orange when ripe. Their thick flesh offers a deliciously sweet flavor. It is a variant of the "California Wonder" variety.

name: Calwonder Early
desc: This old variety produces large fruits with 3 or 4 lobes, about 10 cm long and wide, which ripen from green to red.

name: Calwonder Golden
desc: This old variety produces an abundance of cubic fruits, around 12 cm long. They ripen from green to orange-yellow and contain thick, deliciously crunchy, sweet flesh.

name: Camelina Camelina
desc:
This very old species of the Brassicaceae family, used as a green manure, has upright stems 40 cm high, traditionally used to make brooms. Its flowers are yellow and highly melliferous.

Its powerful roots can break up clods of clay in very heavy s

name: Canabec Rose
desc: This variety from Quebec, adapted to cool climates and short seasons, produces an abundance of clusters of 5 to 6 round, pink fruits with sweet, fragrant, bright pink flesh.

name: Moschata squash Canada Crookneck
desc: This very old variety offers fruits with straight or curved necks, 25 to 45 cm long and 10 cm in diameter at the base. Their smooth skin encloses salmon-red flesh of good quality and pleasant flavor. They contain few seeds and can be stored for up to

name: Moschata squash Canada Mezoides
desc: This variety produces many butternut-like fruits, but with darker flesh and skin. Slightly grainy, their flesh is ideal for pies.

name: Canary Yellow
desc: This ancient variety produces elongated fruits of up to 2 kg with a bright yellow skin. Their white flesh has a juicy texture and a very sweet flavor.

name: Candy Mountain
desc: This early variety adapts to cool soils and produces yellow, 12-row corn that retains its sweet flavor several days after picking. The ears are about 15 cm long.

name: Candy Roaster
desc: This American variety offers large fruits of various shapes, elongated and up to 15 kg, or round and up to 7 kg. The skin can be orange or orange-green, and the flesh is very sweet and fragrant.

name: Pepo squash Candystick Dessert Delicata
desc: This variety offers large, more or less cylindrical, green-striped ochre fruits, from 900 g to over 1.3 kg. Like all Delicata, its flesh is very sweet, but this variety has an even richer, date-like flavor.

name: Canestrino Di Lucca
desc: This very old variety produces red, pear-shaped fruits weighing around 250 g, with more or less contrasting veins. They have excellent keeping qualities.

name: Common Bean Cannellini
desc: This ancient Italian variety was used in association with wheat and barley. Its pods, 12 to 15 cm long, offer 5 to 7 tasty, creamy-white, kidney-shaped, tender and highly digestible kernels.

name: Canoncito New Mexico
desc: This early variety, adapted to cool or mountainous regions, flowers very early in the season. Fruits vary in shape, from 7 to 15 cm long. They ripen from green to red and can be prepared in a variety of ways (dried, roasted, stuffed, etc.).

name: Cantaloup Charentais
desc: This ancient variety, native to the Charente region of France, produces round, smooth, pale-green fruit. Their juicy, orange flesh has a highly appreciated sweet flavor.

name: Capistrano
desc: This variety, adapted to difficult growing conditions, is very vigorous. It produces ribbed fruit with 3 to 4 lobes that ripen from green to red.

name: Rudbeckia Cappuccino
desc: This variety, which can be grown as an annual or biennial, blooms orangey-yellow with bronze highlights from July to October. Ideal for flower bouquets, it blends harmoniously into flower beds and vegetable gardens.

name: Moschata squash Carat
desc:
The Carat squash is a runner variety with smooth, fairly round, beige fruits weighing 1 to 2 kg and a particularly delicious orange flesh. Also known as "Mini-Muscade de Provence".
Characteristics of Carat moschata squash
Carat squash, also known a

name: Carbon
desc: This variety offers "beefsteak"-type fruits, dark red-brown with a gray-black sheen, weighing 250 to 400 g and resistant to shattering. They have an exceptionally rich, fruity flavor. This is one of the blackest tomatoes.

name: Cannabis Carmagnola
desc: This ancient variety is reputed to contain an abundance of CBD, from 4 to 8% (it contains no THC, or less than 0.2%), depending on analysis, terroir, ecotype... and advertising! It can therefore be used for CBD, for its fibers, for its edible seeds o

name: Carmona
desc:
This hardy, vigorous variety produces large, beautiful apples with wavy, green leaves edged in red. They offer a crisp texture and mild flavor.

In danger of extinction, this variety has been revived by Frank Morton of Wild Garden Seeds in Oregon, US

name: Caro Rich
desc: This variety offers orange-red fruit with 10 times the vitamin A content of most other varieties. Its round, slightly flattened fruits, with good flavor, weigh from 120 to 150 g.

name: Carousel
desc: This very low, almost creeping variety produces firm fruit that ripens from yellow to orange to bright red.

name: Carré de Lagnes
desc: This vigorous old French variety produces fruits weighing around 400 g, 15 cm long and 7 to 8 cm wide. They turn red when ripe.

name: Cascabel
desc: This variety, very popular in Mexico, can reach over 1 m in height and produces fruits that are reddish-brown when ripe, in the shape of smooth, round plums, 3 cm in diameter. They are best eaten dried.

name: Cascabella
desc: A magnificent ornamental variety. Each plant produces around a hundred small fruits, 3 to 4 cm long. They ripen from yellow (then very strong) to orange to red, and are most often used when yellow by lovers of very strong condiments.

name: Cascade Village Blue
desc:
This variety produces bunches of 6 to 8 fruits, 2 to 5 cm in diameter, with a red skin that turns an intense indigo in the sun thanks to their anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants.

Fruits come in a wide variety of colors, shapes and flavors.

name: Zucchinis Caserta
desc:
This highly productive Italian variety produces around 30 light-green fruits with darker stripes and firm, excellent flesh!

WHAT ARE THE CHARACTERISTICS OF THE CASERTA ZUCCHINI?

The "Caserta" zucchini, a member of the Cucurbita

name: Casper
desc: This variety, with 80-120 cm plants, produces an abundance of oval, white-ivory, tender fruit. When young, they can be eaten with the skin.

name: Cassiopeia
desc: This variety from southern Oregon, selected for its earliness and the size and number of its ears, produces magnificent multicolored kernels for popcorn.

name: Curly endive Catalogna / Puntarelle Di Galatina
desc: This old variety produces broad white ribs and elongated, serrated green leaves. They turn red with the onset of colder weather, offering a crisp texture and good flavor.

name: Curly endive Catalogna Frastagliata
desc: This hardy variety, also known as "Catalonia dandelion", has long, green, crisp leaves with a slightly bitter taste. It can be harvested as needed throughout the year.

name: Catnip
desc:
This fast-growing barley produces a clump of lanceolate leaves, around 25 cm long. Ideal for indoor cats, it provides an essential plant source for eliminating hairballs accumulated in the digestive tract.

Catnip can also be used to distract felin

name: Cayenne
desc:
This old variety produces up to 40 fruits, 15 to 25 cm long, sometimes curved, which turn red as they ripen. They are easy to dry.

This variety has a vigorous growth habit.

name: Cecei
desc:
This old variety produces an abundance of pointed fruit. They ripen from lemon-yellow to reddish-orange, and have thin flesh with a fruity flavor.
This variety has a vigorous growth habit.

name: Cekirdegi Oyali
desc: This Turkish variety offers small, round fruits weighing around 4 kg, with light-green skin striped with dark green. Their red flesh, with excellent flavor, contains magnificent seeds with extraordinary patterns.

name: Centinel
desc: This ancient Turkish variety offers a myriad of very fine, elongated fruits, 10 to 15 cm long, deep red when ripe. They can be used both fresh and dried.

name: Celosia Century Mix
desc: This early, vigorous, easy-to-grow variety produces magnificent plumes, around 30 cm high, in various shades of pink, red and orange. Ideal for making flower bouquets!

name: Champagne

name: Charleston Belle
desc:
This compact variety, suitable for pot cultivation, offers a multitude of cubic, green fruits, then red when ripe, 7 to 8 cm wide and long.

 

It was the first nematode-resistant bell pepper introduced by Southern Exposure Seed Exchange in 1998.

name: Charlie Green
desc: This vigorous variety produces numerous bunches of 4 to 6 beefsteak-like fruits, flattened and ribbed at the top, averaging 250 to 600 g in weight. Their firm, emerald-green flesh has an excellent, very mild, sweet flavor.

name: Charnue de Huy
desc: This old Belgian variety produces particularly fleshy, smooth, round red fruits weighing around 200-300g.

name: Chartreuse / Butter Tongue
desc: This very unusual variety produces bright yellow-green pointed leaves enveloping a voluminous, swirling apple.

name: Cherokee Chocolate
desc: This variety, with dense foliage and low susceptibility to disease, produces a large number of brown fruits with green streaks on the shoulders. The flesh is very juicy, with a rich, sweet flavor and few seeds.

name: Cherokee Green
desc: This variety, considered by some to be the best green tomato, produces ribbed, green fruits mottled with yellow when ripe, with a thin skin. The flesh is juicy and relatively thick, with excellent flavor.

name: Cherokee Purple
desc:
This ancient variety adapts well to all climates, especially dry seasons. It has abundant foliage and produces fruit with juicy flesh shaded in brown, purple and pink.

After a period of drought, the fruit is susceptible to bursting if abundant wat

name: Cherriots Of Fire
desc:
This magnificent, very vigorous variety, developed by Tom Wagner, offers a multitude of small, variegated, slightly stretched fruits. They take on an almost pearly sheen when ripe, revealing juicy flesh with good flavor.
This variety is the result of

name: Cherry Chadwick
desc: This old, hardy American variety produces an abundance of clusters of around 6 large, round, bright-red "cherry tomatoes" with a sweet flavor. It grows vigorously and reaches over 1.50 m in height.

name: Cherry Delight
desc: This beautiful tall American variety produces a multitude of small red "cherry tomatoes", weighing around 30 g and appreciated for their flavor.

name: Cherry Ghost
desc:
This old-fashioned variety produces an abundance of large "cherry tomatoes" weighing around 40 g, white with yellow and red tints, and with a rounded, somewhat angular shape. The skin is slightly downy, and the flesh is sweet-tasting.
This 1.50 m tal

name: Cherry Glow
desc: This variety, a giant version of the charming field poppy, has beautiful, silky flowers of intense cherry red with black mottling on bluish-green foliage.

name: Cherry Stone
desc:
This productive variety produces a myriad of small, ovoid tomatoes weighing around 15 g, with bright red skin and very sweet flesh.

It's a brand-new Tom Wagner obtention.

name: Cherry Time
desc: This vigorous variety produces an abundance of conical "cherry peppers" that ripen from dark green to dark red. The thick flesh has a very fruity flavor.

name: Chervena Chujski
desc: This traditional, vigorous-growing variety produces elongated, pointed fruit with bright red, very sweet flesh. They ripen from green to brown to red when fully ripe.

name: Chestnut Chocolate
desc:
This hardy variety produces an abundance of round, indigo-tinted red fruits weighing 30 to 60 g. The flavor is fruity and sweet. The flavor is fruity. They have great storage capacity on the plant and after harvest.
This variety was developed by Brad

name: Chicon de Vendée
desc: This very old, particularly hardy variety produces an elongated, very large head. Its tasty red-green leaves offer a crunchy texture.

name: Chicon des Charentes
desc: This ancient, hardy variety can be grown in all seasons and produces large, elongated, light-green apples with tender, flavorful leaves.

name: Chicon Pomme En Terre
desc: This old, hardy variety produces a beautiful small apple with very fleshy green leaves. Excellent flavor.

name: Chinese Five Colors
desc: This highly ornamental Chinese variety gets its name from its conical, upright fruits, which ripen from purple to red, cream, yellow and orange. At all stages of ripening, they are appreciated for their spicy flavor.

name: Bitter eggplants Chinese red
desc: This ancient variety produces small, round, ribbed red fruits, 5 cm in diameter. They have a fine texture and a fairly bitter flavor, particularly appreciated in Asian cuisine.

name: Chinese White Sword
desc: This productive variety, around 60 cm tall, offers an abundance of slender, white fruit. They can reach 15 cm in length, and reveal fine, sweet-tasting flesh.

name: Chioggia
desc:
This ancient variety produces an abundance of round roots with an intense red epidermis and pink flesh veined with white. De Chioggia beet has a firm texture and a mild, sweet flavor that is highly appreciated in cooking.

name: Moschata squash Chirimen
desc: This ancient Japanese variety produces flattened, heavily ribbed fruits weighing 3 to 5 kg, with a very warty skin and a bronze-orange color when ripe. Their bright orange flesh is sweet and fragrant.

name: Chives
desc:
This species forms clumps of round-sectioned, thin, hollow leaves 15 to 25 cm long. Its pink, onion-flavored flowers appear in abundance in early summer.

If the plants are cut back before the seeds have set, a second bloom may appear at the end of

name: Chocolate Beauty
desc: This ancient variety produces large, elongated, ribbed fruits that ripen from bright green to chocolate, with thick, fragrant flesh and a sweet taste.

name: Chocolate Cherry
desc:
Throughout the summer, this variety produces beautiful cherry tomatoes, weighing from 10 to 20 g, in various shades of red, purple and chocolate, depending on ripeness. They reveal a rich, sweet, fruity flavor, and are well suited to drying.

It was

name: Chou de Naples
desc:
This old, hardy variety is slow to bolt. It produces a large, almost flat head with light-green, slightly blistered leaves reminiscent of cabbage. They offer a crunchy texture and good flavor.
This variety originated in England. It was introduced by

name: Chris Cross
desc: This almost extinct variety is a cross between "Hawksbury" and "Dixie Queen". It produces fruits weighing from 6 to 9 kg, with dark green and light green striped skin. Their crisp, red flesh has a fragrant flavor.

name: Cimmaron
desc: This very old variety, slow to bolt, produces a beautiful head 25 to 30 cm high with green leaves broadly tinged with dark red to brown, smooth, crisp and sweet-tasting.

name: Cinderella
desc:
This old-fashioned squash variety has 1 to 4 bright red, slightly flattened, strongly ribbed fruits, weighing from 4 to 20 kg. Their orange flesh is tender and rich in water. They keep for 2 to 4 months.

name: Lamb's lettuce Cirilla
desc: Cirilla is a very early variety of lamb's lettuce, producing rosettes of small, dark-green, thick, round, lightly-veined leaves. It can be sown all year round, providing delicious leaves throughout the seasons!

name: Physalis Cisineros
desc: This ancient variety, also known as Mexican tomatillo, produces large cape gooseberries with a delicate citrus flavor. The apple-green fruits are traditionally used in Mexico in the preparation of Salsa Verde.

name: Clackamas Blueberry
desc: This variety produces large, round, slightly flattened cherry tomatoes, indigo in color and very rich in anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants. Their bright red flesh reveals an excellent aromatic flavor.

name: Clear Pink
desc:
This old Russian variety, adapted to cool climates, produces light-pink, round, smooth fruits weighing 90 to 150 g. They contain few seeds and their juicy flesh has a sweet, slightly acid taste.

It was presented at the Seeds Saver Exchange in 1995 a

name: Garlic bulbs Cledor" AB Pink Garlic Bulbs - 250 g
desc:
Cledor" pink garlic will be available from the second half of February.

name: Clemson Spineless
desc: This vigorous, heat-demanding variety produces an abundance of smooth, angular fruits. They contain a mucilaginous substance, ideal for thickening sauces or stews, and are eaten raw or cooked before they reach 8 cm in length.

name: Climbing Green
desc: This ancient variety produces round fruits weighing 500-800 g. Their dark green, embroidered skin encloses green flesh with a very melting texture, extremely juicy and sweet.

name: Cochiti
desc: This ancient variety, developed by the Cochiti Pueblo people along the Rio Grande centuries ago, offers an abundance of elongated fruits measuring around 3 cm, ripening from green to red.

name: Common Bean Coco Blanc Nain Précoce
desc: This ancient variety produces an abundance of broad, flat pods, around 12 cm long and containing 5 to 6 oval, white seeds. Both the fresh pods and the fresh or dried seeds are eaten.

name: Common Bean Coco Jaune du Limousin
desc: This hardy, drought-resistant variety, native to the Limousin region, produces an abundance of cylindrical pods filled with 5 to 6 small, ovoid, yellow seeds, much appreciated for their rich flavor.

name: Cocopah
desc: This very old, early variety, native to southwestern Arizona, produces magnificent ears of delicious white, pink, blue and yellow seeds.

name: Zucchinis Cocozelle
desc:
This very old variety offers a large number of fruits, best eaten before they reach 20 cm in length. Their dark-green skin, streaked with lighter shades, encloses a tender, white flesh that is much appreciated.

CHARACTERISTICS OF

name: Codair
desc: This highly productive, hardy variety produces long, straight green pods, lightly mottled with purple, stringless, tender and fleshy, with excellent eating qualities.

name: Codonopsis Codonopsis pilosula
desc:
This highly medicinal species, from the Campanulaceae family, offers plants with voluble stems up to two meters long, bearing bell-shaped yellow-green flowers.

 

It's the 30 cm roots that are used in Chinese medicine, which calls it "the poor m

name: Quinoas Colorado Black Shelly 25
desc: This vigorous variety, adapted to short seasons and not very sensitive to cold, has been grown in Colorado for a quarter of a century. It produces large panicles with an abundance of multicolored seeds, though the majority are black.

name: Contender
desc: This very early old variety produces an abundance of green, threadless pods, 10 to 15 cm long. At the end of the season, they contain brown and ochre variegated seeds.

name: Copenhagen Market
desc:
This old variety produces compact, firm, dense apples up to 20 cm in diameter and weighing up to 2 kg. They are covered with light-green leaves. The variety is not very sensitive to bolting.

This variety adapts to a wide range of growing condition

name: Copia
desc: This variety, a cross between "Marvel Striped" and "Green Zebra", produces large, "beef-fleshed" fruits with gold and red stripes. They weigh from 150 to 300 g and are sweet and juicy.

name: Copper Currant
desc:
This variety offers an abundance of small "cherry tomatoes", about 1.5 cm in diameter. It is one of Lee's sweetest cherry tomatoes. The flesh is red and the seeds are surrounded by a green gel.
It's a new variety bred by Lee Goodwin in New Mexico fro

name: Lamb's lettuce Coquille de Louviers
desc: This ancient, cold-hardy French variety produces a small rosette of dark-green, spoon-shaped leaves with a fine texture and mild flavor.

name: Coral
desc:
This very high-yielding variety can be grown for grain, as a green manure or for syrup production. Its thick, sweet stalks, over 2.5 m tall, bear panicles with large purple grains when fully ripe.

 

A little difficult to thresh, but very tasty,

name: Coreopsis Coreopsis grandiflora
desc: This bushy species grows up to 50 cm tall and produces smooth, upright stems bearing, from spring to autumn, large flowers with yellow petals and orange-yellow centers, around 5 cm in diameter.

name: Curly endive Cornet d'Anjou
desc: This very old variety forms a tall, elongated apple with leaves that are long-serrated at the tip. It is suited to autumn harvesting. It is therefore important to protect it in cold regions.

name: Curly endive Cornet de Bordeaux
desc:
This old variety is highly resistant to cold. It forms a cone with long, dark-green, slightly wavy leaves, a firm, crunchy texture and a strong flavor.

This variety keeps well under cover.

name: Curly endive Cornet de Loire
desc: This very old variety forms a tall, elongated apple with leaves that are long-serrated at the tip.

name: Corno di Toro Giallo
desc: Corno di Toro Giallo sweet yellow pepper, also known as "Yellow Bull's Horn", is an ancient variety that produces an abundance of very juicy, horn-shaped fruits, ripening from green to yellow-orange. Perfect for stuffing, they offer a highly apprecia

name: Corrogo
desc:
This old variety requires a lot of heat. It produces abundant clusters of 3 to 5 "Calabash"-type fruits, flattened and very ribbed. The firm, juicy flesh has a pronounced flavor.

This variety can also be grown for its decorative value.

name: Cosimo Purple Red White
desc:
This variety, with its cut foliage, has a compact habit and doesn't grow taller than 60 cm. It quickly produces an abundance of raspberry-pink flowers, with varying shades of white, over a very long period.

 

Cosmos are a favorite with birds. T

name: Cosmic Red
desc:
A magnificent variety with double flowers, red tinged with yellow in the center. They measure 5 cm in diameter and the plants are no taller than 60 cm.

This species is very popular with birds. The flowers can be left to dry in autumn, so that they

name: Costoluto Di Chivasso
desc: This ancient variety, originally from Piedmont in Italy, produces beautiful flattened, ribbed, red fruits weighing 300 to 350 g. Suitable for fresh consumption in salads.

name: Costoluto Fiorentino
desc: This old, highly productive variety, originally from Florence, offers around fifty dense, juicy red "beef-flesh" fruits. Strongly ribbed on the upper half, they weigh between 180 and 250 g and can be eaten fresh or preserved.

name: Cote d'Zebra
desc:
This variety offers large "Green Zebra"-type fruits with orange flesh.
It is a recent variety developed by Tom Wagner. It was selected from crosses of "Green Zebra", "Turk's Turban" and "Sundrop".

name: Cougar
desc: This variety produces a beautiful lettuce with crisp leaves, bronze-yellow tinged tips and a slightly crinkled appearance. Selected by Frank Morton, it is the result of a cross with the "Brown Goldring" variety.

name: Common Bean Countess de Chambord
desc: This variety produces numerous flattened, pointed pods - around 8 cm long and suitable for eating young as green beans. They contain 4 to 6 oval, thin-skinned, white beans, slightly tinged with green.

name: Country Gentleman
desc: Plants of this ancient variety reach over 2 m in height. They feature long, 20 cm ears of milky-white, irregularly-arranged kernels. They are sweet, full-flavored and perfect for grilling.

name: Cow Horn
desc:
This ancient, heat-demanding variety can reach over 2 m in height and produces green, horn-shaped fruit. They are eaten immature, raw or cooked, before they reach 8 cm in length.

 

The fruits contain a mucilaginous substance ideal for thickenin

name: Craquerelle du Midi
desc:
This very old variety has dark green, slightly lobed leaves with a thick, crunchy texture. It has excellent resistance to cold and bolting.

Characteristics of Craquante d'Avignon lettuce

Craquante d'Avignon or Craquerelle du Midi, Lactuca sativ

name: Cream Of Saskatchewan
desc:
This ancient variety, perfectly adapted to cool climates, produces an abundance of beautiful round fruits, 35 cm in diameter and weighing 3 to 6 kg, with a speckled green skin. Their juicy, pale-yellow flesh has a very mild, sweet flavor.

 

Thi

name: Cream Sausage
desc: This highly productive variety produces bunches of 6 to 8 yellowish-white, oval-shaped fruits, reminiscent of a sausage, hence its name. It is not very sensitive to drought or mildew.

name: Crenshaw
desc: Adapted to hot, dry climates, this variety produces an abundance of beautiful oval fruit with acid yellow skin. Their salmon-colored flesh is highly aromatic.

name: Crimean Black
desc:
The Crimean Black tomato is a vigorous variety with an abundance of round, dense, red-brown flesh that darkens in the sun. It has a very mild, acid-free taste.

The variety is not very sensitive to drought.

Characteristics of the Black Crimean t

name: Crimson Sweet
desc:
This variety produces very short, almost round fruit. Their thin skin is striped with dark and light green. The flesh has a fine texture and a very mild flavor.

This variety has low susceptibility to fusarium and anthracnose. It is a cross between

name: Criolla de Cocina
desc:
The fruits of this variety have the appearance of a Habanero but are completely sweet, very fruity and fragrant. They measure 10 cm in length and have a thin skin. Its earliness makes it well suited to cool regions.
This variety originated in Nicarag

name: Crisp Mint
desc:
This vigorous variety produces an upright, compact apple 25 cm high. The thick leaves offer excellent flavor.
This old variety originated in the United States.

name: Crnkovic Yougoslavian Pink
desc:
This old variety produces bunches of 3 to 6 fleshy, juicy "beef-flesh" fruits weighing over 500g. They are not very sensitive to bursting, keep well and are suitable for drying.

This vigorous variety is not very susceptible to disease.

name: Cuban Black
desc: This variety produces clusters of 2 to 5 "beef-flesh" type fruits, mahogany red with dark brown highlights and ribbed green shoulders. Their flesh is very rich in vitamin C and has a mild flavor.

name: Cubanelle
desc: This ancient variety, very popular as a frying bell pepper, continually produces an abundance of thin-skinned, yellow-green fruit that turns orange-red when ripe. The flesh is thick and sweet, with an incomparable flavor.

name: Cucuzza Longa Serpente
desc: This magnificent variety offers an abundance of edible fruit, 1 m long, which is highly prized in Sicilian cuisine. They are eaten like zucchini, when they reach 40 cm. The apical stems with young leaves (called "tenerumi", tendrons) are also eaten i

name: Cuneo
desc: This variety produces beautiful, heart-shaped, bright golden-yellow fruits with 3 or 4 lobes, ending slightly in points. Their very thick flesh offers a firm, crunchy texture.

name: Cupid
desc: This vigorous, hardy variety produces fine pods, 16 to 20 cm long, with no threads as they grow. Of excellent taste, fresh, frozen or jarred, they can be harvested young, at the fillet or mangetout stage.

name: Cyklon
desc: This Polish variety offers an abundance of conical, slightly curved fruits that are not persistent in their pungency. They ripen from green to red and dry easily, thanks to their fine flesh, to make paprika.

name: Cylindra
desc: This ancient Danish variety produces cylindrical roots, 15 cm long, with easy-to-peel skin. The flesh has a fine texture and a very sweet taste.

name: D'Espelette / Gorria
desc:
This very famous, ancient and vigorous variety comes from the Basque country. It produces a large number of conical, elongated, sometimes slightly curved, thin-skinned fruits. They contain thick flesh with a distinctive fruity flavor.

Piment d'Esp

name: Chervil D'Hiver de Bruxelles
desc: Winter chervil is an early, very vigorous and hardy variety. It offers highly aromatic foliage with a fine flavor and goes well with a multitude of dishes. Frost-resistant, it is ideally suited to regions with late winters.

name: Dancing With The Smurfs
desc: This variety, bred by Tom Wagner, produces two-metre-high plants bearing red and indigo cherry tomatoes.

name: Danish Flag
desc: This variety has slightly silvery foliage and upright stems, around 1 m tall, topped by single flowers with bright red and white bicolored petals and pale yellow hearts.

name: Dark Green
desc: This variety produces apples with light to dark green leaves. They are very fleshy and sweet-tasting.

name: Zucchinis Dark Green
desc:
This variety produces an abundance of fruit up to 30 cm long, very dark green with firm, light-colored flesh.

WHAT ARE THE 

CHARACTERISTICS OF THE Verte Noire Maraichère zucchini?

The "Verte Noire Maraichère" zucchini, a member of the Cucurb

name: Celosia Dark Pink Plume
desc: This variety, with its astonishing and attractive flowering, offers magnificent dark pink conical "plumes" that will create a sensation in flowerbeds from July until the first frosts!

name: Dark Queen
desc:
This heat-adapted variety produces dense foliage and flattened, beefsteak-like fruit with variegated purple and metallic green stripes, weighing 200 to 500 g and with a fruity flavor.
It is very similar to "Pink Berkeley Tie-Dye", also developed by B

name: Zucchinis Dark Star
desc:
This large variety has no stinging hairs and is highly resistant to drought. It produces elongated, smooth, dark-green fruits. The flesh is light and tender, with a pleasant flavor.

WHAT ARE THE 

CHARACTERISTICS OF THE Dark Star zucchini?

Th

name: Brussels sprouts Darkmar
desc: This variety produces firm, dense, dark-green apples with good flavor. Harvesting can be spread over a long period.

name: Datterini
desc: This vigorous variety, with bushy foliage, can reach 2 m in height and produces clusters of 12 date-sized and date-shaped fruits. Their flesh contains few seeds and has a very mild, sweet flavor.

name: Quinoas Dave 407
desc:
This short-season variety can reach 1.50 m and produces orange panicles containing an abundance of yellow-brown seeds.

This variety was named in honor of Dave Cusack, who introduced quinoa to the USA.

name: / Kales Dazzling Blue
desc:
This variety is very cold-hardy. It produces a rosette of elongated, waffle-shaped, blue-green leaves with pink veins and good flavor.

It is a recent varietal creation by Frank Morton in Oregon from various Lacinato strains.

name: De Barao Jaune
desc: Adapted to cool or short-season regions and particularly vigorous, this variety offers an abundance of delicious plum-shaped yellow berries. With their paste-like flesh, they are excellent in coulis, preserves or dried.

name: De Berao
desc: This vigorous variety is called "tree tomato" because it can reach 5 m in height. It produces a large number of firm, dense, orange-red, sweet-tasting "Roma"-type fruits. Excellent keeping qualities!

name: De Bresse
desc: Ideal for pot cultivation, this old French variety grows to no more than 50 cm tall, and thrives in both hot and cool weather. The fruit is conical with firm flesh, red when fully ripe.

name: De Tremont
desc:
This old, hardy French variety, also known as "Goutte de Sang", produces a large head in late winter, with blond-green leaves spotted with brown and a crunchy, mild flavor.

This variety can also be grown as a spring lettuce.

name: Déhibé
desc: This variety, originally from Syria, produces beautiful, round, dark-green fruits 15 to 20 cm in diameter, with intense red flesh that is very refreshing.

name: Hibiscus Dekkan hemp
desc:
This annual Malvacea, also known as Kenaf, produces plants 1.5 to 2 m high. They bear magnificent, solitary flowers with silky, white-yellow petals spotted with purple at the base.

Traditionally cultivated for its fibers, it was used to make paper

name: Pepo squash Delicata
desc: This old variety produces green-striped cream fruit, up to 20 cm long and weighing almost 1 kg. Its firm, yellow-orange flesh is reminiscent of reinette apples when raw, and of chestnuts when cooked.

name: Pepo squash Delicata Zeppelin
desc:
This variety produces 500 g oblong fruits with green-striped ivory skin. These stripes turn light orange during storage, and the ivory can sometimes turn yellow. Their orange flesh has a very sweet flavor.

Fruit can be eaten up to mid-January.

name: Délice d'Or
desc: This compact variety produces yellow-orange fruits 6 to 8 cm in diameter, weighing 90 to 120 g and containing few seeds. Their firm skin is not sensitive to bursting, and encloses a sweet-tasting flesh that is much appreciated.

name: Délice de la Table
desc: This old French variety produces very ribbed, orange-yellow fruits when ripe, looking like small pumpkins, weighing 250 to 500 g. Their soft, orange flesh offers excellent sweetness.

name: Delicious 51
desc: This early variety, resistant to pathologies found in cooler regions, produces slightly ribbed, round to oval fruit with sweet orange flesh.

name: Delicious by Burpee
desc: This variety, which is not very sensitive to shattering, produces large, flat "beef-flesh" type fruits, weighing from 500 g to 1 kg and containing few seeds. Their firm, juicy flesh has a sweet, tangy flavor.

name: Delinel
desc: This hardy, highly resistant variety produces an abundance of long, green pods, 16 to 18 cm long, with no threads and black seeds. It keeps well both sterilized and frozen.

name: Curly endive Demi-Hâtive de Malines
desc: This old Belgian variety produces dense, relatively elongated endives. They can be forged in a cellar or in the ground.

name: Density Yellow
desc: This long-lasting variety offers an abundance of elongated bulbs, around 10 cm in diameter, golden yellow in color and juicy in texture.

name: Rudbeckia Denver Daisy
desc:
Until the first frosts, this variety offers an abundance of enormous golden flowers with a brown central cone, illuminated by a rust-colored ring, around 15 cm in diameter. Perfect for bouquets.
It was selected in honor of the 150th anniversary of th

name: Des Andes Orange
desc: This variety produces elongated, horn-shaped fruits, like those of the essential "Andean tomato". Their firm, juicy flesh contains few seeds and is ideal for salads or coulis.

name: Desert King
desc:
This extremely heat-resistant variety is considered the most resistant to desert conditions. Fruits are almost round with a very light green skin. The yellow flesh offers a highly appreciated flavor.

This variety keeps very well: the fruit can rem

name: Desmodium Desmodium canadense
desc: This perennial species can reach 1.20 m in height and, from August to October, produces an abundant inflorescence of pale pink flowers followed, after fertilization, by very attractive pods containing 4 to 5 seeds.

name: Desmodium Desmodium sessilifolium
desc: This perennial species can grow up to 1 m tall and produces an abundance of pink or white flower panicles from August to October.

name: Detroit Dark Red
desc:
This old variety produces upright, light green foliage with dark red veins. It has round, sometimes oval, red roots.

Harvesting can begin 3 months after sowing.

name: Devil's Ears
desc:
This very old French variety produces arrow-shaped, pointed leaves of a lovely garnet-red, crisp texture.

The peripheral leaves are cut off, one by one, as needed. New ones develop after successive harvests.

name: Devil's Tongue
desc: This variety has a loose rosette of fairly thick, very slender leaves, up to 17 cm long, with an intense, dark red color. It was developed by Franck Morton and the color of the seeds, still heterogeneous, can be black or white.

name: Dewako One Bite
desc: This Japanese variety owes its name to the small, purple-black fruits, weighing around 10 grams. They are ideal for pickling and can be eaten in a single bite.

name: Di Bagnacavallo
desc:
This old Italian variety produces large, rounded, light-green fruits with dark-green stripes, weighing 6 to 10 kg. They contain tasty, sweet pink flesh. It was saved thanks to Eugenio Mingozzi's selection work using seeds found in Canada.

 

Thi

name: Di Faenza
desc: This variety produces round fruits weighing 3 to 6 kilos, with green skin and particularly crunchy red flesh.

name: Diamond
desc: This old-fashioned, highly productive variety produces clusters of 4 to 6 slender, 15 to 20 cm-long fruits with a very good, bitter-free flavor. The small size of its plants, no more than 60 cm high, makes it perfect for container cultivation.

name: Moschata squash Dickinson
desc: This old American variety produces rounded, slightly ribbed fruit, 45 cm long and weighing up to 20 kg. Their ochre skin encloses sweet-tasting orange flesh.

name: Dix Doigts de Naples
desc: This old variety produces bunches of oval, elongated "San Marzano"-type fruit, weighing 50 to 100 g and suitable for canning. Their firm flesh contains few seeds and little juice.

name: Doe Hill Golden Bell
desc: This old variety has a compact habit and produces an abundance of yellow-orange fruit, with thick, crunchy flesh and a very sweet taste. They are flattened and slightly variable in shape.

name: Dolciva (Improved Nantaise)
desc:
This variety produces smooth, cylindrical, thick roots. Their orange skin contains little heart. They keep very well over winter.
Characteristics of the improved Nantaise Dolciva carrot
Dolciva carrot, Daucus carota, is an improved Nantaise variety

name: Moschata squash Doran Round
desc: This unique variety of butternut, originally from the Netherlands, produces small, more or less rounded fruits weighing 1 to 2 kg. They keep for over 1 year in good conditions, and their sweet flesh improves with age.

name: Dorothy Green
desc: This old-fashioned variety offers flattened, "beef-fleshed" fruit, amber-green with a slight pink tinge when ripe. They weigh from 350 to 500 g. Their very firm flesh is excellent, sweet and spicy.

name: Dottenfelder Dauer
desc: This variety produces a short-stemmed, very firm, oval apple that is resistant to shattering. Excellent for preserving, it is particularly suitable for making sauerkraut.

name: Chrysanthemum Double Garland
desc:
This variety has young shoots, leaves and mostly yellow double flowers, with a strong flavor reminiscent of watercress and juniper berries. In traditional Chinese and Japanese cuisine, they are eaten raw, in salads, or cooked, in stir-fries.
This var

name: Double Red
desc: This variety has reddish-purple stems, leaves, bristles and spikes. The red seeds are rich in anthocyanins, a powerful antioxidant.

name: Double Rich
desc: This productive variety, adapted to cool regions, produces delicious, vitamin C-rich fruit, weighing 200 to 500 g, with firm flesh and few seeds. Ideal for making juices and preserves.

name: Double Standard
desc:
This very early variety, adapted to cool regions, produces ears around 15 cm long containing 12 to 14 rows of white and yellow seeds.
white and yellow seeds.

It was developed by Johnny's Seeds from two old varieties: "Burnell", a yellow variety orig

name: Double Yield
desc:
This extremely prolific variety produces dark green fruits, around 15 cm long when ripe. They can be eaten fresh, like a cucumber, or pickled in vinegar.

It was introduced in 1924 by the Joseph Harris Company of Coldwater, New York, with the claim t

name: Peas Douce Provence
desc: This dwarf variety produces an abundance of pods containing 7 to 9 smooth seeds. The seeds have a sweet, juicy, fruity flavor. They can be used fresh or dried.

name: Dourga
desc:
This hardy variety produces an abundance of white-ivory fruit up to 20 cm long and weighing up to 600 g. The flesh is fine and the flavor mild.

This variety has a low susceptibility to disease.

name: Doux d’Espagne
desc: This highly productive variety produces beautiful, fleshy, slightly lobed fruits, 15 to 20 cm long. They can be eaten green or red when ripe, and are ideal for stuffing.

name: Dr. Carolyn
