desc: This vigorous, highly branched variety produces an abundance of cherry tomatoes, about 2.5 cm in diameter, ivory in color, turning pale yellow when ripe. They have a rich, sweet-tart flavor.

name: Dracocephalum Dracocephalum moldavicum
desc:
This medicinal species features long, blue flower stalks on foliage with a powerful lemongrass fragrance. Its leaves and flowers, delicious in herbal teas, also flavour any dish.

 

When you welcome the Dracocephalus of Moldavia into your garden

name: Celosia Dracula
desc: This cockscomb-type variety grows from 30 to 40 cm tall, with foliage that changes from green to dark purple as the plants grow, and incredible deep-red inflorescences.

name: Dragon Tongue
desc: The Dragon's Tongue bean, an ancient variety native to the Netherlands, produces an abundance of flattened, pale-yellow pods streaked with purple, around 15 cm long. They are best eaten fresh, after quick cooking.

name: Druzba
desc: This robust old variety produces an abundance of round, juicy tomatoes, weighing from 200 to 400 g. It has very resistant foliage and its fruit has a slight tendency to burst in wet weather.

name: Dulce de Horno
desc: This Spanish variety produces deeply ribbed, cheese-stone-shaped fruits weighing 5 to 7 kg, which turn orange and then ochre when fully ripe. Their sweet flesh is traditionally used to make pies.

name: Dwarf Champion
desc: This vigorous, compact variety produces round, ribbed, reddish-purple "beef-flesh" fruits weighing 150 to 200 g. Their melt-in-the-mouth flesh, containing few seeds, has a sweet, tangy flavor.

name: Dwarf Grinch Cherry
desc:
This variety, ideal for cultivation in pots or containers, offers a multitude of small, sweet-tasting, cocktail-like, light-green fruits that should be picked when they are draped in a yellow veil, the sign of optimum ripeness.

 

Its vegetation

name: Dwarf Mayo
desc:
On plants 1 metre or slightly taller, this early variety produces panicles with a rich, deep reddish-brown color.

The anthocyanin-rich sheaths of Sorghum bicolor leaves are used as a natural red dye. This use is widespread in Africa.

name: Coreopsis Dyer's coreopsis
desc: This easy-to-grow annual produces upright stems up to 1 m high, and all summer long, flowers with dark centers and cut yellow and reddish-brown petals.

name: Isatis Dyer's Woad
desc: This tinctorial species, also known as "Woad", can grow to over a metre in height and, in its 2nd year, develops a strong stem with numerous small yellow flowers, grouped in large decorative clusters.

name: turnip Dyna
desc: This hardy variety, suitable for autumn cultivation, produces large, highly decorative purple turnips. They have firm flesh and keep very well.

name: Early Cherry
desc:
This compact variety produces an abundance of small, round to oval red fruits weighing around 40 g over a period of 3 to 4 weeks, even in cold, rainy summers.

Characteristics of the Early Cherry red cherry tomato

The Early Cherry red tomato, So

name: Early Dwarf Red
desc:
This very old, early variety, adapted to cool climates and ideal for forced cultivation, produces clusters of 3 to 6 small, ribbed, red fruits.

This very old variety is described in James J. H. Gregory's "Annual Circular, Retail Catalogue, Warranted

name: Early Moon Beam
desc: This variety produces fruits weighing 2 to 4 kg with smooth, green skin flecked with white. Their yellow flesh has a firm texture.

name: Early Pearl
desc: This very old American variety, 1.5 to 2 m tall, produces 15 cm-long spikes made up of 12 to 14 rows of delicious white seeds. Each plant produces 2 spikes, the second of which is often larger than the first.

name: Early Prescott
desc: This very old French variety produces spherical, ribbed "Cantaloup"-type fruits weighing 800 g to 1 kg, with green-mottled, slightly warty skin. The flesh is orange, firm and juicy, with a mild, sweet flavor.

name: Early Siberian
desc: This productive variety produces clusters of 3 to 5 red, round, slightly flattened, ribbed fruits, weighing 50 to 200g. Their firm flesh reveals a sweet flavor.

name: Early Sue
desc: This variety offers round, dark-red fruit with orange streaks. Very low susceptibility to mildew.

name: Early Wonder
desc: This old-fashioned variety produces large, glossy, highly appreciated foliage and 10 cm-diameter rounded roots with smooth, red skin. Their dark red flesh offers a firm texture.

name: Easter Rose
desc:
The Ostergruss radish, or Pink Easter radish, produces pink, pointed roots 15 to 20 cm long. Their white, crunchy flesh has a very fine, pleasantly sweet flavor.
This versatile variety, much appreciated by market gardeners, can be sown from early sp

name: purpurea
desc:
This species grows to between 60 cm and 1.50 m tall, and from June to October produces slender, upright stems bearing a flower with violet petals that curve slightly towards the ground.

It is a major medicinal species in Native American pharmacopo

name: Eel River
desc: This original variety, adapted to dry conditions, produces beautiful teardrop-shaped, buff-yellow fruit with green speckles, weighing from 1 to 3 kg. Their creamy-textured orange flesh has a sweet, delicate flavor.

name: Egyptian Flat Rooted
desc: This ancient variety produces green, garnet-veined leaves and very smooth roots lying flat on the ground. The reddish-purple skin encloses delicious dark-blood-red flesh.

name: Zucchinis Egyptian white
desc:
This very old variety offers an abundance of potbelly squash-like fruits, up to 25 cm long and weighing up to 900 g. Their skin is cream to pale green and their flavor is highly appreciated.

WHAT ARE THE 

WHAT ARE THE CHARACTERISTICS OF THE EGY

name: Lamb's lettuce Élan
desc: This vigorous, mildew-resistant variety produces an abundance of rosettes with dark, round foliage and a crunchy texture.

name: Eleonora
desc: This variety, bred for resistance to basil blight, has very large, slightly serrated, flat green leaves, 6 to 7 cm long.

name: Elf Ears Oak
desc: This variety, selected by Franck Morton in the USA, is one of the smallest oak leaves available. Resistant to drought and pathologies, it offers a dense heart and crisp, light-green, sharply-cut leaves, ideal for mescluns.

name: Elidia
desc: This "Vert de Gênes" variety is very compact and ideal for pot growing. It is not very sensitive to bolting.

name: Elka White
desc: This variety has delicate flowers with white petals and a pinkish-purple base. They give way to gigantic seed capsules with numerous white seeds for consumption.

name: Emerald Apple
desc: This variety produces very large green fruits with excellent flavor. They can weigh up to 700 g.

name: Emerald Fan
desc: This variety produces voluminous lettuces with bright golden-green leaves. They form a pretty heart within a fan-shaped swirl, and offer a firm texture and pleasant flavor.

name: Emerald Gem
desc: This ancient variety produces green, oval-shaped fruits weighing from 1 to 1.5 kg. Their yellow-orange flesh is very fragrant, with a very good sweet taste.

name: Emerald Oak
desc:
This variety produces compact, oak-leaf lettuces with light-green foliage. At maturity, it forms a dense head of leaves with a melting texture.

It was created by Frank Morton, in Oregon, from a cross between "Green Deer Tongue" and "Blushed Butter

name: Emerite
desc: This particularly productive early variety produces melting, tasty pods, 23 to 25 cm long, straight and stringless.

name: Emily's Circle
desc: This variety, bred by Tom Wagner, offers beautiful, round, "beef-flesh" type fruits, dark red with green tints. The flesh has a very good, sweet flavor.

name: Emmy
desc: This ancient variety, originally from Transylvania, produces small, round, orange fruits weighing around 80 g, with an excellent flavor.

name: Eros
desc: This compact Czech variety produces medium-sized, round, red fruit with a pleasant flavor.

name: Escondida
desc: This old, very vigorous variety produces pointed fruit that ripen from green to reddish-brown.

name: Esmeralda Golosina
desc:
This vigorous, hardy variety produces large, green fruits with a complex, sweet, rich flavor. Plants reach 1.50 m in height.
It's a new variety bred by Lee Goodwin in New Mexico.

name: Esperanza de Oro
desc:
The Esperanza de Oro Spanish melon is a variety with large, sometimes irregularly shaped fruits, most of which are elongated. The thin, yellow skin encloses green to salmon-colored flesh, which is highly fragrant and sweet.

Adapted to hot, dry con

name: Esther Hess Yellow
desc: This productive, vigorous variety produces an abundance of small, orangey-yellow cherry tomatoes 2-4 cm in diameter. Their crisp, juicy flesh reveals a lovely sweet-tart flavor.

name: Lamb's lettuce Étampes
desc: This very old French variety, particularly resistant to the cold, offers a compact rosette of tasty, round, dark-green, thick, fleshy leaves.

name: Étoile Blanche d’Anvers
desc: This old variety, native to northern Europe, produces bunches of 4 to 5 creamy-white, swollen, flattened fruits, weighing around 100g. They contain juicy, sweet-tasting flesh.

name: Evertender
desc: This vigorous, productive old variety produces many smooth, angular green fruits that are easy to harvest. Their richly-flavored flesh remains tender for a long time.

name: Extra Early Nutmeg
desc: This very old variety offers musky fruits weighing around 1 kg, slightly flattened and ribbed like acorn squash. Their green flesh with a salmon-colored center is sweet and juicy.

name: Fahrenheit Blues
desc:
This magnificent, highly productive variety produces an abundance of small fruits, about 2.5 cm in diameter. They turn indigo in the sun thanks to the action of anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants.
Its hemmed leaves let the sunlight in.

name: Fakel
desc: This early, very productive variety offers an abundance of clusters of 8 to 10 round, firm "cocktail"-type fruits, weighing 25 to 50 g. Heavy watering after a drought can cause the fruit to burst.

name: Chrysanthemum Fancy Pants Mix
desc: This variety offers flowers in a variety of colors: orange, white, lavender, pink, red, etc.

name: Fargo Yellow Pear
desc:
This old variety, of determinate to semi-determinate growth, produces hundreds of clusters of 5 to 7 small pear-shaped fruits. Their flesh has a sweet, non-acidic flavor that is much appreciated.

Developed in the 1930s in the United States, it is th

name: Farthest North Galia
desc: This gene pool has been selected to produce very small, tasty melons that are tolerant of cold climates. Fruits come in a variety of colors, with juicy green flesh and a good sweet flavor.

name: Farthest North Mix
desc:
This gene pool, selected to produce small, tasty melons that are tolerant of cold climates, produces fruits of various colors. All contain delicious, fragrant, sweet flesh. 

This gene pool was developed by Tim Peters of Peters Seed and Research in O

name: Feher Ozon Paprika
desc: This compact variety produces an abundance of clusters of conical, shiny, thin-skinned fruit, about 10 cm long. They ripen from yellowish-white to orange and reveal excellent flavor. Ideal dried, powdered or stuffed!

name: Fengyuan Purple
desc: This old variety, originally from Taiwan, produces an abundance of particularly long fruit, up to 40 cm long, light purple when ripe. Their very thin skin encloses sweet-tasting, creamy white flesh.

name: Feurio
desc: This variety's bright red ribs are fine and very attractive. They can be eaten in omelettes or mixed with vegetables, and retain their color when cooked. The leaves can be prepared in the same way as spinach.

name: Chinese cabbage Fidel
desc: This variety, selected by Sativa Rheinau, offers a compact, cylindrical apple with pale-green leaves and broad white ribs. Mild and sweet-tasting, they can be eaten raw or cooked, or ideally lacto-fermented like kimchi.

name: Fiesta
desc: This highly ornamental variety has a compact habit. It produces clusters of erect, elongated, pointed fruit that ripen from ivory to red, yellow and orange.

name: Filderkraut
desc: This semi-late variety has a large, very conical, white apple weighing 3 to 5 kg. Rich in sugar and very tasty, it is ideal for making sauerkraut.

name: Fin de Bagnol
desc:
This netted variety develops very quickly and produces long, cylindrical, thin, straight, green pods. The pods are picked at the fillet stage.

This very old variety, originally from France, dates back to the 19th century. It was introduced in 1957

name: Fin de Meaux
desc: This very old French variety, vigorous and hardy, produces a myriad of small, long, cylindrical green fruits.

name: Fine Petal Pink Peony
desc: This sumptuous variety boasts large, fully double flowers with multiple, bright-pink, finely cut petals. With their tassel-like appearance, these singular flowers are the perfect addition to any garden or flowerbed!

name: Fino Verde
desc: This traditional Italian variety has a very compact habit and lends itself perfectly to pot cultivation. Its small green leaves are highly aromatic and ideal for making pestos.

name: Fiolaro Di Creazzo
desc:
This rare and atypical variety of broccoli has been cultivated since ancient times. It doesn't form an apple, but offers an abundance of delicious side shoots along the central stem, in the manner of collards and kale.

Its shoots are traditionally

name: Fioletovyi Kruglyi
desc: This Russian variety, with its grayish foliage, produces clusters of 6 violet-black, round, thin-skinned fruits that keep well. Their juicy flesh, with green mucilage, has a rich fragrance.

name: Fire Blue
desc:
This magnificent variety produces clusters of round fruit weighing 10-20 g. When ripe, they are yellow and indigo in color.

This is a brand-new obtention from Tom Wagner.

name: Fireworks
desc: This vigorous variety produces abundant clusters of 8 to 12 red fruits, sometimes slightly mottled with yellow, round, fleshy and delicious. They are not very sensitive to bursting.

name: Flakkee
desc: This highly productive variety offers orange roots, around 30 cm long, renowned for their exceptional storage qualities, both in silos and in the ground.

name: Flame
desc: This variety of lettuce is slow to bolt and produces glossy red "Oak Leaf" foliage. Its leaves, 30 to 45 cm long, offer a crunchy texture and pleasant flavor.

name: Flamed Yellow
desc: This old French variety, not very susceptible to disease, produces clusters of 8 to 12 apricot-sized orange fruits weighing 40 to 60 g. Their juicy, marbled orange flesh reveals a unique, intense, spicy flavor.

name: Flashy Butter Gem
desc: This variety, halfway between the "romaine" and the "pommée", produces a beautiful little Sucrine-type apple with short, crimson-red leaves. They have a superb firm texture and excellent flavor.

name: Flashy Butter Oak
desc: This variety, derived from "Flashy Troutback" and "Emerald Oak", is the first selection from a research program on "spotted oak leaf" varieties. It offers emerald-green oak leaves spotted with bright red, forming a compact head. The texture is tender

name: Flashy Lightning Butter Oak
desc:
This early variety produces a large apple with a whitish-pink heart. Its dark-green, red-spotted, oak-leaf leaves offer a tender texture and mild flavor.

 

Developed by Franck Morton of Wild Garden Seeds.

name: Flashy Trout Back
desc: This vigorous variety, a selection of "Queue de Truite", produces a very uniform rosette with a bleached heart. Its spoon-shaped, green leaves are speckled with reddish-brown and offer a crunchy texture and good flavor.

name: Flat White Boer
desc: This vigorous old variety produces 2 to 4 pure-white fruits 20 to 50 cm in diameter and weighing 4 to 10 kg.

name: Sweet Pea Flora Norton
desc: Flora Norton is an old variety of sweet pea, particularly appreciated for its graceful, bewitchingly fragrant flowers. Their pale blue to sky-blue hues bring a touch of romantic sweetness to any garden.

name: Floral Spires White
desc: This very compact variety has pointed leaves similar to the "Thai" type, with a pleasant aniseed fragrance and magnificent white flowers.

name: Peas Fondant de Saint Désirat
desc:
This old, local variety from the Ardèche region, with its abundant, sustained production, bears a profusion of beautiful, thick, fleshy yellow pods with no parchment.

It's known as the "melting pea" because its pods swell well before the seeds dev

name: Fordhook Giant
desc: This old variety produces very large white ribs with a tender texture, popular in soups, soufflés and vegetable stews. It is fairly early and not very sensitive to cold.

name: Forellenschluss
desc:
This beautiful old variety is highly resistant to bolting. It produces an apple with dark green leaves spotted with reddish-brown, crisp texture and excellent flavor.
It's a traditional variety from Austria, whose name literally means "spotted like a

name: Freedom Mix
desc:
This "genetic reservoir", created by Frank Morton, was obtained by crossing his highly resistant varieties with his tastiest lettuces. The result combines the best characteristics of these different varieties.

Frank Morton specifically requests that

name: French Breakfast
desc:
This old French variety has semi-long, red roots with white tips and tender, crunchy flesh. Ideal for very early crops, it can also be grown all year round.

This very early radish will give you your first harvest just 2 or 3 weeks after sowing!

name: Peas Frieda Welten
desc: This early, very vigorous old variety easily reaches 2 m in height and produces delicious large pods. It is particularly suited to autumn sowing, for an early harvest the following spring.

name: Friendship
desc: This early, compact variety is very productive and produces bright orange, slightly elongated fruit with juicy flesh, delicious raw or cooked.

name: Curly endive Frisée de Meaux
desc:
This very old, hardy variety produces very large rosettes, 45 cm in diameter, with long, curly, frizzy leaves. The fairly broad ribs are tinted pink at the base.

Good heat resistance.

name: From the Galapagos
desc: This species of tomato, found on the Galapagos Islands, produces small, pear-shaped, bright yellow-orange fruits.

name: Moschata squash Futsu Black
desc: Futsu Black squash is an ancient Japanese variety that produces round, deeply ribbed fruits weighing up to 1.5 kg. The very hard, more or less warty skin, dark green then ochre with a grey coating when ripe, encloses firm, sweet, very fruity, yellow-

name: Lamb's lettuce Gala
desc: This famous early and vigorous variety produces compact plants with large, round, dark-green leaves and a mild flavor. Resistant to frost and mildew, it can be grown all year round.

name: Gamma
desc: This slow-growing, bolting-resistant variety has smooth, very dark, rounded foliage. It is highly resistant to spinach mildew and is particularly suitable for summer harvesting.

name: Curly endive Géante Maraîchère
desc: This vigorous, hardy escarole-type variety is adapted to both hot and cold climates. It produces upright, blond-green foliage and a heart about 55 cm in diameter, which whitens easily.

name: Gem State
desc:
This very early variety with potato foliage offers a profusion of small, round, tasty tomatoes weighing 45-60 g. It was developed by the University of Idaho in 1980 for the cold regions of the Rocky Mountains.
The variety is derived from "Pixie Hybri

name: Cannabis Gene Pool
desc: This gene pool brings together different varieties such as Sour, Kush, Skunk, etc., and contains 5 to 12% CBD. It offers an incredible diversity of plants, perfect for making your own selection either for fiber and biomass production, or for flower p

name: Gene Pool from Turkey
desc: This Ocimum basilicum gene pool is a blend of five populations (from Malatya, Van, Maras, Aydin and Izmir) produced from seed, in the same area, using open pollination. It's a genetic pool of Basilics with diverse flavors, fragrances and medicinal pr

name: Geneva Red
desc: This old Swiss variety produces round, flat, dark purple bulbs. They have a mild flavor and can be eaten raw or cooked. They keep well and are ideal for braiding.

name: Zucchinis Genovese
desc:
This old variety produces elongated "Cocozelle"-type fruits with light-green skin marbled with gray and completely smooth. Their creamy-white flesh has a delicate flavor.

WHAT ARE THE 

WHAT ARE THE CHARACTERISTICS OF GENOVESE ZUCCHINI?

The "

name: Genovese
desc: This ancient variety, traditionally used in Italy, has very large, tender, fragrant, inverted-cup-shaped green leaves and white flowers.

name: Georgescu Chocolate
desc: This Romanian variety produces magnificent elongated peppers, 10 to 12 cm long and 5 cm thick. When ripe, their skin turns chocolate-colored and encloses thick, sweet, flavorful flesh.

name: Georgia Flame
desc: Over a very long period, this variety produces an abundance of conical fruits 15 to 20 cm long. They ripen from green to dark red and offer crunchy, fruity flesh, ideal for salsa and grilled dishes.

name: Georgia Rooster
desc: This variety produces an abundance of large, elongated fruits, with orange-pink skin when ripe, similar to those of the "Pink Banana Jumbo" variety. They can reach 30 kg, but we recommend picking at 15 kg.

name: Geranium Kiss
desc: This fantastic variety, with very low susceptibility to mildew, produces geranium-like plants 50-60 cm high. It produces 3 to 4 clusters of 10 to 25 small, red, sweet-tasting fruits weighing around 30 g.

name: German Beer
desc: This ancient variety produces conical white roots the size of turnips. Their white flesh offers a firm texture and sweet flavor. Traditionally, they are sliced, dipped in sugar or eaten with beer.

name: German Johnson Pink
desc:
This old variety can grow up to 3 m tall and offers numerous round, slightly flattened, ribbed, pink "beef-flesh" fruits. Their juicy flesh contains few seeds.

It was a favorite of Pennsylvania Dutch populations, and is one of the four varieties beh

name: German Red Strawberry
desc: This variety is adapted to difficult growing conditions and produces an abundance of "beef-flesh" type fruit, red with a green collar. They offer dense, sweet-tasting flesh.

name: Giant Belgium
desc: This ancient variety produces large, slightly flattened, ribbed pink fruits with pale yellow-tinted shoulders, weighing up to a kilo. Their dense flesh is exceptionally sweet, with no acidity.

name: Giant Double Cream
desc: This vigorous variety can reach heights of up to 90 cm. It has large, double flowers in unusual creamy hues that are highly attractive.

name: Giant Oxheart Reif Red
desc: This old variety, which is not very sensitive to shattering, produces very large, fleshy, heart-shaped fruits weighing 500 to 700g. Their dense flesh contains few seeds and has a very mild flavor.

name: Giant Paste
desc: This ancient Italian variety produces clusters of 2 to 3 round or slightly oval fruits, up to 1 kg, with excellent flavor. Ideal for sauces and preserves.

name: Gigantic
desc:
This variety offers huge, green "Pélerine"-type fruits, 50-60 cm long.

The gourds dry in a dry, ventilated place and can be painted, sculpted or turned into various objects.

name: Gilfeather
desc: This unique, ancient variety produces elongated roots with white flesh, somewhere between a turnip and a rutabaga, offering a soft texture and sweet flavor. The leaves and flowers resemble those of the rutabaga, while the rootstock resembles that of

name: Giraumon Galeux d'Eysines
desc: Giraumon Galeux d'Eysines is an ancient variety that produces beautiful salmon-pink fruits with gray to beige excrescences on the skin. Their yellow-orange flesh has a very sweet taste. The fruits are highly decorative, suitable for making soups, and

name: Ficoid Glacial Ficoid
desc:
This spreading plant, also known as coarse purslane, produces soft green, fleshy leaves along its stems, with a frosted appearance and a slightly iodized, tangy taste. Eat fresh or cooked like spinach!

Ficoïde glaciale can also be used as an ornam

name: Glacier
desc: This very early variety, less than 1 m tall, produces round, orange-red fruits weighing 60-90 g, even in cold weather. The flavor is relatively mild for such an early tomato. The foliage is reminiscent of potatoes.

name: Glass Gem
desc: This dazzling variety, 2 to 3 m high, has several stems and spikes 10 to 20 cm long, containing a myriad of extraordinary translucent, kaleidoscopic seeds.

name: Globetrotter
desc:
This variety can grow up to 90 cm tall and produces an abundance of pink-purple flowers and aromatic foliage.

It is a recent variety developed in 2010 by Brian Kabbes in Holland.

The name Agastache comes from the Greek aga, meaning "abundance",

name: Gloire de Deuil
desc: This remarkable variety, originally from Artois, is fast-growing and vigorous. It produces an abundance of fine, straight pods containing beige seeds mottled with purple when fully ripe.

name: Gloire de Malines
desc: This old variety has good vigour and produces round fruit with firm, delicately flavoured flesh.

name: Gloire de Nantes
desc: This old local variety, originally from Brittany, produces a firm apple with lightly embossed greenish-blond leaves. They have a crunchy texture.

name: Gloire du Dauphiné
desc: This old French variety produces apples that are bright green with red tints, voluminous and crisp. Ideal for cool climates, it adapts well to spring and autumn crops.

name: Oenothera Glowing Magenta
desc: This low-growing, highly ornamental variety has a rosette of dark green basal leaves surrounding mauve flowers.

name: Gniff
desc:
This old Swiss variety produces roots with purple skin and white flesh with a very fragrant taste. They keep well.
This variety is slow-growing.

name: Cardoons Gobbo Di Nizza Monferrato
desc:
This ancient Italian variety has many fleshy, white, humped ribs. Particularly crunchy and sweet when buttered, they can even be eaten raw!

Gobbo di Nizza Monferrato means "hunchback of Nizza Monferrato", in reference to its shape and original gro

name: Gold Dust
desc: This compact, disease-resistant variety produces well-rounded, orange fruits weighing 100-300 g, with firm flesh and good flavor.

name: Gold Lumen
desc: This butter bean variety, which is not very susceptible to mosaic virus, produces an abundance of golden-yellow cylindrical pods with melting, fine flesh and white seeds.

name: Gold Nugget
desc: This compact variety produces an abundance of golden, slightly oval cherry tomatoes weighing around 10g. Their flesh contains few seeds and has a sweet, rich flavor.

name: Gold Nugget
desc: This bushy, compact variety, ideal for pot cultivation, offers 3 to 5 orange fruits, sometimes slightly striped with green, with yellow-orange flesh of excellent quality.

name: Purslane Golden
desc:
This ancient variety produces small, fat, thick leaves with a crunchy texture and a tangy, refreshing flavor. They are mainly eaten raw, in salads, but can also be cooked.
This compact variety is undemanding and adapts to a wide range of growing cond

name: Lamb's lettuce Golden
desc: A very old variety of lamb's lettuce, more resistant to late-winter bolting than green-leaf varieties, offers a delicious rosette of golden-yellow variegated leaves in spring.

name: Quinoas Golden Afternoon
desc: This Frank Morton-bred variety offers beautiful panicles loaded with an abundance of yellow seeds. It is the result of a cross involving "Oro de Valle" and varieties from the University of Washington.

name: Golden Ball
desc: This variety produces fruits that are yellow when ripe and can weigh up to 5 kg. Their white flesh offers a juicy texture and a sweet, fruity flavor.

name: Golden Bantam
desc: This old variety produces 1 to 2 ears of about 15 cm, made up of 8 rows of yellow seeds. The milky phase is short, so pick the ears as soon as they are ready.

name: Golden Burpee
desc: Golden Burpee beet is a very old variety with sweet, fragrant green foliage and rounded roots weighing up to 500g. Their golden-yellow flesh has a tender texture and mild flavor. Harvested small, they are used to make vinegar preserves.

name: Curly endive Golden Giant
desc: This variety produces large Chicory with a dense golden heart, over 35 cm wide. Ideal in salads, they can also be braised or roasted to accompany meat...

name: Golden Hubbard G
desc:
This ancient variety produces orange-red fruit with varying degrees of wartiness, up to 25 cm in diameter and weighing up to 5 kg. Their firm, dry flesh has an excellent mild, low-sugar flavor.

The fruit can be stored for up to 1 year.

name: Golden Jenny
desc:
This productive variety offers ochre-colored, flattened turban-shaped, embroidered fruits weighing 500 to 900 g. Their juicy flesh reveals an excellent sweet, fragrant flavor.
This selection of the "Jenny Lind" variety was developed by Merl Neidens.

name: Golden Jubilee
desc:
This variety produces an abundance of round, slightly flattened, bright orange fruits weighing around 200g. They contain few seeds and their firm flesh has a very mild, non-acidic flavor.

It is quite sensitive to cool climates, and is a cross betwee

name: Cutting Celery Golden Kin Tsai
desc: This very early variety of celery for cutting has hollow, gold-colored stalks and a lovely flavor.

name: Golden Midget
desc: This vigorous variety grows to no more than 1.2 m in height and produces small spikes 8 to 12 cm long. The yellow seeds are particularly tender, with a mild flavor. They can be eaten fresh, lightly boiled or roasted.

name: Golden Sunray
desc: This vigorous American variety produces round, smooth-skinned, orange-yellow fruit when fully ripe, perfect for making coulis. Picked green, they can be kept for up to 2 months.

name: Peas Golden Sweet
desc: This ancient variety, originally from India, is very vigorous and produces magnificent, flat, golden-yellow pods. 8 cm long and 3 cm wide, they can be eaten as "mangetout" peas when immature and, when ripe, as shelled peas.

name: Golden Tennis Ball
desc: This very old variety produces firm, light-green leaves that form a dense, fast-growing, long-lasting apple. Seeds are white.

name: Golden Treasure
desc: This ancient variety produces thin-skinned, green fruits that turn orange-yellow when ripe. They are elongated, pointed and over 20 cm long. Their thick, very juicy flesh offers a sweet flavor.

name: Goldfield
desc: This variety, suitable for both open field and sheltered cultivation, produces an abundance of flat, wide, golden-yellow pods with a tender texture.

name: Goldie
desc: This magnificent variety produces numerous round- or pointed-tipped fruits, 8 to 15 cm long and weighing 100 to 120 g. They ripen from pale green to orange-yellow to red.

name: Zucchinis Goldini 2
desc:
This Goldini improvement, developed by Carol Deppe, shows excellent precocity and vigor. It produces an abundance of golden-yellow fruit, delicious raw, cooked or dried. They reveal their full flavor when they weigh from 400 to 900 g.

This variety

name: Goodman
desc:
This fast-growing variety develops heavy foliage around small, firm, deep-white apples.

This variety is ideally suited to early summer production and can therefore also be sown, under cover, from February onwards.

name: Gotte à Graines Noires
desc: This variety produces loose, green apples about 15 to 20 cm in diameter, excellent for spring and summer salads. Traditionally, whole apples were eaten in brine.

name: Bitter eggplants Goyo Kumba
desc: This variety reaches 1 m in height and produces an abundance of beautiful, round, red, ribbed fruit.

name: Grand Pa Admire
desc:
This very old variety, not very sensitive to bolting, forms a wide rosette of loose, bronze-tinted green leaves. They remain tender even in hot weather.

The peripheral leaves can be cut off, one by one, as needed. New leaves develop after successi

name: Grandma Mary's Paste
desc:
This old variety, vigorous and tolerant of cold climates, offers an abundance of red, elongated, fleshy fruits, suitable for sauces and salads. Their juicy flesh contains few seeds.
It was selected by Jeanne Griffin, based in western Maine, for its f

name: Grappoli Corbarino
desc: This very old Italian variety offers generous clusters of 7 to 10 dark red plum-shaped fruits.

name: Greek
desc: This old-fashioned variety forms a ball of extremely fine, aromatic leaves that are highly fragrant. Flowering begins in early to mid-autumn, when all other varieties have already gone to seed.

name: Green Brandy
desc:
This vigorous variety, developed by Tom Wagner, offers round, green and slightly striped fruits, slightly larger than those of "Green Zebra".

Selected from a cross between "Green Zebra" and two strains of "Black Brandywine", it can display a wide ra

name: Green Deer Tongue
desc: This very old, vigorous variety, which is not very sensitive to bolting, produces very loose, triangular leaves with thick green ribs and good flavor.

name: Green Fleshed Pineapple
desc: This ancient variety produces an abundance of "Sucrin"-type fruit up to 15 cm in diameter. Their pale-green flesh has a firm, juicy texture and a sweet, highly aromatic flavor.

name: Green Fleshed Rocky Ford
desc:
This ancient variety produces round fruits, around 12 cm in diameter and weighing 0.5 to 1.5 kg, with an embroidered, slightly ribbed skin. Their sweet, green flesh has a firm, fine texture.

This variety is also known as Eden Gem.

name: Green Ghabbana
desc: This variety, developed by Tom Wagner, offers beautiful, round, slightly flattened "beef-flesh" type fruit, green with orange stripes and delicious flavor.

name: Green Giant
desc: This vigorously-growing variety offers an abundance of clusters of 5 to 6 juicy, smooth "beef-flesh" type fruits, flattened and ribbed, weighing over 300 g. Fruits are sensitive to bursting and can be harvested just before full ripeness.

name: Green Grape
desc:
This variety, with its well-developed, hardy foliage, produces an abundance of very small, green-yellow fruits in grape-like clusters. The flesh is green and has a sweet, spicy flavor.
An abundant supply of water following a drought can cause the fru

name: Green Hubbard
desc: This ancient variety offers a large number of spindle-shaped fruits up to 25 cm long and weighing up to 10 kg. Their bronze-green skin encloses thick, dry, sweet-tasting orange flesh. The fruit can be stored for almost 1 year.

name: Green Nutmeg
desc: This old-fashioned variety produces fruit up to 1.5 kg. The fragrant flesh is light green with a salmon-colored center.

name: Tulsis Green Pepper
desc: This variety produces pink-purple flowers and rounded, shiny, dark-green leaves. They have a green bell pepper fragrance, probably due to the presence of metoxypyrazines.

name: Green Pineapple
desc: This old variety, with its bushy habit, produces large, marbled green "beef-flesh" fruits weighing 150 to 400 g. They are harvested just before full ripeness, revealing juicy flesh with a rich flavor.

name: Green Plum
desc: This highly productive variety produces beautiful plum-shaped cherry tomatoes weighing around 50 g, with shades of green-yellow. They reveal juicy, aromatic, sweet-tasting flesh and keep for a long time after harvest.

name: Green Sprouts
desc: This very old variety produces a large, green central head, followed by numerous elongated shoots to be picked as they grow, before the flowers bloom.

name: Tulsis Green Tulsi from Thailand
desc: This ecotype, native to Thailand, has green leaves and stems. Plants are 30/60 cm tall, with a highly branched habit. The flowers are mauve and white. This Tulsi grows well in temperate regions.To be discovered on the Kokopelli blog: "Tulsis and othe

name: Green Velvet
desc: This variety, one of the sweetest and tastiest of the green tomato varieties, offers green, golden-yellow-tinged "beef-flesh" fruits when ripe. They weigh around 120 g and have chartreuse-green flesh.

name: Green Zebra
desc: This vigorous, high-yielding variety produces many small, round fruits weighing around 90 g, green with light greenish-yellow streaks and emerald-green flesh with a sweet, spicy flavor.

name: Green Zebra Arizona Hawai Strain
desc: This vigorous variety, bred by Tom Wagner and descended from "Green Zebra", produces numerous round, green fruits streaked with lighter shades. They have emerald-green flesh and a sweet, rich flavor.

name: Green-Fleshed Honeydew
desc: This old variety produces large, round to oval fruits weighing 1.5 to 2.5 kg, with smooth, hard skin that is creamy white when ripe. Their juicy, emerald-green flesh is deliciously sweet and spicy.

name: Greener Zebra
desc: This variety, a recent obtention from Tom Wagner, produces very intense green "Green Zebra"-type fruit, but with darker, rougher foliage and more compact growth.

name: Greenwich
desc:
This hardy, productive variety produces greenish-yellow fruit, tinged with amber when ripe, with a fairly irregular shape and weighing 200-300g. Their dense, greenish-yellow flesh reveals a mild, well-balanced flavor.
This variety, developed by Tom W

name: Gregory Altai
desc: Native to the Altai Mountains in Siberia, this "beef-flesh" variety offers large, purplish-pink fruits weighing 200 to 500 g with a sweet flavor. Their dense, firm cores enable them to be sliced very thinly.

name: Grevys Green Zebra
desc:
This productive "Green Zebra" variety offers larger fruits on more developed plants than the classic variety. The flavor is highly appreciated.

It's a brand-new obtention from Tom Wagner.

name: Zucchinis Greysini / Grisette de Provence
desc:
This early, highly productive variety produces light-green fruit with dark-green marbling, 14 to 16 cm long, with tender, fine white flesh. Excellent eating quality, they can be eaten raw or quickly cooked.

WHAT ARE THE 

CHARACTERISTICS OF THE

name: Grison de Loire / de Saumur
desc:
This vigorous variety produces smooth, uniform pods up to 20 cm long. Green with dark purple to black mottling, they have a firm texture and excellent flavor.

 

Particularly cultivated in Anjou by market gardeners until the late 60's, it was ab

name: Brussels sprouts Groninger
desc: This old, precocious, tall variety offers a multitude of small, firm, tender-green apples. Originally from the Netherlands, it is very hardy and can remain in place all winter long.

name: Grosse Blanche
desc: This vigorous old variety produces abundant foliage. It produces juicy "beef-flesh" fruits with a fruity flavor somewhat reminiscent of guava. They contain few seeds.

name: Grosse Blonde Paresseuse
desc: This ancient, heat-resistant French variety produces wavy, blistered, pale-green leaves. They form a voluminous, rather round and flat apple, about 30 cm in diameter.

name: Curly endive Grosse Pancalière
desc: This ancient, fast-growing variety has abundant, curly foliage that whitens naturally due to its thickness. Young shoots are also used in salads or cooked.

name: Curly endive Grosse Pommant Seule
desc: This heat-resistant French variety produces a large apple with finely-cut leaves and a tight core that whitens easily. The young shoots can also be used raw, in salads, or cooked.

name: Physalis Ground cherry
desc: This species produces delicious orange-yellow fruit with a citrus-like flavor. Placed in a dry, well-ventilated place, sheltered in their calyx, cape gooseberries can be stored for several weeks.

name: Grub's Mystery Green
desc:
This relatively early variety produces very large, green, "beef-flesh" fruits weighing up to 700 grams. Its flavor is highly appreciated.

This is a new variety "discovered" by David Lockwood in Australia in 2005 among plants of the black variety "

name: Curly endive Grumolo Verde
desc: This old variety is very hardy and produces loose apples, very close to the ground, with thick, round green leaves. They have a tender texture and mild flavor.

name: Grushovka
desc: This compact variety, less than 1 m tall, produces around 40 oblong, pink fruits weighing 180-250 g. Their flavor is highly appreciated. Their flavor is highly appreciated.

name: Guajillo / Mirasol
desc: This ancient Mexican variety produces elongated, green fruits that turn orange-red when ripe. They have a fruity flavor and, when dry, take on a deep, luminous red color.

name: Guernsey
desc: This old, productive and hardy variety has fleshy, half-long, fairly stocky roots with smooth, white skin. They are 15 to 20 cm long, with flesh of excellent taste.

name: / Kales Gulag Stars
desc:
This particularly cold-hardy variety produces leaves in a variety of shapes and colors, from purple to green. They have a tender texture and good flavor.

It is a gene pool created by Tim Peters in Oregon by crossing varieties of Siberian kale (Bra

name: Habanero Chocolate Brown
desc: This variety offers fruits around 5 cm long, in the shape of a crumpled lantern. They ripen from green to chocolate-brown and are often eaten raw so as not to lose their delicious, fruity flavor.

name: Habanero Peach
desc: This variety produces peach-colored "Habanero"-type fruit with a slightly slender shape.

name: / Kales Halbhoher
desc: This compact, early, frost-resistant variety has many dark green, very curly leaves, with superior flavor and vitamin content after the first frosts.

name: Hales Best Jumbo
desc: This ancient, particularly drought-resistant variety produces elongated fruit weighing up to 1.5 kg. Their dark green, highly embroidered skin encloses salmon-colored flesh with a sweet flavor.

name: Halfmoon China
desc: This variety produces slightly flattened, "beef-flesh" type fruits weighing 150 to 350 g, with pale yellow skin tinged with a pink blush when ripe. The flesh is clear and juicy, with a mild, melon-like flavor.

name: Handy Lady
desc:
This variety, which is not very sensitive to bursting, produces an abundance of fruit, 10 to 15 cm long, with a characteristic very pointed nipple and good shelf life.
Selected by Dylana Kapuler, it is extremely resistant to mildew.

name: Portulaca Happy Hour Coconut
desc: This variety forms small, low, non-creeping clumps of slender green leaves. Its double flowers feature delicately crinkled white petals and a yellow heart.

name: Harris Model
desc: This variety produces very white roots with a fairly flat collar. They can reach 30 cm in length and have very tender flesh.

name: Hâtive d'Heverlee
desc: This excellent spring variety, originally from Belgium, produces a very large apple with green leaves iridescent with pink. They have a firm texture and good flavor.

name: Hawaiian Pineapple
desc: This variety offers an abundance of fairly ribbed, "beef-flesh" orange fruits, weighing 500 to 700 g and irregular in shape. They have an intense, fruity pineapple flavor when perfectly ripe.

name: Hearts Of Gold
desc:
This old variety is extremely productive, vigorous and resistant to sunburn. Its fruits are almost round, heavily embroidered, weighing 1.5 to 2 kg. The flesh is dark orange, highly fragrant and sweet.
The "Heart of Gold" variety was developed by Rol

name: Heirloom Mix from Bourgogne
desc: A Kokopelli exclusive: this blend offers a number of old Burgundy melon varieties. Their color can be green, yellow or purple, and their skin smooth, embroidered or ribbed.

name: Millets Hell's Canyon
desc: This variety, adapted to cool summers, is highly productive and graces ornamental gardens with its beautiful, dense heads, whose seeds are easy to harvest. The panicles are almost reddish-brown, and the leaves and stems are green with purple stripes.

name: Skullcap Helmet
desc: Skullcap is a perennial herbaceous plant native to southern Europe. Sometimes used in tobacco blends, this species has been recognized for centuries for its medicinal virtues! Neuroprotective, anxiolytic, muscle relaxant, antispasmodic, etc.

name: Helsing Junction Blues
desc: This variety, with its bluish stems, produces clusters of a dozen small, round fruits about 2.5 cm in diameter. They take on a violet-blue hue in the sun thanks to their anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants.

name: Sweet Pea Henry Eckford
desc:
This old variety bears highly fragrant, orange-red flowers. It can reach up to 2 m in height.

It was introduced by Henry Eckford in 1904.

name: Hens And Chicks
desc: This variety produces straight, upright stems bearing a solitary lilac-pink flower. Its name "Poule et Poussins" is inspired by its huge seed capsule surrounded by smaller ones.

name: Hidatsa
desc: This variety was bred by Oscar H. Will in Dakota in 1953 from the Japanese variety collected in 1929 in Hokkaido. It has small, light-green kernels with a black dot, containing over 50% protein.

name: High Country
desc: This early variety, adapted to cool climates, was developed in Montana. It produces around 100 bright-red, elongated fruits weighing 90-120 g. They are ideal for canning.

name: Hinkelhatz
desc: This ancient variety offers small, pointed fruits, traditionally used for pickling, ripening from green to bright red. Its name means "chicken heart".

name: Scorsonera Hoffmanns
desc:
Hoffmanns Scorsonera is very hardy and can be grown like salsify. Harvested from October to March, it produces long, black roots reminiscent of salsify, with a crunchy texture and pleasant flavor that can be eaten raw, cooked or fried.

It is advis

name: Hokkai Green
desc: This new Japanese variety, with its compact habit, produces an abundance of large, beautiful pods containing delicious green seeds with a fine, hazelnut-like taste, highly prized for their nutritional qualities.

name: Hokkaido Black
desc: This late variety, 1 m tall, produces green pods enclosing black seeds that offer a fine texture and good flavor.

name: Homsi
desc: This old, drought-resistant variety produces oval, lavender-colored fruits with white stripes, 12 cm long. Their fine flesh offers excellent flavor.

name: Honey Rock
desc: This old variety produces 5 to 7 fruits, 15 cm in diameter and weighing 1.5 to 2 kg. Their green-gray skin is covered with rough embroidery. Their dark salmon-colored flesh has a thick, firm texture and a pleasant fruity flavor.

name: seeds Hopi Black Dye
desc:
This ancient variety, over 2 m tall, can withstand light frosts and bears a yellow inflorescence 18 to 30 cm in diameter. The purple-black seed husks are used to dye basketry and clothing.

The flower petals can be added to salads, and the seeds ar

name: Hopi Orange
desc: Vigorous plants produce bright orange fruits that can weigh 6-7 kg. The yellow-orange flesh is of good quality. Keeps well.

name: Pepo squash Howden
desc: This very runny variety produces large, barrel-shaped fruits weighing from 10 to 15 kg. Their beautifully ribbed, orange skin encloses firm flesh, ideal for autumn festival carvings.

name: Hubbard Anna Swartz
desc: This variety produces dark gray fruits weighing from 2.5 to 4 kg with a very hard skin. Their high-quality flesh has a sweet potato flavor.

name: Hubbard Baby Blue
desc: This is a blue Hubbard variety with smaller, teardrop-shaped fruits. The skin is blue-grey. Fruits with very sweet flesh weigh 2 to 3 kg.

name: Hubbard Golden Delicious
desc:
This fast-growing variety offers 2 to 4 smooth, orange-red fruits that can weigh up to 8 kg. Their fine, orange flesh, rich in vitamin C, is widely used for baby purées, and their flavor is much appreciated.
The fruit keeps for 4 to 8 months.

name: seeds Hungarian black
desc:
This variety, around 2 m tall, produces sturdy, single-flowered, yellow stems 20 to 30 cm in diameter. They bear an abundance of black seeds.

The flower petals enhance salads, and the seeds are a favorite with birds.

name: Hungarian Blue
desc:
This old, very runny variety produces 2 to 4 blue-gray fruits weighing 4 to 8 kg. Their thick, dense, orange-yellow flesh has a mild, low-sugar flavor. They keep for 3 to 6 months.

Description of Bleu de Hongrie squash

The Hungarian Blue squash

name: Hungarian Bread
desc: This variety, with its upright stems, has flowers with petals ranging from white to pink to lavender-blue. When fully mature, the large capsules contain blue or black seeds used in pastries for their nutty flavor.

name: Hungarian Oval
desc: This old variety, with developed foliage, produces pink, oval to cordate "beef-flesh" fruits. Their firm flesh contains few seeds and has an intense, fruity, sweet flavor.

name: Hyper Mix
desc: This particularly ornamental hollyhock, about 2 m tall, has spikes of single flowers in a variety of pastel shades: white, orange, pink, etc.

name: Hyper Red Oak
desc:
This variety, created by Frank Morton, offers deep-red lettuces similar to "Merlox Red Oak", but with smaller leaves. It is derived from "Hyper Red Rumple", "Emerald Oak" and "Merlot".

This variety is not very susceptible to mildew.

Peripheral

name: Ice Candle
desc:
The Chandelle de Glace radish is a very old variety, with hairy foliage that produces elongated white roots about 15 cm long. Their thick, tender, crunchy flesh has a fine, piquant flavor.
Characteristics of Chandelle de Glace radish
The Chandelle

name: Ida Gold
desc:
This compact variety produces, over a long period, a multitude of clusters of around 15 small, round, bright-orange fruits weighing 40 to 60 g. Their juicy flesh reveals a delicious sweet flavor with a hint of acidity.

This variety requires no pruni

name: Immune
desc: This very vigorous Czech variety, formerly known as "Irion", produces small, flat, well ribbed tomatoes weighing 50-60 g, with juicy, sweet, slightly tart flesh.

name: Imperial Black Beauty
desc: This old variety produces 4 to 6 fruits in the shape of a large ribbed pear, sometimes weighing over 1 kg and purple in color, sometimes black. The skin is free of thorns and the flesh is tender.

name: Improved
desc: This ancient, very hardy variety from the Mediterranean basin produces fine, elongated, tender roots with a white skin.

name: Imur Prior Beta
desc: This very early, potato-leaved variety produces round, slightly elongated fruits weighing around 60g. The variety is suited to high-altitude regions and short seasons.

name: Inca Rainbow
desc: This very hardy variety produces 20-30 cm spikes of sweet, multicolored seeds on plants sometimes 3 m high. Stems and leaves are sometimes purple.

name: Indian Red Popping
desc:
This variety from India, 2 to 3 m high, has red to black seeds. They are eaten toasted, like "popcorn", in flour or whole.

 

The leaf sheaths of Sorghum bicolor, rich in anthocyanin, are used as a natural red dye. This use is widespread in Afri

name: Indigo Apple
desc:
This variety, resistant to shattering and disease, produces well-rounded fruits weighing from 60 to 120 g. Entirely red, they turn a dark blue-purple in the sun thanks to the action of anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants.

 

This creation by Bra

name: Oenothera Innocence
desc:
This compact, widely-spreading variety of pale evening primrose produces large, subtly-scented white flowers, gradually tinged with pink, from late spring to early autumn. Perfect for flower beds!

Although perennial, it has difficulty with temperatu

name: Integrata Red
desc:
This variety produces an open head with dark-red, crinkled leaves. It resprouts spontaneously in autumn, producing an abundance of young lettuces towards the end of winter.

Characteristics of Integrata Red romaine lettuce

Integrata Red romaine

name: Isis Candy Cherry
desc: Throughout the season, this variety produces an abundance of round and oval "cherry tomatoes", marbled with red and yellow and measuring 2.5 cm in diameter. The flesh, also red and yellow, is rich and fruity.

name: Ispolin
desc: This vigorous variety, well-suited to cool or short-season regions, produces large, flattened, pink-red "beef-flesh" fruits weighing around 300g. Their dense, juicy flesh reveals a delicious flavor.

name: Italian Red Marconi
desc:
This ancient variety produces large, elongated, three-lobed fruits about 30 cm long, perfect for salads and frying. They ripen from green to bright red and offer a very fruity flavor.
This variety adapts to difficult growing conditions.

name: Italian Yellow Marconi
desc:
This variety produces elongated, 3-lobed fruits. They ripen from green to yellow-orange and offer an extremely fruity flavor.

This variety adapts to a wide range of growing conditions.

name: Quinoas Ivory
desc: This variety was selected by Frank Morton for its white seeds, sturdy stems, resistance to sprouting in autumn and white panicles that do not stain the seeds when it rains.

name: Ivory Egg
desc: This old-fashioned, highly productive variety produces an abundance of ivory-yellow fruit, then creamy yellow when fully ripe, with a hen's egg appearance, weighing 30 to 50g. The flavor is very sweet, with a hint of lemony acidity.

name: Pepo squash Jack Be Little
desc:
This slow-growing variety produces 6 to 12 small, decorative dark orange pumpkins. 2 to 3 cm high and 5 to 8 cm in diameter, they weigh between 90 and 120 g. Jack Be Little squash can be eaten stuffed, and will keep for over a year.

What are the c

name: Jack Ice
desc:
This upright variety forms an apple surrounded by serrated green leaves. They offer a very crunchy texture.
This vigorous-growing variety tolerates a wide range of growing conditions. It is native to the United States.

name: Pepo squash Jack O'Lantern
desc:
This variety produces large orange fruits, traditionally carved for Halloween, weighing 5 to 8 kg and slightly ribbed. The flesh is a softer, lighter orange with a sweet flavor.

What are the characteristics of Jack O'Lantern squash?

Jack O'Lant

name: Common Bean Jacob's Cattle
desc: This ancient variety, appreciated in regions with cool climates, is very popular in the United States. Its berries are white with burgundy highlights. They can be eaten green, semi-dry or dry.

name: Jan's
desc:
This compact variety produces small, round, orange cherry tomatoes with a slight red tint. They have an exceptionally rich flavor.

This new variety, bred by Lee Goodwin in New Mexico, is the result of a cross between "Snow White" and "Isis Candy".

name: Janosik
desc: This famous Polish variety, adapted to cool climates, produces beautifully rounded, dark-green fruits weighing 4 to 5 kg. The yellow flesh is deliciously sweet, crisp and refreshing.

name: Japanese Black Trifele
desc: This variety offers an abundance of pear-shaped, brick-purple fruits weighing 120 to 250 g. They are smooth, with no cracks, and their thick skin is easy to peel. The flavor is well balanced between sweet and acidic.

name: Japanese Pickling
desc: This old-fashioned, precocious variety, suitable for pot cultivation, produces very slender fruits 25 to 40 cm long. Their deep purple skin encloses soft-textured, sweet-tasting flesh.

name: Perillas Japanese Red
desc:
This variety's red leaves are highly decorative in the garden, but can also be used as a condiment in the kitchen. The taste of the seeds is reminiscent of sesame.

Find out more on the Kokopelli blog: "Qualités Médicinales des Perillas".

name: Japonaise de la Levée
desc: This vigorous, drought- and disease-resistant variety produces a multitude of elongated, smooth-skinned, shiny, deep purple fruits early in the season.

name: Jarrahdale
desc: This old, highly ornamental variety produces fruit with very pronounced ribs, weighing from 3 to 10 kg. Their slate-gray skin encloses yellow-orange flesh that is dry, non-fibrous and moderately sweet. The fruit keeps for 4 to 9 months.

name: Jasper Violet
desc:
This purple tomato is a very productive variety, well suited to short-season or cool-summer regions. Its fruits are round and fairly regular, weighing from 30 to 90 g. They are purplish-red with green stripes, and have a lovely flavor.
This variety,

name: Jaune du Doubs
desc:
This ancient Franc-Comtois variety produces elongated, yellow roots with sweet flesh. It keeps very well and is considered a forage crop, but is also appreciated as a vegetable for its gustatory qualities.

Characteristics of the Doubs yellow carro

name: Jaune Gros de Paris
desc: The Gros de Paris yellow pumpkin is a variety that grows to 5 to 6 m in length and produces fairly flattened, spherical fruits 25 to 70 cm in diameter. Their yellow to salmon-colored skin is cracked or embroidered when ripe. Their yellow-orange flesh

name: Jeanne
desc:
This hardy variety, slow to bolt, produces a beautiful apple with bronze-green leaves. They offer a tender texture and delicious flavor.
This lettuce is also known as "melon salad".

name: Jenny Lind
desc: This ancient variety produces flattened turban-shaped fruits weighing 0.5 to 1.5 kg, with orange-brown skin marbled with green when ripe. The green flesh is very sweet and pleasantly fragrant.

name: Jericho
desc: This ancient variety, developed to withstand desert heat, produces a rosette of elongated green leaves. They offer a crunchy texture and mild flavor.

name: Jersey Oxheart
desc: This very old variety, not very sensitive to bolting, produces dark green conical apples. They have a thick texture and good flavor.

name: Jester
desc: This variety produces an apple whose wavy-edged, half-embossed leaves are brightly colored red like a jester's costume. Crisp as ice, bright and juicy as an apple.

name: Millets Jester
desc: This variety has very attractive foliage, changing as it develops. It evolves in shades of green, burgundy and bronze-purple, just like "Purple Majesty". Its flower spikes measure up to 30 cm.

name: Jewel
desc: This highly productive variety, originally from Manchuria, produces fleshy pods containing 3 grains, yellow at maturity and rich in 37% protein. Plants reach 60 to 90 cm in height.

name: Bitter eggplants Jilo Tingua Verde
desc: This ornamental variety, with purple, thorny stems, produces an abundance of oblong, green, then bright orange-red fruits when ripe. Traditionally, they are harvested and eaten immature.

name: Jimi's Flag Mix
desc: It's a beautifully colored variety, with shades of violet, indigo, bright red and pink, but its flowers are sometimes completely white. The seeds also come in a variety of colors.

name: Jimi's Purple Haze
desc: This variety, introduced by Frank Morton in Oregon, has large, 10 cm-diameter, purple flowers with darker spots in the center. They produce tasty, bitter-free seeds.

name: Jimi's Snowflakes
desc: At the end of its upright, straight stems, this variety offers magnificent, single white flowers 10 cm in diameter. The brown seeds are delicious and bitter-free.

name: Jl Midnight Select
desc:
This highly productive variety offers numerous clusters of 6 small fruits, intense indigo blue, sometimes with a hint of green, very rich in anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants. They contain dense flesh with good flavor.
This is a recent variety crea

name: Rutabaga Joan
desc: This variety produces very homogeneous, round roots with purple collars and creamy-white flesh, offering a very soft texture and good flavor.

name: Joker
desc: This close relative of "Jester" produces a more compact apple with green leaves tinged with red, deeply embossed and broadly toothed. They have a firm, crisp texture.

name: Jubilee
desc: This old, very productive variety offers long, pale-green fruits with darker green stripes and rounded ends, weighing around 10 to 15 kg. The flesh is red, refreshing and very tasty.

name: June Pink
desc: This old, vigorous variety produces long-lasting bunches of 6 to 10 "beef-flesh" type fruits, pink with yellow shoulders, round and smooth, weighing 120 to 150 g and not very sensitive to bursting.

name: Jupiter
desc:
This vigorous variety has dense foliage and low susceptibility to tobacco mosaic virus. It produces an abundance of fruits with 3 to 4 lobes, which ripen from green to red and offer thick flesh.

Staking is recommended for plants up to 70 cm tall.

name: Kagraner Sommer
desc: This variety produces a medium-sized apple with a firm, fairly open core. The light-green leaves are relatively smooth and offer a juicy texture and mild flavor.

name: Kajari
desc: This ancient Indian variety produces magnificent round fruits, from 800 g to 1 kg. Their skin is streaked with colors ranging from green to orange and copper, and their flesh reveals green-pink hues.

name: Kaki Coing
desc: This French variety produces beautiful fruit with a bright apricot-orange hue, slightly stretched and suitable for canning. Their thick skins enclose tasty, dense, firm and very sweet flesh.

name: Kalibos
desc: This traditional Czech variety produces a large, pointed apple, weighing 2 to 2.5 kg, with red leaves that are fairly loose, crisp, juicy and very sweet. They can be eaten raw, cooked or lacto-fermented.

name: Kamo
desc: This ancient variety from the Kamo region of Japan produces rounded, slightly pear-shaped fruit with dark purple, almost black skin.

name: Kanchanaburi
desc: This compact Thai variety, selected for seed production, flowers more quickly than leaf-type coriander. The roots and leaves of this variety are also highly fragrant.

name: Kanner Hoell
desc: This very old variety has large, shiny red "beef-flesh" fruits that can exceed 10 cm in diameter. Their delicious flesh is well-balanced, sweet with a touch of acidity.

name: Kapia Yag Biberi
desc: This traditional variety, originally from Turkey, offers horn-shaped fruits, 10 to 15 cm long, which are a beautiful red color when ripe. They have thick flesh and reveal their full flavor when oven-roasted or marinated.

name: Peas Karina
desc: This vigorous variety, which is not very sensitive to lodging, produces an abundance of straight, dark green pods containing 8 to 9 wrinkled kernels used fresh or dry.

name: Karma
desc: This variety, bred by Frank Morton and descended from the "Stocky Red Roaster" line, offers conical, bright orange to dark red fruit with smooth skin and firm, sweet flesh. Enjoy them in salads, fried or roasted!

name: Kashmiri Brinjal
desc: This robust, compact variety, native to Kashmir, produces chubby, oblong fruit with a shiny, white skin that turns lavender on sunny surfaces. The flesh is white and sweet, with no bitterness.

name: Kazakh
desc:
This early variety, which is not very sensitive to drought, produces fruit weighing 0.5 to 1.5 kg, with a white and bright yellow skin when fully ripe. The flesh is completely white, juicy and very pleasant-tasting.
It can be trained on trellises.

name: Kazakhstan
desc: This compact, early variety produces beautiful, oval, rather stocky, shimmering purple fruit. It is distinguished by its bracts, which wrap neatly around the fruit.

name: Kevin's Early Orange
desc:
This very early variety of bell pepper produces beautiful cubic, lobed fruits, 9 cm high and 10 cm wide, with an intense orange color. They contain firm, flavorful flesh and are perfect for stuffing recipes.

It has been selected from the market ga

name: Moschata squash Kikuza
desc: This ancient variety, originally from Japan, offers strongly ribbed, fully ochre fruits weighing up to 2 kg. Their excellent orange-yellow flesh reveals a sweet, fruity, nutty flavor.

name: Sweet Pea King Edward
desc: This old variety, introduced by Henry Eckford in 1903, can reach 2.5 m in height and offers a profusion of highly fragrant, bright-red flowers.

name: King Humbert
desc: This ancient variety is vigorous and produces abundant clusters of 4 to 5 scarlet-red fruits. Cylindrical to oval in shape, and sometimes pear-shaped with a small nipple, the fruits are perfectly suited to drying or canning, and their flesh is firm a

name: King Of The North
desc: This variety produces an abundance of ribbed fruit, with 3 or 4 lobes, which ripen from dark green to dark red and offer thick, crunchy flesh.

name: Kivumbasi Lime
desc: This old-fashioned variety, 20 to 40 cm high, has white flowers and fine green leaves, covered with a light down, revealing an intense lemon fragrance.

name: Klari Baby Cheese
desc: This ancient variety produces an abundance of round, "tomato chili" type fruits that ripen from yellow to orange to red. Their juicy, very thick flesh offers a sweet flavor.

name: Klondike Blue Ribbon Stripe
desc:
This old, hardy variety produces fruit up to 60 cm long and weighing up to 14 kg. The skin, streaked with light green and darker shades, encloses juicy flesh.
This variety was bred by Porter in California around 1900.

name: Kolasca
desc: This variety produces horn-shaped fruits 20 to 25 cm long and red in color when fully ripe. They are suitable for drying and can be powdered.

name: Kolb
desc: This old variety produces "beef-fleshed" fruit weighing up to 500 g. Mild flavor.

name: Komohana
desc: This vigorous variety has bushy foliage and is adapted to hot, humid climates. It produces an abundance of plum-shaped, shatter-resistant fruit suitable for drying and making sauces.

name: Cannabis Kompolti
desc:
This Hungarian variety is renowned for its CBD content, which can reach 8-10% depending on terroir, ecotype and analysis. Appreciated for its medicinal qualities and its pine and lemon aromas, it is also used for its high biomass production.

This

name: Konasu
desc: This productive variety offers rounded, dark purple fruit with tender skin and white flesh. Adaptable to cool seasons and pot culture.

name: Kootenai
desc: This variety, with its well-developed foliage and compact habit, produces numerous clusters of 6 to 8 smooth, round fruits weighing 50 to 90 g, with juicy, sweet-tasting flesh.

name: Koralik
desc: This determinate, highly productive variety produces an abundance of bunches of 6 to 8 small red tomatoes, about 2.5 cm in diameter, with a highly appreciated flavor.

name: Perillas Korean
desc:
This variety produces serrated leaves that are dark green and purple on the underside. They offer a mild flavor of basil, mint and cumin, much appreciated in Asian cuisine.

Find out more on the Kokopelli blog: "Qualités Médicinales des Perillas".

name: Kotlas
desc: This very early variety, with a fairly compact habit, produces small, round fruits weighing around 60 g, with excellent keeping qualities. Not very sensitive to cold, it adapts to a wide range of growing conditions.

name: Kriakolokia
desc:
This variety produces very long, edible fruits with an asparagus-like taste and a pea-like odour. They can exceed 1 m in length and reach a diameter of 15 cm when ripe.

The gourds dry in a dry, ventilated place and can be painted, sculpted or transf

name: Kumba
desc:
This new mildew-resistant Belgian variety offers a profusion of round, pink fruits, with firm, juicy flesh and a deliciously fragrant, sweet flavor. Ideal both raw and cooked, this tomato keeps well at room temperature.

It was ranked 4th in the be

name: Kurume Long Purple
desc: On tall, vigorous plants, this popular Japanese variety produces shiny, dark purple, almost black fruits, around 25 cm long. The flesh is soft, tender and of excellent quality.

name: Küttingen White
desc: This ancient, hardy variety is fast-growing and vigorous. It produces large white roots with fine flesh and a mild, aromatic flavor, less sweet than other carrots.

name: La Brillante
desc: This spring variety, which is heat-resistant and slow to bolt, produces fairly tall, pointed apples with thin, blistered green leaves. They have a tender texture and mild flavor.

name: La Victoire
desc: This old French variety produces an abundance of green pods, about 15 cm long, very fleshy and with a tender texture. Suitable for late sowing.

name: La Vigneronne
desc: This old, vigorous variety, with pink flowers, produces an abundance of stringless, fleshy, purple-marbled pods. They measure 15 to 25 cm in length and are well suited to freezing.

name: Trachymene Lacy Blue
desc: This species of the Apiaceae family produces architecturally impressive blue umbels on plants with delicately chiselled foliage. It is one of the most interesting flower species for dried flower bouquets.

name: Pepo squash Lady Godiva
desc:
This ancient variety produces large fruits containing 50 to 100 g of delicious seeds, devoid of seed coats and extremely rich in nutrients. Raw or roasted, they are the perfect addition to any recipe.

The orange-yellow fruits, streaked with green,

name: Cyclanthere Lady's Slipper
desc: This ancient variety produces small green fruits, covered with soft spines, 10 to 15 cm long. Immature, they can be prepared in salads or as a sautéed vegetable. When ripe, they are cooked and eaten in soups or stuffed.

name: Ladybird
desc:
Over a long season, this variety offers 30 cm stems covered with semi-double flowers in shades of yellow, orange and red. They are highly melliferous.

This species is very popular with birds. The flowers can be left to dry in autumn, so that they

name: Lakota
desc: This old variety produces fruits weighing 2 to 3 kg, with either green and orange or completely orange skin. The flesh is fine, orange and nutty.

name: Lao Green Stripe
desc: This ancient variety, which grows to over 1 m in height, produces an abundance of round fruit, with tender flesh and a delicious flavor, and a thick skin with purple, green and white shades.

name: Lao Lavender
desc: This old variety, with its compact habit, produces magnificent, round, pale violet fruits, 8 to 10 cm in diameter and weighing around 100 grams. The flesh is fine-textured.

name: Lao White
desc: This ancient variety produces round fruits 5 cm in diameter, white in color, turning yellow when ripe. Their tender flesh is best eaten before full maturity.

name: Perillas Laos violet
desc:
This annual ornamental variety boasts magnificent, all-purple foliage, whose spicy flavor is highly prized in Japanese cuisine.

Discover it on the Kokopelli blog: "Qualités Médicinales des Perillas".

name: Large Drumhead Savoy
desc: This old, hardy, vigorous and productive variety quickly forms a conical, grey-green apple up to 40 cm tall, with large, rounded, fairly thick leaves.

name: Large Leaf
desc:
Widely grown in gardens and in the kitchen, this ancient Italian variety offers an abundance of large, rounded, powerfully fragrant leaves. A vigorous grower, Basil Grand Vert adapts to a wide range of growing conditions.

What are the characterist

name: Latino
desc: This dwarf variety, perfect for growing in pots, blooms late and offers an abundance of small, light-green, highly fragrant leaves.

name: Lattughino Verde
desc:
This old Italian variety, which is slow to bolt, has tasty, tender-green, highly dissected "oak-leaf" foliage.

The peripheral leaves are cut off, one by one, as needed. New leaves develop after successive harvests.

name: Launch
desc: This variety has tapered, smooth roots with a sweet taste.

name: Rutabaga Laurentian
desc: This ancient variety produces spherical roots up to 15 cm in diameter, with no visible collar. The bright yellow epidermis with dark purple top encloses pale yellow flesh with a mild flavor and fine texture.

name: Chrysanthemum Leaf Garland Chrysanthemum
desc:
This old, undemanding and hardy variety, also known as crown chrysanthemum, is traditionally eaten in Chinese and Japanese cuisine.

Its foliage has a strong flavor, reminiscent of watercress and juniper berries, and is eaten raw in salads or cooke

name: Zucchinis Lebanese White Bush
desc: This very old variety offers compact plants with an abundance of beautiful, fairly short, stubby, white to pale green fruit. Their creamy-yellow flesh reveals a sweet flavor that is much appreciated.

name: Lednicky
desc: This ancient and rare variety produces light-green, ample, rounded leaves that form a very compact apple with a firm texture and excellent flavor.

name: Mixed grain crop Legume Meslin Rye
desc: This mixture of rye - baker's and forest rye - peasant wheat, field pea and hairy vetch is very covering, dark green, and can exceed 2m in height. Ideal as green manure or poultry feed.

name: Pepo squash Lemon
desc: This very vigorous variety, with its two-tone stems, produces an abundance of small fruits whose size, shape and color are reminiscent of lemons. Excellent flavor.

name: Lemon Drop
desc: This variety, adapted to cool, damp climates, offers an abundance of small, light-yellow, almost translucent fruits, 1.5 to 2 cm in diameter. They contain deliciously sweet, tangy and refreshing flesh.

name: Lemon Mrs Burns
desc: This variety has white flowers and small, pointed leaves with a strong lemony flavor.

name: Oenothera Lemon Sunset
desc: The flowers of this variety change from pale yellow to orange-red when fully mature. It is a perennial that can be grown as an annual in cooler climates.

name: Leonotis Leonotis nepetifolia sp. nepetifolia
desc:
This hardy, vigorous species boasts magnificent shaggy, round inflorescences adorned with a corolla of tubular orange flowers, as well as numerous medicinal and culinary properties.

 

Leonotis is also known as lion's ear or lion's tail.

name: Leopard
desc:
This bremia-resistant variety produces a medium-sized rosette with glossy green leaves spotted with red and curly edges. They offer a crisp texture and pleasant flavor.

It was bred by Frank Morton in Oregon.

name: Leaf
desc: This variety has very fragrant leaves up to 10 cm long and almost as wide.

name: Liberty Bell
desc: This vigorous old variety, productive in all climatic conditions, produces red fruits that look like small peppers. They contain very few seeds and have a mild, non-acidic flavor. Perfect for stuffed tomato recipes!

name: Licorice
desc: This very robust variety with purple flowers produces dark-green leaves marbled with purple, with a strong licorice fragrance. They are often used to make desserts.

name: Lilac Bell
desc: This variety produces beautifully colored, lobed fruits, 6 to 8 cm long and 5 to 6 cm wide. They ripen from creamy green to lilac to red, with thick, juicy, sweet flesh.

name: Lily of the Valley
desc: This productive, early-ripening, disease-resistant variety offers an abundance of clusters of 9 to 10 very sweet, oval-shaped fruits ending in a small nipple.

name: Lime / Thai Lemon
desc: This ancient "lemon basil" variety, known as "Maenglak" in Thailand, produces small, elongated, pointed leaves with a lemony flavor.

name: Lime Green
desc:
This compact variety, ideal for pot cultivation, produces numerous clusters of 7 to 9 small, round, yellow-green fruits weighing 100 to 150 g when ripe. Their firm flesh offers a deliciously tangy flavor.

It was developed by Tom Wagner and introdu

name: Limoges yellow
desc: This Limousin selection of Jaune Paille des Vertus is well adapted to mid-altitude areas, with rounded bulbs around 10 cm in diameter and a coppery yellow skin.

name: Lipstick
desc: This variety, adapted to cool climates and not very sensitive to the tobacco mosaic virus, produces 12 to 15 conical fruits about 10 cm long. They ripen from green to bright red, revealing very juicy flesh.

name: Listada Da Gandia
desc:
This old variety produces oval, cream-colored fruits with a pink-mauve stripe and a green, spiny calyx. The flesh is firm and of excellent quality.

This variety should be picked when fairly young.

name: Little Bells
desc: This compact, productive variety is suited to cool regions. It produces an abundance of tasty berries that ripen from green to red, transparent yellow and orange.

name: Little Fingers
desc: This variety produces clusters of 5 to 10 very fine, easy-to-harvest fruits, 10 to 17 cm long. Their shiny purple skin encloses sweet flesh. Excellent both fried and pickled!

name: Little Gem
desc:
Little Gem romaine lettuce is an old-fashioned variety, halfway between "romaine" and "pommée". It produces a small head of wavy green leaves, with a firm creamy-yellow center, and offers a crunchy texture and excellent flavor.

Characteristics of

name: Little Queen
desc:
This old variety produces dark-green "fat" leaves, crisp and fleshy, with an almost sweet taste, forming a firm apple up to 20 cm in diameter.

This variety is highly resistant to drought.

name: Little Sailor
desc: This mid-early Russian variety offers small oval fruits with white and violet-pink variegations. Their white flesh has a creamy texture and mild flavor.

name: Little Sparrow
desc: The Petit Moineau variety, or red currant, produces a myriad of juicy mini-tomatoes, around 1 cm in diameter, with a mild, sweet, fragrant flavor. Famous for its vigor, resistance and productivity, it forms a large bush that needs to be well staked.

name: Livingston's Magnus
desc: This old variety produces clusters of 4 to 6 reddish-pink, well-rounded, regular fruits weighing 180 to 250 g. Their firm flesh is sweet and deliciously fragrant.

name: Livingston's Main Crop Pink
desc: This ancient variety was introduced before 1900 by Alexander Livingston, the greatest breeder of tomato varieties in the USA during the 19th century. It produces succulent, dense, fleshy fruits weighing around 300 g, flattened and slightly ribbed at

name: Lobelia Lobelia syphilitica
desc: This hardy perennial grows up to 1.20 m tall. On upright stems, it produces long spikes with an abundance of tubular, bright-blue flowers.

name: Lollo Bionda
desc: This very old variety produces light-green, slightly wavy foliage that is tender and crunchy. Its flavor is highly appreciated.

name: Lollo Rossa
desc:
This old variety offers lettuces with magnificent bronze-green, bright red foliage, finely curled at the tip. It is appreciated for both its flavor and its decorative appeal.
The peripheral leaves are cut off, one by one, as needed. New ones develop

name: Long Early Violet
desc: This very old, sometimes thorny variety produces violet-black, club-shaped fruits, 20 to 30 cm long and 8 to 10 cm in diameter. Vigorous-growing, cold-hardy plants reach 1 m in height.

name: Long Poids d’Horloge
desc: This vigorous, productive and storable variety produces beautiful, voluminous roots with slightly spicy white flesh.

name: Long Standing Batavian
desc:
This variety produces large, upright heads with thick leaves. It is one of the most resistant batavian lettuces to bolting.

Characteristics of Long Standing Batavian lettuce

Long Standing Batavian lettuce, Lactuca sativa, is a mid-season variet

name: Moschata squash Longue de Nice
desc:
This ancient variety produces curved fruit up to 1 m long and weighing up to 10 kg. Their ochre skin at maturity encloses firm, orange flesh with a slightly sweet, musky flavor.

The fruit can be eaten young, like zucchini, or when fully ripe.

W

name: Zucchinis Longue de Toscane
desc:
This variety produces large, green and yellow-striped fruits up to 40 cm long. Their lighter-colored flesh has a pleasantly sweet flavor.

WHAT ARE THE 

CHARACTERISTICS OF LONGUE DE Toscane?

The "Longue de Toscane" zucchini variety, Cucurbita

name: Longue des Fermes de Cascine
desc: This Italian variety has long, shiny green pods, 30 to 35 cm long. They contain 6 to 10 delicious, pale-green, tender beans with a mild flavor.

name: Louisiana Long Green
desc: This old, thornless variety, native to Louisiana, produces numerous banana-shaped fruits striped with pale and dark green. The flesh is white and soft, with a sweet flavor.

name: Lubiana
desc:
This variety produces abundant foliage and roots about 20 cm long, pointed with strong collars. They are yellow in color and have a sweet, spicy flavor that improves with storage.

This variety originates from Croatia.

name: Luc's half-length
desc: This old, productive variety has conical roots, about 15 cm long, perfectly adapted to heavy, difficult soils. They don't form a heart, and their flesh reveals a fine, sweet flavor.

name: Lunar White
