desc: This productive variety produces creamy-white roots with fine, soft flesh. Their flavor is highly appreciated.

name: Lunchbox Red
desc: This beautiful variety produces small, light-red peppers 5 to 7 cm long, slightly ribbed and pointed, with firm flesh and a pleasant flavor.

name: Lunéville
desc:
This old Lorraine variety, highly resistant to cold and humidity, produces pale-green, dark-green mottled fruit with marked ribs, round or oval, weighing 1.5 to 2.5 kg. Their juicy, melt-in-the-mouth orange flesh has a delicious flavor.

name: Lunga Di Napoli
desc: This ancient, early and very productive variety offers intense purple fruits, to be harvested before they reach 20 cm in length, with firm, tasty flesh containing few seeds.

name: Silene Lychnis chalcedonica
desc: This very old flowering species from our gardens of yesteryear is particularly hardy, with magnificent scarlet-red flowers contrasting with dark green foliage.

name: Mac Gregor Favorite
desc:
This variety, especially appreciated for its foliage, produces beautiful magenta-purple leaves with a metallic sheen. They have a tender texture and delicious flavor.

It is a very old variety originating in Scotland.

name: Macedonian Gene Pool

name: Curly endive Macun
desc: Macun is a productive, robust variety. After root forcing, it produces firm, compact chicory with a pleasant flavor. The roots need no covering of soil to produce chicory.

name: Madagascar
desc: This Madagascan variety produces egg-shaped, pinkish-red cherry tomatoes with a yellow-orange tinge, weighing between 30 and 50 g, with a good shelf life. Their flesh is firm and juicy, with a sweet, fragrant flavor.

name: Majestic Red
desc: This vigorous variety, slow to bolt, produces a long, cylindrical, somewhat loose rosette. The purple-tinged green leaves have a firm texture and a very pleasant flavor.

name: Major
desc: This buttery, productive, disease-resistant variety produces an abundance of golden-yellow, stringless, fleshy pods, 15 to 18 cm long. Use fresh, for freezing or canning.

name: Rutabaga Major Dunne
desc:
This variety produces large, slightly elongated, cream-colored roots with purple collars. The flesh is firm and full-flavored.

The roots are partly exposed above ground, making this variety relatively resistant to nematodes.

name: Malawi Camphor
desc:
This 40 cm-high ecotype produces white flowers and long green leaves with a strong camphor fragrance.
Ocimum americanum is resistant to the fungus Fusarium oxysporum ssp. basilicii as well as to basil blight, Peronospora belbahrii.

name: Malaysian Dark Red
desc: This ancient variety produces an abundance of slender fruits up to 25 cm long, purple in color, sometimes crimson. Their flesh has a tender texture and mild flavor. Ideal for frying.

name: Mandarin
desc: This productive variety, one of the earliest, offers an abundance of fruit, orange when ripe, with the appearance of flattened, ribbed tomatoes, easy to stuff. Their thick flesh reveals a fruity, very sweet flavor.

name: Mango

name: Manoa
desc: This variety is highly resistant to heat and dry necrosis, and produces beautiful, very compact apples with deep-green, wavy, half-embossed leaves. They have a tender texture and good flavor.

name: Manyel
desc:
This ancient Amerindian variety, vigorous and productive, offers an abundance of round, sometimes slightly flattened and ribbed, golden-yellow fruit. Their flavor is a blend of sweetness and acidity. Its name means "many moons".

name: Curly endive Maraichère Très Fine
desc: This variety produces a voluminous apple with a naturally whitening core, fine ribs and sharply cut curly leaves. They offer a thick texture and pleasant flavor.

name: Marblehead Yakima
desc: This very old variety produces beautiful, more or less elongated fruit weighing from 3 to 11 kg, with a very hard, blue-gray skin. Their dense, yellow-orange flesh has an excellent flavor. They keep for up to 9 months.

name: Rutabaga Marian
desc: This hardy, vigorous variety has round roots with red collars up to 20 cm in diameter. The creamy-yellow flesh has good flavor.

name: Maritime Pink
desc:
The round fruits of this old variety weigh up to 300 g and have juicy flesh with an excellent sweet, somewhat lemony flavor.

After a period of drought, the fruit is prone to bursting in the event of abundant watering.

name: Marizol Gold Cherry
desc: This variety produces large cherry tomatoes, weighing 25 to 40 g, sometimes a little flattened. Their yellow, orange and red marbled skin encloses delicious, sweet-tasting flesh.

name: Marizol Purple
desc:
This old variety produces bunches of 3 to 4 oval fruits that can reach 1 kg and are not very sensitive to bursting. Their dense flesh is juicy and slightly sweet. The well-developed foliage is reminiscent of potatoes.

name: Market Miracle
desc: This compact variety produces round, slightly flattened fruits weighing 250 to 350 g. Their flavor is a good balance between sweetness and acidity.

name: Marmande
desc: Even at low temperatures, this old variety produces an abundance of irregularly-shaped red fruits weighing around 250 g. Their firm flesh is very tasty. Their firm flesh is highly prized.

name: Marsha's Starfighter Beefsteak
desc: This variety, the result of Marsha Eisenberg's "Star Wars" project in the United States, offers large, indigo-red to streaked and dotted brown fruits, weighing from 200 to 400 g. Their beefsteak-like flesh is firm and juicy, with a rich flavor.

name: Martian Purple
desc: This unique variety produces dark-red stems, leaves and bristles, as well as 20 cm-long spikes containing dark-red, almost black seeds.

name: Matador / Viking
desc: This productive variety, slow to bolt, produces rosettes of dark green, triangular, broad and thick leaves. They can be eaten raw or cooked, immediately after harvesting.

name: Matina
desc: This vigorous, productive German variety is ideal for cool climates. It produces clusters of 5 to 10 perfectly round, red fruits weighing 90 to 160 g, with firm, sweet flesh.

name: Matt's Black
desc:
This hardy variety produces an abundance of small, oblong, mahogany-colored fruits, about 2 cm in diameter. It is the result of a cross between a currant tomato(Solanum pimpinellifolium) and a Russian black tomato.

This new, relatively stable, fou

name: Matt's Folly
desc:
This variety produces small, elongated, variegated fruits in yellow and red. The flesh is red. Fruits can vary widely in color, shape and flavor.

name: Matt's Wild Cherry
desc:
This very early variety produces an abundance of cherry tomatoes about 2 cm in diameter, even in cold weather.

The fruit has a beautiful texture and high sugar content.

This variety grows wild in eastern Mexico.

name: Sweet Pea Matucana
desc:
This old variety offers beautiful, particularly fragrant purple and violet flowers on voluble plants. Perfect for decorating any surface!

name: Mauritius Warty
desc:
The magnificent fruits of this ancient variety are warty, rather cylindrical, not very elongated and covered with excrescences.

The gourds dry in a dry, well-ventilated place and can be painted, carved or turned into various objects.

name: May Queen
desc:
This ancient, hardy, fast-growing variety produces wavy, light-green leaves tinged with reddish-brown, forming a tight-core head.

name: Mayo Giant Bule

The fruits of this variety reach 60 cm in length and are bottle-shaped. The skin, initially green, turns brown when ripe.

The gourds dry in a dry, ventilated place and can be painted, sculpted or transformed into various objects.

name: Medium Leaf
desc: This vigorous, fast-growing variety has medium-sized, rounded green leaves with a particularly pleasant aroma.

name: Mélange Creusois
desc:

name: Melitopolski
desc: This early variety produces 30 cm-long fruits with light and dark green striped skin. Their red flesh has a mild, sweet flavor. Seeds are reddish-brown.

name: Melnicky Maj
desc: This very rare variety, originally from Czechoslovakia, produces beautiful apples with smooth, slightly curved leaves, a soft, shiny green tinged with red.

name: Melonga

name: Pepo squash Melonnette Jaspée de Vendée
desc:
The Melonnette Jaspée de Vendée squash is a very old variety that produces 5 to 7 yellow-orange fruits, round and cracked like a melon, up to 20 cm in diameter and weighing up to 3 kg. Their thick, orange flesh has a very sweet taste.

Characterist

name: Memories Of Mona
desc:
This extremely hardy, drought-resistant variety has flowers around 5 cm wide in warm hues. Their vivid colors range from deep red to intense orange, with red edges and streaks.

This variety, over 2 m tall, produces thick, vigorous stems that don't wilt. They produce beautiful panicles rich in orange-red, partially shelled grains.

This vigorous, frost-resistant variety forms a very open rosette of crisp, reddish-purple leaves, reminiscent of the Merlot grape whose name it bears.

name: Merveille de Verano
desc: This variety has large, dense apples and crisp, blistered foliage. Heat-resistant, it can be grown in all seasons.

desc: This very old, vigorous French variety can be grown in any season. Even in hot weather, it produces firm, bright-red leaves that quickly form a rounded head.

This very old variety originated in Australia. Suitable for warm climates.

desc:
This early, highly productive variety can reach 60 cm in height. It produces a multitude of pods containing 2 to 3 large, delicious beans with a nutty flavor.

Edamame beans are traditionally eaten immature, simply after a brief boiling in water.

name: Miel du Mexique
desc: This old variety, ideal for late harvests, offers an abundance of bunches of 10 to 15 small, round, bright-red fruits that are not very sensitive to bursting. The flesh is very juicy and low in acidity.

name: Moschata squash Milk Pumpkin
desc: This variety produces beautiful, round, flattened, slightly ribbed fruits weighing 6 to 7 kg. Their skin, ochre when fully ripe, encloses a soft, pale orange, slightly stringy flesh.

name: Miniature Chocolate Bell
desc: This variety produces up to 75 ribbed peppers, with 2 or 3 lobes, ripening from green to chocolate brown when fully ripe. They have thick flesh and are good for stuffing.

name: Miniature Yellow Bell

name: / Kales Mixed

name: Moldovan Green

desc: This variety produces round, pale-red fruit with a sweet-tart flavor.

name: Moscow

name: Arnica Mountain Arnica
desc:
This perennial species, native to the mountainous regions of Europe and southern Russia, produces a rosette of downy leaves and an upright stem ending in a sunny, melliferous, medicinal flower 6 to 8 cm in diameter.

name: Mountain Gold
desc:
This variety offers orange-yellow fruits weighing 150 to 300 g, fairly round and slightly ribbed at the top. They are not very sensitive to bursting and keep very well.

This variety is not very sensitive to Fusarium and Verticillium.

This ancient variety has beautiful panicles bursting with red seeds.

Its sweet stalks can be eaten as a delicacy when the seeds are ripe.

name: Cotton Msb Green
desc: This productive variety produces light-green cotton bales weighing 5 to 6 grams each. The flowers are yellow, turning pink when pollinated by insects!

name: Muchacha
desc: This variety, bred by Lee Goodwin in New Mexico from crosses with a wild tomato, produces a myriad of small, very sweet red fruits.

name: Muscat

This ancient variety produces very ribbed, spherical fruits that can reach 50 cm in diameter and weigh up to 20 kg. Their green epidermis, which turns ochre as they ripen, encloses firm, musky, sweet flesh. They keep for 4 to 9 months.

name: Moschata squash Musquée du Maroc

name: Moschata squash Musquée du Népal

name: Rutabaga Nadmorska
desc: This old variety produces yellow, rather elongated, oval roots. The flesh is also yellow, with a firm texture.

desc: This ancient variety produces an abundance of elongated, slightly curled and crinkled fruits over 20 cm long, excellent for frying. Their firm flesh offers a delicately spicy, fruity and smoky flavor.

name: National 3
desc: This early variety produces beautiful, round, dark pink roots with white tips in a very short space of time. The flesh is tender and has excellent flavor.

name: National Pickling
desc:
This old-fashioned, highly productive variety produces short, green fruit no larger than 12 cm, which can be candied or eaten as a salad. It is resistant to cucumber mosaic and cucumber cladosporium.

name: Navajo Winter
desc:
This old variety produces beautiful round fruits with pale green skin. Their pinkish-red flesh has a sweet, fragrant taste.

This watermelon has a very long shelf-life.

name: Neckargold
desc:
This vigorous, hardy variety produces an abundance of butter-yellow, rounded pods up to 25 cm long. They are tender, fleshy and contain white seeds.

 

This variety can be eaten fresh or dry, with harvesting staggered until frost.

This variety can be sown from spring to early

name: Nerum Boer

Sorghum, which is highly resistant to drought, is an annual plant used for the nutritional qualities of its "popping" grains or for making syrups. This variety grows to almost 3 meters in height and bears orange-brown panicles.

name: Nesvizhskaya
desc: This variety of sugar beet produces conical, white roots. Its sugar content is 17-18%.

name: Pepo squash New England Pie / Small Sugar

This very old variety, suited to small gardens, produces 6 to 12 round, flattened orange fruits weighing 2 to 3 kg. Their orange-yellow flesh is firm and thin, with a sweet flavor.

The fruit keeps for 3 to 6 months.

desc: This variety offers distinctive fruit, variegated with red and orange, slightly square and hollow inside, perfect for stuffing. Their flesh contains little pulp.

name: Zucchinis Nimba

name: Noir des Carmes
desc: This very old, early variety produces round, ribbed fruits weighing 1 to 1.5 kg with a green-black skin that turns orange when fully ripe. The orange flesh is sweet and very tasty.

name: Noir Long de Caluire
desc:
This low-insect-susceptibility variety produces elongated, white-fleshed roots with a tender, creamy texture and excellent sweet flavor.

Can be sown from July to August.

name: Common Bean Nombril de Bonne Sœur
desc: This old French variety, reputed to be very easy to digest, produces an abundance of white seeds punctuated by reddish spots on the umbilicus. They can be eaten fresh or dried, and have a very fine chestnut taste.

desc: This low-growing variety, 50 to 70 cm high, produces an abundance of large, flat pods with a sweet flavor. They are eaten whole when young. When fully ripe, they contain delicious, round, smooth seeds.

desc: This variety, adapted to cool regions, produces clusters of 5 to 6 large, variegated, yellow-orange and pink fruits, weighing 200 to 400 g. They contain "beefsteak"-type flesh, orange marbled with red, with a mild, sweet flavor.

desc: This very early variety produces compact, firm, light-green apples, 30 cm long, with a mild flavor. Ideal for making kimchi.

name: Oda

name: Odessa

name: Zucchinis Odessa

name: Ogen

name: Oka du Québec

This old variety produces fruits weighing 2 to 4 kg, with ochre-colored skin and an embroidered appearance. Their thick, orange flesh is very flavorful and fragrant.

name: Zucchinis Ola Escaladora

name: Olive Green

This ancient variety produces smooth, egg-shaped fruit up to 25 cm in diameter, weighing 2 to 3 kg. The olive-green skin is easy to peel, and the yellow flesh is firm and stringy, improving with storage.

Fruits keep for 3 to 7 months.

name: Opalka

This species offers a multitude of elegant pink flowers followed by small, shiny copper capsules, ideal for making dried bouquets.
Its edible leaves are eaten like purslane or spinach.

name: Orange
desc:
This variety with its cut foliage produces an abundance of luminous yellow and orange flowers right up to the frosts. The flowers, left to dry on the ground in autumn, provide seeds that are highly prized by birds.

This species is very popular wit

name: California poppies Orange
desc: This variety offers an abundance of magnificent flowers with delicate, wispy golden-yellow petals. Perennial in mild-climate regions, it can self-sow in others.

name: Orange Banana
desc: This variety produces cylindrical, orange banana-like fruits, 8 to 10 cm long with a small point at the tip, weighing 60 to 80 g. They have a mild flavor and are suitable for sauces and preserves as well as for drying.

name: Orange Bourgois
desc:
This variety offers an abundance of clusters of around twenty small, round, orange fruits, weighing around fifty grams and with excellent flavor.

It was developed by Mr. Bourgois, a French collector on the Ile d'Oléron.

name: Orange Centiflor
desc: This variety produces bunches of hundreds of small, round, delicious fruits! Very ornamental, it was obtained by crossing several cherry tomato varieties with a wild tomato species.

name: Argyrosperma Orange Cushaw
desc: This variety offers an abundance of beautiful pear-shaped fruits, with often twisted necks, orange with beige stripes. Particularly appreciated for pie-making, their yellow flesh has a rich flavor.

name: Orange Fleshed Honeydew
desc: This ancient variety produces round to oval fruits up to 18 cm in diameter and weighing up to 2.5 kg. The smooth, hard skin turns creamy-white when fully ripe. The juicy orange flesh is sweet and spicy.

name: Orange Glow
desc: This hardy variety produces oval, grey-green fruits weighing up to 25 kg. Their orange flesh has a firm, crunchy texture and excellent flavor. Seeds are cream-colored. It is reputed to be one of the best orange-fleshed varieties.

name: Orange Habanero
desc: This ancient, highly ornamental variety produces an abundance of large, lantern-shaped fruits with crinkled skins, which turn yellow-green and orange when ripe. Often eaten raw for their full flavor, these extremely hot peppers are also used to make

name: Orange Oxheart
desc: This variety produces a large number of very fleshy fruits weighing close to 500g. They contain few seeds and their firm flesh has a very mild flavor.

name: Pepo squash Orange Patty Pan
desc: This variety produces beautiful orange, slightly domed star-shaped fruits up to 20 cm in diameter. The raw flesh of the young fruit is firm and thick, with an artichoke-like flavor. When ripe, they can be stuffed.

name: Orange Queen
desc: This variety, one of the most popular orange tomatoes, has bright orange, medium-ribbed, beef-flesh fruits weighing 120 to 180 g. The flesh is very firm and juicy. The flesh is very firm and juicy.

name: Orange Strawberry
desc: This variety produces many large, heart-shaped, orange, fleshy fruits. Their flesh, not very juicy and dense, offers a pronounced, well-balanced flavor.

name: Ordono
desc:
This dazzling, highly ornamental variety offers an abundance of upright fruits, about 2 cm long, in various shades of green, yellow, orange, violet and red. The fruits are edible and can be eaten fresh or sautéed.

 

Native to Batopilas Canyon,

name: Oregon Delicious
desc: This old American variety produces oblong-shaped fruits weighing several kilos. Their juicy orange flesh reveals an exquisitely sweet flavor.

name: Oregon Spring
desc:
This variety, very well adapted to cool climates, grows to no more than 1 m tall. The fruit is slightly flattened, weighing 150 to 220 g, juicy, with few seeds and a sweet taste.

It can be planted four weeks before the last frosts, but should be pro

name: Orenburg Giant
desc: This Russian variety produces large, almost round, pinkish-red fruits, up to 1 kg in weight. Their pink flesh contains few seeds and has a sweet flavor that is much appreciated.

name: Orissa
desc:
This variety produces round fruit up to 12 cm in diameter, with a white skin zebra-striped with green, turning green-yellow when ripe.

This variety is native to India.

name: Oroma
desc: This ancient variety, around 1 m high, produces tasty cylindrical fruits, often with a pointed end, 6 to 8 cm long. Suitable for making sauces, they are easy to peel and their dense flesh contains few seeds.

name: Zucchinis Ortolana Di Faenza
desc:
This hardy old variety has fairly short fruit, slightly enlarged at the base. The skin is light green with darker stripes, and the flesh has a highly appreciated flavor.

WHAT ARE THE 

CHARACTERISTICS OF THE ORTOLANA DI FAENZA ZUCCHINI?

Ortol

name: Osmin
desc: This highly decorative variety of basil, with its serrated purple leaves, has a particularly strong fragrance, an aniseed flavor and mauve flowers.

name: Rutabaga Ounakollane
desc: This variety offers 10 to 15 cm long purple roots with yellow collars and flesh, a firm texture and tasty flesh.

name: Oxheart  Yasha Yugoslavian
desc: This old variety with light foliage produces an abundance of dense, fleshy, dark-pink fruit weighing 300-800 g. It can be eaten raw, in salads, or cooked in sauces or coulis.

name: Oxheart Akers
desc: This highly productive variety, with its fine, chiselled foliage, produces large, fleshy, firm fruits weighing 300 to 600 g.

name: Oxheart Hungarian
desc:
This "Coeur de Bœuf" variety, with its light foliage and good productivity, offers an abundance of large fruits, often over 500 g, containing very few seeds.
Originally from a small village near Budapest, it was brought to the United States in 1901.

name: Oxinel
desc: This essential variety produces an abundance of long, green pods, without threads or parchment, around 18 cm long. Their excellent taste makes them ideal for both fresh consumption and canning.

name: Padron
desc:
This variety, traditionally grown in Galicia, offers a multitude of conical fruits. Young and green, they have a mild flavor, becoming hot when ripe. However, around 10% of immature chillies can be hot!

 

Capsaicin content can vary according to

name: Chinese cabbage Pak Choy / Prize Choy
desc: This very elegant, vase-shaped variety has smooth, soft, elongated stems of medium thickness.

name: Chinese cabbage Pak Choy with White Stems
desc: This variety produces a very open, non-headed rosette, with fleshy white stems and glossy green leaves offering excellent flavor.

name: Palatine
desc: This old variety is one of the best and most hardy. It produces a full-green apple with a reddish-brown tinge. Its leaves offer a crunchy texture and excellent flavor.

name: Celosia Pampa Plume
desc: This surprisingly attractive variety produces magnificent conical "plumes" in bright, luminous colors. Ideal for creating a sensation in flower beds and bouquets of fresh or dried flowers.

name: Pantos
desc: This abundantly-growing variety, particularly vigorous and tolerant of soil-borne pathogens, can reach over 2.5 m in height. Its elongated, slightly pointed fruits, 15 to 20 cm long, offer melting, juicy, sweet flesh.

name: Papalo
desc: Native to South America, this heat-demanding annual offers abundant aromatic foliage. It reveals a fresh, spicy flavor, reminiscent for some of coriander. Its leaves are eaten raw, to season both dishes and sauces.

name: Papaver apokrinomenon
desc:
This drought-resistant species has slender, upright stems 30 cm high, each bearing a single 4-petaled orange flower with a yellow-green center.

It is native to western Turkey.

name: Papaver nudicaule
desc: This perennial mix has slender, upright stems 40 to 60 cm high, bearing a single yellow, orange, white or pink flower.

name: Paradicsom Alaku Sarga Szentes
desc: This compact variety produces flat, ribbed yellow peppers 6-8 cm in diameter, resembling mini pumpkins. Their thick, crunchy, juicy flesh offers excellent flavor.

name: of Milan Paradiesler
desc: Originating in Switzerland, this old late-harvest variety produces beautiful, rounded, dense apples weighing 1 to 1.5 kg with slightly blistered leaves. Very hardy, it is particularly appreciated for its exceptional fine flavor.

name: Paragon
desc: This very old, vigorous variety produces an abundance of beautiful, round, smooth fruit, weighing 250 to 400 g. It was Alexander Livingston's first selection and is said to be the ancestor of many modern tomatoes.

name: Parris Island
desc: This variety, which produces very large, elongated apples 25 to 30 cm high, has soft, crunchy, dark-green leaves and a tasty, creamy-white core! Perfect for summer and autumn harvests.

name: Pasilla
desc: This variety, very popular in Mexican cuisine, offers pointed fruits that ripen from dark green to chocolate brown. Their fine, moderately spicy flesh is reminiscent of raisins.

name: Pasquier
desc: This old variety has smooth, crisp, bronze-green leaves, forming a very firm apple. It is suitable for spring and summer cultivation and is resistant to bolting.

name: Passion Blonde
desc: This old French variety produces dense apples with smooth leaves lightly tinged with red. They have a crisp texture and excellent flavor.

name: Pastel Sleeves
desc:
This variety offers elongated or sometimes bell-shaped fruits. They weigh from 100 to 150 g. The skin is variegated with red, yellow, orange and green. The flesh is completely green.
This is a recent variety - developed by Tom Wagner - which can disp

name: Pepo squash Patty Pans in Mix
desc: This blend of old varieties produces fruit that is sometimes warty, in the shape of swollen discs, crowned with yellow, white, orange and green. Their white flesh is fine, firm and slightly floury. When young, they can be eaten raw, with an artichoke

name: Paul Robeson
desc: This variety offers dark fruits weighing 150 to 400 g, fairly ribbed and flattened. They are not very sensitive to bursting. Their flavor is very sweet and spicy.

name: Chinese cabbage Pe-Tsai
desc: This variety, the most common on French shelves, has a dense rosette of light-green leaves with broad white ribs, 20 to 35 cm high. Mild and sweet-tasting, they can be eaten raw or cooked.

name: Peacevine
desc: Extremely rich in vitamin C, this variety offers an abundance of small, firm, sweet fruit with excellent shelf life. Its name is inspired by its high content of the amino acid gamma-amino-butyric acid, known for its soothing effect.

name: Peach Blow Sutton
desc: This old-fashioned variety has downy foliage with yellow and red highlights, and produces pinkish-red fruit blurred with yellow and covered in down. Their dense, juicy flesh offers a sweet flavor.

name: Pearly Pink Cherry
desc: This vigorous variety produces an abundance of clusters of 6 to 9 purplish-pink, green-streaked, round fruits, sometimes noticeably stretched. They contain few seeds and their dense flesh is somewhat mealy.

name: Peasant
desc: An ideal variety for preserves and sauces, it produces, on compact plants, around a hundred "Roma"-type fruits weighing 90 to 120 grams.

name: Penta Green
desc:
This old variety, well adapted to cool, short seasons, can reach 3 m in height and produces tender, dark-green fruit with a perfectly rounded cross-section. They can be eaten raw or cooked before they reach 8 cm.

 

The fruits contain a mucilagi

name: Quinoas Peppermint
desc: This variety, almost 2 m tall, has white panicles and seeds, some of which are very slightly brown. It is resistant to seed germination on panicles during the rainy season.

name: Perestroika
desc: This variety offers round, more or less elongated fruits with smooth, orange-red skin, weighing 200 to 300 g. Their dense, juicy flesh contains few seeds and reveals a highly appreciated fruity flavor.

name: Perfection
desc: This variety, which is not very susceptible to the mosaic virus, produces heart-shaped fruits that ripen from green to bright red. Their thick, fleshy flesh offers a very fruity flavor.

name: Perkins Long Pod
desc:
This highly productive variety produces light-green fruits, 10 to 15 cm long. They can be eaten raw or cooked, before reaching 8 cm, and have a slight eggplant flavor.

 

The fruits contain a mucilaginous substance that thickens soups, sauces an

name: Perky Peony Mix
desc: This variety offers voluminous, double, peony-shaped flowers on very tall plants. Their brilliantly variegated colors - white, pink, purple and mauve - are a delight in fresh bouquets.

name: Persimmon
desc: This very old variety has large, orange-gold, "beef-flesh" type fruits, heavily ribbed and flattened, weighing from 500 g to 1 kg. They contain firm flesh with few seeds and a unique flavor.

name: Peruvian
desc:
This variety produces few lavender flowers and flat, pointed green leaves. They offer a somewhat "medicinal" flavor, blending scents of mint, oregano and camphor.

Found in the southern USA and most of Latin America.

name: Peruvian Bush
desc: This vigorous, bushy variety produces clusters of 8 to 10 small, round, red fruits. Their juicy flesh delivers a hint of acidity.

name: Peshawar
desc: This variety produces snow-white flowers followed by huge pods containing the seeds generally used for breads and pastries.

name: Petch Siam
desc: This productive, easy-to-grow Indian variety produces small, round, green fruits about 5 cm in diameter, with darker stripes. They are very popular in Indian, Thai and Vietnamese cuisine.

name: Petit Carré de Nice
desc: This old variety, native to southern France, produces thick-fleshed, red, cubic fruits with 3 to 4 lobes.

name: Petit Gris de Rennes
desc:
This very old variety, well adapted to cool regions, produces an abundance of "Sucrin"-type fruit, weighing around 1 kg, with coarsely serrated skin and delicious, well-flavored flesh.
This variety has a vigorous growth habit.

name: Petit Jaune d'Antibes
desc: This variety produces fruits 10 to 15 cm long, turning yellow when fully ripe. They are very tasty.

name: Petit Marseillais
desc: This traditional southern variety, reputed to be slow to set seed, produces small, slender green leaves with a powerful aroma, often eaten raw to preserve the strength of their flavors.

name: Petit Marseillais
desc: This highly productive, West Indian-type variety produces elongated, slightly crinkled, misshapen fruits that are yellow/orange in color when ripe. Their very fine flesh has a sweet, delicately peppery and piquant flavor.

name: Zucchinis Petite Grise d'Alger
desc:
This ancient, drought-resistant variety, originally from Algeria, produces "marrow gourd" type fruits, light green in color with gray flecks. The flesh is sweet and mild.

WHAT ARE THE 

CHARACTERISTICS OF THE "Petite Grise d'Alger" zucchini?

name: Petros
desc: This variety grows to less than 1 m tall and produces bunches of 5 to 6 round, slightly undulating fruits right up to the frosts. Their juicy flesh has a sweet flavor.

name: Petrowski Early Round Yellow
desc:
This old, very early variety originated in Berlin. According to Goethe, it is the finest turnip variety, with a fabulous taste. Its firm, tender white flesh has a sweet taste.

This very early variety can be grown from spring to autumn.

Characte

name: Peyote Ceremonial
desc:
This ancient, very early variety produces small, two-lobed gourds, 5 to 10 cm long, ochre-colored when ripe. They are traditionally used for Amerindian Peyote ceremonies.

The gourds dry in a dry, ventilated place and can be painted, sculpted or tr

name: Philadelphia White Box
desc: This old-fashioned variety produces charming, rounded, pure-white radishes with crunchy, sweet, flavorful flesh. Fast-growing, it is ideal for forced cultivation under cover.

name: Picotee
desc:
This magnificent variety boasts vigorous foliage topped by an abundance of 6 to 7 cm-diameter flowers in various shades of white, white with magenta, pink, carmine red and more.

Cosmos are a favorite with birds. The flowers can be left to dry in a

name: Piel de Sapo
desc:
Piel de Sapo, also known as Peau de Crapaud or Piñonet, is a green Spanish melon with oval fruits weighing 3 to 4 kg. They contain a tasty, crunchy white flesh, very sweet and intensely fragrant.

This melon is harvested from September onwards and

name: Piennolo Del Vesuvio
desc:
This very old variety produces abundant clusters of pear-shaped cherry tomatoes weighing 20 to 30 g and ending in a nipple. It is a sweet-tasting drying tomato.

What are the characteristics of the Vesuvius tomato?

The Vesuvius tomato or Pomodor

name: Pierre Bénite
desc: This ancient variety produces blistered, wavy leaves with curly edges, pale green in color, forming a crunchy, sweet apple up to 30 cm in diameter.

name: Pike
desc: This old variety, selected for its ability to thrive in heavy, dry soils, produces elongated, embroidered fruits weighing from 1 to 3 kg, depending on conditions. It was selected in the 1930s by Aaaron Pike in Monmouth, Oregon, from crosses between "

name: Pimento Super Red
desc: This variety, adapted to warm regions and not very sensitive to the tobacco mosaic virus, produces rounded, ribbed, slightly flattened fruits. They ripen from green to red and offer delicious, thick, sweet flesh.

name: Pineapple
desc:
The pineapple tomato is a very old variety, withbeefsteak-like fruits that can reach up to 1 kg. The yellow skin is marbled with red, and the flesh is very firm, reminiscent of pineapple.

The fruit contains few seeds and has a rich, sweet flavor w

name: Pineapple Blues
desc: This recent variety, developed by Tom Wagner, produces dense fruit of varying shape, color and flavor. Mostly round and fleshy, they are pink to orange and turn indigo in the sun.

name: Pineapple Pig
desc:
This variety offers fleshy fruits weighing around 400 g, creamy-yellow with pink streaks. The flavor is sweet.

Developed by Brad Gates of Wild Boar Farms in California.

name: Centratherum Pineapple Sangria
desc: This perennial plant, native to Brazil, forms small bushes with toothed leaves and a delicate pineapple fragrance. Throughout the summer, it produces an abundance of lavender-pink, melliferous flowers, about 5 cm in diameter.

name: Pineapple with Potato Leaves
desc:
This weather-sensitive variety produces large, beefsteak-like fruits up to 1 kg. Their firm, juicy flesh contains few seeds and has a rich, sweet, slightly acidic flavor.
This variety is similar to "Ananas", but with potato-like foliage.

name: Pink
desc: This variety, with its serrated foliage, offers incredible pink, pompon-shaped flowers up to 10 cm in diameter, on upright, rigid stems. It reseeds very easily.

name: Pink Banana Jumbo
desc: This ancient variety produces banana-shaped fruits weighing from 5 to 30 kg, best eaten before they reach 90 cm in length. Their blue-grey skin, which turns pink when ripe, encloses bright orange flesh, dry and low in sugar, perfect for preserves and

name: Pink Beauty
desc: This variety, with its dense foliage, produces bright pink, slightly oval roots, resistant to bursting and very crunchy.

name: Pink Berkeley Tie Dye
desc: This variety produces ribbed "beef-flesh" type fruits, purple-black streaked with brown and dark green. They contain few seeds. Their dense flesh is both sweet and tangy.

name: Pink Bumble Bee
desc: This variety has small, round fruits with green, yellow and pink stripes.

name: Pink Furry Boar
desc:
This variety offers an abundance of fleshy, peach-skinned, velvety-skinned fruits weighing 60-120 g. They are pink with yellow-orange stripes and have a sweet flavor. They are pink streaked with yellow-orange and have a mild flavor.
Developed by Brad

name: Garlic bulbs Pink Garlic Bulbs "Flavor" AB - 250 g
desc:
Flavor" pink garlic will be available from the second half of February.

Discover "Flavor" pink garlic, a late spring variety with high dormancy. A rose d'Auvergne variety, it produces a high yield of ivory-white bulbs with 9 to 14 light-pink clove

name: Pink Peach / Fuzzy
desc: This beautiful variety offers tasty, smooth, round, deep-pink fruits. Their velvety skin is covered with a peach-like down. The foliage is reminiscent of potatoes.

name: Pink Pop Socks
desc:
On upright stems with airy foliage, this variety offers an abundance of sublime single and sometimes double flowers in various shades of pink.

This species is very popular with birds. The flowers can be left to dry in autumn, so that they can feed

name: Pinot
desc:
This bremia-resistant variety produces very wavy, deep-red leaves. They have a crisp texture.
It was bred by Frank Morton in Oregon from "Merlot" and "Lollo di vino".

name: Curly endive Pintone
desc: This magnificent variety, dark red with pronounced white veins, has thick leaves forming a compact, oblong head. It is ideal for storage.

name: Pirat
desc: This variety produces a dense apple with a cream-colored core. Its bronze-marbled green leaves offer a crisp texture and excellent flavor.

name: Platte Witte Mei
desc:
This fast-growing variety produces flat, white turnips with green collars and white flesh. Fairly resistant to bolting, the Platte Witte Mei turnip can be harvested as soon as it reaches 6 to 7 cm in diameter, and keeps well over winter.

Its name

name: Podland Pink
desc: This old Polish variety, highly resistant to drought, produces an abundance of small, oblong fruits in clusters, weighing 30 to 50 g and with a rather original color, ranging from yellow to pink when fully ripe.

name: Arroch Pointed-leaved Violet
desc: This hardy variety produces pointed purple leaves with a tender texture. They can be eaten raw or cooked like spinach.

name: Pointu de Chateaurenard
desc:
This very old, early and hardy variety, adapted to spring cultivation and southern regions, produces a beautiful apple with smooth, blue-green leaves, slightly tinged with red, weighing around 1 kg and ending in a point.
Not very sensitive to bolting

name: Poitou chicken leg
desc: This variety, halfway between onion and shallot, is also known as echalion. The bulbs are pink and elongated, from 10 to 18 cm long, and have an excellent shelf life. The flesh is pink, juicy and fragrant, and can be eaten raw or cooked.

name: Pokey Joe
desc: Very popular with organic growers on the West Coast of the United States, this variety is slow to bolt and has a taste considered to be the tastiest of the coriander varieties.

name: Polish Linguisa
desc: This very old variety produces an abundance of clusters of 4 to 5 elongated red "Andean retort"-type fruits, firm and suitable for sauces, right up until the frosts. Their sweet flesh contains few seeds.

name: Polish Nights
desc: This variety offers beautiful dark pink fruit, flattened at the poles, with rounded shoulders and light green streaks. Their fleshy, juicy, "beef-flesh" type delivers a smoky flavor.

name: Pomehana Apple
desc:
This variety produces an abundance of small, heart-shaped fruits, weighing from 60 to 180 g, with orange-yellow flesh streaked with pink. They reveal a deliciously fragrant, fruity flavor.

Developed by Tom Wagner, from "Green Grape" and "Hawaiian Pi

name: Pepo squash Pomme d'Or
desc:
Pomme d'Or squash produces 10 to 20 spherical fruits, 6 to 8 cm in diameter and weighing 50 to 200 g each. The hard skin, orange when ripe, encloses a thin flesh reminiscent of artichokes. They keep for up to 1 year and can be dried.

Pomme d'Or sq

name: Ponderosa Golden
desc: This variety, with its fragile foliage, produces clusters of 4 to 6 large, flattened, ribbed yellow fruits, weighing 300 to 500 g. Their dense, juicy flesh is yellow with pink veins, offering a sweet, fruity flavor.

name: Ponderosa Red
desc: This vigorous variety, adapted to humid climates, produces very large, flattened, ribbed "beef-flesh" fruits. They weigh from 600 g to 1 kg, with firm flesh and few seeds.

name: Pongo
desc: This dwarf stringless bean produces an abundance of very fine, dark-green, shiny pods, around 15 cm long and 7 to 8 mm in diameter. Carried above the foliage, they are easy to pick and reveal excellent gustatory qualities.

name: Popovich
desc: This Siberian variety, adapted to cool regions and short seasons, produces round, red fruit weighing 100 to 400 g with firm flesh and excellent flavor.

name: Potager de Vilvoorde
desc: This old Belgian variety, highly productive and well adapted to cool regions, produces bunches of 4 to 6 smooth, round fruits, weighing 100 to 200 g. Their juicy flesh reveals a sweet, fruity flavor.

name: Potimarron à Gros Fruits
desc: This ancient variety produces pear-shaped fruit weighing from 2 to 4 kg. The thin, dark-orange-red skin encloses sweet, yellow flesh with a chestnut-like flavor. The fruit keeps for 4 to 8 months.

name: Potimarron Red Kuri
desc:
This variety produces small, top-shaped, brick-red fruits weighing up to 2 kg. Their sweet orange flesh has the characteristic chestnut flavor of a pumpkin.

The fruit keeps for 4 to 8 months, depending on storage conditions.

name: Potimarron Solor
desc:
This variety, the earliest of all the pumpkin varieties, produces small orange-red fruits weighing around 1 kg with an excellent sweet taste.
"Solor" is a biodynamic research variety. It was selected by Bart Vosselman of De Bolster, Holland.

name: Potiron Blanc de Mayet
desc:
This variety produces creamy-white fruits 30 cm in diameter and weighing 3 to 6 kg. Their slightly fibrous orange flesh offers a sweet flavor.

Description of the Mayet white pumpkin

Mayet white pumpkin, Cucurbita maxima, is an old variety from

name: Potiron d’Iran
desc:
This old ornamental variety produces flattened, ribbed fruit with light-green skin flecked with pink. Their firm flesh has a good flavor and contains seeds of slightly variable size and color.

This population can offer fruits with slightly variable

name: Precocibec
desc: This dwarf, cold-hardy variety produces an abundance of clusters of 8 to 10 round, red fruits. Their firm, juicy flesh with a fruity taste is ideal for making sauces or homemade preserves.

name: Première récolte
desc: This old-fashioned variety is fast-growing, robust and suitable for autumn and winter cultivation. It produces a rosette of triangular, light-green leaves.

name: Pride Of Wisconsin
desc: This American variety produces "cantaloupe"-type ribbed melons with embroidered skin. They weigh from 1.5 to 3.5 kg, and their sweet, juicy flesh is highly prized.

name: Prima Belle
desc:
This traditional variety of stuffing bell pepper, adapted to cool climates, produces large, dark-green, then bright-red fruits when ripe, with 4 lobes and 20 to 30 cm in length. The flesh is very thick and has a delicious flavor.

This variety, dev

name: Primary Colors
desc:
This recent variety, developed by Tom Wagner, offers fruits weighing from 60 to 120 g, with colors ranging from yellow to indigo and sometimes with a silky peach skin. The juicy flesh also comes in a variety of colors.

This variety was selected fr

name: Prize Of The Trials
desc: This highly productive, vigorous variety produces an abundance of spherical, apricot-sized fruit that is not very sensitive to bursting. Among cherry tomatoes, it stands out for its exceptional flavor.

name: Puget Gold
desc: This old variety, adapted to humid or short-season regions, produces small spikes containing yellow seeds.

name: Pure White
desc: This variety's cut foliage produces pure white flowers from summer to autumn. Birds are particularly fond of the seeds, which can be left to dry on the ground in autumn.To discover on the Kokopelli blog: "Cosmos: extremely medicinal species maligned

name: Purple Ball
desc: This bushy variety with abundant foliage produces 6 to 8 beautiful, oblong, dark purple fruits, 8 cm in diameter and 15 cm long.

name: Purple Beauty
desc: This variety, which has a low susceptibility to tobacco mosaic virus, produces cubic fruits whose color changes from green to red to very dark purple when fully ripe. They turn green when cooked.

name: Purple Bumble Bee
desc:
This variety offers a myriad of variegated bronze-purple and metallic-green cherry tomatoes, weighing from 20 to 40 g and resistant to shattering. Their flesh reveals a complex, sweet flavor.
An Artisan Seeds selection.

name: Purple Calabash
desc:
This variety is highly adaptable to arid climates. Its pink fruits turn bronze when fully ripe. They have ribbed, sometimes even tormented shapes, and excellent resistance to shattering.

Fruits of this variety have good keeping qualities.

name: Purple Cape
desc: This ancient, hardy variety spends the winter in the ground and the following spring produces purple apples 15 to 20 cm in diameter, completely protected by foliage. The flesh, with its excellent flavor, turns green when cooked.

name: Purple Delight
desc: This highly ornamental variety has green and violet foliage and violet flowers. It produces pointed fruits about 3 cm long that ripen from green to red to deep purple.

name: Purple Giant Hyssop
desc: This hardy perennial produces abundant bright-green foliage and a multitude of upright stems bearing beautiful spikes of mauve flowers, which are particularly melliferous. Its fragrance of licorice and aniseed is perfect for enhancing any recipe.

name: California poppies Purple Gleam
desc: This variety, which is not very sensitive to cold, reaches 30 to 40 cm in height and has bluish-gray foliage. It offers an abundance of magnificent flowers with delicate mauve-pink petals and white-yellow centers.

name: Purple Jalapeno
desc: This variety, with its purple stems, produces an abundance of smooth, conical fruit with thick flesh. Sweeter than other "Jalapeño" varieties, they turn from green to dark purple before turning burgundy-red when fully ripe.

name: Purple Queen
desc: This magnificent, early, highly productive variety produces dark purple, stringless beans that turn green when cooked. When young, they can be eaten raw and make a wonderful addition to salads. Adapted to cool climates and shady areas.

name: Purple Top White Globe
desc: This very old, cold-hardy early variety produces white roots with purple collars, firm flesh and a mild flavor. They can be harvested when they are 5 to 7 cm in diameter and keep very well.

name: Python
desc: This splendid Russian variety produces an abundance of very long, thin, twisted sweet peppers, bright red when ripe, resembling little snakes. They measure 25 to 30 cm in length and reveal delicious, thin-walled flesh.

name: Queen Anne's Pocket
desc: This variety, which is easy to grow on trellises, produces almost round fruits 7 cm in diameter. Their velvety skin is yellow and orange. Primarily grown for their intense fragrance, their white flesh is practically tasteless.

name: Queensland Blue
desc:
This ancient variety offers blue-grey fruit, flattened on both sides and deeply ribbed. They reach 20 cm in diameter and weigh up to 6 kg. Their orange flesh is thick, dry and very flavorful.

The fruit can be stored for up to 9 months.

name: Quetzali
desc: This mildew-tolerant American variety, adapted to cool climates, produces small, round, thick-skinned fruits weighing 4 to 5 kg. The flesh is bright red and firm, with a particularly sweet taste.

name: Quintal d’Alsace
desc:
This ancient, hardy and highly productive variety produces very large, flattened, firm apples in the late season. Its smooth leaves, varying from light green to ashen, are highly prized.

Ideal for sauerkraut production.

name: Radichetta
desc: This ancient variety produces bright green leaves with a fine texture and a delicate, bitter-free flavor reminiscent of doucette. Extremely hardy, this Moroccan lettuce is easy t

name: Rak Tamachat
desc: This variety is so named because it was propagated by the Rak Tamachat permaculture center in Thailand. The plants have giant leaves up to 5 cm long. The flavor is milder than that of other varieties.

name: Ramiro Orange
desc: This Italian variety, adapted to all conditions, produces beautiful, elongated, pointed, bright orange fruits, 25-30 cm long. They offer delicious, crunchy, particularly sweet flesh.

name: Rancho Solito
desc:
This recent, particularly early variety produces clusters of numerous small, round red fruits with a thin nipple at the end. Their firm flesh offers a fragrant flavor.
After a period of drought, the fruit is susceptible to bursting if too much water

name: Rasmus
desc:
This early, vigorous variety produces a beautiful, very firm, fine-textured central apple. After harvesting the main head, it produces numerous delicious lateral shoots.

Selected by Sativa Rheinau.

name: Rat Tail
desc: This ancient variety is not grown for its roots, but for its tender siliques, picked before full maturity. Thin and very elongated, often tinged with purple, they have a mild, slightly spicy flavor and can be eaten raw, cooked or preserved.

name: Rave de Margeride
desc:
This hardy old variety, originally from the Margeride massif, has large, smooth, flattened, white roots with green collars. Adapted to high-altitude cultivation, it can be harvested all winter long, even in the snow!

This variety is highly reputed a

name: Rave de Treignac
desc: This variety from Corrèze, adapted to high altitude cultivation, produces flattened white roots with green collars. Their flesh has a fine, delicate taste. This delicious turnip can be eaten raw or cooked.

name: Rave Rose Longue Saumonée
desc: This ancient variety produces fine, strongly elongated roots, about 15 cm long and only 1 cm in diameter. The smooth, deep-red skin encloses firm, slightly pinkish, almost transparent flesh.

name: Rayolle des Cévennes
desc: This old French variety, suitable for summer harvests and highly productive, produces bulbs around 10 cm in diameter, with a straw-yellow skin and particularly soft, sweet flesh.

name: Really Red Deer Tongue
desc:
This productive variety forms a loose rosette of dark red, almost black, arrow-shaped leaves. They offer a crunchy texture and excellent flavor.

Peripheral leaves are cut off, one by one, as needed. New ones develop after successive harvests.

name: Millets Red
desc: This magnificent red variety produces long spikes measuring around 30 cm. They can be used before maturity to make bouquets. When fully ripe, the seeds are easy to harvest. Birds love them!

name: Bitter eggplants Red African
desc: This variety, native to Africa, offers red fruits, sometimes weighing 120 g, eaten young on vines up to 1.50 m long.

name: Lamb's Quarters and Company Red Aztec Spinach
desc:
This variety can reach 1 m in height and produces triangular leaves and bright red inflorescences. The leaves are eaten like spinach, both raw and cooked.
This goosefoot, also known as Red Aztec Spinach, is grown mainly in Mexico.

name: Red Brandywine
desc: This old-fashioned variety produces a large number of round "beef-flesh" fruits weighing 250-350 g. Vigorous growth and good development.

name: Brussels sprouts Red Bull
desc: This 1 m tall variety produces beautiful deep purple apples. Dense and firm in texture, they offer excellent flavor and retain their color when cooked.

name: Red Burgundy
desc:
This heat-demanding variety combines red stems, green leaves and pink flowers to produce very tender red fruits, which are best eaten - raw or cooked - before they reach 8 cm.

The fruits contain a mucilaginous substance that thickens soups, sauces

name: Red Butter
desc:
A cross between "Outredgeous" and "Sucrine", this variety offers lettuces with upright, deep-red leaves surrounding yellow-green leaves and a pink heart. The inner leaves are tender, while the outer ones are crisper.
This variety is relatively insens

name: Red Centiflor Cherry
desc:
This variety offers clusters of hundreds of small, round fruits with a sweet, highly appreciated flavor. It is very ornamental with its cluster of flowers.
It was bred by Alan Kapuler by crossing several cherry tomato varieties with a wild tomato spe

name: California poppies Red Chief
desc: This 20-25 cm tall variety with bluish-grey foliage produces an abundance of flowers with delicate, deep-red petals.

name: Quinoas Red Faro
desc: This variety, adapted to a variety of climates and growing conditions, produces an abundance of small white seeds that are highly prized for their flavor.

name: Red Fig
desc: This ancient variety, originally from the United States, produces an abundance of red, pear-shaped "cherry tomatoes" with an incredibly sweet taste.

name: Red Flamingo
desc: This magnificent variety, highly resistant to cold and suitable for late-season cultivation, produces a fairly open rosette with red, waffle-shaped leaves that are crunchy in texture and mild in flavor.

name: Red Fleshed Pineapple
desc:
This ancient variety offers an abundance of small, round fruits weighing 300 to 500 g. Their gray-green skin, lightly speckled with white, encloses firm, juicy red flesh with a deliciously sweet flavor.
This variety can be grown on trellises.

name: Cotton Red Foliated
desc: This early, highly ornamental variety boasts deep red stems and leaves, yellow-pink flowers and white-fibered cotton. Easy to grow, it adapts well to container or pot culture.

name: Red Furry Boar
desc: This variety produces an abundance of round, creamy-yellow marbled orange fruit with a firm, downy peach-skin skin. They have a sweet, slightly floury flavor.

name: Quinoas Red Head
desc: This vigorous variety, which is not very sensitive to late-season humidity, can reach 1.5 m in height. It produces beautiful pink panicles with an abundance of seeds.

name: Red Hearted Chantenay
desc:
This variety produces orange, conical, half-long roots - from 12 to 18 cm - with a rounded tip. Their firm, red flesh has a highly appreciated flavor.

This hardy variety is suited to heavy soils and can be stored for several months.

Origin of t

name: Red Hearted Colmar
desc: This vigorous old variety, adapted to heavy, clayey soils, produces long, stubby roots, up to 30 cm long, with firm, red flesh that is sweet to the core.

name: Red Oak Leaf
desc: This magnificent variety offers beautiful, tender, crisp, oak leaves with a sublime, deep purple color!

name: Red Peach
desc: This variety produces large, round, red cherry tomatoes weighing around 50 grams. The skin is velvety and downy, like a peach.

name: Red Pear
desc:
This vigorous and productive old variety of cherry tomato produces clusters of small, red, pear-shaped fruits weighing 10 to 15 g each all summer long. Firm and sweet, the fruit is ideal for preserving.

Characteristics of the Poire Rouge cherry to

name: Red Rhubarb
desc: This variety has bright red ribs and delicious, bright green, embossed leaves.

name: Red Robin
desc: This very early variety produces an abundance of round, sweet "cherry tomatoes", 2 to 3 cm in diameter and weighing around 15 g. Suitable for pot cultivation, this variety reaches 20 to 30 cm in height.

name: Red Siberian
desc: This old variety produces bunches of 4 to 5 large, flattened fruits, slightly arched at the poles, weighing 200 to 500 g. Their juicy, "beefsteak"-like flesh reveals a lovely sweet flavor.

name: Red Swan
desc: This variety produces an abundance of stringless, slightly flattened beans of a rare dark pink-purple color. Turning green when cooked, the 10-15 cm pods offer excellent, meaty flesh!

name: Red Top Norfolk
desc: This old-fashioned variety has round roots with white skin tinged with violet-red at the collar. Their flesh is white, tender and rich in water.

name: / Kales Red Ursa
desc: This variety produces reddish-purple leaves with wide curls and pronounced veins. When ripe, the central leaves offer excellent flavor.

name: Sorrel Red Veined Sorrel
desc: This ancient Oriental variety has clumps of green leaves with red veins and an oblong shape, with a tangy flavor. They can be eaten raw or cooked, but in moderation, as sorrel is rich in oxalic acid.

name: Red Warty Thing
desc: This "Turban"-type variety offers warty, totally orange fruits, sometimes exceeding 10 kg. Although it has always had a tendency to deviate, it was nevertheless introduced because of its excellent flavor.

name: Red with white tips
desc: This old variety has red roots with white tips, 3 to 6 cm long. Small and young, they are slightly prickly, becoming sweeter as they grow.

name: Red Zebra
desc: This red version of the famous "Green Zebra", which is not very susceptible to disease and is very productive, offers an abundance of small, round, bright red fruits with yellow streaks, weighing 80 to 120 g.

name: Red's Red Sweet
desc:
This variety, specially adapted to cool climates, has beautiful panicles full of red seeds and particularly sweet stems. It's perfect for making syrups!

 

The leaf sheaths of Sorghum bicolor, rich in anthocyanin, are used as a natural red dye.

name: Redcrest
desc:
This variety reaches a height of 80 cm and produces upright stems with cut, bushy foliage. They bear an abundance of flowers, sometimes double, with orange petals hemmed in red.
This species is very popular with birds. The flowers can be left to dry

name: Redder Ruffled Oak
desc:
This variety offers an oak-leaf lettuce that is highly resistant to frost and adapted to cold soils. Its kinship with "Lollo Rossa" gives its foliage a somewhat embossed appearance and serrated edges.
The peripheral leaves are cut off, one by one, as

name: Redfield Beauty
desc: This old, highly productive variety produces clusters of round, flattened pink fruit weighing from 180 to 350g. Their juicy flesh reveals a sweet flavor with a hint of acidity.

name: Redina
desc: This variety, with very low susceptibility to mildew and Sclerotinia fungus, produces Grand Rapids-type lettuces with intensely red foliage. They offer a crisp texture and delicious flavor.

name: Cutting Celery Redpar
desc: This variety, developed by Frank Morton, has long, thin, red stalks with dark green, cut leaves. Particularly appreciated in salads, it can also be cooked like other celery cuttings.

name: Redstem Wormwood
desc:
This Traditional Chinese Medicine species produces highly branched stems and filiform leaves that are highly aromatic and rich in essential oils. The flowers form white-green panicles.

Mugwort, known as "Yin Chen Hao" in Chinese pharmacopoeia, is as

name: Reine de Juillet
desc:
This old, vigorous variety produces a very firm, round apple that remains perfectly white inside. Its outer leaves are large, well blistered and greenish-blond in color.

This variety is slow to bolt.

name: Relleno Red
desc: This ancient variety is adapted to cool climates. It produces numerous fruits that ripen from green to red, with variations in color. Their conical shape makes them perfect for stuffing.

name: Curly endive Riccia Cuor d'Oro
desc: This variety produces a voluminous apple with a naturally yellow core and fine, juicy ribs. The sharply cut leaves offer a crisp texture and good flavor.

name: Robecq Zebra
desc:
This variety has round, mottled "Green Zebra"-type fruits, but with more yellow coloring. They weigh from 80 to 120 g.

This is a new obtention from Tom Wagner.

name: Robuschka
desc:
This vigorous, early variety produces well-rounded roots with smooth skin and bright garnet-red flesh. They keep well in the cellar and have a particularly fruity, sweet taste.

 

This variety is recommended for raw and lacto-fermented recipes.

name: Rocotillo
desc:
This old variety produces an abundance of thick-fleshed, lantern-shaped fruit, ripening from green to red.
This variety is adapted to difficult growing conditions.

name: Rocoto Mini Red
desc:
This variety of Rocoto offers a multitude of small, heart-shaped fruits, 2 to 4 cm in diameter, red when ripe. Not well adapted to very hot climates, it is suitable for mountainous areas and can withstand light frosts.

Like all varieties of this c

name: Rodelika
desc: This variety, well adapted to heavy soils, produces roots up to 30 cm long, with a strong red color and sweet flavor, and excellent shelf life.

name: Common Bean Rognon de Coq
desc:
This old French variety, vigorous and adapted to difficult growing conditions, produces an abundance of pods containing kidney-shaped seeds - light red when fresh and darker when dry - with excellent flavor.

Ideal for chili con carne and other Mex

name: Roi des Belges
desc: This old variety, with its bushy habit and purple flowers, produces an abundance of long, straight, green pods containing small black seeds. They should be picked when young.

name: Roma Paste
desc:
This very old and renowned variety offers an abundance of red, elongated fruit, suitable for drying and making preserves and sauces. The fruit is firm and dense, with few seeds.

What are the characteristics of the Roma Paste tomato?

The Roma Pa

name: Roman Candle
desc:
This vigorous variety, suitable for drying, salads and sauces, offers very fleshy, almost seedless fruit, elongated with a nipple.

This variety is a spontaneous mutation of "Swenson's Speckled Roman", itself the result of a spontaneous cross between

name: Romanesco
desc: This sumptuous old variety has a compact, golden spiral-shaped apple, with a beautiful, tender green color and an excellent hazelnut-like flavor. Sulfur-free, it is reputed to be easily digestible.

name: Romanian Hot
desc: This variety offers very fleshy fruits, 10 cm long, which ripen from yellow to orange-red.

name: Zucchinis Ronde de Nice
desc: This old variety produces numerous round fruits with fine, slightly protruding ribs, dark green in color, punctuated with white. They can weigh from 600 g to 1.5 kg and keep for several months. The Nice round zucchini is a vigorous grower in the vege

name: Ronde de Valence
desc: This old variety produces round fruit with a shiny, dark purple skin. They are sometimes colored greenish-white on the stalk and have good flavor.

name: Zucchinis Ronde di Piacenza
desc:
This variety offers round fruit very similar to the "Ronde de Nice" but slightly darker in color. They are slightly ribbed, light green with dark green stripes, and can be eaten when they are the size of a tennis ball.

WHAT ARE THE 

CHARACTERIS

name: Ropreco Paste
desc:
This old variety, vigorous and earlier than the "San Marzano", produces stocky stalks about 1 m high, with oval-shaped fruit suitable for preserves and sauces. Their sweet flesh is slightly floury.
Plants of this variety do not require staking.

name: Rosa Bianca
desc: This old variety produces white-mottled, round, pink-lavender fruits 10 to 15 cm in diameter, surrounded for several days by the green calyx. They contain firm flesh with excellent flavor and no bitterness.

name: Mallow Rose
desc: This fast-growing species produces an abundance of beautiful mauve flowers on magnificent shrubby plants, similar to those of hollyhocks or hibiscus, ideal for making bouquets of fresh flowers.

name: Common Bean Rose d'Eyragues
desc:
This very old variety from the south of France produces beige and pinkish-purple seeds. They can be eaten semi-dry or dry, and offer a chestnut-like flavor much appreciated in pistou soup.

The color of the beans darkens with storage.

name: Rose Dawn
desc: This vigorous variety, adapted to cool climates, has a compact growth habit and produces slightly flattened, pink "beef-flesh" fruits weighing up to 900g. Their dense flesh offers a fruity flavor.

name: Rose de Berne
desc: This old, highly productive variety, one of the tastiest, offers an abundance of round, slightly flattened fruit, weighing from 180 to 240 g. Their thin skin encloses very juicy, fragrant flesh.

name: Rose de Cagnolle
desc: This variety, selected over many years at the Cagnolle farm in the Dordogne, produces excellent fruit in the shape of a large pink heart. The flesh is firm and very tasty, with few seeds.

name: Rossa Di Trento
desc: This old variety is slow to bolt and can be sown all year round in mild climates. It produces a beautiful apple with green leaves tinged with red on the edges, curly and firm in texture.

name: Rossa lunga di Firenze
desc: This ancient Italian variety produces elongated pink to bright red bulbs with juicy, sweet-tasting flesh. Unsuitable for preserving, they can be eaten before ripening, as and when required.

name: Bitter eggplants Rosso Di Napoli
desc:
This variety offers an abundance of round fruits 4 to 6 cm in diameter. Their color changes from green to pale orange with green stripes, then to dark orange. Immature, they reveal a sweet, bitter-free taste.

Cooked at over 150°C, the fruit becomes

name: Rosso Duemila
desc: This early variety produces conical fruits, about 15 cm long, turning from green to dark red as they ripen. Their sweet flesh has a particularly fruity flavor.

name: Rosso Sicilian
desc: This variety grows to less than 1 m tall and offers flattened, strongly ribbed fruit with sweet, fragrant flesh, suitable for making sauces and stuffings.

name: Cardoons Rouge d'Alger
desc:
This thornless, heat-resistant variety has magnificent silvery-green foliage and delicious pale-green chard with a pinkish base.

 

A close relative of the artichoke, cardoon can also be grown in the ornamental garden for its unrivalled aestheti

name: Rouge de Cavaillon
desc:
Melon de Cavaillon, or Rouge de Cavaillon, is an ancient French variety that produces round, smooth-skinned fruit similar to Charentais melons. Their orange flesh has a pleasantly sweet taste.

Characteristics of the Rouge de Cavaillon melon

The

name: Rouge de Namur
desc: This old variety produces round "Marmande"-type fruits weighing around 200 g. They are red in color.

name: Rouge Pâle de Niort
desc: This old, productive variety, suitable for autumn cultivation in western France, produces large, flat, copper-pink bulbs 8 to 10 cm in diameter. Despite their thin skins, they keep well.

name: Rougette de Montpellier
desc: This very old variety, also known as "Rougette du Midi", produces a small apple with green leaves tinged with red, very crunchy and tasty.

name: Round Mauve
desc: This compact variety produces an abundance of mauve, round, thin-skinned fruits 10 cm in diameter, which should be picked fairly young. The flesh is white and fleshy, with a sweet, bitter-free flavor.

name: Rox Orange
desc:
This variety produces an abundance of panicles containing orange-red grains. It is used to make a very fragrant, light-colored molasses-type syrup.

 

Sorghum bicolor leaf sheaths, rich in anthocyanin, are used as a natural red dye. This use is

name: Roxy
desc: This vigorous variety has tender leaves, broadly tinged with brilliant dark red, forming a voluminous, tight-knit head. It is resistant to heat, viruses and various strains of mildew.

name: Pepo squash Royal Acorn
desc:
This variety of acorn squash produces an abundance of dark-green, hard-skinned fruit. They can weigh up to 2 kg, and their dry, fragrant orange flesh has a nutty flavor that improves with age.

Fruits of this variety keep for 4 to 8 months.

name: Royal Golden
desc: This ancient variety produces rounded fruit with a thick skin that turns gold when fully ripe. Their pink-red flesh is crisp and sweet-tasting.

name: Royal Purple
desc:
This vigorous, productive variety produces large, black fruits with dark green veins at the collar, weighing from 250 to over 500g. Their deep red flesh reveals an excellent, rich, complex flavor.
This new variety, offered by Lee Goodwin in New Mexic

name: Rubenza
desc:
This variety produces upright stems with cut foliage and an abundance of bright red flowers 15 cm in diameter.

This species is very popular with birds. The flowers can be left to dry in autumn, so they can feed on the seeds.

To discover on the

name: Rubin
desc: This large, highly ornamental variety produces rounded purple leaves 8 cm long. They have a very fragrant taste and can be used to make very fine vinegars that take on their color.

name: Ruby King
desc: This old variety produces very large, ribbed fruits 15 cm long. They ripen from green to cherry red and contain delicious, thick, flavorful flesh.

name: Ruby Vase
desc: This recent variety, selected for its resistance to tip scorch, has upright, vase-shaped red leaves spotted with green and an often pink heart.

name: Rudbeckia Rudbeckia hirta
desc:
This biennial species can reach up to 1 m in height and produces an abundance of yellow flowers with brown centers from spring to hard frost.

Rudbeckia hirta and Rudbeckia serotina were used by Native Americans to stimulate the renal system.

name: Rudbeckia Rudbeckia speciosa
desc: From June to September, this hardy perennial produces an abundance of yellow flowers with black centers that brighten up any garden.

name: Rudi
desc: This fast-growing, regular variety has well-rounded, red roots and strong foliage. Easy to bundle, the roots do not burst or dig in.

name: Millets Rudukes
desc: This variety produces long, cylindrical yellow or orange inflorescences. Used before maturity to make bouquets, the seeds are easy to harvest when fully ripe.

name: Curly endive Ruffec
desc: This very old French variety produces dense apples with finely cut green leaves, a full core and thick, wide ribs. It adapts to a wide range of growing conditions.

name: Zucchinis Rugosa Friulana
desc: Common in northeastern Italy, this zucchini is prized for its succulent, rich, floral flavor with hints of artichoke. Its pale yellow, very warty, thin-skinned young fruits offer very firm, dense flesh.

name: Rugoso Di Cosenza
desc: This old Italian variety, vigorous and productive, offers oval, canary-like fruits weighing around 2 kg, with delicious, juicy, sweet, tender and refreshing flesh, similar to that of the watermelon.

name: Russian
desc:
This very old variety forms an apple surrounded by green, red-speckled, slightly wavy leaves. It has a juicy texture and mild flavor.

Characteristics of De Russie winter lettuce

De Russie winter lettuce or Goutte de Sang, Lactuca sativa, is a v

name: Russian 117 Oxheart
desc: This old, vigorous "cœur de bœuf" variety produces large fruits weighing 300-800 g. The flesh is very dense and juicy, with few seeds. The flesh is very dense and juicy, contains few seeds and has an excellent, rich, complex flavor.

name: Russian Cossack
desc:
This variety produces medium-sized, round fruit with smoky-black flesh. The skin is black streaked with green and pink, with indigo at the collar.

Fruits can vary widely in color, shape and flavor.

name: / Kales Russian Frills
desc: This hardy variety, closely related to "Russian Red", produces curly, dark-green leaves with reddish-purple veins. They have a crisp texture and good flavor.

name: Russian Giant
desc:
The Russian Giant red tomato is a variety with large, very ribbed, round fruits weighing 300 to 600 g. Sweet and juicy, these fleshy fruits offer a highly appreciated flavor.

Characteristics of the Russian Giant red tomato

The Russian Giant red

name: / Kales Russian Hunger Gap
desc: This Russian variety has very broad, serrated leaves in red and green. This variety is extremely resistant to extreme cold and, what's more, sets seed later.

name: Russian Lemon
desc: This variety produces numerous clusters of 4 to 5 yellow, slightly hollow fruits divided into two lobes, resembling lemons, with firm flesh and a sweet, refreshing flavor.

name: Russian Persimmon
desc: This compact variety, around 1 m tall, produces an abundance of large, smooth, round orange fruits weighing 200-250 g. Their sweet flavor is excellent. Their sweet flavor is excellent.

name: / Kales Russian red
desc: This old-fashioned variety is vigorous and produces red stems and green leaves, lightly tinged with red and deeply lobed. They have a tender texture and a flavor that improves with frost.

name: Rutgers Devotion
desc: This variety, developed for its resistance to mildew, produces highly productive "Genovese" type plants! The broad, slightly blistered leaves are a magnificent light green and offer delicately sweet, spicy aromas.

name: Rutgers Obsession
desc: This variety of sweet basil is slow to bolt, vigorous and highly resistant to mildew, offering an abundance of shiny, dark-green, pointed leaves with a slightly spicy aroma.

name: Mixed grain crop Rye and Vetch
desc: This mixture of common vetch and mainly rye, which provides excellent coverage, can reach 2 m in height and is ideal for green manure or forage crops.

name: Tulsis Sacred / Temperate Tulsi
desc:
This holy basil, native to Ethiopia and easy to integrate into gardens in temperate zones, has a highly branched habit. Its dark green leaves with serrated edges offer a spicy fragrance and flavor of vanilla, tutti frutti or myrrh.

Discover it on

name: Saint Antoine
desc:
This old variety produces an apple with green leaves, tinged with pink-brown, offering a tender, crunchy texture.

It is adapted to a wide range of growing conditions.

For example, on January 17, St. Antoine's Day, it was customary in many place

name: Saint Blaise
desc: This ancient, heat-resistant, slow-maturing variety produces a 30 cm-high rosette with a creamy-white center and slender, dark-green leaves. They offer a crunchy texture and good flavor.

name: Saint Pierre
desc:
This old-fashioned tomato variety produces large, round fruits weighing between 200 and 400 g, with firm, juicy, flavorful flesh. Its abundant production and excellent taste make it a favorite with gardeners.

What are the characteristics of the Sa

name: Saint Turjan
desc: This variety, native to the island of Oléron where it is known as "rosé des sables", produces round, pink-purple onions with a very mild flavor. They are ideal for both bundling and preserving.

name: Saint Vincent yellow
desc: This remarkable variety produces yellow pepper-shaped fruits, weighing 80 to 100g. Ideal for stuffing, their flesh is firm and very tasty!

name: Salt And Pepper
desc: This mildew-resistant variety produces white fruits 5 to 10 cm long with crunchy flesh.

name: San Marzano
desc:
This vigorous tomato variety bears clusters of elongated red fruit, 6 to 7 cm long. San Marzano is resistant to shattering, suitable for drying and making tomato preserves and sauces. Its firm, dense flesh has a mild flavor.

What are the character

name: San Marzano Redorta
desc: This variety produces clusters of 3 to 4 elongated, horn-shaped fruits, much appreciated for home-made coulis or preserves. Their firm flesh contains few seeds and has a very sweet, fruity flavor.

name: Sandul Moldovan
desc: This old variety, with sparse foliage, produces large, slightly flattened "beef-flesh" fruits. They offer a good balance between sweetness and acidity.

name: Santa Fe Grande
desc: This variety is highly ornamental and not very susceptible to tobacco mosaic. It produces short, conical, fleshy "Floral Gem" type fruits, which ripen from yellow to orange to red.

name: Santa Fe Red
desc:
This variety reaches 2 m in height and produces large panicles bursting with brick-red grains.

 

The leaf sheaths of Sorghum bicolor, rich in anthocyanin, are used as a natural red dye. This use is widespread in Africa.

name: Santa Rainier
desc: This highly productive variety offers an abundance of bright red, oval to pear-shaped cherry tomatoes weighing 8 to 10 g. It's a brand-new Tom Wagner obtention.

name: Santiam
desc: This very early, productive variety, resistant to Fusarium and Verticillium, produces bunches of 7 to 8 round, slightly flattened fruits weighing 100 to 200g. The flesh is very sweet and juicy, with few seeds.

name: Sara's Black
desc:
This old German variety offers round, noticeably flattened, purple to black "beefsteak"-type fruits. They contain delicious, dense flesh with a rich, sweet and spicy flavor.
This variety, brought to light by Joe Bratka, is also known as "Sarah Schwar

name: Sart Roloise
desc: This sublime Belgian variety produces a generous, regular crop of "beefsteak"-type fruits, orange-yellow with blue marbling on the shoulders, weighing 200 to 350 g. Their dense, pale-yellow flesh contains few seeds and reveals a sweet flavor.

name: Sasha Altai
desc: This very early, exuberant-growing variety produces an abundance of juicy, round, slightly flattened, bright-red fruit. Their dense flesh offers a sweet flavor.

name: Brussels sprouts Sativa selection
desc: This medium-height, frost-resistant variety has an upright, vigorous, full-grown habit. From November onwards, it produces a profusion of delicious little grey-green cabbages.

name: Zucchinis Sativa selection
desc:
This early, open-growing, non-runner variety produces long, smooth yellow fruit with very fine, tender flesh.

WHAT ARE THE 

CHARACTERISTICS OF THE "Sélection Sativa" COURGETTE?

The "Sélection Sativa" variety, Cucurbita pepo, is characterized

name: Saucy Green
desc: This variety, recently bred by Tom Wagner, produces large "cherry tomatoes" with a slightly greenish yellow color and green flesh inside.

name: Savoy-Leafed/Bloomsdale
desc: This very old variety produces large, sometimes slightly angular, blistered leaves like those of a Savoy cabbage, with a shiny dark green color.

name: Scaly Bark
desc: This old variety, which keeps well, has slightly oval fruit. Their rough, blue-green skin resembles tree bark. The flesh is red and crunchy, and particularly sweet.

name: Scarlet Pumpkin
desc: This variety produces large, scarlet-red fruits ideal for stuffing. The flesh is dense and very fragrant, with few seeds.

name: Scarlet Starfire / Starfire Isis
desc:
This vigorous variety can reach 1.5 m in height, and produces small, crunchy, orange-striped red fruits. They keep very well.

This is a new variety of "variegated cherry tomato" bred by Lee Goodwin in New Mexico.

name: Schilling Giant Oxheart
desc: This vigorous old variety, not very sensitive to shattering, produces an abundance of large, heart-shaped, very uniform fruits, weighing 400 to 500 g. Their dense flesh offers a rich, sweet flavor.

name: Nepeta Schizonepeta tenuifolia
desc: This medicinal species from the Asian pharmacopoeia (anti-allergic, febrifuge and anti-inflammatory), from the Lamiaceae family, has beautiful, highly fragrant violet flowers. Like Nepetas, this plant makes cats dance!

name: Skullcap Scutellaria barbata
desc:
Bearded skullcap, or ban zhi lian, is a bushy herbaceous perennial that can grow to almost 1.60 m in height. Its hairless, serrated leaves set off pretty little blue or violet flowers, visible in summer.

 

This medicinal plant originates from A

name: Selection
desc: This variety, suitable for early crops and bundling, produces straight, orange roots about 20 cm long, with good flavor.

name: Peas Serpette Guilloteaux
desc: This highly productive variety produces a multitude of curved, snake-like pods containing 8 to 10 delicious, well-rounded seeds. Their excellent quality makes them ideal for canning.

name: Chinese cabbage Shanghai Pak Choy
desc:
This 12-15 cm-high variety produces non-headed cabbages with smooth green leaves and long white stalks reminiscent of chard ribs, but denser. The young leaves are used in salads and the ribs are cooked.

This variety is very early.

name: Sharlyn
desc:
This very old Honeydew variety, adapted to warm climates, produces beautiful fruit with a lightly embroidered skin and an orange-yellow color when ripe. Their creamy-yellow flesh reveals a very sweet taste with hints of pineapple and coconut.

Fruits

name: Shimoda
desc:
This bushy variety produces oblong, dark purple fruit with a white demarcation on the stalk. Edible cooked or raw, thanks to their thin skin, they reveal a sweet flavor, especially when picked young.

 

This eggplant spread thanks to an exchange

name: Shinonome
desc: This ancient Japanese variety is ideal for making tofu, soy milk and miso. Highly productive and easy to grow, it produces its first harvest 85 days after sowing.

name: Shirofumi
