desc: This early, vigorous variety grows to around 1 m tall and produces beautiful pods containing 3 to 4 seeds with 40% protein and 20% oil. They have a smooth, tender texture and excellent flavor.

name: Moschata squash Shishigatani / Toonas Makino
desc: This ancient variety produces diabolo-shaped fruits up to 18 cm in diameter, 25 cm long and weighing up to 3 kg. Their hard, warty skin is dark green, turning ochre when ripe. Their orange flesh has a fine texture and a nutty flavor.

name: / Kales Siber Frill
desc: This very hardy, cold-hardy variety, selected from a gene pool, produces blue-green, strongly curled leaves with a firm texture and excellent flavor.

name: Siberia
desc: This compact variety, adapted to cool climates, produces bunches of 20 to 30 round, red fruits weighing 50 to 150 g, even on cool nights, with little tendency to burst.

name: Sicilian violet
desc: This vigorous variety produces a well-rounded, compact purple apple weighing 1 to 1.5 kg with excellent flavor. The purple color turns green when cooked.

name: Sieger
desc: This variety, suitable for field cultivation, produces an abundance of round, red fruit weighing around 80 g. Their fine flesh offers a mild flavor, ideal for both coulis and salads.

name: Sierra Gold
desc: This variety has robust foliage and smooth, embroidered fruit measuring around 15 cm in diameter and weighing up to 1.5 kg. The flesh is salmon-colored and very tasty. This variety seems to have little susceptibility to mildew and powdery mildew.

name: Asclepias Silky Gold
desc: This perennial, a favorite with butterflies, has beautiful golden-yellow flowers. After fertilization, they produce enormous seed pods, each adorned with a silky parachute. Ideal for flower beds!

name: Asclepias Silky Red
desc: This variety, selected for its vibrant colors and the size of its flowers, offers splendid bicolor inflorescences of yellow and red. Fertilized flowers produce huge seed-filled pods, each adorned with a silky parachute.

name: Silver Bell
desc: This old variety offers bell-shaped, gray-green fruits weighing 1 to 2 kg. Their color changes to pink during storage. They contain delicious, dry, sweet orange flesh.

name: Silvery Fir Tree
desc: This variety's originality lies in its finely chiselled green-grey foliage, reminiscent of carrots. It produces an abundance of flattened, orange-red fruit with firm flesh and thin skin.

name: Chervil Simple
desc: This very hardy variety, with its umbels of white flowers, has green, cut leaves with an aniseed and licorice fragrance, much appreciated as a condiment. It adapts to a wide range of growing conditions.

name: Skyreacher
desc:
This variety, developed by Tom Wagner, has clusters of 3 to 5 small "Red Zebra" type fruits, dark red with yellow stripes, weighing 40 to 60 g. They reveal a flavor of excellent gustatory quality.

Offered to the public before complete stabilization,

name: seeds Skyscraper
desc:
This giant variety produces a solid, straight stem, 4 m high, topped by a very large, single inflorescence, up to 35 cm in diameter, with yellow petals and an orange heart.

The flower petals enhance salads, and the seeds are highly prized by birds

name: Slankard Oxheart
desc: This variety produces large, fleshy pink fruits weighing 500 to 700 g. They contain few seeds and their flavor is highly appreciated.

name: Sleeping Lady
desc:
This vigorous variety, with its compact habit and rather frail foliage, produces clusters of 4 to 6 round, slightly flattened, black-tinged mahogany-red fruits weighing 120 to 180 g. Their dense, juicy flesh offers a balanced flavor.

Originally the

name: Slenderette
desc: This vigorous, disease-resistant variety, around 50 cm tall, produces an abundance of dark-green pods 10 to 15 cm long. They have fine flesh and no threads.

name: Slim Jim
desc: This compact variety is ideal for growing in pots. Its purple foliage makes it particularly ornamental. It produces clusters of small, 15 cm-long, purple-lavender fruits, whose fine flesh offers a highly appreciated flavor.

name: Slovenian Black
desc: This pathogen-resistant variety produces numerous clusters of a dozen excellent dark, slightly pear-shaped fruits with dense, juicy flesh.

name: Small Leaved Lemon
desc: This white-flowered variety produces short, pointed, light-green leaves. They offer an exquisite lemony flavor, even after drying, ideal for enhancing dishes and herbal teas.

name: Small Pineapple
desc:
This variety offers small, round fruits, weighing 40 to 70 g, with skin and flesh in a beautiful monochrome of yellow and red with a pink veil. Their juicy flesh reveals a delicious, sweet, fruity flavor.
This is a brand-new release from Tom Wagner.

name: Small Shining Light
desc: This ancient Russian variety, adapted to cool climates, produces beautiful round fruits weighing 2 to 3 kg. Their dark green skin encloses delicious red flesh.

name: Small Turkish Turban
desc:
This highly decorative variety produces white, green and orange turban-shaped fruits weighing 100 to 800 g, with pale orange flesh that is firm, not very thick and sweet-tasting.

Characteristics of Petit Turban Turc squash

Petit Turban Turc or

name: Tulsis Smooth-leaved Tulsi Mtule
desc: This basil can grow up to 2 m tall, especially in greenhouses, but its range is much more moderate in temperate gardens. It produces beautiful, smooth, serrated green leaves that exude a very strong scent of camphor and clove.Discover it on Kokopelli

name: Snow White
desc: This variety produces bunches of 10 to 20 small, well-rounded cherry tomatoes, ivory in color and pale yellow when ripe. Their flesh has a mild flavor without being too sweet.

name: Snowball Peony
desc: This "snowball" variety has beautiful, immaculate white double flowers with slightly crinkled petals.

name: Peas Snowflake
desc: This robust gourmet pea variety offers medium growth and flat, large pods after an attractive white bloom.

name: Snapdragon Snowflakes
desc: This snapdragon, or wolf's mouth, has long spikes of lush, pure-white flowers. In the vegetable garden, in flowerbeds or rock gardens, this variety is at home in any garden!

name: Soissons
desc: This old French variety, suitable for summer cultivation, has crisp, slightly blistered, light-green leaves tinged with reddish-brown, with unrivalled flavor. They form a very large, loose head.

name: Sol Gold
desc: This bushy variety, around 1 m tall, produces numerous smooth, fairly round, yellow-orange fruits weighing 90-150 g. The flesh is juicy, sweet and slightly acidic. The flesh is juicy, sweet and slightly acidic; it's best not to eat the fruit when it'

name: Solar Flare
desc:
This variety offers numerous, slightly flattened fruits, variegated with red and yellow. They weigh from 180 to 300 g, and their flavor is appreciated.
It was developed by Brad Gates of Wild Boar Farms in Napa, California.

name: Sonata White
desc:
This dwarf variety, ideal for pot cultivation, has a very compact habit. It offers a host of majestic white flowers with golden centers, 7 cm in diameter.
This species is very popular with birds. The flowers can be left to dry in autumn, so that they

name: Sora
desc:
This variety produces well-rounded, bright-red roots. Their flesh remains firm and tender for several weeks.
This variety can be grown in spring, summer or autumn.

name: Sosulka Chernaya
desc: This variety offers elongated, brown-black fruits weighing 50 to 100 g with a rich flavor. Their firm, paste-like flesh contains little juice. Ideal for drying and tomato coulis.

name: Pepo squash Spaghetti Squash
desc:
This old variety offers 2 to 5 fruits, white then amber when ripe, weighing 1 to 3 kg. Their low-calorie, orange-yellow flesh breaks down into spaghetti-like strands when cooked. They keep for 6 to 12 months.

This variety grows very vigorously.

name: Speckled Amish Butterhead
desc: This vigorous old variety produces a dense apple with a pink heart surrounded by broad, wavy leaves of light green speckled with red. They have a crisp texture and excellent flavor.

name: Speckled Roman
desc: This variety offers bunches of 5 to 7 elongated fruits, ending in a nipple, around 10 cm long and weighing 150 to 300 g. They are suitable for drying and contain dense flesh.

name: Speckled Snake
desc:
This variety produces superb, elongated, snake-shaped fruits. They are green speckled with white and can reach 1.50 m in length.

The gourds dry in a dry, ventilated place and can be painted, carved or made into various objects.

name: Speckled Swan
desc:
This variety produces dark-green, yellow-flecked, bottle-shaped fruits with long, rounded necks. They resemble birds, especially when growing on the ground. Perfect for all creative workshops!

 

The gourds dry in a dry, ventilated place and can

name: Cardoons Spiny Argenté de Plainpalai
desc: This ancient variety, closely related to the artichoke, produces numerous delicious, fleshy, fairly thorny, highly decorative ribs. They have excellent keeping qualities.

name: Spitze
desc: This variety produces excellent fruit, both for canning and for eating raw in salads. They are 10 to 15 cm long and elongated, ending in a point.

name: Mixed squash Squash
desc:
This blend offers a magnificent selection of several species - pepo, maxima and moschata - and varieties of squash in a wide range of shapes, colors and tastes.

 

It includes the varieties :

- Butternut Sonca Orange moschata squash ;

- Ca

name: St. John's wort St John's Wort Perforated
desc:
St. John's Wort, a hardy perennial, blooms beautifully and brightens any garden or fallow land with golden yellow. This powerful protector can be used as an antidepressant, anti-inflammatory, wound-healer and antiseptic.

A genuine natural antidepr

name: Stella Blue
desc: This American variety produces small blue "Kabocha"-type fruits weighing 1 to 2 kg. Their bright orange flesh has an excellent hazelnut flavor.

name: Stocky Golden Roaster
desc: This variety offers orange-colored fruit with thick flesh. They measure up to 15 cm and are easy to roast.

name: Stocky Red Roaster
desc:
This variety, developed by seed grower Frank Morton, produces an abundance of sweet, horn-shaped fruits that have no trouble ripening and can be cooked in a variety of ways, ideal for roasting in the oven!
The skin of Stocky Red Roaster peppers, when

name: Stowells Evergreen
desc: This old variety produces 2 to 3 ears of about 20 cm long, with white seeds and a delicate flavor.

name: Strawberry Cabbage Lettuce
desc: This old, compact French variety produces a beautiful apple 20 to 25 cm in diameter, with green leaves speckled with blood-red towards the heart. They have a tender texture and good flavor.

name: Strawberry Crown
desc: This Brazilian variety produces 4 to 6 brown-gray fruits with a slightly pink stem, rounded and ribbed, weighing 2 to 3 kg. Their flesh has a fine texture and excellent flavor.

name: Zucchinis Striato d'Italia
desc:
This ancient variety, originally from Italy, produces fruits up to 30 cm long and weighing up to 1 kg. Their green skin, streaked with lighter shades, encloses creamy-white flesh.

WHAT ARE THE 

CHARACTERISTICS OF Striato d'Italia zucchini?

T

name: Striped German
desc: This variety, adapted to warm regions, produces one of the most beautiful tomatoes. The fruit is pineapple-shaped, flattened and heavily ribbed, and can weigh up to 500g. The yellow and red epidermis encloses yellow flesh with a red heart.

name: Strubbes Orange
desc: This old American variety, 2 m tall, has 17-20 cm-long spikes containing toothed seeds, mainly orange and sometimes red or yellow, ideal for making semolina.

name: Stuffing Yellow
desc:
This vigorous, productive variety offers an abundance of beautiful yellow, pepper-like fruits. With their almost hollow interior containing very few seeds, they're ready for any kind of stuffing!

Also known as "Yellow Stuffer".

name: Stump Of The World
desc: This old variety has beautiful pink "beef-flesh" fruits, rounded, slightly flattened and ribbed. They can weigh up to 500 g and their dense flesh, containing very few seeds, has a sweet flavor.

name: Stupice
desc: This old variety, very early and adapted to cool climates, has resistant potato foliage. It produces an abundance of clusters of 6 to 8 bright-red, very juicy and flavorful fruits.

name: Stuttgarter
desc:
This old variety is slow to bolt, stores well and is very productive. Its bulbs are large, round and slightly flattened, with a thick, coppery-yellow skin.

What are the characteristics of the Stuttgarter onion?

The Stuttgarter or De Stuttgart o

name: Pepo squash Styrian Hulless
desc:
This variety produces 2 to 6 fruits weighing 10 kg. Their seeds, also known as "naked seeds", are high in protein and rich in nutrients. They can be eaten dry, plain or roasted, and yield a high-quality oil. The flesh is generally not eaten.

The f

name: Submarine Blush Cherry
desc:
This new American variety offers hundreds of pear-shaped, rosy-gold fruits when ripe. They have the sweet, subtle flavor of both their parents.
It is a new variety selected in its fourth generation by Lee Goodwin in New Mexico, from a cross between t

name: Sucrin de Tours
desc: This old French variety produces spherical, sometimes slightly oblong fruits, covered with very thick embroidery. The flesh is orange-red and firm, with a sweet flavor.

name: Moschata squash Sucrine du Berry
desc:
This ancient variety of squash produces 5 to 8 pear-shaped fruits up to 25 cm long and weighing up to 3 kg. They keep for 3 to 6 months. The dark green skin turns ochre when fully ripe, and encloses orange flesh with a sweet, musky flavor.

What ar

name: Sugar Baby
desc: This early, productive variety offers several round fruits weighing 3 to 4 kg. Their thick, green, almost black skin encloses firm, orange-red flesh with an excellent sweet taste.

name: Curly endive Sugar Loaf
desc: This very old variety forms a tight, elongated, greenish-white apple, 40 to 45 cm high. Its cone-shaped leaves offer a very crunchy texture and a flavor that improves with frost.

name: Summer Cider Apricot
desc: This variety, with its potato-like foliage, produces many large, fleshy fruits, ribbed at the shoulders, with orange flesh veiled in pink, deliciously sweet and reminiscent of mango.

name: Summer Savory
desc: This species produces small, elongated, rounded green leaves, offering a less spicy, aromatic flavor than perennial savory. Its scent repels aphids and mosquitoes.

name: Sumter
desc: This very vigorous variety produces an abundance of small, fairly short, green fruits, about 8 cm long. It is not very sensitive to many of the species' diseases.

name: Super Colossus
desc:
This variety produces firm, dark pink to bright red "beef-fleshed" fruit. They are juicy with a hint of acidity.

This variety has a vigorous growth habit.

name: Super Shepherd
desc: This most popular Italian variety produces pointed, irregularly curved fruits, about 15 cm long, dark red when ripe. The flesh is sweet, thick and juicy.

name: Super Snow White
desc:
This variety produces round fruits the size of ping-pong balls, with a mild, slightly sweet flavor. Their ivory color turns yellow when ripe.
Fruits are not very sensitive to bursting.

name: turnip Superschmelz
desc:
This giant, slow-growing variety produces a green-white apple weighing up to 2.5 kg. Whatever its size, the flesh remains tender without ever becoming fibrous.

It is not very sensitive to bolting.

name: Suptol
desc: This beautiful variety produces very mild "pepper-like" chillies. They ripen from creamy white to orange to red.

name: Sweet Casady
desc:
This variety, developed by Tom Wagner, has small, elongated red fruits with yellow-gold streaks, 3 to 6 cm long. They have a mild, sweet flavor and keep well after picking.

This variety is a cross between "Raisin Vert" and "Casady's Folly".

name: Sweet Chocolate
desc:
This early variety, adapted to cool climates, offers elongated, three-lobed fruits that ripen from green to chocolate red. They have thick flesh with a mild, sweet flavor.

What are the characteristics of the Chocolate bell pepper?

Derived from

name: Sweet Dakota Rose
desc:
This medium-sized, classic-looking watermelon variety is fairly early. Its flesh is red, firm, crunchy and sweet, with few seeds. The fruit keeps very well!

This variety is suited to short seasons in cooler areas, while being highly resistant to h

name: Sweet Dani
desc: This "lemon basil" variety produces pointed, light-green leaves with a spicy, minty, lemony flavor.

name: Pepo squash Sweet Dumpling
desc:
Sweet Dumpling is a variety that produces 4 to 10 fruits weighing 300 to 800 g, ivory in color with green stripes. Their firm, yellow-orange flesh has a chestnut and hazelnut flavor. They keep for 3 to 6 months.

Characteristics of Sweet Dumpling s

name: Sweet Freckles
desc: This variety, adapted to cool climates, produces pear-shaped fruits that are green, then orange with green flecks when ripe. Their firm flesh offers excellent flavor.

name: Sweet Granite
desc: This is one of the earliest varieties, well adapted to cool climates. Its lightly embroidered skin encloses well-flavored orange flesh. The fruit only keeps for a short time.

name: Sweet Meat
desc: This vigorous variety produces 2 to 5 round, blue-gray-skinned fruits weighing 5 to 7 kg. Their orange flesh is very firm and dry, with a pleasant hazelnut flavor. They keep for 4 to 9 months.

name: Sweet Painted Hills
desc:
This variety produces, on plants 2 m high, multiple spikes with multicolored seeds offering a slightly sweet flavor.
This variety germinates well in cold soil.

name: Sweet Siberian
desc:
This ancient variety produces fruit up to 60 cm long and weighing up to 4 kg. The light-green skin encloses juicy yellow flesh. Seeds are small and brown.
This old variety originated in Siberia.

name: Peas Swiss Giant
desc:
This old variety with violet flowers produces large, tender pods about 10 cm long.

This old Swiss variety can reach a height of 1.50 m.

name: Syrian Stuffing
desc: This ancient variety, originally from Syria, produces elongated, violet-black fruits 10 to 15 cm long. Their tender flesh is extremely flavorful. Traditionally used for stuffing.

name: Szentesi
desc: This ancient Hungarian variety produces beautiful, 10 cm-long fruits that turn from ivory to orange-yellow, then red when ripe. Delicately spicy, they can be eaten fresh or dried and ground into paprika.

name: Tabasco Short Yellow
desc:
This highly ornamental variety produces small fruits about 2.5 cm long. They ripen from yellow-green to yellow-orange. Plants can reach 1 m in height.

This variety is native to the Tabasco region of southeastern Mexico. This is a region of swamps

name: Zucchinis Tabergmouzt from Morocco
desc:
This old variety offers fairly short, cylindrical, strongly ribbed fruits, light to dark green in color and weighing up to several kilos. The light-colored flesh remains tender even when fully ripe.

WHAT ARE THE 

CHARACTERISTICS OF THE MOROCCAN

name: Pepo squash Table Gold
desc:
This variety of the "acorn" group produces 5 to 8 conical fruits, yellow then orange when ripe. Their thick orange flesh has a nutty flavor that improves with storage.

The fruit can be stored for up to 6 months.

name: Moschata squash Tahitian
desc: This old variety, to be grown in warm climates, produces up to 50 kg of butternut-type fruit, weighing up to 15 kg. Their orange flesh can be eaten raw or cooked, and improves during storage.

name: Takanotsume
desc:
This productive variety, with its compact habit, offers clusters of erect, pointed fruit resembling hawk's talons. They ripen from light green to bright red.

 

This variety is also known as "Hawk Claw" and "Hahong Koch'o".

name: Talisman
desc: This hardy, productive variety produces extra-thin, stringless green beans measuring 15 to 20 cm. They can be harvested both very young in net or a little later as mangetout.

name: Purslane Tall Green
desc: This ancient, heat-resistant cultivar produces thick, juicy, emerald-green leaves. Rich in omega 3, they can be eaten raw, in salads, or cooked like any other vegetable.

name: Moschata squash Tan Cheese
desc:
This ancient variety produces round, slightly ribbed fruits weighing 3 to 6 kg. Their skin turns ochre when ripe, and they contain orange-red flesh with a delicious carrot and hazelnut flavor.
The fruit keeps for 4 to 9 months.

name: Tangerine
desc: This very old variety offers an abundance of tangerine-shaped, "beef-fleshed" fruit, up to 500g. The thick flesh offers a delicious flavor.

name: Tango
desc: This variety produces oakleaf lettuces with wavy, dark-green foliage, a crunchy texture and mild flavor.

name: Tarahumara Chile Colorado
desc:
This variety offers conical, "Poblano"-type fruits that ripen from bright green to dark red and can be dried.

This variety is native to southern Chihuahua, Mexico.

name: Tarahumara Small Bule
desc:
This traditional Tarahumara variety is used for rattles, tobacco, etc. Its fruits are round, slightly pear-shaped and short-necked. Its fruits are round, slightly pear-shaped with a short neck. They reach 15 to 20 cm in diameter.

The gourds dry in

name: Common Bean Tarbais
desc:
Haricot Tarbais - String beans for shelling

The Tarbais bean is a very old variety with pods around 20 cm long. They offer melt-in-the-mouth beans, traditionally used in the preparation of cassoulet.

This variety can grow up to 2.50 m tall.

name: Tardif d'Angers
desc:
This old French variety can withstand temperatures as low as - 5°C. It produces a beautiful, dense, creamy-white head weighing from 0.5 to 1 kg, which keeps for several days after harvesting and can be frozen.

These hardy cauliflowers overwinter i

name: Buckwheat Tartary buckwheat
desc: This variety, with green stems even at maturity, produces numerous bunches of white flowers. Hardly cultivated for food use, it is perfect for use as green manure.

name: Chinese cabbage Tatsoi Spoon
desc: This variety is resistant to temperatures as low as -10°C and produces dark-green, spoon-shaped leaves with a soft, tender texture and a subtle, distinctive flavor.

name: Celosia Temple Bell
desc: This original variety offers astonishing multicolored inflorescences, resembling human brains, from 10 to 20 cm in diameter. Ideal for making bouquets of fresh or dried flowers!

name: Quinoas Temuco
desc: This variety, adapted to maritime climates, produces huge green panicles containing small creamy-white seeds. Plants are 1.50 m high.

name: Tender And True
desc: This old variety, with its long, white, regular roots, has a mild flavor that makes a pleasant addition to soups. It is particularly resistant to canker.

name: Tendre de Châteaurenard
desc: This variety, from a population in the Avignon region, is very productive. It produces excellent Italian-style fruit, semi-long, square and bright red when ripe.

name: Tête Noire
desc: This short-stemmed variety produces small, dense, dark purple apples about 20 cm in diameter. They can be eaten raw, in salads, or cooked, in stews or stuffed.

name: Tetragonal Tetragonia tetragonoïdes
desc: This ancient species produces fleshy, triangular, dark-green, almost fat leaves. They can be used both raw and cooked, in the same way as spinach, whose flavor they recall.

name: Moschata squash Texas Indian
desc: This vigorous variety produces an abundance of flattened, slightly ribbed fruits weighing 5 to 7 kg, whose orange-ochre skin encloses tasty flesh.

name: Thai Lavender Frog Egg
desc: This dazzling variety offers a myriad of small, cherry-tomato-sized fruits in lavender-pink hues tinged with cream. Their tender flesh reveals a strong flavor. Ideal for steaming, roasting, etc.

name: Thai Long Green
desc: Reputed to be one of the best eggplants, this variety offers slender, light-green, sweet-fleshed fruits 25-30 cm long. At around 60 cm in height, it is drought-resistant and tolerates light frosts.

name: Thai Long Purple
desc: This fairly late variety produces regular, long, narrow, violet-black fruits. They measure 20 to 30 cm in length and offer soft, tender flesh with a pleasant flavor.

name: Thai Red Stem
desc: This vigorous, productive variety has broad green leaves with reddish-purple stems and flowers. The leaves have a strong scent of aniseed and cloves, and are used in many Asian dishes.

name: Thai Round Green
desc: This old variety produces small, round, greenish-white fruits about 5 cm in diameter. They sometimes have many seeds and a slight bitterness. The fruit is highly prized in South Asian cuisine. Plants can grow to almost 1 m tall.

name: Thai Round Purple
desc: This ancient, drought-resistant variety has many small, round, shiny dark purple fruits with smooth skin, around 5 cm in diameter. It is very popular in vegetable curries and stews.

name: Thai White Ribbed
desc: This very old, small variety produces an abundance of white, rounded, very ribbed fruit. They have a tender texture and an appreciated flavor.

name: Thessaloniki
desc: This hardy old variety produces smooth, spherical fruits weighing around 150 g, which are not very prone to bursting and are ideal for storage. The flesh is firm and juicy, with an excellent sweet flavor.

name: Thompson Seedless Green Grape
desc: This vigorous variety, with its well-developed foliage, produces numerous bunches of round, yellow-green fruit. Their juicy green flesh offers an unusual, sweet and spicy flavor.

name: Perillas Tia To
desc:
This variety produces fairly small green leaves with a purple underside, offering a stronger, more pronounced spicy flavor than other shiso varieties.

Find out more on the Kokopelli blog: "Qualités Médicinales des Perillas".

name: Cannabis Tiborszallasi
desc:
This Hungarian hemp variety, photoperiodic and dioecious, can grow up to 4 m tall. It contains around 5% CBD and offers spectacular inflorescences, ideal for the production of resinous and aromatic flowers.

 

It is listed in the European catalo

name: Tiffen Mennonite
desc: This variety produces clusters of 4 to 5 large, often flattened, ribbed pink fruits, weighing 250 to 500 g. Their dense, "beef-flesh" type flesh has an excellent flavor, similar to that of the "Brandywine" variety.

name: Tiger
desc: This variety produces an abundance of 500 g fruits with bright yellow skin striped with fiery red. Some fruits remain yellow only. Their white flesh is sweet and fragrant.

name: Coreopsis Tiger Mix
desc: This annual variety has numerous flowers with petals graded from red to yellow and dark red centers. Splendid in bouquets, they can also be used to make dyes. It seeds spontaneously.

name: Tiger Tom
desc: This old variety offers round, sometimes slightly lobed fruits weighing 60 to 80 g, in bunches of 7 to 9. They are red with yellow stripes when ripe, and have a juicy red center.

name: Tigerella
desc:
This variety, similar to "Tiger Tom", offers
an abundance of small fruits in clusters of 3 to 5 round tomatoes about 5 cm in diameter. They are red streaked with orange and somewhat acidic. The variety is very hardy.

name: Togo Clover
desc: This variety, which can reach over 2 m in height, produces an abundance of clusters of 5 to 7 small pinkish-red fruits, roughly shaped like clover leaves. Their juicy flesh has a sweet flavor.

name: Bitter eggplants Togolese Spinach Gboma
desc: This eggplant-like plant is grown for its leaves, which are eaten like spinach. Rich in nutrients, they are traditionally prepared in sauce, steamed or fried. Its small, bitter white fruits can be cooked in the same way as eggplants.

name: Tohono O'Odham Sugarcane
desc:
This ancient variety, once used to make molasses, produces abundant panicles of red seeds. Packed with sugar, the stalks can be chewed.

 

The leaf sheaths of Sorghum bicolor, rich in anthocyanin, are used as a natural red dye. This use is wides

name: Tom Thumb/Stone Tennis Ball
desc: This old-fashioned variety produces small, dark-green lettuces with embossed leaves, about the size of large apples. This variety is resistant to heat and bolting.

name: Mix with small fruits
desc:
This blend offers a fantastic rainbow of color and flavor, ideal for discovering new varieties, to delight the eyes and taste buds!

 

It contains the varieties:

- Matt's Wild Cherry Precocious Red Tomato ;

- Ambrosia Gold Early Orange Che

name: Tommy Toe
desc: This old-fashioned, highly productive and hardy variety produces an abundance of clusters of 6 to 10 round to oval fruits, about the size of apricots. Very juicy, they offer an appreciated flavor.

name: Tonda Musona
desc: This early, very cold-hardy variety from Italy produces lovely, round, white, firm and delicious bulbs. They are ideal for both bundling and storage.

name: Topepo Rosso
desc: This small, old-fashioned variety produces fully ripe, red pepper-tomatoes 5 cm in diameter. Their flesh is thick, crunchy and very tasty. Excellent in salads and stuffings.

name: Touquet Vert
desc: This variety produces clusters of 4 to 5 round, brick-red, green-striped "Black Zebra" type fruits, well distributed throughout the season.

name: Trebona
desc: This early, productive variety, suitable for both indoor and outdoor cultivation, produces beautiful, rounded green pods, 25 to 27 cm long.

name: turnip Trero
desc: This variety, suitable for early cultivation, is not very sensitive to bolting. It produces light foliage and white, round, flattened turnips up to 20 cm in diameter.

name: Tri Star
desc: This old variety offers 2 to 5 fruits, with three ribbed lobes, weighing from 2 to 8 kg. Their very hard skin encloses firm, sweet-tasting, pale-orange flesh. The fruit can be kept for up to 2 years.

name: Trifecta
desc: This variety, which is highly resistant to mildew, produces many smooth, slightly serrated fruits with yellow-ochre skin that keep well. Their dark orange flesh is firm yet soft, with a mild, sweet flavor.

name: Triolaise
desc: This variety produces very large, dense apples with wavy leaves and a pinkish-green color. They have a firm texture and excellent flavor.

name: Triomphe de Farcy
desc:
This ancient variety produces an abundance of long, thin pods, green in color and slightly mottled with purple. They can grow up to 18 cm long and offer a tender texture when eaten young.

This variety has a compact habit.

name: Triomphe de Liège
desc: This old variety produces clusters of 2 to 4 bright-red, round or slightly flattened fruits, weighing 90 to 150 g and with very thin skin. Their dense, not very juicy flesh is perfect for making coulis and sauces.

name: Triple Play
desc: This early, highly productive, cold-hardy variety produces numerous ears with multicolored seeds - white, yellow, purple, blue, etc. - and a particularly rich flavor. - with a particularly rich flavor. They are harvested when blue grains begin to app

name: Moschata squash Tromba d'Albenga
desc:
This old variety produces 4 to 6 cylindrical, highly curved fruits, up to 1.50 m long and weighing up to 4 kg. They keep for up to a year. Their light-green skin, which turns ochre when ripe, encloses light-orange flesh.

Description of Albenga squ

name: Trophy
desc: This very old, productive variety produces bunches of 4 to 5 large, round, firm, completely smooth fruits with juicy, sweet-tasting flesh. It is also known as the "Rouge Grosse Lisse" tomato.

name: True Gold
desc: This variety produces spikes around 20 cm long with yellow seeds on 2 m-high plants. They contain high levels of zeaxanthin, one of the main protective pigments for the eyes.

name: Nepeta Tuberous Catnip
desc: This perennial and medicinal species, around 70 cm high when in full bloom, offers downy, highly aromatic, green-blue foliage, followed by an abundance of large, dense spikes of pink-purple flowers.

name: Tuberous chervil Tuberous-rooted Chervil
desc:
This species produces white roots resembling small, stubby carrots that can be eaten raw or cooked. They have a very fine flavor, reminiscent of chestnuts and potatoes.

The leaves are not edible.

name: Pepo squash Tuffy
desc: This variety of acorn squash, or gourd, produces elongated, heavily ribbed fruits weighing from 500 g to 1 kg. They contain dry, yellow flesh with a very sweet, nutty taste, and keep for 3 to 6 months. The flavor improves with storage.

name: Tulsis Tulsi Kali
desc:
This white-flowered Tulsi has pointed, slightly elongated green leaves with a lemony fragrance. This basil is very popular in Thailand, Vietnam, Cambodia, etc.

Ocimum canum is resistant to the fungus Fusarium oxysporum ssp. basilicii as well as to b

name: Tulsis Tulsi Kapura
desc:
Tulsi produces green, downy leaves with a strong camphor fragrance. It also produces white flowers with orange-red pollen.
It can reach 1 meter in height and spread, and sometimes almost 2 meters in very good growing conditions.
Ocimum kilimandsharic

name: Tulsis Tulsi Krishna with Purple Leaves
desc:
This is an ecotype of Tulsi Krishna whose leaves and stems are more purple than green. Plants are 30 to 60 cm high, with a highly branched habit. Flowers are mauve or sometimes white. This Indian ecotype grows well in temperate regions.

To be disc

name: Tulsis Tulsi Rama
desc: This Tulsi has a highly branched habit, with green, serrated leaves and mauve and white flowers. It has a very long growing cycle and requires a lot of heat.Discover it on the Kokopelli blog: "Tulsis et autres Vérités Basilico-moléculaires pour se li

name: Tulsis Tulsi Vana
desc:
Sacred Basil, or Tulsi, can reach 2.50 m in height. It has a highly branched growth habit and offers an abundance of fine, serrated green leaves with a very spicy fragrance of camphor and cloves.

Basil is native to Asia and Africa. In regions with

name: Turga
desc: This variety, an improvement on the "Guernsey" parsnip, produces smooth, white roots, 25 to 30 cm long, with a sweet, coconut-like flavor. The young leaves can be eaten in salads and the rest in soup.

name: Turk's Turban
desc:
This early, old and rare variety offers an abundance of clusters of 10 to 15 small, reddish-scarlet, pear-shaped, more or less ribbed fruits. Their flesh is dense and fleshy, with a sweet taste.

The fruit seeds are located near the surface, almost

name: Turkish Tulip
desc: This is a magnificent variety with fine, cut foliage. It has straight, slender, hairy stems bearing solitary red flowers with black petals.

name: Turkish Turban
desc:
This old variety is one of the most interesting for decoration. It offers 4 to 6 fruits weighing 1 to 5 kg, adorned with a brightly colored cap. The sweet, floury orange flesh is excellent in soup.

The fruit keeps for 4 to 12 months and can be dri

name: Tuxana
desc:
This 1.5 to 2 m tall variety averages two 18 to 20 cm-long ears with 16 to 18 rows of mainly white seeds.

 

This sweet corn variety was recently bred by Jonathan Spero in southern Oregon. It is currently being stabilized. Jonathan's aim is to c

name: Uchiki Kuri
desc: This ancient Hokkaido-type variety produces smooth, thin-skinned orange pumpkins weighing from 900g to 1.7 kg that keep well. They can be eaten with the skin, and reveal creamy, nutty flesh.

name: Ukrainian Pear
desc: On vigorous plants, this old variety produces an abundance of pinkish-purple, pear-shaped fruit, especially in the shape of an Anjou pear. They weigh 180 grams and are 8 to 10 cm long. Their flavor is sweet and highly appreciated.

name: Ukrainskaya Krupnoplodnaya
desc: This old variety produces large, blue-gray fruits weighing 6 to 10 kg. The bright orange flesh has a mild flavor.

name: Una Hartsock Beefsteak
desc: This ancient variety, originally from the United States, produces large, dark-pink, round to oval, slightly ribbed fruits, weighing 300 to 500 g. Their firm, juicy flesh has a mild, sweet flavor.

name: Uncle David Dakota Dessert
desc:
This old, very hardy variety produces "Buttercup"-type fruit, resembling a "Green Turkish Turban", weighing 2 to 3 kg. Their dark green skin encloses bright orange flesh with excellent flavor and pleasant sweetness.
Originally from North Dakota, this

name: Uralskij Mnogoplodnij
desc: This old variety, whose fruit has an original flavor combining acidity and sweetness, produces an abundance of small, round, red fruits, very slightly ribbed towards the neck.

name: Val d'Orge
desc: This ancient, cold-hardy variety, ideal for winter cultivation, offers a voluminous apple with smooth, fairly thick, blond-green leaves.

name: Valencia
desc: This old variety, adapted to cool climates, has good vigor. It produces clusters of 4 to 6 bright orange, flattened fruits weighing 250 to 350 g. They contain few seeds and have very dense, juicy, fragrant flesh.

name: Valmaine
desc: This hardy traditional French variety produces tall, elongated apples with dark green leaves and excellent flavor.

name: Variegata
desc: This very compact variety boasts magnificent variegated green, white and purple foliage and small, oblong dark purple fruits that turn red when ripe, passing through green, yellow and orange.

name: Variegated
desc: This variety has splendid, delicate green and creamy-white variegated foliage, sensitive to the sun's rays. It produces clusters of 5 to 6 small fruits with juicy flesh, whose flavor develops when fully ripe.

name: Védrantais
desc: This early, vigorous variety produces beautiful, round, cantaloupe-like fruits weighing around 1 kg. Their smooth, light-green skin encloses firm, deep-orange flesh with an exceptionally fragrant, sweet taste.

name: Vee crane
desc: This variety produces clusters of green fruit, 1.5 to 2 cm in diameter. The flesh is green. This is a recent variety developed by Tom Wagner. It was selected from crosses of "Texas Wild" and "New Green Zebra".

name: Vegetarian
desc:
The vegetarian sweet pepper, popular in the West Indies, produces an abundance of fruits about 10 cm long. They ripen from green through orange to brilliant red. When fully ripe, they have a fruity flavor, similar to that of Habaneros.

About a ten

name: Velvet
desc: This old variety with white flowers can reach 1 m in height. It produces an abundance of fluffy pods with 2 to 3 green seeds and velvety leaves, hence the variety's name.

name: Venetian
desc: This variety has delicious crunchy, elongated leaves with finely serrated edges reminiscent of chestnut. Vigorous, fast-growing and resistant to bolting.

name: Venus nipple
desc:
The Téton de Vénus tomato is a variety with drooping foliage that produces bright-red, pointed heart-shaped fruits weighing 70-300 g on slender stems. Suitable for drying, they contain few seeds and have melting flesh.
Characteristics of the Téton d

name: Verdil
desc: This variety produces a rosette of crisp, triangular green leaves. It is particularly suitable for winter and spring cultivation, and is very fast-growing.

name: Verna Orange
desc: This ancient variety produces magnificent, heart-shaped fruits weighing up to 500g. The flesh is firm, soft, sweet and delicious, with few seeds. It originated in Indiana, USA, and was reintroduced in 1990 by the Seed Savers Exchange.

name: Verona
desc: This old, vigorous and productive variety produces large, very dark green fruits weighing 10 to 20 kg, with a thin skin and firm, red flesh that is fragrant and very sweet-tasting.

name: Curly endive Verona Red
desc: This old variety, which is hardier and later than the "Rouge de Trévise", produces a rounded, very compact apple, with round leaves that turn red when cold. They offer a crunchy texture and pronounced flavor.

name: Verrières
desc: This very old French variety produces a beautiful, tight apple weighing around 100 g, with glossy green foliage and an appreciated flavor.

name: Vert Petit de Paris
desc:
This old variety produces an abundance of short, cylindrical, dark-green, prickly fruits, best harvested before they reach more than 5 cm in length.
This hardy variety has a vigorous growth habit.

name: Lamb's lettuce Verte à Coeur Plein
desc: This old-fashioned, fast-growing field variety has a small, compact rosette with smooth, rounded, light-green leaves.

name: Lamb's lettuce Verte de Cambrai
desc: This old, very cold-hardy variety, similar to "Coquille de Louviers" lamb's lettuce, has beautiful, rounded, tender green leaves with excellent flavor.

name: Verte de Limoges
desc:
This variety produces compact, voluminous apples with thick, green foliage. Perfectly adapted to heat and dry conditions, it will thrive in sunny gardens!

Very hardy, Verte de Limoge is slow to set seed.

name: Mallow Verticulated mauve
desc: This species, with its soothing properties, has beautiful white flowers and broad, green leaves with curly tips and a mild flavor. Young, they can be eaten raw in salads or cooked like spinach. Older ones can be enjoyed cooked.

name: Vertus Marteau/Jersey
desc:
This ancient variety produces cylindrical roots, swollen at the bottom, 12 to 16 cm long. The flesh is tender with a fine, slightly sweet taste. This turnip lends itself well to forced cultivation.
Harvest the roots when they are still young, as they

name: Chinese cabbage Vibrant Joy
desc: This blend, developed by Frank Morton, offers crisp, succulent leaves in vibrant shades of purple and pink. The color saturation is particularly impressive, and stands out all the more in winter.

name: Vieille France
desc: This old, early variety is easy to grow and produces light-green, slightly oval, ribbed fruits that turn yellow when fully ripe. Their juicy orange flesh has an intoxicating fragrance.

name: turnip Vienna Violet
desc: This very old, early variety produces a magnificent, very tender, purple rabe 5 to 10 cm in diameter, to be eaten young. Its white flesh has a fine texture and mild flavor.

name: Violet de Gournay
desc: This ancient variety produces a root up to 25 cm long, voluminous and purplish, with very sweet white flesh.

name: Cleome Violet Queen
desc: This fast-growing variety boasts an abundance of large clusters of fragrant, melliferous purple flowers with very long stamens at the end of its central, thorny stem. They bloom all summer long.

name: Violet Sparkle
desc: This variety's fruits are purple with light yellow stripes, shaped like a spinning top. They have the advantage of giving off a strong chili pepper fragrance when cut, while being totally free of heat and spiciness.

name: Violetta di Firenze
desc: This old variety, 1 to 1.2 m high, produces between 8 and 10 huge, pale mauve fruits with white shading, oval in shape and weighing 100 to 200 g. Their tender flesh has a mild flavor.

name: Violette de Toulouse
desc: This variety, native to the Toulouse region, produces a large quantity of elongated, cylindrical fruit with very white, sweet flesh, 20 to 22 cm long.

name: Moschata squash Violina
desc:
This variety produces violin-shaped, slightly crenellated "Butternut"-type fruits, with ochre-colored skin when fully ripe. Their firm orange flesh is of excellent quality, with a musky flavor.

Description of Violina squash

Violina squash, Cucu

name: Zucchinis Virginia White
desc:
This old-fashioned, highly productive variety offers "marrow squash" type fruits, swollen on the blossom side and up to 35 cm long. Their creamy-white skin encloses a highly prized flesh.

WHAT ARE THE CHARACTERISTICS OF THE VIRGIN

name: Lamb's lettuce Vit
desc:
This very fast-growing variety produces a rosette of rounded, dark-green leaves. They are thick-textured, fleshy and very flavorful.
It is also highly productive and tolerant to mildew.

name: Wädenswil
desc:
This variety produces large, tight, round apples with a green-gray color. They have a firm texture and good flavor, and are used to make sauerkraut.

This variety has a vigorous growth habit.

name: Curly endive Wallone
desc: This vigorous, hardy variety produces one of the largest apples. Its core whitens easily, and its green, tightly-curled leaves offer a crunchy texture.

name: Warty Green Hubbard
desc: This old variety, also known as "Hubbard Chicago Warted", produces large, bronze-green, warty fruits weighing 5 to 7 kg. They contain thick, dry flesh with excellent flavor, and keep for 8 to 10 months.

name: Washington Cherry
desc: This vigorous variety produces bunches of small, round, dark-red fruit weighing 25-30 g, with firm, flavorful flesh. They keep particularly well on the vine or in the cellar.

name: Beefsteak
desc:
This old variety produces an abundance of huge, pink, "beef-flesh" type fruits, flattened and ribbed. Their purplish-red, firm, juicy flesh contains few seeds.
After a period of drought, the fruit is susceptible to bursting if too much water is appli

name: Peas Weggis
desc: This beautiful old variety, with its violet blossoms, produces numerous small pods on 1.80 m climbing plants.

name: / Kales Western Front
desc:
This very cold-hardy variety produces beautiful curly leaves in variegated colors ranging from green to purple. They have a firm texture and good flavor.

It was selected - during an excessively harsh Oregon winter - by Tim Peters from 10% Siberian

name: Whippersnapper
desc: This productive, very compact variety doesn't grow taller than 50 cm. It offers an abundance of clusters of 5 to 18 elongated fruits, particularly appreciated for their flavor and beauty.

name: Starflower White
desc:
This variety, with its hairy stems and foliage, produces an abundance of white flowers. Fresh leaves can be eaten raw or cooked as a condiment. Dried leaves and flowers are used in infusions.

The appearance of stains on the sachets is due to the o

name: White African Sugar
desc:
This ancient variety produces an abundance of white seeds with black skin. The very sweet stalks are also eaten.

 

The leaf sheaths of Sorghum bicolor, rich in anthocyanin, are used as a natural red dye. This use is widespread in Africa.

name: Pepo squash White and Green Patty Pan
desc: This ancient variety produces green and white variegated fruit in the shape of a slightly domed star. When young, their raw flesh is firm and thick, with an artichoke-like flavor. When ripe, they can be stuffed.

name: White Beauty
desc: This highly original old variety has a creamy-white skin and flesh. The round, slightly flattened fruits are fairly fleshy and weigh 250 grams, with few seeds. The flavor is extremely sweet due to its high sugar content.

name: White Beauty from Canada
desc: This old, vigorous variety offers an abundance of beautiful, very pale yellow, almost transparent, flattened and slightly ribbed "beefsteak"-type fruits. Their white flesh reveals a sweet flavor.

name: White Bush
desc: This vigorous, compact variety grows to no more than 1 m tall. It produces an abundance of clusters of 4 to 5 white, yellow-tinged fruits, with firm, sweet flesh and little tendency to burst.

name: White Cherry
desc: This French variety offers an abundance of round, slightly flattened, creamy-white cherry tomatoes, weighing around 30 g. They reveal a uniquely tart, sweet flavor.

name: White egg
desc: This ancient variety produces an abundance of oval, egg-sized fruits weighing around a hundred grams, whose color changes from white to yellow when ripe. Their tender flesh has a mild flavor that becomes spicy when fully ripe.

name: White laceflower White Finch
desc:
On finely cut foliage, this wild species offers delicate umbels of pure white flowers, 6 to 9 cm in diameter, with an intricate lace-like pattern. Ideal for bouquets of fresh or dried flowers.
The decoction of flower heads is traditionally used in th

name: White Flowers
desc:
This frost-resistant variety, selected for its white flowers, can reach a height of 1.20 m and produces an abundance of highly melliferous flower spikes. Its foliage has a powerful aromatic fragrance.

To discover on the Kokopelli blog: "Agastaches

name: White Gem
desc: This vigorous variety is suited to heavy soils. It produces medium-sized white roots whose flavor is much appreciated.

name: California poppies White Linen
desc: This variety, perennial in milder climates but often annual in our regions, produces an abundance of flowers with delicate white petals on plants 30 to 40 cm high.

name: White Mountain Apache
desc:
This ancient variety, native to the Apache people, produces large panicles bursting with red seeds.

 

The anthocyanin-rich sheaths of Sorghum bicolor leaves are used as a natural red dye. This use is widespread in Africa.

name: Pepo squash White Patty Pan
desc: White Pâtisson squash is a very old, non-runner variety that produces white, slightly domed star-shaped fruits up to 25 cm in diameter and weighing up to 2.5 kg. When young, their raw flesh has an artichoke-like flavor and, when ripe, they can be stu

name: White Potato Leaf
desc: This variety produces bunches of 2 to 3 fruits, flattened and ribbed, perfect for making sauces. Their ivory-colored flesh is more tart than sweet.

name: White Prescott
desc: This very old "Cantaloup" variety produces fruit weighing from 2.5 to 4 kg. Their dark-green, deeply ribbed and warty skin encloses thick, sweet, juicy, melting orange-red flesh.

name: White Queen
desc:
This variety, which is not very sensitive to bursting, offers an abundance of creamy-white, slightly pink-tinged "beef-flesh" fruits, weighing from 250 to 400g. Their juicy, very sweet flesh reveals a rich, complex flavor.
It is considered the best a

name: / Kales White Russian
desc: This very hardy variety is one of the most resistant to cold and waterlogged soils. It produces white stems with green veins and moderately curled green leaves with a tender texture.

name: Teff White Seeded
desc: This early variety adapts to both short seasons and hot weather. Each plant produces beautiful stems bearing panicles of white seeds, which are used to make Injeras, a kind of large pancake typical of Ethiopian cuisine.

name: White Tomesol
desc: This vigorous, productive variety produces smooth fruit ranging from cream to yellow when fully ripe. Both sweet and rich in flavor, it is one of the most popular white tomatoes.

name: White Velvet
desc:
This old, heat-demanding variety produces a large number of white-green, downy-skinned fruits with a rounded cross-section. They are highly prized for homemade preserves, and can be eaten raw or cooked before they reach 8 cm.

Okra fruits contain a

name: Millets White Wonder
desc: This highly ornamental variety produces long, cylindrical, light-green inflorescences. They can be cut at the immature stage to make bouquets, or at full maturity to harvest the seeds.

name: Luffas White-seeded Sponge Gourd
desc: This white-seeded variety produces cylindrical fruits 20 to 40 cm long. When young, they can be eaten like zucchini. The fibers of ripe, dried fruit act as a vegetable sponge.

name: Wild Fred
desc:
This compact variety offers an abundance of red fruit weighing 200-250g. It is the result of a cross between "Carbon" and "New Big Dwarf" carried out in 2006 by Bruce Bradshaw and named "Sleazy" at the time.

This variety has benefited from the "Cr

name: Quinoas Wild Garden Breeder's Mix
desc: This quinoa mix, from numerous strains grown by Frank Morton in the USA, will offer a wide variety of colors, sizes, earliness, etc.! Ideal for budding breeders or the curious looking for something new!

name: / Kales Wild Garden Kale Mix
desc: This variety is suited to autumn cultivation, but is also highly resistant to extreme cold. It produces large leaves and colourful shoots that can be harvested all winter long.

name: / Kales Wild Red
desc: This very hardy variety produces an abundance of beautiful flat, embossed, mossy or curly red leaves.

name: Rutabaga Wilhelmsburger
desc: This old, very hardy variety has great storage capacity. Its roots are light yellow with a broad green collar, enclosing delicious, firm, sweet orange flesh.

name: of Milan Winter King Savoy / Roi de l'Hiver
desc: This old variety, highly resistant to cold, produces a large, dark-green apple with thick, crunchy leaves. Perfect for cabbage rolls, they have a slightly sweet flavor.

name: Pepo squash Winter Luxury
desc:
This old variety produces round, slightly flattened fruits, 15 to 20 cm in diameter and weighing 2 to 5 kg. Their orange-yellow flesh has a sweet, fragrant flavor of excellent quality. The fruit keeps for 3 to 6 months.
It is found under many names:

name: Winter Olive Green
desc:
The Vert Olive winter melon is an old variety that produces elongated, wrinkled, dark-green fruit that is more or less tanned when ripe. The greenish-white flesh with a yellow heart is fairly thick, juicy and very sweet.

This variety can be stored

name: Winter Wonder
desc: This old French variety, very hardy and cold-resistant, produces large green apples with excellent flavor.

name: Wisconsin Lakes
desc: This vigorous variety, developed between 1952 and 1954 in the United States, produces 120-150 g red peppers with a sweet, fruity flavor.

name: Witkiem
desc: This upright variety produces pods up to 20 cm long, enclosing 2.5 cm white seeds that turn brown when cooked and are highly prized raw when half-ripe.

name: Morelle Wonderberry
desc:
This nightshade, also known as sunberry, offers an abundance of juicy, flavorful black berries, which can be eaten raw or cooked.

It is grown in the same way as tomatoes.

 

It is the famous creation of Luther Burbank, the great genius of var

name: Woolly Green Zebra
desc:
This variety, with slightly bluish-green foliage, produces fruits 5-6 cm in diameter, with a peach-skinned green skin streaked with yellow. They are sweeter than those of the classic "Green Zebra".
This is a recent variety - developed by Tom Wagner -

name: Wrinkled Old Man
desc: This variety has bushy vegetation and produces elongated, wrinkled fruits that are red when ripe and have a slightly peppery flavor.

name: Xanthos
desc:
This compact variety blooms all season long in a charming pastel-yellow hue. Its name "Xanthos" - Greek for yellow - is a reminder of its unique coloring. In a vase, they remain beautiful for a long time.
This species is very popular with birds. The

name: Yella Bella
desc: This variety produces beautiful Italian-style yellow peppers with very thin skins and flavorful flesh. Ideal for salads or light, tasty sauces!

name: Yellow Belgium
desc: This ancient variety produces large, round, orangey-yellow fruits weighing 150 to 300 g. They contain few seeds and their dense, juicy flesh has a mild, slightly sweet flavor.

name: Yellow Bell
desc: This old variety produces an abundance of clusters of 5 to 12 bright yellow, pear-shaped fruits, about 5 cm long. Their very dense flesh offers a mild flavor, suitable for preserves and sauces.

name: Yellow Brandywine
desc: This vigorous-growing variety produces an abundance of very large, golden-yellow "beef-flesh" fruits weighing from 350 to 700 g. Plants can reach over 3 m.

name: Yellow Centiflor
desc:
This highly ornamental variety produces extraordinary clusters of hundreds of small, round, yellow fruits, weighing between 15 and 30 g, with a sweet, fruity flavor that is much appreciated.
It was bred by Alan Kapuler by crossing several cherry toma

name: Yellow Cheese Pimento
desc: This ancient variety produces flattened, "tomato-pepper" type fruits that are green, then yellow when ripe. Perfect for stuffed peppers, their flesh is thick, juicy and very tasty.

name: Zucchinis Yellow Crookneck
desc: This variety offers an abundance of lemon-yellow, bottle-shaped, slightly angled fruits. When they reach 10 cm in length, they reveal a sweet flavor. When ripe, their skin is very hard and warty. They are then used as ornaments like coloquintes. The

name: Yellow Currant
desc: This variety, which can self-sow, offers a myriad of tiny fruits, perfect for aperitifs. Grouped in clusters of 6 to 10, these juicy tomatoes offer concentrated flavor.

name: seeds Yellow Dwarf
desc:
This dwarf variety has large, bright yellow inflorescences, around 20 cm in diameter. Perfect for pot cultivation or windy gardens, it blooms for almost 3 weeks.

The flower petals enhance salads, and the seeds are a favorite with birds.

name: Yellow Fleshed Moon-Stars
desc: This variety produces fruits that can reach 10 to 12 kg. Their dark green skin, with numerous spots including one large one, is reminiscent of a starry, illuminated sky. Their yellow flesh has a very sweet flavor.

name: Yellow Furry Boar
desc: This productive variety produces an abundance of fruit with a yellow skin marbled with pink and green, downy and peach-skinned. Their firm flesh reveals a delicious flavor, both sweet and delicate.

name: Yellow Oxheart
desc: This old variety offers an abundance of huge, smooth, heart-shaped fruits weighing 250 to 600 g, pale yellow or orange in color. They are fleshy and contain little gel and seeds. Their flavor is very mild.

name: Yellow Pear
desc:
This ancient, vigorous variety produces an abundance of sweet-tasting, pear-shaped "cherry tomatoes" in bunches right up until the frosts.

In autumn, the whole plant can be pulled up and hung in a dry place to dry.

name: Yellow Perfection
desc: This ancient variety offers an abundance of small, smooth, round fruits, about 5 cm in diameter and bright yellow in color. Their thin skin encloses a juicy flesh that is much appreciated. The foliage is reminiscent of potatoes.

name: Yellow Venus nipple
desc: This Italian variety, with its slender stems, produces an abundance of small, lemon-shaped fruits weighing 80 to 100g. Their flesh is firm and full, juicy and sweet. They are well suited to drying.

name: Yeşilçomlu
desc: This old Turkish variety produces light yellow, thin-skinned, round to slightly elongated, lightly ribbed fruits. The salmon-orange flesh is particularly sweet and fragrant.

name: Moschata squash Yokohama
desc: This magnificent old variety produces beautiful, flattened, strongly ribbed fruits weighing 3 to 4 kg, blue-gray in color, turning ochre when ripe. Their dry flesh has a fine texture and sweet flavor.

name: Yolo Wonder
desc: This vigorous variety has dense foliage and low susceptibility to tobacco mosaic virus. It produces cubic, 4-lobed fruits with thick, firm flesh and a sweet taste.

name: Moschata squash Yuxijiangbinggua
desc: This very vigorous variety produces original, beige to brown, strongly ribbed fruit. Although they can be eaten at any stage of ripeness, the young and green ones offer a tender texture and a nutty taste.

name: Zahir
desc: This variety has single, silky-petaled flowers of a beautiful violet-pink, with a darker violet spot in the center. They give way to very large capsules containing a large quantity of seeds.

name: Zakopane
desc: This vigorous variety can reach heights of over 2 m, and offers an abundance of clusters of 4 to 5 fruits, weighing from 300 g to 1 kg, with very pronounced lobes like those of peppers. Perfect for stuffed tomatoes!

name: Zapalitto Del Tronco
desc: This bushy variety produces small, round, shiny-green fruits, 10 to 12 cm in diameter. They are eaten young and unripe, in the same way as zucchinis and pâtissons. Their white flesh has a creamy texture and nutty flavor.

name: Zarianka
desc: This very compact variety, whose name means "Sunrise" in Russian, produces rounded, reddish-pink fruit weighing 100-180g. It is one of the most popular of the Siberian varieties.

name: Zebra Creg
desc: This unstabilized Tom Wagner creation offers a multitude of plum-shaped tomatoes, medium-sized, around 5 to 10 cm, golden red or ochre yellow with green stripes.

name: Zogola
desc:
This old variety has good vigour and abundant foliage. It produces clusters of 2 to 3 very ribbed "beef-flesh" type fruits. They sometimes weigh over 500 g and offer juicy flesh.
Fruits contain few seeds and are not very susceptible to apical necrosi

name: Zucchinis Zuboda
desc:
This variety produces an abundance of elongated, dark-green, slightly mottled fruit with firm, sweet flesh. Foliage is green speckled with white. This variety has a vigorous growth habit.

WHAT ARE THE 

characteristics of zuboda?

The "Zuboda"

name: Curly endive Zuccherina Di Trieste
desc: This ancient variety forms a small rosette of rounded leaves, with a tender texture and mild flavor. They are harvested as they mature, when they are 8 to 10 cm long.

name: Physalis Zuni
desc: This variety's yellow-green fruit is traditionally cooked with garlic, chillies and herbs to make a delicious sauce. Sheltered in their calyxes and kept in a dry, well-ventilated place, they keep for several weeks.

name: Zürich Market
desc: This variety produces an abundance of white, conical roots, which are highly prized.

name: Lamb's lettuce A Grosses Graines
desc:
This early variety is very hardy, generous and ideal for autumn salads, with long, tasty, green, slightly flat leaves.

 

Lamb's lettuce is also known as "Doucette" and is eaten mainly in autumn. However, some varieties can be grown all year rou

name: Lamb's lettuce À Grosses Graines de Hollande
desc: Productive, cold-hardy and disease-tolerant, this large-seeded lamb's lettuce is fast-growing and vigorous. It produces abundant harvests of delicious rosettes with long, dark-green leaves.

name: Cutting Celery À Jets Fins de Huy
desc: This highly productive, hardy Belgian variety has long, thin hollow ribs and abundant, dark-green, particularly aromatic foliage.

name: of Milan A Pied Court de Plainpalais
desc: This old variety produces round, slightly flat apples with curly, blistered leaves. They have a firm texture and good flavor.

name: Abraham Lincoln
desc: This old variety with vigorous, well-developed bronze-green foliage produces "beef-flesh" type fruits that can exceed 1 kg. They contain few seeds and offer dense, sweet, juicy flesh.

name: Cyclanthere Achocha
desc: This highly ornamental species climbs to over 4 m in height and offers an abundance of small, elongated, slightly curved fruits, shaped like small parakeets. They can be eaten raw, like cucumbers, or cooked.

name: African Kettle
desc:
This ancient African variety produces rounded green fruits, 30 to 45 cm long, with a smooth skin.

The gourds dry in a dry, ventilated place and can be painted, sculpted or transformed into various objects.

name: Agastache cana
desc: This herbaceous species is perennial, but can be grown as an annual in our climate. It has vigorous, upright stems up to 1 m long, on which, from June to September, spikes of tubular, mauve-pink flowers exude a slightly camphorated, aniseed fragrance

name: Agastache Faux-Népéta
desc:
This species produces dense, white-yellow flower spikes and aromatic foliage. It is also known as "Giant Yellow Hyssop" in the USA. The name Agastache comes from the Greek aga, meaning "abundance", and stachys, meaning "ear".

Find out more on the

name: Agastache mexicana
desc:
This species produces blue-mauve flowers with a white variation, and leaves with a lemony fragrance.

It is native to the southwestern USA and Mexico. The name Agastache comes from the Greek aga, meaning "abundance", and stachys, meaning "ear".

name: Agastache rupestris
desc:
This perennial species can grow up to 1.20 m tall and produces an abundance of orange flower spikes. The plant exudes a fragrance and flavor of anise and mint. It is one of the hardiest and most perennial agastache species.

This is one of the hard

name: Agastache scrophulariifolia
desc:
This hardy species can grow up to 2 m tall and produces light purple flower spikes and pleasantly aromatic foliage.

In the United States, it is known as "Giant Purple Hyssop". The name Agastache comes from the Greek aga, meaning "abundance", and s

name: Agastache urticifolia
desc:
This perennial species reaches 1.20 m in height. It produces an abundance of mauve-colored, highly melliferous flower spikes and pleasantly aromatic foliage.
It is a medicinal plant used by many Amerindian peoples (Okanagan, Miwok, Paiute) for fevers

name: Common Bean Agate Pinto
desc: This early variety is ideal for cooking spicy dishes. Its beige beans, spotted with brown, have thin skins that become very tender after cooking.

name: Ailsa Craig
desc: This Scottish variety with dark foliage, adapted to cool, mountainous climates, produces clusters of 4 to 7 round, red fruits with thin, smooth skin and a sweet, acid-tinged flavor.

name: Ailsa Craig Exhibition
desc: This old variety, also known as Kelsae Sweet Giant, has large, sweet-tasting yellow bulbs. Originally from England, it is perfectly suited to short seasons.

name: Alamos
desc: This variety produces light-green, elongated fruit in the shape of a banana or teardrop.The gourds dry in a dry, ventilated place and can be painted, sculpted or turned into various objects.

name: Alaska Mix
desc: This magnificent nasturtium has a very compact habit, with yellow, orange, red or salmon flowers above atypical green and white variegated foliage.

name: Alba
desc: This perennial species has large flowers on hairy, upright stems, with a central orange cone with green then brown highlights, surrounded by long white petals. Bushy, it thrives in ordinary, not too dry, sunny soil.

name: Albino
desc:
This ancient variety, vigorous and well adapted to cool climates, produces small, creamy-white fruits, 6 to 8 cm long and with delicious, thick flesh, on very compact plants, which turn red when fully ripe.

This variety is also known as "White Bul

name: Albino
desc:
This ancient variety produces delicate, delicious, vitamin-rich foliage. Its round roots with thick, white flesh offer a mild, very sweet flavor. They can be used to make sugar.

Characteristics of Albino white beet

White Albino beet, Beta vulga

name: Alice Sunshine
desc: This versatile, vigorous and productive variety produces an abundance of tasty flat pods, 15 to 20 cm long, on 50 cm-high plants. When ripe, they contain delicious pale yellow beans with brown spots, which can be eaten as dried beans.

name: Allu Jola Popping
desc:
This early variety starts flowering two months after sowing and produces ochre-coloured kernels, perfectly suited to being popped, popcorn-style. They can also be eaten whole or in flour.

 

Sorghum bicolor leaf sheaths, rich in anthocyanin, are

name: Almetia
desc: This vigorous variety produces "Roma"-type (slightly oval), red or yellow-orange fruit with sweet, not very juicy flesh. They contain few seeds.

name: Chickpeas Alta Anoia
desc: This traditional variety, from the Catalan canton of Alta Anoia, produces very tasty peas with a fine skin, a melting texture and an intense, delicate taste.

name: Sweet Pea America
desc: This very old annual variety can climb up to 3 m in height and produces an abundance of clusters of white flowers streaked with red and very fragrant.

name: Amish
desc: Whatever the growing conditions, this ancient variety produces an abundance of oval fruits up to 22 cm long and weighing up to 3 kg. Their orange flesh is very juicy and has a musky flavor.

name: Amish Deer Tongue
desc: This vigorous, compact variety produces an abundance of triangular, pointed green leaves with a thin rib. They offer a firm texture and very good flavor.

name: Amish Pie
desc: This very old variety produces spotted, spinning top-shaped orange fruits, generally weighing 2 to 5 kilos when ripe. Their thick, sweet, pale orange flesh is delicious in cakes, gratins or purées.

name: Ammi Ammi visnaga
desc:
This herbaceous plant is well known in traditional Eurasian medicine. It can grow up to 1.20 m tall, with large, anise-green umbels that turn white.
It is used, for example, to treat kidney stones, angina pectoris and asthma. It is also known as toot

name: Amorpha Amorpha canescens
desc:
This perennial species grows up to 1 m tall and produces downy gray foliage. It has long spikes of violet flowers.
False indigo" foliage can be used to dye fabrics, especially that of "Amorpha fruticosa", but contrary to its name, it gives a lemon-ye

name: Amsonia Amsonia illustris
desc: This species of the Apocynaceae family, with its narrow, glossy leaves, produces star-shaped, light-blue bouquets of flowers in mid-spring. It can reach a height of 1 m.

name: An Dunscaja
desc: It is a variety of indeterminate growth and plants can reach 1 meter in height. This variety has white flowers followed by flat, elongated pods containing 2 to 3 bronze and black seeds. The seeds contain 46% protein and 18% oil. This variety originat

name: Rutabaga Angela
desc: This variety offers small to medium-sized white roots with purple collars. The yellow flesh has a tender texture and good flavor.

name: Anise
desc: This aniseed-scented variety has small, pointed green leaves on slightly purplish stems no more than 50 cm high.

name: Anthrax
desc: This highly productive French variety produces beautiful reddish-brown "beef-flesh" fruits weighing 200 to 500g. Their dense, juicy flesh reveals a rich, sweet flavor.

name: Anuenue
desc:
This variety offers a beautiful head with shiny green outer leaves and a compact core, similar to "Iceberg" lettuce. Very well adapted to heat and slow to bolt, it has the ability to germinate in warm soils.

Developed by the University of Hawaii in

name: Appia
desc: This must-have variety, which is cold-tolerant and resistant to bolting, produces voluminous apples with smooth, fine, crisp leaves and excellent eating qualities.

name: Apricot
desc: This dwarf variety has many light-orange flowers. Leaves, flower buds, flowers and seeds are edible.

name: Apricot Lemonade
desc: This variety offers an abundance of cosmos in unprecedented colors. Its pale yellow to apricot flowers, with light pink undersides, feature a pink halo at the center. Perfect for adding a romantic touch to flower bouquets and beds!

name: Argemone Argemone pleiacantha
desc: This medicinal species has white flowers 16 cm in diameter, blue-green foliage and seed capsules almost 4 cm long. The whole plant is covered with prickles, hence its botanical name "plei/acantha" - meaning "many thorns" in Greek.

name: Argemone Argemone polyanthemos
desc: This species, an annual in our climate, reaches 60 cm in height and produces hollow, blue-green stems and white flowers with yellow centers that bloom for 2 to 3 days.

name: Argemone Argemone sanguinea
desc: This 40-80 cm-high species is native to Mexico. It thrives in dry, sandy soil and sunny locations, with deciduous leaves and goblet-shaped flowers in white, light pink or lavender.

name: seeds Arikara
desc:
This ancient variety, native to the Arikara people - a farming tribe in the Dakotas - has large plants, 3 to 4 m high, with a broad, usually single flower head with yellow petals and a heart with shades of brown.

Some individuals may have multiple

name: Cotton Arkansas Green Lint
desc: This variety has short, light-green fibers and dark-green seeds, as well as a magnificent pinkish-yellow bloom.

name: Arkansas Traveler
desc: This old variety is adapted to a hot, humid climate and is not very susceptible to disease. It produces bunches of 4 to 5 round, flattened fruits with juicy, sweet and tart flesh.resistant to bursting.

name: Aromata
desc: This variety offers a uniquely mild, sweet flavor, particularly suited to consumption with crudités. Its roots are long, thin, tapered, cream-colored, firm and juicy.

name: frigida
desc:
This slow-growing perennial reaches a maximum height of 40 cm. Not very sensitive to drought, it has magnificent, deeply divided, silky-grey-green foliage, which is highly ornamental.
Traditionally used by Amerindians, this medicinal plant is also pa

name: ludoviciana
desc:
This species can reach 1 m in height and produces an abundance of stems with gray-green leaves offering a highly aromatic fragrance and a bitter-sweet flavor.
The tender, sweet young shoots can be used in salads or aperitifs.Find out more on the Koko

name: Arto Gorria
desc:
This ancient variety of popcorn, native to the Basque country, produces ears with magnificent dark red to burgundy-red kernels.

Ground, they are used to make taloa, a traditional corn cake.

name: Asclepias Asclepias physocarpa
desc: This species, also known as the "balloon tree" because of the "swollen" nature of its follicles, produces magnificent clusters of 5 white flowers, each adorned with 5 fine petals. The crown-shaped center of the flower has five pinkish lobes.

name: Asclepias Asclepias syriaca
desc:
This species reaches up to 2 m in height and produces an abundance of pink-red flowers whose buds and immature pods can be eaten like vegetables.
Highly nectariferous, it is appreciated by hummingbirds, butterflies and other pollinators.

name: Asclepias Asclepias tuberosa
desc:
This perennial species has an abundance of red flowers, sometimes fading to yellow and orange. The stem is not milky and the taproot is long and slender. It can withstand frosts of around -20°C.
Highly nectariferous, it is appreciated by hummingbirds

name: Asclepias Asclepias verticillata
desc:
This perennial species can reach 50 cm in height and produces elongated foliage and an umbel of around twenty white-green flowers. The seed pods are 10 cm long.
Highly nectariferous, it is appreciated by hummingbirds, butterflies and other pollinator

name: Ashworth
desc: This early variety, originally from New York State, reaches 1.5 m in height and has 15 to 17 cm-long ears, each containing 12 rows of delicious yellow seeds.

name: Astrakhanski
desc: This early variety, ideal for all growing conditions, produces very sweet, rounded fruits 30 cm in diameter with smooth, dark-green skin flecked with light green. The flesh is firm and juicy, with a delicious flavor.

name: Atlantic Giant
desc:
The competition variety par excellence! These extraordinary fruits reach 45 to 50 kg, under good irrigation and fertilization conditions, and up to 100 kg for the largest specimens. Their orange flesh is used in soups, tarts, purées, etc.

 

To

name: Atlas poppy
desc: This variety produces a rosette of basal leaves up to 30 cm wide and slender, upright stems bearing a single orange flower 6 to 8 cm in diameter.

name: Atomic Red
desc:
This variety produces elongated roots with a bright red color due to a high content of lycopene, one of the precursors of carotenoids. Their crunchy flesh offers excellent flavor.

The color is retained, and even enhanced, during cooking.

Origin

name: Auriga
desc: This vigorous Swiss variety produces abundant clusters of 4 to 7 small, bright-orange, carotene-rich fruits with thick skin and firm, juicy flesh with a rich, sweet flavor.

name: Awesome Emma
desc: The exceptionally flavorful and productive Genial Emma offers an abundance of gorgeous little yellow tomatoes splashed with indigo, 2 to 3 cm in diameter, with a uniquely rich, sweet and spicy taste.

name: Bacalan de Limoges
desc: This traditional Limousin variety produces a round, loosely packed, white apple, ideal for eating raw. Renowned for its hardiness, it is particularly well adapted to colder climates.

name: Skullcap Baikal Skullcap
desc: This cold-hardy perennial forms a small bush with numerous inflorescences of blue-purple, helmet-shaped flowers. Traditionally used in Chinese medicine, its dried roots have tranquilizing properties.

name: Balady Aswan
desc: This ancient variety, asparagus lettuce from Egypt, has magnificent green foliage and is harvested when the stems are 30 to 40 cm high.

name: Daturas Ballerina Mix
desc:
This dwarf variety, suitable for pot cultivation, produces cone-shaped flowers in creamy yellow, violet or white.

The entire plant, including the seeds, is toxic to humans and animals.

name: Daturas Ballerina Purple
desc:
This dwarf variety, suitable for pot cultivation, produces cone-shaped flowers in creamy yellow, violet or white.
The entire plant, including the seeds, is toxic to humans and animals.

name: Balloon
desc: This variety offers an abundance of "Rocotillo-like" fruit, green then orange-red when ripe. The flesh has a crunchy texture and mild flavor, while the seeds and placenta are very pungent.

name: Baptisia Baptisia alba
desc: This perennial species, much appreciated by pollinators, can reach 1.20 m in height and offers an abundance of slightly branched stems ending in a cluster of white flowers.

name: Baptisia Baptisia australis
desc:
This perennial prairie species grows up to 1 m tall and produces rounded foliage and an abundance of purple, lupin-like flower clusters.
This species is highly appreciated by pollinators.

name: Baptisia Baptisia tinctoria
desc:
From June to August, this perennial species produces an abundance of small yellow flowers. Its leaves were formerly used as a substitute for indigo. It is also a medicinal plant, mainly used to treat infectious diseases.
This species is highly apprec

name: Bardo des Dombes
desc: This variety, native to France, produces spikes with shiny, almost pearly, white and red or variegated seeds.

name: Curly endive Batavia Maraîchère
desc: Market-grown Batavia chicory produces very large, compact apples 30 cm in diameter. The leaves are dark green with a cream-colored center.

name: Garlic bulbs Bear's garlic
desc: Bear's garlic is a medicinal, aromatic and melliferous perennial. In early spring, it produces a rosette of elongated leaves with a subtle taste halfway between garlic and chives. They can be eaten raw - in pesto, salads, etc. - or cooked. - or cooke

name: Bedford Monarch
desc: This very old English variety has long, soft roots that can become immense with age.

name: / Kales Beedy's Camden
desc: This very hardy variety produces dark green, curly foliage. After a few white frosts, this cabbage offers a texture and a sweet taste that's much appreciated!

name: Bellot
desc: This variety, ideal for early sowing, produces conical, orange-red roots 10 to 12 cm long, with excellent flavor. This population does not belong to a variety listed in the official catalog.

name: Lamb's Quarters and Company Beta Mix
desc: This mix offers a selection of chard and beet varieties to be sown thickly to make a "Beta" mesclun of young shoots. It includes red, yellow and pink chard ribs, as well as different varieties of beet.

name: Beurre de Berne
desc: Beurre de Berne, a traditional Swiss variety, has well-developed foliage and large, flat, golden-yellow pods. Harvest them when they turn from green to yellow, to appreciate their remarkable flavor.

name: / Kales Beurré de Jalhay
desc: This old, non-headed variety produces very large, light-green leaves with a wavy, embossed appearance. They have a very tender texture and a pleasant flavor.

name: Zucchinis Bianca Di Sicilia / White of Sicily
desc: Typical of the Sicilian tradition, this early, highly productive variety produces long, pale-green zucchini, around 30 cm long. Appreciated for their gustatory quality, they are excellent fried, baked or sautéed.

name: Big Apple / Speckled Apple
desc: This variety produces large apple-shaped fruits, 15 to 25 cm long, with dark green skin flecked with white. Dried in a dry, ventilated place, they can be painted, sculpted or transformed into various objects.

name: Big Boston
desc: This very old, hardy and vigorous variety produces beautiful, compact apples, around 30 cm in diameter, with green leaves tinged with reddish-brown on the edges, slightly wavy, and excellent flavor.

name: Gazania Big Kiss White Flames
desc: This variety with lanceolate foliage reaches 30 cm in height and offers numerous 10 cm-diameter flowers, with outer florets striped with white and crimson pink. It blooms exceptionally well from June to October.

name: Gazania Big Kiss Yellow
