desc: This annual variety offers flowers with golden-yellow petals, tinged red at the base, up to 12 cm in diameter. Its resistance to heat and drought makes it an excellent plant for rock gardens.

name: Gazania Big Kiss Yellow Flames
desc: This annual variety has bright red-striped yellow flowers up to 12 cm in diameter. Its resistance to heat and drought makes it an excellent plant for rock gardens.

name: Big Sky Brandy
desc: This variety offers large, round fruit with red and orange hues, "Montana sunset" according to Tom Wagner, its breeder.

name: Quinoas Biobio
desc: This old, early variety, not very sensitive to very cool climates, produces beautiful pink panicles followed by an abundance of small, very white seeds.

name: Biquinho Red / Petit Bec
desc:
This magnificent little pepper, suitable for pot cultivation, offers an abundance of teardrop-shaped fruits, around 2 cm in diameter. They ripen from yellow-green to a brilliant bright red, revealing a fruity, smoky flavor.

Traditionally, they are p

name: Biquinho Yellow
desc: A yellow version of the famous petit bec pepper! Suitable for pot cultivation, it produces an abundance of teardrop-shaped fruits, around 2 cm in diameter and bright yellow when ripe. They reveal a fruity, lemony flavor.

name: Bird's nest
desc:
The fruits of this ancient variety are bottle-shaped, with a very swollen base. They were once used to carry water for pilgrims on the roads to Santiago de Compostela.

The gourds can be dried in a dry, ventilated place, and can be painted, sculpte

name: Black
desc: This hardy variety, which can reach 2.5 m in height, offers a profusion of magnificent flowers, which are astonishing for their very dark red to black color, depending on the amount of sunlight. It seeds abundantly.

name: Cotton Black Beautiful
desc: This highly ornamental variety offers a surprising contrast of color in the garden, with its dark, almost black foliage and charming pink then dark red flowers. It can be grown both in pots and in the ground.

name: Black Forest
desc: This variety offers 4 to 5 round "Kabocha"-type fruits, up to 16 cm long and weighing up to 2 kg. The dark green skin, lightly spotted with lighter grooves, encloses sweet orange flesh.

name: Black Hungarian
desc: This vigorous variety produces purple flowers followed by juicy, conical "Jalapeño-type" fruit with good flavor. They ripen from dark purple, almost black, to dark red when fully ripe.

name: Black Mexican
desc: On several stems, this ancient variety produces spikes with pale pink bristles, consisting of 8 to 12 rows of tasty milky-white berries that turn bluish-black when ripe.

name: Black Prince
desc: This variety produces beautiful round or slightly pointed fruits, weighing 100 to 150 g. Their dark color is sometimes tinged with green at the shoulders. Their flesh has an incredibly sweet flavor.

name: Black-Seeded Simpson
desc:
This ancient variety produces an abundance of pale-green, blistered leaves. They have a crunchy, juicy texture and a much-appreciated mild flavor.

This variety is best suited to spring cultivation, but can also be grown in summer.

name: Silene Bladder Campion
desc: This herbaceous plant native to Europe is hardy enough to provide greenery all year round. It is also used in risottos and omelettes. In Italy, its flavor is similar to that of arugula and chicory, but slightly sweeter. Older leaves, on the other han

name: cockle Blanche
desc:
All summer long, the white cornflower offers a profusion of graceful, pure-white flowers. This messicolous, honey-bearing plant brings a rural touch to any garden!

The whole plant, and especially the seeds, contain a triterpene saponoside, agrosthem

name: Blanche du Dauphiné
desc: This ancient variety, originally from the Dauphiné region, produces very large white fruit that can reach over 40 kg. Their pinkish-white to yellowish-white flesh has a mild flavor and is of good quality.

name: turnip Blaril
desc: Suitable for autumn harvesting, this variety produces large, pinkish-purple rows weighing up to 1 kg, with tender, flavorful flesh. They can be stored frost-free until March.

name: Peas Blauwschokkers
desc:
A very productive variety with beautiful pink flowers. Its purple pods are slightly downy and can be eaten whole when picked young. Its seeds are meltingly tasty.

Peas for shelling can be used fresh or dried.

name: Blonde
desc: This variety produces fairly large white chard with a tender texture and light green foliage with serrated edges and very good flavor.

name: Arroch Blonde
desc: This old-fashioned variety, not as tall as "Opera", adapts to a wide range of growing conditions. Its rounded, blond-green leaves are very tender and can be eaten raw or cooked like spinach.

name: Blonde à Bord Rouge
desc: This old variety, not very sensitive to bolting, can be grown all year round in the garden or under cover. It produces beautiful non-head lettuces with tender green foliage, tinged with red, very crisp and full of flavor.

name: Curly endive Blonde à Cœur Plein
desc: This old French variety produces voluminous apples with full, blond cores, around 40 cm in diameter. The light-green, wavy leaves, with dense white ribs, offer a tender texture and good flavor.

name: Bloomsdale Long Standing
desc: This old variety, particularly tolerant of hot summer weather, has been popular since 1925 and produces large, thick, tender dark-green leaves.

name: Blot
desc: This splendid variety, adapted to cool or short-season regions, offers elongated, lobed fruits in hues ranging from yellow-purple to orange-purple, weighing 120 to 180 g. With their thick, juicy, sweet flesh, they're perfect for stuffed or roasted pe

name: Blue Jade
desc: This small variety, less than 1 m tall, produces 4 to 6 ears of blue berries rich in anthocyanins and with a soft texture.

name: Blue Kuri
desc: This variety produces bluish, rounded, flattened fruits that keep very well. Their slightly floury, orange flesh offers excellent flavor and is particularly suitable for jam-making.

name: Blue Ridge Mountain
desc: This North Carolina variety is vigorous and produces large, flat, beef-fleshed fruits, bright pink tinged with yellow, with a delicious flavor.

name: Blue Streaks
desc: This variety offers red-striped fruits that turn indigo in the sun thanks to their anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants. Their flavor is highly appreciated.

name: Bolero
desc: This variety, a favorite of butterflies and hummingbirds, grows up to 60 cm tall and produces an abundance of pinkish-purple flower spikes and dark green foliage mottled with red.To discover on the Kokopelli blog: "Agastaches to treat malaria, depres

name: Zucchinis Bolognese
desc:
This variety produces beautiful, oval-shaped, light-green fruit. Their creamy-white flesh has a fine texture and mild flavor.

WHAT ARE THE 

CHARACTERISTICS OF THE BOLOGNESE ZUCCHINI?

The "Bolognese" zucchini, a member of the Cucurbita pepo s

name: Bon Jardinier
desc:
This old, vigorous variety has thick, green leaves, slightly tinged with red, forming smooth, voluminous apples. Suitable for both dry and warm seasons.

This traditional variety is also known as "Rhenania". It is harvested in summer and autumn.

name: Bonny Best
desc:
This old variety, vigorous and not very sensitive to cold, produces bunches of 6 to 8 round, bright-red fruits weighing 150 to 250 g, with very firm, sweet and tart flesh.

 

It is advisable to protect the fruit from "sunburn" by creating a ligh

name: Bourguignonne
desc:
Bourguignonne winter lettuce forms large, tall, rounded heads of a beautiful pale green color. This very hardy, early-growing and productive old variety adapts to a wide range of growing conditions.

Characteristics of Bourguignonne winter lettuce

name: Brandywine Pink Joyce's Strain
desc: This variety, which is earlier than its "Pink Brandywine" relative, produces an abundance of large, pink, "beefsteak"-type fruits weighing 200 to 500g. They contain dense flesh of excellent flavor, both rich and sweet.

name: Bright Lights
desc: This disease-resistant variety offers chard with gold, pink, orange, red, white and other bright variations with green or bronze leaves.

name: Quinoas Brightest Brilliant Rainbow
desc: This variety produces panicles of various colors, from light green to creamy-white, yellow, orange and pink. They offer an abundance of edible seeds.

name: Brimmer
desc: This very old, very vigorous variety produces pink-purple fruits up to 10 cm in diameter and 1.3 kg. They contain few seeds and almost no core. The flesh is very firm.

name: Welsh onion Broad Leaved Nira
desc: This variety produces very broad, fine leaves that are easy to prune as needed. They are green in color, with a mild flavor.

name: Brown Winter
desc:
This old variety is one of the hardiest. It produces a beautiful apple with light-green foliage, tinged with reddish-brown, and offers a firm texture and good flavor.

Sown in spring, it sets seed quickly.

Characteristics of Brown Winter Lettuce

name: Brun Hatif Rouest
desc: This purple-flowered variety produces delicious pods containing two or three seeds. They contain around 18% lipid and 35% protein.

name: Buckwheat Buckwheat
desc:
This fast-growing species, also known as "buckwheat", produces clusters of small, melliferous white or pink flowers on upright stems, followed by edible seeds. It can also be used as a green manure.

In the right conditions, this species can grow u

name: Budenovka
desc: This Russian variety produces clusters of 8 to 10 pinkish-red, oval-shaped fruits with firm, fragrant flesh, suitable for preserves and sauces.

name: Bull Balls
desc:
The Bull's Balls tomato is an old Spanish variety with large red fruits weighing from 300 to 600 g, but which can exceed one kilo. The flesh is dense, rich and well-balanced, with excellent flavor and a wide range of culinary uses.
It is also known a

name: Bupleurum Bupleurum rotundifolium
desc: This annual species, also known as "Hare's Ear", is hardy and offers umbels of small yellow flowers surrounded by green bracts. Ideal for bouquets and floral arrangements, Bupleurum rotundifolium amazes with its originality! What's more, it's a medic

name: Burbank
desc:
This old variety, highly productive and resistant to harsh conditions, produces beautiful, round, red fruits weighing around 100g. They have delicious, juicy flesh with a high amino acid content.

It was bred around 1915 by the great master gardene

name: Burracker's Favorite
desc: This densely foliaged variety produces pineapple-like fruits that can exceed 1 kg, ranging from yellow-orange to pinkish-red. The flesh is dense and juicy, with a sweet, fruity flavor.This variety has a vigorous growth habit.

name: Buttercup Burgess
desc: Buttercup Burgess squash is an old-fashioned variety with very flat turban-shaped fruits up to 20 cm in diameter and weighing up to 2 kg. Their dark-green skin, striped with gray-green stripes, encloses bright-orange, fine, sweet flesh. The fruit kee

name: Moschata squash Butternut Honey Nut
desc: This mildew-resistant variety produces small, butternut-like fruits weighing from 500 g to 1 kg. Their green skin turns ochre when ripe, and their very fine orange-yellow flesh has a nutty flavor.

name: Moschata squash Butternut Sonca Orange
desc:
This variety, which originated in Hungary and is still relatively unknown, produces magnificent fruit weighing from 1.5 to 2 kg, with firm orange flesh and a pronounced hazelnut aroma.

In Hungarian, it's known as Soncatök (ham squash).

name: Camo Oakheart Mix
desc: This gene pool, proposed by Frank Morton in Oregon, is the result of a cross between the Deer Tongue variety and a Red Romaine, followed by a cross with Green Oakheart. The blend produces dense apples of varied volume. Their leaves are diversely lobe

name: Common Bean Canadian Wonder
desc: This ancient, vigorous and productive variety has pods around 15 cm long, containing 5 to 6 delicious deep-red seeds. They can be eaten fresh or dried, and are ideal for chili con carne recipes.

name: Candy Stripe
desc:
This variety, with its cut foliage and upright stems, has an abundance of pinkish-white flowers, edged with red. In autumn, birds come to feast on the flowers that have gone to seed.

To discover on the Kokopelli blog: "Cosmos: extremely medicinal

name: Cantaloup Obus/Kroumir
desc:
Over a long period, this ancient variety produces an abundance of fruit up to 20 cm long. They are deeply ribbed, sometimes slightly embroidered or scabby, and greenish-black when ripe. Foliage is very dense.
This variety has a very vigorous growth h

name: Capriglio
desc: This variety produces round, sometimes slightly three-lobed heart-shaped fruits, 6 to 8 cm in diameter, which are red or yellow when ripe. Their very thin skin encloses a thick, very sweet flesh.

name: Capriola
desc: This variety, closely related to Picotée, is distinguished by its more compact size and early flowering. It offers beautiful flowers where each white petal, edged with a broad pale pink border, surrounds a deep pink center and a yellow heart.

name: Sweet Pea Captain Of The Blues
desc: This old variety has highly fragrant, mauve-blue flowers. It was one of Henry Eckford's earliest breeding efforts of the Cupani variety, in 1899.

name: Carentan
desc: This hardy, early variety, with its long, heartless red roots adapted to heavy soils, offers excellent flavor. It can be harvested young, in bunches, or as a storage carrot.

name: California poppies Carmine King
desc: This variety reaches a height of 40 cm. It has bluish-gray foliage and an abundance of flowers with delicate petals of bright red at the center, fading to pink and white at the tips.

name: Carmine Red
desc:
This variety offers an abundance of magnificent carmine-red flowers 15 to 20 cm in diameter.

This species is very popular with birds. The flowers can be left to dry in autumn, so they can feed on the seeds.

To discover on the Kokopelli blog: "C

name: Carnival Mix
desc: This variety produces numerous spikes of magnificent double, pompon-like flowers in shades of red, pink, yellow and white all summer long, on 150 cm high vines.

name: Carolina Wonder
desc: This variety, similar to "Yolo Wonder B" but more resistant to nematodes, produces cubic, 4-lobed peppers about 7 to 8 cm long and wide. They ripen from green to red and have a very good flavor.

name: Peas Carouby de Maussane
desc:
This variety has very large pods, 7 cm long. Its green seeds turn grayish, then reddish with brown speckles.

This very old variety is mentioned in Vilmorin-Andrieux's "Les plantes potagères". It was highly prized in the south of France.

name: Casaba Golden Beauty
desc: This ancient variety, originally from the Middle East, is specially adapted to a hot, dry climate. Its fruits have a bright yellow, fairly hard skin and a very sweet white flesh. They keep for months!

name: Casaque Rouge
desc:
This variety produces bunches of 5 to 6 round, smooth fruits, whose firm flesh offers a highly appreciated semi-sweet, semi-acidic flavor. The sparse foliage is reminiscent of potatoes.

It is advisable to protect the fruits of this variety from "s

name: Casper
desc: This relatively mildew-resistant variety produces 4 kg fruits with smooth white skin. They are round or slightly oblong in shape. Their firm, dark orange flesh has a chestnut-like flavor.

name: Caspian Pink
desc: This very old variety offers numerous pink, "beef-flesh" type fruits, weighing 300 to 400 g. Fleshy, with few seeds, they have a rich flavor comparable to that of Brandywine, but slightly more acidic.

name: Curly endive Castel Franco
desc:
This very old variety forms a round, loose apple with magnificent green leaves variegated with red and white, tender and slightly bitter. Protect from extreme heat, for a late-season harvest.

 

Also known as Variegata di Castel Franco.

name: Cowpea Catjang
desc: This productive, drought-tolerant species produces long, green pods (18-20 cm) with seeds rich in protein, tryptophan and lysine.

name: Nepeta Catmint
desc: This highly fragrant perennial can grow up to 1 m tall. It produces gray-green foliage topped by spikes of white flowers speckled with purple. Like all nepetas, this plant makes cats dance!

name: Nepeta Catnip
desc: This perennial, highly fragrant species can grow up to 1 m tall and produces gray-green foliage and white flowers spotted with purple. Cats love to rub against it.

name: Cavendish Banana
desc:
This variety produces "Orange Banana"-type fruit, with slightly shorter berries weighing from 60 to 80 g. The skin is uniformly colored orange-yellow and the flavor is excellent.
This is a brand-new obtention from Tom Wagner.

name: Cayenne Golden
desc: This variety offers 15 cm-long fruits that ripen from green to yellow-orange. The variety is very productive.

name: Cha Kurakake
desc: This little-known variety, 60 to 70 cm tall, produces an abundance of pods containing 3 to 4 flattened yellow-brown seeds, rich in protein and with a thick texture. The seeds can be eaten fresh or dried.

name: Quinoas Chadmo
desc:
This ancient variety has light-green panicles with an abundance of creamy-brown to dark-red seeds. It is one of the quinoa varieties richest in polyunsaturated fatty acids.

 

Native to the island of Chiloé, off the coast of Chile, it is highly

name: Chadwicks Rodan
desc:
This ancient variety forms a loose rosette of slender, light-green leaves with bronze tips. They have a crisp, tender texture and a mild, slightly sweet flavor.
The peripheral leaves are cut off, one by one, as needed. New leaves develop after succes

name: Chalk's Early Jewel
desc: This old variety, introduced by James Chalk of Norristown, Pennsylvania, USA in 1899, offers an abundance of red, almost round fruit, weighing 90-120 g.

name: / Kales Champion
desc: This very heat-resistant variety produces an abundance of beautiful, flat, blue-green leaves. They have a very tender texture and a pleasantly sweet flavor.

name: Charleston Cayenne
desc: This robust, compact variety has elongated, slightly curved fruits that ripen from yellow-green to orange to red. They are stronger than traditional cayenne peppers.

name: Lamb's Quarters and Company Chenopodium formosanum
desc:
This species, known as Djuli in Taiwan, can grow up to 2 m tall and boasts magnificent, highly decorative dark-red flower stalks. Its young leaves are eaten cooked, and its seeds are used in cereals.

 

It has many medicinal qualities, including

name: Cherry Belle
desc:
This variety produces round, smooth, bright-red roots. Their white flesh offers a crunchy, firm texture and fine flavor.

This variety tolerates poor soils.

name: Rudbeckia Cherry Brandy
desc: At the end of its sturdy stems, this variety offers multiple star-shaped flowers with unique deep cherry-red petals and velvety black centers.

name: Quinoas Cherry Vanilla
desc: This highly productive variety produces large panicles - ranging in color from beige to bright pink - with an abundance of high-protein white and cream seeds. When young, the leaves can also be eaten like spinach!

name: Chicon Batavia
desc: This old variety, vigorous and slow to bolt, produces beautiful bronze-colored leaves lightly edged with red. They have a crunchy texture and excellent flavor.

name: of Milan Chieftain
desc: This old variety produces beautiful, flat, green apples weighing 2 to 3.5 kg. Their wavy green leaves offer a firm texture.

name: Chilhuacle Negro
desc: This variety has green stems tinged with purple and produces apple-shaped fruits that ripen from velvety green to chocolate-brown and offer a smoky flavor.

name: Chimayo
desc:
This variety produces smooth, elongated, curved fruits 10 to 20 cm long. They ripen from green to red and can be eaten fresh when green or dried when fully ripe. Their firm flesh has a sweet flavor.

This legendary variety was developed by Spanish

name: China Rose
desc: The Rose de Chine winter radish is a very old variety that produces bright pink roots 10 to 12 cm long. Their white flesh has a very firm, compact texture and a very fragrant, slightly spicy flavor. They can be stored all winter in gauges, silos or c

name: Welsh onion Chinese Chives
desc:
Chinese chives are hardy perennials with tufts of flat, full green leaves and beautiful star-shaped white flowers. Slightly milder than garlic, it is a staple of traditional Chinese cuisine.

What are the characteristics of Chinese spring onions?

name: Chinese Giant
desc: This old-fashioned variety has a low, compact habit and produces cubic green fruits that turn bright red when ripe. Their thick flesh offers a fruity flavor. Staking is recommended due to the weight of the fruit.

name: Chocolate Pear
desc: This variety produces pear-shaped fruit with firm, juicy flesh. Dark red to brown in color, with traces of green on the shoulders, they offer both rich flavor and excellent keeping qualities.

name: Churra Plum
desc:
This variety, with its magnificent downy grey-green foliage, produces oval or elongated fruits, variegated with yellow and red. They come in a wide variety of colors, shapes and flavors.
The variety's name is inspired by the Spanish sheep breed.

name: Ciliegia Piccante
desc: This ancient Italian variety of cherry pepper produces a myriad of magnificent small round fruits, red when ripe, about 4 cm in diameter and particularly prized in stuffed pepper recipes.

name: Cima Di Rapa Novantina
desc: This variety has all the qualities of mustard and broccoli. Leaves, stems and flower buds can be eaten raw or lightly cooked. They are both sweet and bitter. Stems are often preferred to leaves, as their flavor is milder.

name: Cima Di Rapa Sessantina
desc: This ancient variety has all the qualities of mustard and broccoli. Leaves, stems and flower buds can be eaten raw or lightly cooked. They are both sweet and bitter. Stems are often preferred to leaves for their less pronounced flavor.

name: Cinnamon
desc:
This ancient variety of aromatic plant, with lavender-colored flowers and stems lightly tinged with purple, produces green leaves with a cinnamon-like flavor.

What are the characteristics of cinnamon basil?

Cinnamon basil, Ocimum basilicum, is

name: Cipollini Yellow
desc: This traditional Italian variety - unsuitable for preserving, but ideal for grilling or pickling in vinegar - has very flat bulbs 7 to 10 cm in diameter, yellow to light brown in color, with a slightly spicy flavor.

name: Cleome Cleome spinosa
desc: This early annual species, also known as spiderflower, features graceful, spectacular white, pink and purple flowers arranged at the top of the central stem, forming a spider-like ball.

name: Coban Chile
desc: This ancient variety produces triangular fruits about 2 cm long, ripening from green to red. They have thick flesh and are traditionally dried over a fire.

name: Common Bean Cocagne
desc:
This early, highly productive variety produces beautiful, fairly flat, pale yellow pods with purple speckles, containing 7 to 10 ivory-white seeds with an incomparable flavor. They can be eaten semi-dry or dry.

An improvement on the Coco Paimpolai

name: Common Bean Coco de la Meuse
desc: Originally from Lorraine, this is an old, very productive early variety. It produces an abundance of small yellow pods containing beautiful, round, burgundy-pink beans. They can be enjoyed both dry and semi-dry.

name: Common Bean Coco de Prague
desc:
This hardy, productive variety produces long pods with dark red stripes when ripe. The dark beige berries are decorated with purple and can be harvested fresh or dried. They are very popular in pistou soups and salads.

It is possible to observe co

name: Quinoas Cocoa Cherry
desc: This variety, the result of a cross between Cherry Vanilla and a gene pool from Washington State University in the USA, produces black and red seeds. This joint breeding project between Frank Morton (a long-standing Kokopelli producer) and the Univer

name: Coeur de Velours
desc: This variety produces clusters of 2 to 3 "beef-flesh" red fruits, slightly flattened and ribbed, sensitive to bursting in wet weather and very fleshy. They contain few seeds.

name: Collective Farm Woman
desc: An early variety with medium-sized fruit and very sweet, white-yellow flesh. The skin is yellow-orange when ripe.

name: Pepo squash Connecticut Field
desc: This variety produces large, slightly elongated orange fruits weighing 7 to 12 kg. Their thin, hard skin is orange. Their thick, orange flesh offers excellent sweetness.

name: Coreopsis Coreopsis lanceolata
desc: This bushy species reaches up to 60 cm in height, with smooth, upright stems bearing a single compound flower with yellow petals cut at the ends, all summer long.

name: Coreopsis Coreopsis palmata
desc: This perennial species grows to 70 cm and produces upright, branched stems bearing 8-petaled yellow flowers with large yellow hearts from June to August.

name: Coreopsis Coreopsis tripteris
desc: This perennial species can reach 2 m in height. It features handsome palmate leaves on slender stems, as well as several 5 cm-diameter flowers with wide-spread yellow petals and a brown heart.

name: Peas Corne de Bélier
desc:
This highly productive variety produces large, slightly curved pods. They measure 10 to 12 cm in length and are very tasty.

 

This very old variety is mentioned in Vilmorin-Andrieux's "Les plantes potagères". It was highly prized in Switzerland

name: Corno di Toro Rosso
desc: This old, vigorous variety produces an abundance of horn-shaped fruit. They ripen from green to bright red and contain juicy flesh with a highly appreciated flavor, perfect for stuffing.

name: Corsican Flat
desc:
This variety produces beautiful light-green, rounded and flattened fruits, 15 cm in diameter.

The gourds dry in a dry, ventilated place and can be painted, sculpted or turned into various objects.

name: Cosmic Purple
desc: This variety produces purple roots about 15 cm long. Their orange flesh has a crunchy texture and a very fragrant taste.

name: Cosmic Yellow
desc:
This variety grows to no more than 60 cm in height, and produces an abundance of slender, upright stems bearing single or double golden-yellow flowers on cut, bushy foliage.

This species is very popular with birds. The flowers can be left to dry i

name: parviflorus
desc:
This annual species, common in North America, has airy, cut foliage and numerous small, particularly delicate flowers with white or pink petals and yellow centers.

 

Cosmos are a favorite with birds. The flowers can be left to dry in autumn, so

name: Zucchinis Costata Romanesco
desc:
This old variety offers an abundance of "Cocozelle"-type fruit, ribbed and green-gray in color with light streaks. Their firm flesh can be eaten both raw and cooked.

WHAT ARE THE 

WHAT ARE THE CHARACTERISTICS OF COSTATA ROMANESCO ZUCCHINI?

T

name: Cracoviensis
desc:
This old, very hardy variety produces reddish stems with purple-red edged leaves that can reach over 40 cm and form a loose head.

This variety adapts to a wide range of growing conditions.

name: Daisies Crazy Shasta
desc: This selection, by Frank Morton, of the Great Shasta Daisy, developed by Luther Burbank in 1901, offers various variations of its petals, hemmed, elongated, twisted and so on.

name: / Kales Creole
desc:
This old "Chou collet" variety is tolerant of poor soils and produces flat, green-blue leaves 70 cm to 1 m high. They have a tender, juicy texture and excellent flavor.
Can be harvested even under snowy conditions.

name: Crimson Giant
desc: This variety produces beautiful, rounded, dark-red roots that keep well. Their white flesh is tender and crunchy, with a mild flavor.

name: Sweet Pea Cupani
desc: This ancient variety, introduced to England in 1699 by Franciscus Cupani, a Sicilian monk, is close to the wild type. It grows to a height of 2.50 m and produces an abundance of highly fragrant purple and dark purple flowers.

name: Cupcakes
desc:
This exceptional variety of cosmos has graceful, cup-shaped flowers with toothed, fused petals that look like mini cakes. Although they are often pure white, some show pale pink hues.

Cosmos resow abundantly when birds spare their seeds.

name: Cutting Celery Cutting
desc: This old, hardy variety produces plants with abundant, upright foliage and fine, hollow white ribs with a tender, brittle texture.

name: Dakota Tears
desc:
This highly productive variety produces beautiful golden-yellow bulbs weighing around 450 g. It has been selected over 20 years for its vigor, size, storage capacity and disease resistance.

It is a Dave Podoll obtention.

name: Danvers
desc:
This old variety, ideal for autumn cultivation, produces orange roots with fairly wide collars, perfectly suited to heavy soils. The flesh is fine-textured, and the flavor is fragrant and sweet.

It was developed in Danvers, Massachusetts, in 1871.

name: Dark Galaxy
desc:
This variety has dense, curled foliage. It produces clusters of 10 to 12 plum-shaped fruits, green and purple, turning rusty-red and heavily mottled, containing lots of gel and seeds.

This variety is sensitive to hot weather.

name: Daydream
desc:
This variety has upright stems and cut foliage. It is distinguished by pale pink petals edged with bright pink towards the heart of the flower.

This species is very popular with birds. The flowers can be left to dry in autumn, so they can feed on

name: De Bellegarde
desc: This old "Cantaloup" variety offers slightly elongated, ribbed fruit with green spots before ripening. Their orange-red flesh is very sweet and mild.

name: De Cicco
desc: This very old Italian variety, with its compact habit, reaches a height of 60 cm. Highly productive and precocious, it has a central head 8 to 10 cm in diameter, followed by numerous shoots. They can be harvested over several months.

name: Lupin De Hartweg En Mélange de Couleurs
desc: This annual lupine has dark-green bushy foliage and sumptuous spikes of flowers in a wide range of colors, from white to blue, pink and purple. Ideal for adding height to the ornamental garden!

name: Brussels sprouts De Rosny
desc: This old-fashioned variety, suited to winter harvesting, produces an abundance of small, dense, firm, gray-green apples, evenly arranged. They last a long time before opening, and offer a delicious flavor that improves with frost.

name: Curly endive De Saint Laurent
desc: This variety produces small apples, 25 to 30 cm in diameter, with dark-green, extremely dissected, curly, frizzy leaves. They have a crunchy texture and a good, pronounced flavor.

name: Snapdragon Defiant Mix
desc: This variety of wolf's mouth, or snapdragon, produces beautiful plants - very easy to grow - with vibrant colors ranging from cherry red to bright orange, through various shades of yellow. Ideal for brightening up beds and perfect for bouquets!

name: Des Landes
desc: This old variety produces an abundance of green to red ripening fruits 8 to 15 cm long. They are thin-skinned, with firm flesh and a fruity flavor.

name: Desmanthus Desmanthus illinoensis
desc:
This perennial species can reach 2 m in height, with an abundance of mimosa-like leaves and a cluster of small white flowers.

Thanks to its high protein content, it is suitable for feeding livestock and wild animals, and is used for soil regenerat

name: Desmodium Desmodium glutinosum
desc: This perennial species can reach 1 m in height. Its flowers are very light pink and grouped in terminal panicles over 40 cm long.

name: Deweese Streaked
desc: This old, vigorous variety produces pineapple-like fruits, slightly ribbed at the top, weighing up to 700g. They are mottled red and yellow and have good flavor.

name: Diamex
desc: This variety, of the Charentais type, produces beautiful round fruit with juicy, sweet, bright orange flesh. They ripen as the season progresses, enabling staggered harvests.

name: Dinner Plate
desc: This old, vigorous and highly productive variety produces large, heart-shaped fruits, up to 1.8 kg, with delicious, dense, juicy flesh. One slice is enough to cover a plate!

name: Cannabis Dioica 88
desc: This dioecious hemp variety can grow to over 3 m in height and offers high biomass and straw yields. It is also suitable for flower and seed production, and generally contains less than 6% CBD.

name: Dolly
desc: This compact-growing variety produces large, rounded leaves of a slightly lighter green color than the "Vert de Gênes" variety.

name: Double Density
desc: This vigorous, slow-growing, bremia-resistant variety produces a compact, upright apple twice as dense as "Winter Density". The green leaves with red spots and very wavy edges offer a crunchy texture and excellent flavor.

name: Douce de Picardie
desc: This very old variety offers many smooth, round, medium-sized, creamy-white fruits. Mild flavor.

name: Fedia Doucette d'Alger
desc:
This species, closely related to lamb's lettuce, has delicious, fleshy green leaves. They keep for up to 60 days after harvest and add a nutty flavor to salads.

 

Algerian sweet potatoes have small, pink flowers, which are highly decorative and

name: Rice Duborskian
desc: This ancient variety, originally from Russia, produces fifteen or so stalks of short seeds on robust plants. Hardy, it is fully adapted to dry cultivation and even resists light frosts.

name: Dubya Dapple Density
desc: This bolting-, bremia- and mildew-resistant variety - one of the smallest, brightest and densest of the Romaines, according to Frank Morton - offers succulent, wavy, reddish-green leaves surrounding a compact, pinkish-white heart.

name: Dubya Green Density
desc:
This compact, bremia-resistant variety produces a dense apple with smooth, green leaves. They offer a crisp texture and good flavor.
A variety bred by Frank Morton in Oregon.

name: Dubya Red Density
desc:
This Frank Morton-bred variety is, in his opinion, the tiniest, brightest, densest and most wavy-edged mini-romaine he's ever grown. It's half the volume of "Winter Density" and twice as dense. It has a pinkish-white heart.
This variety is fully resi

name: / Kales Dwarf Blue Curled Scotch
desc: This old, compact variety has finely chiselled, curly leaves 50-60 cm long, blue-green in color and excellent flavor. It can also be grown in spring.

name: Dwarf Champion Improved
desc: This variety, comparable to "Dwarf Champion", has slightly larger leaves and dark pink, "mini" beefsteak-type fruit with a milder flavor. Suitable for both salads and preserves.

name: Dwarf Cherokee Tiger Large Red
desc:
This compact variety, ideal for growing in pots or containers, produces beautiful, bright-red beefsteak-like fruit with fine gold stripes, weighing 180 to 300 g, with thick, juicy, tangy flesh.

It is a cross between the "Tigrette" and "Cherokee Pu

name: Dwarf Shadow Boxing Long
desc: This compact variety produces plum-shaped, orange-red fruit with dark purple spots. The flesh is particularly appreciated for its sweet, slightly spicy flavor.

name: Anthemis Dyer's chamomile
desc: This perennial species was used to produce yellow dye. Bushy and dense in appearance, it produces numerous bright yellow daisy-like flowers throughout the season.

name: Eagle Pass
desc: This variety offers large green fruits to be eaten raw or cooked, before they reach 8 cm in length. They contain a mucilaginous substance used to thicken sauces and stews, and have an eggplant-like flavor.

name: Early Green
desc: This early, vigorous variety has a compact habit and produces a central green head, which can reach 24 cm, and is followed, when cut, by an abundance of lateral shoots.

name: Brussels sprouts Early Half Tall
desc: This very old French variety, mentioned in Vilmorin-Andrieux's Les Plantes Potagères, produces small, well-rounded, pale-green apples measuring 1 to 2 cm on plants 50 to 75 cm tall.

name: Early Naples Giant
desc: This ancient variety, originally from Italy, is an early ripener and produces very large white apples protected by a beautiful leafy covering.

name: Early Niagara Giant
desc:
This variety, adapted to cool climates, produces an average of 6 to 8 ribbed fruits with 3 to 4 ripening lobes, ranging from green to red. Thick flesh.

We recommend staking this variety.

name: Curly endive Early Red Treviso
desc:
This old-fashioned variety has large, thick green leaves that turn red as cold weather approaches, with a pleasant flavor and an elongated head. It can be forced to produce chicory.

This variety first forms a large rosette of leaves that disappear

name: Early Snowball
desc: This very old variety produces a very white, fine-grained apple with a crunchy texture. The outer leaves are slightly corrugated and fold down towards the ground. The stem is fairly short.

name: Early Snowball / Nain Hâtif d'Erfurt
desc: This very old variety produces a very white, fine-grained apple with a crunchy texture. The outer leaves are slightly corrugated and fold down towards the ground. The stem is fairly short.

name: angustifolia
desc:
This perennial species grows to between 15 and 30 cm in height and produces, from May to October, an abundance of flowers with horizontal or drooping pink petals surrounding a large, domed purple heart.

It is a major species in Native American pha

name: paradoxa
desc:
This very rare species reaches heights of up to 1.20 m and, from May to July, produces slender, upright stems bearing a flower with bright yellow petals curving sharply towards the ground and a domed, purple center.

Echinacea paradoxa is the only

name: simulata
desc: This species grows up to 1 m tall and, from June to July, produces slender, upright stems bearing a flower with pink to violet petals 4 to 9 cm long, curving towards the ground. It can be distinguished fromEchinacea pallida by its yellow pollen.

name: tennesseensis
desc: This rare and endangered perennial variety produces slender, upright, branched stems bearing skyward-pointing violet-petaled flowers from May to October.

name: Eclipta Eclipta prostrata
desc: This annual medicinal species, in the Asteraceae family, has low-growing plants with small white "daisy" flowers. It is highly reputed in the three Indian pharmacopoeia systems - Ayurveda, Unani and Siddha - as a liver and scalp tonic, but also to tr

name: Eden Gem
desc: This old variety is not very susceptible to rust, producing fruits 15 cm in diameter and weighing 1 to 1.5 kg. Their ochre-colored skin is embroidered and their green flesh offers a firm texture and delicious flavor.

name: Edisto 47
desc: This productive, disease-resistant variety produces "Cantaloup"-type fruits weighing 1 to 2 kg. Their orange flesh, with a fairly small seed cavity, offers a sweet, juicy flavor even in rainy years.

name: Cotton Egyptian Fine
desc: This variety offers pure white fiber and flowers that are yellow when they bloom, then pink once pollinated by insects. Each "fruit" produces between 4 and 6 grams of cotton.

name: Elberta Leeway
desc: This variety produces slightly elongated fruit with a fluffy "peach skin" and firm, red and orange-striped flesh, offering an intense, fragrant flavor. This recent selection may display some "unstable" traits.

name: Garlic bulbs Elephant Garlic bulblets AB - Allium ampeloprasum
desc:
Elephant Garlic bulblets will be available for sale in September 2024.

Elephant garlic, or oriental garlic, is halfway between garlic and leek. It has very large bulbs, 7 to 10 cm long and weighing up to 400 g, made up of 4 to 6 large cloves with

name: Emerald Giant
desc:
This vigorous variety, adapted to hot, dry regions, produces an abundance of 4-lobed, ribbed fruit. They ripen from dark green to red, revealing thick, juicy flesh with a crunchy texture.

 

Staking is recommended due to the weight of the fruit.

name: Emilia Emilia javanica
desc: From July to October, this species produces magnificent, small, pompon-shaped red flowers. It produces abundant foliage that can be eaten raw, in salads, or cooked, like spinach.

name: Empress Of India
desc: This old variety, very popular in Victorian times, has dark-green foliage and fiery-red flowers. Leaves, flower buds, flowers and seeds are edible.

name: Enorma
desc:
This red-flowered variety can reach heights of over 3 m, and produces an abundance of pods around 50 cm long, containing purple seeds with black variegation.
This variety is very productive.

name: Envy
desc: This variety produces an abundance of pods containing green seeds, which can be eaten dry or semi-dry.

name: Eraly Bush Beefsteak
desc: In cool, difficult climates, this vigorous, compact variety produces an abundance of very large, round, slightly flattened fruit. They contain few seeds and have firm, sweet flesh.

name: Eritrean
desc: This variety from Eritrea has a compact habit on plants up to 80 cm tall. The green leaves and creamy-pink flowers exude a spicy fragrance.

name: Cotton Erlene's Green
desc: This variety has magnificent pinkish-yellow flowers 6 or 7 cm in diameter. After fertilization, they form fruits containing light olive-green cotton fibers. They should be harvested as soon as they open, so that the fibers retain their full color.

name: Eureka
desc: This ancient variety produces clusters of 5 to 7 red, round fruits that are highly resistant to shattering. Suitable for sauces and preserves.

name: Eva's Purple Ball
desc: This very old, vigorous variety, adapted to hot, humid climates, produces clusters of 3 to 5 juicy, round, slightly flattened, pink fruits with a slight white tinge.Once ripe, the tomatoes detach naturally.

name: Oenothera Evening Primrose Crown
desc: During the summer, this slender oenothera reveals spreading clusters of large white to pinkish flowers, around 5 cm in diameter. Drought-resistant, it is ideal for rock gardens, borders or flowerbeds.

name: Evergreen
desc: This vigorous variety produces ribbed, amber-green fruit when ripe. Excellent for preserves and salads, they offer very firm, soft, emerald-green flesh.

name: Extra Long Handle Dipper
desc:
This variety produces light-green fruit with a spherical base and a narrow, very long neck.
The gourds dry in a dry, ventilated place and can be painted, sculpted or turned into various objects.

name: Farmer's Market Jalapeño
desc: This rare and little-known Jalapeño, sometimes called Jalapeño patate, offers an abundance of large, conical fruit, red when ripe. Their skin is adorned with strange, cork-like embroidery, and they reveal a sweet, mildly spicy flavor. Perfect as a st

name: Fatalii
desc: This ancient variety produces an abundance of ribbed, pointed fruit, which ripen from pale green to bright yellow. They have firm flesh.

name: Fife Creek
desc:
This highly productive variety produces light-green, curved fruit about 20 cm long. They remain tender longer than most other varieties. It is said to have been given to the Fife family by a Creek Indian woman.

 

Okra fruits contain a mucilagin

name: Siam squash (Ficifolia) Fig Leaf Squash
desc:
This variety produces 5 to 15 green, watermelon-like fruits with white lines and spots, weighing up to 6 kg. Their soft white flesh becomes very stringy when cooked.

This variety is ideal for jam-making.

What are the characteristics of Siam squ

name: Common Bean Flageolet Chevrier Vert
desc: This ancient variety, less than 50 cm tall, produces flat, straight green pods about 15 cm long. They contain pale green seeds that turn white when fully ripe. Best eaten semi-dry or dry.

name: Welsh onion Flagpole
desc: This variety produces 60 cm-high, violet-green leaves with a white, swollen base.

name: Celosia Flamingo
desc: This annual variety with dark-green foliage produces magnificent pink and white flowers, grouped in long spikes. Flowering lasts a long time.

name: Flat with Purple Pods
desc: This variety, which can grow up to 3 m high, produces pods around 15 cm long, purple in color, turning green when cooked, and containing brown seeds.

name: Flemish Antique
desc: Simply majestic, this poppy unveils imposing, peony-like double flowers. Composed of countless crumpled petals, they come in shades of antique pink to pastel red with creamy stripes.

name: Floral Gem
desc: This variety offers small, conical fruits, 5 to 7 cm long, turning from light yellow to red when fully ripe. They have firm flesh.

name: Cotton Florida Green
desc: This variety, ideal for a family garden, produces light-green cotton bales and ornamental red foliage. The seeds separate quite easily from the fiber.

name: Pepo squash Fordhook Acorn
desc: This ancient variety of acorn squash produces an abundance of atypical 1 kg fruits whose skin takes on a dark ochre color when fully ripe. Their firm flesh has a strong, pleasant flavor.

name: Four Seasons
desc:
This old variety has excellent keeping qualities. It has a large, tight, round, slightly flattened head with firm, full, bright green leaves. The stem is half-short.

This variety has a fairly spreading habit.

name: Frances Perry
desc: This hardy, hardy perennial variety produces orange-red flowers from the first year onwards, creating a beautiful effect in the garden.

name: Frank Morton's Secret Mix
desc: This blend contains a great diversity of varieties, textures and shapes: "Batavias", "Romaines", highly embossed "Lollo" in all colors, intensely crimson-red or dotted "Tongues", densely red "Oak Leaves", "Leopards" crossed with "Manoas", and many va

name: Frank's Unsung Crispy Mix
desc: These varieties have the bremia tolerance of the "Merlot" variety combined with the crunchy qualities of the "Reine des Glaces" and the flavours of various romaine lettuces.

name: Quinoas French Vanilla
desc:
This variety can sometimes reach over 2 m in height. It doesn't branch and therefore bears fruit uniformly. Its yellow-brown panicles bear creamy-white seeds.
It is a Frank Morton selection of the "Brightest Brilliant" variety.

name: Fresno
desc: This not very cold-sensitive variety has a bushy habit and produces smooth, conical fruits that ripen from light green to deep red. They have a sweet flavor and can be smoked or dried.

name: Friariello Di Napoli
desc: This highly productive sweet pepper from Naples, Italy, is traditionally used fried. Nevertheless, its mild, sweet flavor is a perfect accompaniment to all kinds of dishes, raw or cooked.

name: Curly endive Frisée Maraîchère
desc: This variety, with less curly foliage than "Maraîchère Très Fine", has a rosette of finely cut leaves with white ribs. They can be harvested as needed, as young shoots or when fully mature.

name: From Auvergne
desc: This French variety produces fairly low-growing plants with half-long pods, about 15 cm long, containing 3 to 5 delicious beans. When steamed, they reveal a soft, melt-in-the-mouth texture.

name: From Haute Loire
desc: This local variety produces round, flattened turnips 10 to 15 cm in diameter. Their white skin and light green collar enclose firm white flesh with a subtle taste. They can be eaten raw or cooked.

name: Perillas From Japan / Hojisho
desc:
This ancient variety has beautiful green, serrated leaves with a violet-red underside. They can be eaten raw or cooked, and are highly prized in Asian cuisine.

 

Its name "Hojisho" means "heads", as its terminal bouquets are used preferentially

name: Sorrel From Transylvania
desc:
This variety offers long, tasty leaves all winter long. Extremely hardy, it tolerates dry spells very well.

It was collected by The Seed Ambassadors project from a Hungarian farmer at the Cluj market in Romania, during a trip to Transylvania in 2008

name: From Trébons
desc: This ancient variety produces white bulbs up to 10 cm long, with an extremely mild, sweet flavor. They keep for up to 8 months.

name: Perillas From Vietnam / Jeok Ssam Ip
desc: This variety of shiso has magnificent, aesthetically pleasing leaves, green on top and entirely purple on the reverse. Smaller than Japanese varieties, they also reveal a more intense flavor.Find out more on the Kokopelli blog: "Qualités Médicinales

name: Fruit Punch
desc: This variety, hardy and perennial in certain climates, produces magnificent semi-double flowers with a crumpled silk texture and shimmering colors of dark red, violet or deep pink.

name: Fushimi
desc: This variety produces thin-skinned, elongated, crumpled and pointed fruits that ripen from bright green to red. Suitable for drying.

name: Gakhaa
desc:
This rare variety produces large, bottle-shaped fruit with a shallow neck and dark green skin flecked with white. They can be eaten young or used, when ripe, to make handicrafts.

 

The gourds dry in a dry, ventilated place and can be painted, s

name: Galina
desc: This variety produces a large number of round, yellow "cherry tomatoes" weighing 15 to 20 g. The flavor is both sweet and complex. The flavor is both sweet and complex. The developed foliage is reminiscent of potatoes.

name: Mixes Garden Birds
desc:
This seed mix, created in cooperation with the LPO, offers a wide range of species and varieties suitable for feeding many birds: goldfinches, turtle doves, blue tits and other passerines.

It includes :

- Titan sunflower seed, 25% ;

- Cultiv

name: Sweet marjoram Garden Marjoram
desc: This perennial, woody species, 30 to 60 cm high, forms beautiful bushes with small, oval, downy, gray-green leaves and an incomparable flowery aroma. A symbol of joy and love since ancient times, marjoram is used in both cooking and medicine.

name: Garden Sun
desc: This variety offers small yellow fruits. It has recently been developed by Tom Wagner and can show a very wide range of colors, shapes and flavors. It is a cross between "Garden Lime" and "OP Sun Sugar".

name: Garden Sunshine
desc:
This variety produces firm, ribbed fruits that remain creamy-yellow for weeks before turning orange, then red when fully ripe. Plants do not exceed 40 cm in height.
Suitable for pot cultivation.

name: Gardener's Delight
desc: This variety offers an abundance of beautiful clusters of 6 to 12 round, thin-skinned fruits, 2 to 4 cm in diameter. The flesh is firm and juicy, with a good, sweet flavor and a hint of acidity.

name: Asclepias Gay Butterflies
desc: This highly ornamental perennial variety offers numerous clusters of small flowers in flamboyant shades of yellow, bright orange or pink. Magnificent in bouquets or in the garden, where they attract butterflies.

name: Perillas Gene Pool
desc: To discover on the Kokopelli blog: "Qualités Médicinales des Perillas" (Medicinal qualities of Perillas)

name: German giant
desc: This variety produces round, red roots that can reach the size of a tennis ball and weigh up to 200g. Their white flesh has a firm, crunchy texture.

name: Garlic bulbs Germidour Purple Garlic Bulbs 250 g AB - Allium sativum
desc:
Germidour garlic will be back on the market in autumn 2024.

This very early autumn variety, adapted to cool regions, offers large purple or white bulbs with purple stripes, made up of 10 to 15 cloves. This strong-tasting purple garlic is renowned

name: Scorsonera Giant Black Russian
desc: This hardy species can be grown like salsify, whose taste it also resembles. It forms long, black roots that, once scraped and washed, can be prepared in a variety of ways: au gratin, sautéed, in fritters, and so on. The young, nutty leaves are delic

name: Curly endive Giant Chioggia Catalonia
desc: This ancient Italian variety produces rosettes of thick, juicy, crunchy leaves with very long ribs. They have the characteristic bitter taste of chicory.

name: Pepo squash Gill's Golden Pippin
desc: This very tasty acorn squash variety has 10 small, bright yellow fruits that are very easy to stuff. Perfect for autumn decorations.

name: Gillogly Pink
desc: This vigorous variety produces beautiful, round, red "beef-flesh" fruits that are low in seeds, very firm and full-flavored. Tends to burst.Fruits are susceptible to bursting in the wet season.

name: Glasnost
desc: This variety, less than 1 m tall, has a compact habit. It is vigorous and adapted to short seasons. It produces round, dense "beef-flesh" fruit with a highly appreciated flavor.

name: Glaucium flavum
desc:
This species, with its large, strongly lobed and toothed basal leaves, has solitary flowers up to 8 cm in diameter. They have 4 crinkled golden-yellow petals.

This species, also known as Glaucie, Glaucienne jaune or Chélidoine glauque, can reach 8

name: Globe Red
desc: This American variety, with its reduced foliage, quickly produces a small scarlet-red root, globe-shaped or sometimes slightly oval, with crisp white flesh.

name: Gloria
desc:
This variety offers upright stems, sometimes up to 1.50 m tall, with airy, cut foliage. Numerous pink flowers with red centers grace the garden from early summer through to frost.

Cosmos are a favorite with birds. The flowers can be left to dry in

name: Peas Gloriosa
desc: This vigorous, productive variety produces an abundance of pods containing delicious wrinkled seeds with a sweet, fruity flavor. It tolerates difficult climatic conditions.

name: Licorice Glycyrrhiza yunnanensis
desc:
This species of licorice, native to Yunnan in China, produces lilac flowers on plants over 1 metre tall, followed by ornamental red pods.
It is highly prized in traditional Chinese pharmacopoeia.

name: Zucchinis Golden
desc:
This variety produces an abundance of yellow fruit with creamy-white, soft, sweet flesh. They can grow up to 30 cm long without becoming warty, but are best at around 10 cm.

WHAT ARE THE 

CHARACTERISTICS OF THE SMOOTH-NECKED YELLOW ZUCCHINI?

name: Golden
desc: This ancient variety produces small, round roots with bright golden-yellow skin. Their light yellow flesh has a tender texture and excellent flavor, sweeter than the red varieties.

name: Golden Acre
desc: This variety produces round, green-gray "Copenhagen Market" apples weighing around 2 kg and measuring 15 cm in diameter. The stem is fairly short and the outer leaves are tightly wrapped. It has a tender texture and good sweet flavor.

name: Golden Ball
desc:
This ancient variety produces very smooth, spherical roots about 10 cm in diameter, with yellow skin and flesh. They have a sweet, slightly bitter taste. When young, the root can be eaten raw.

This semi-hardy variety is recommended for autumn harv

name: Golden Drop
desc: This English variety of soft wheat has white ears with no burrs and large white kernels. Its straw is robust. It was introduced at the end of the 19th century.

name: Golden Gopher
desc: This variety produces very ribbed orange fruits weighing around 1 kg. Rich in sugar, they tend to burst when ripe.

name: Golden Grex
desc:
This variety produces huge yellow-orange roots. Their flesh features alternating yellow and red rings, and is tender and tasty despite the large volume of the roots.
Also used for forage.

name: Golden Jubilee
desc:
This hardy perennial variety can reach 1 m in height and produces blue-purple flower spikes and chartreuse-green foliage with a licorice fragrance used as an aromatic.

It is a cultivar ofAgastache foeniculum. The name Agastache comes from the Gree

name: Golden Tankard
desc: Large, elongated, smooth, yellow, cylindrical root. Very sweet yellow flesh. Very good shelf life. Variety also used as fodder due to large root volume.

name: Rudbeckia Goldsturm
desc: This perennial, rhizomatous variety reaches up to 1 m in height and produces branched stems bearing an abundance of yellow-orange flowers with dark-brown centers.

name: / Kales Grand Vert du Nord
desc: This old French variety has a magnificent plume of deeply-cut, extremely curly leaves at the end of a stem over 1 m high. Even in the harshest winters, it provides an excellent fresh vegetable!

name: Grasse de Morges
desc: This old Swiss variety, vigorous and hardy, produces a voluminous, dense rosette of blistered, fat leaves, slightly bronze-tinged at the edges. They have a particularly crunchy texture.

name: Nepeta Greek
desc: On plants up to two meters tall, this species offers highly fragrant violet-purple flowers borne on strong stems. Foliage is gray-green.

name: Lentil Green
desc: This lentil, with its light green teguments, has slender, highly branched stems, forming an upright tuft 20 to 40 cm high, bearing small pods containing one or two seeds. Its small white or pale blue flowers bloom from May to August.

name: Arroch Green
desc: This ancient variety, very hardy and adapted to heat, is fast-growing and has highly embossed green leaves. They can be eaten raw or cooked like spinach, with a flavor reminiscent of spinach.

name: Green Bell Pepper
desc: This variety produces clusters of 8 to 9 fruits with green and yellow stripes. They come in a variety of shapes, often reminiscent of peppers, and are sometimes juicy, sometimes not very fleshy, perfect for stuffing.Some of the firm, juicy fruits are

name: Green Doctors Frosted
desc:
This very vigorous variety produces light-green cherry tomatoes with transparent skin and a very rich flavor.

It is a mutation of the "Green Doctors" cherry tomato variety, itself a mutation of the "Dr. Carolyn" variety.

name: Green Hokkaido
desc:
The Hokkaido Green Pumpkin is an ancient variety with 1 to 3 round, slightly ribbed fruits, up to 15 cm in diameter and weighing up to 2.5 kg. They keep for almost 1 year. Their yellow flesh is dry, not very fibrous and sweet.

Green pumpkin charac

name: seeds Green manure. Mix
desc:
Sunflowers offer high biomass yields and make excellent honey meadows. They can also be grown in mixtures with other green manures, such as phacelia or sorghum!

The flower petals are great for salads, and the seeds are very popular with birds.

name: Green Mariner
desc: This variety produces dense, light-green apples. They have a crunchy texture.

name: Ammi Green Mist
desc: This ecotype offers plants with very sturdy stems, finely cut blue-green leaves and chartreuse-green flowers. These flowers, which blend perfectly into bouquets, also attract pollinating insects!

name: Green Oakheart
desc:
This variety produces extremely compact, dense lettuces of the "Feuille de Chêne" type. They have a crisp texture and a mild, delicate flavor.

Selected by Frank Morton in Oregon, it is a cross between Emerald Oak, Reine des Glaces and Merlot.

name: Green Sausage
desc:
This compact variety, not very sensitive to drought or mildew, offers a multitude of small, elongated, pointed fruits, green tinged with yellow when ripe, with little juice. They are ideal for sauces and drying.

 

"Green Sausage doesn't grow ta

name: Green Star
desc:
This variety produces pear-shaped gourds with light green skin and white flesh.
The gourds dry in a dry, well-ventilated place and can be painted, sculpted or transformed into various objects.

name: Green Top Bunching
desc: This ancient, hardy variety has flattened, dark-red roots with a tender texture and good flavor. Its green foliage is veined with red.

name: Green with White Card
desc: This old variety produces broad, white ribs with a tender texture, topped with elongated, light-green leaves.

name: Gréoux
desc: This vigorous variety has well-developed foliage and produces dense "beef-flesh" fruits that can exceed 1 kg. They hold up well after picking and are ideal for preserving.

name: Guatemalan Blue
desc: This variety produces very elongated fruits, 40 cm long and up to 3 kg in weight. Their skin is blue-grey and their bright yellow flesh has a sweet flavor. The fruit occasionally takes on a darker blue hue.

name: Guldaeno
desc: This frost-resistant Danish variety produces delicious, one-third-buried, orangey-yellow, ovoid roots with excellent shelf life. They are very easy to pull up and were traditionally used for fodder.

name: Habanero Combahee Red
desc: This ancient variety produces an abundance of wrinkled, lantern-shaped fruit, which ripen from green to red and offer very fruity flesh. Beware, it has been graded at 256,433 Scoville units, making it one of the most powerful varieties.

name: Habanero Pink
desc: This prolific variety produces salmon-orange fruit in the classic "Habanero" shape, but with little pungency. Their juicy, tender flesh offers a sunny, fruity taste with just a hint of spice.

name: Habanero White
desc: This variety has a compact habit and produces cubic "Habanero"-type fruits that retain their creamy-white color when ripe.

name: Hakucho
desc: This excellent variety from Japan produces large green beans with a creamy texture and sweet taste. This determinate-growing soybean is harvested in a single pass and grows into compact, productive plants 50cm tall.

name: Halblange White
desc: This winter-tolerant variety produces white, conical roots, sometimes 30 cm long, which can be harvested from autumn to winter. Easy to pull, they have a slightly sweet taste.

name: Halo Cream
desc: This rust-resistant variety can reach 2 m in height and offers magnificent cream flowers with a large purple halo in the center.

name: Halo Red
desc: This variety has large, red flowers with yellow centers, 12 to 14 cm in diameter. The robust plants produce 3 to 5 stems over two meters tall.

name: / Kales Hanover Salad
desc: This variety produces rosettes of smooth, lobed green leaves. Eaten young, they offer a tender texture and excellent mild flavor.

name: Portulaca Happy Hour Banana
desc:
This variety, adapted to dry regions and rock gardens, produces small, non-creeping clumps of regular, thin green leaves and double, brilliant yellow flowers that close at night.

It generally flowers earlier than other ornamental purslane varietie

name: Portulaca Happy Hour Peppermint
desc: This drought-resistant variety has many large, double fuchsia, white, yellow or pink variegated flowers. This mix of colors, highlighted by curious, fleshy foliage, stands out in sunny rock gardens.

name: Hâtif d'Angers
desc: This early variety, suitable for winter and spring production, produces a delicious white apple with a fine, firm texture.

name: Healthy Pepper
desc: Adapted to harsh conditions, this variety produces pointed fruit that ripen from yellow to orange to red. Their crunchy flesh has a mild, sweet flavor.

name: seeds Helena
desc:
This ancient variety, very hardy and adapted to a wide range of growing conditions, offers excellent potential for oil production. The medium-sized, striped seeds are also ideal for feeding birds in winter!
The flower petals enhance salads, and the s

name: Helios / Early Yellow Radish
desc: This old variety produces round, yellow roots with firm flesh and short, sparse foliage all year round, as long as sowing is staggered.

name: Welsh onion Heshiko
desc: A staple of Asian cuisine, this variety is particularly well suited to bundling. It produces chives around 35 cm high, with bright white bulbs. They can be eaten fresh or cooked, in the same way as spring onions.

name: Hibiscus Hibiscus sabdariffa
desc: This species has large, pinkish-white, yellow-tinged flowers with purple-red centers. After fertilization, they produce dark-red fruits used to make a drink called bissap. The young shoots and leaves can also be eaten raw or cooked.

name: Hickory King
desc: Plants 3 and sometimes even 4 meters tall produce ears 20 to 22 cm long with 10 to 12 rows of seeds. They are flat, white and among the largest known.

name: Millets Himalayan Finger
desc:
This ancient variety of hard cereal, rich in amino acids, has been cultivated in the arid mountainous regions of Africa for 5000 years, and in Asia, in the Himalayas up to 2300 m altitude, where the seeds are also used to make beer.

Eleusine is na

name: Hinona Kabu
desc: This very rare old Japanese variety produces elongated, white roots, 25 to 30 cm long, with bright pink-red collars. They have a remarkable flavor, both sweet and peppery.

name: Hon Tsai Tai
desc: This variety, similar to "Brocoli-raab", produces dark-green leaves with purplish veins, offering a delicate, mild flavor. The stems bear small sprays that should be picked before they bloom.

name: Honey Drip
desc:
This traditional variety, originally from Kentucky, can grow to over 3 m tall, with beautiful golden-brown panicles. Perfect for making molasses, it produces an excellent, easy-to-cook syrup.

The leaf sheaths of Sorghum bicolor, rich in anthocyanin,

name: Hong Vit
desc: This fast-growing variety produces almost hairless green leaves with a red midrib. They are ideal for sprinkling on salads. When fully mature, the roots are red and very small in diameter.

name: Hopi Pale Grey
desc: This ancient Amerindian variety produces oblong, grey-blue fruit with thick, orange flesh. It has a very long shelf life.

name: Hopi Rattle
desc:
This variety produces "Pèlerine"-type fruits, 7 to 8 cm long. Their skin takes on an ochre color when ripe.
The gourds dry in a dry, well-ventilated place and can be painted, sculpted or transformed into various objects.

name: Common Bean Horticultural Taylor's Bean
desc: This very old, compact variety is vigorous and produces an abundance of pink pods with white stripes. They are best eaten young, when the seeds are still tender, or dry in soups and stews.

name: Hot Portugal
desc: This old variety, with its upright habit, produces an abundance of pointed, curved, shiny fruit, around 10 to 15 cm long, appreciated for its fine skin and pungent flavor.

name: Lamb's Quarters and Company Huauzontle - Aztec spinach
desc: This Mexican goosefoot is entirely edible, with delicious foliage and inflorescences that can be eaten like broccoli shoots. In autumn, the whole plant takes on shades of orange and red.

name: Hungarian Hot Wax
desc:
This ancient variety is very easy to grow, especially in cool climates, and produces an abundance of elongated, banana-like fruit. When fully ripe, they ripen from yellow to red, revealing a tangy flavor.

Perfect for pickled, stuffed, raw or sauce

name: Hungarian Red
desc: This very vigorous variety produces ribbed fruit, bright red when ripe, with thick, juicy flesh and a very sweet flavor. Perfect for stuffing or preserving in oil after drying.

name: Hungarian Semi-Hot
desc:
This variety is adapted to a wide range of growing conditions. It produces banana-shaped fruits 15 cm long, which ripen from yellow to orange to red and are used for stuffing.
This ancient variety originated in Hungary.

name: Hyper Red Rumpled Waved
desc: This variety produces an apple with embossed, brightly colored leaves. They have a thick texture and very good flavor.

name: Ianto's Return
desc:
This magnificent variety of broad bean, specially selected for its adaptability to harsh winters during autumn sowing and for its gustatory qualities, offers pods filled with colorful beans ranging from purple to green, beige or black.

 

They c

name: Ice Queen
desc: This old-fashioned variety, adapted to heat and late crops, produces dark green, serrated leaves forming a tight head. It can be used as a cutting lettuce, or eaten raw or cooked.

name: Icelandic
desc: This variety produces an abundance of round, thick-skinned fruits weighing around 60g. Their flesh is soft and sweet.

name: Desmodium Illinois Desmodie
desc: This perennial species, over 1 m tall, has an elongated cluster of pink-tinged flowers on a slender stem. After pollination, they reveal attractive flat pods.

name: Imperial Star
desc:
This variety of leafy vegetable produces 7 to 8 heads 10 to 15 cm in diameter. Green with purple tints, they offer fine flesh and excellent flavor.

Imperial Star artichokes are vigorous growers.

What are the characteristics of the Imperial Star

name: Infrared
desc:
This variety, proposed by Frank Morton in Oregon, is the result of a cross between the "Pinot" and "Mascara" varieties. The apple is large. Its leaves are highly embossed, with colors ranging from bright reds to purples.

The phenotypic traits of the

name: Iowa Sweet
desc:
This rare variety can reach 3 m in height and is grown like corn. It is interesting for its ornamental appearance as well as for its juicy stalks, from which a sweet syrup is extracted, and its nutritionally rich seeds.

The leaf sheaths of Sorghum b

name: Iroquois
desc: This American variety, said to be the first to develop resistance to fusariosis, produces large, highly embroidered fruits with sweet orange flesh.

name: Island Gem Mix
desc: This high-contrast mix of small and medium-sized compact lettuces is the result of a cross between the Manoa and Leopard varieties, from which a number of lines can be selected. Offered by Franck Morton in Oregon, Island Gem Mix is a gene pool that o

name: seeds Issanka
desc:
This variety is very hardy and adapted to a wide range of growing conditions, offering excellent potential for oil production. The medium-sized black seeds are also ideal for feeding birds in winter!

The flower petals are an excellent addition to

name: Italian Sweet
desc: This old variety, not very sensitive to the tobacco mosaic virus, produces bright red fruit when ripe, with very fine skin and excellent flavor, suitable for grilling.

name: California poppies Ivory Castle
desc: This variety, with its cut, bluish-gray foliage, produces 30 to 40 cm stems and an abundance of flowers with delicate white petals.

name: Jalapeno
desc: This variety, native to Mexico, produces around 30 conical fruits, which ripen from green to red and offer thick, juicy flesh. Particularly suitable for smoking, it can also be eaten fresh in a wide range of preparations!

name: of Milan January King
desc:
This very old late variety produces large, dense apples with bluish-green, coarsely blistered leaves. They take on pinkish-purple hues when cold, revealing a sweet, delicate flavor.
The variety can withstand temperatures down to -15°C.

name: Millets Japanese
desc:
This highly ornamental variety produces plants up to 1.50 m tall, which can be cut before maturity to make bouquets. The seeds are used by the Japanese to make porridge and are easy to harvest.

It is also an excellent soil restorer, with a root sy

name: Perillas Japanese green / Ao Shiso
desc:
This 60 cm-high variety produces green leaves with a tender texture and a spicy flavor much appreciated in Asian cuisine.

What are the characteristics of green shiso?

Japanese green perilla, Perilla frutescens, also known as "green shiso" or "a

name: Japanese Hulless White
desc: This vigorous variety, 1.5 m tall, produces ears around 10 cm long with irregular rows of small, very soft white kernels. Once popped, they reveal a pure white popcorn with no hard core.

name: Japanese Oxheart
desc: This variety, with its finely cut foliage, produces an abundance of large, fleshy, dark-pink, smooth-skinned, heart-shaped fruits. Their firm flesh contains few seeds and has a sweet flavor.

name: Japanese Shishitou
desc: This variety produces an abundance of slender fruits that ripen from green to red, with a sweet flavor that becomes more pronounced at the end of the season.

name: Japanese wormwood
desc:
This perennial wormwood can grow to over a metre in height. Extremely medicinal, it is used in the composition of moxas in Chinese and Japanese medicine. Its leaves repel insects and are used in fumigation rituals.

Very popular in Japan - where th

name: Jarales
desc: This ancient variety produces fruits of various shapes and sizes, red in color when fully ripe.This variety originates from Jarales, New Mexico.

name: Jaune de Desme
desc: This variety has purple flowers and pods with 2 or 3 yellow seeds containing 16% lipids and 47% protein. It was entrusted by the Société Botanique de France to the USDA seed bank in 1950 (PI189886).

name: Jaune Paille des Vertus
desc:
This old, highly productive variety produces 8-10 cm diameter, copper-yellow bulbs with firm, thick husks. Its abundant foliage is dark green.

Bulbs keep for up to 8 months.

name: Jewel Box Mix
desc: This mix offers the finest lines from "Camo Oakheart" and "Camo Gem backgrounds". It offers lettuces around 20 cm tall with leaves in jewel-box colors: ruby, emerald, topaz...

name: Jewel Mix
desc: This dwarf, fast-growing variety produces flowers in shades of red, yellow and orange from early summer to autumn. Leaves, flower buds, flowers and seeds are edible.

name: Joe's Round
desc: This magnificent traditional variety produces an abundance of small, round, very strong fruits, light green then dark red when ripe, 2 cm in diameter.

name: Joelene Red Italian
desc: This variety produces red fruits, 18 to 20 cm long. They have excellent post-harvest shelf life and are ideal for grilling.

name: Jumbo
desc: This variety produces many firm, ribbed fruits, deep pink with green streaks. Their hard skin is not very sensitive to bursting. The juicy flesh has a sweet, slightly acidic flavor.

name: Millets Juosves
desc: This Lithuanian variety produces ears containing large seeds with orange-red pericardium. When fully ripe, the seeds are easy to harvest.

name: Jupiter
desc: This variety offers clusters of round, slightly flattened, "beef-flesh" fruits weighing 80 to 230 g.

name: Kabocha
desc:
Kabocha squash is a very old variety that produces smooth, oblong fruits weighing from 2 to 3 kg. Their dark green skin encloses a highly prized bright orange flesh, mealy and sweet, perfect for soups and gratins.

Characteristics of Kabocha squash

name: Pepo squash Kakai Hulless
desc: This bare-seeded variety (the seeds are eaten), of Japanese origin, offers a few green, yellow-striped, non-ribbed fruits weighing 2 to 3 kgs.

name: / Kales Kale Coalition
desc: Genetic pool of 17 varieties: "Hoj Amager Grunkohl" (Denmark), "Madeley" (UK), "Westphalian" (UK), "Westland Winter" (Germany), "Westländer Winter" (UK), "Asparagus Kale" (Ireland, UK), "1 000 Headed Lale" (Germany), "Roter Krauskohl" (Germany), "Alt

name: Kaleidoscope
desc: This 40 cm variety has orange, pink, yellow or bicolored flowers. Leaves, flower buds, flowers and seeds are edible.

name: Kaleidoscopic Jewel
desc: This hardy, vigorous variety produces clusters of 5 to 7 round, green fruits, striped indigo by the sun through the action of anthocyanins. They are rich in seeds and gel and have a mild flavor. Recently developed by Brad Gates in California, Kaleido

name: Kalman's Hungarian
desc: This 60 cm tall variety produces flattened, very ribbed red fruit with very thick, tasty flesh.

name: Kandil Dolma
desc: This variety, native to India, produces thick-fleshed, ribbed, 4-lobed fruits, ripening from green to red.

name: Karmazyn
desc: This compact variety produces green pods containing 4 to 5 pink seeds with a highly appreciated flavor.

name: Quinoas Kaslala
desc: This variety can reach 2 m in height and produces pink and orange panicles with an abundance of multicolored seeds.

name: Keystone Giant
desc: This variety has low susceptibility to tobacco mosaic virus and is adapted to a wide range of growing conditions. It has dense foliage and produces large 4-lobed fruits, ripening from dark green to dark red.

name: Pepo squash King Bush table
desc: This non-runner variety of acorn squash produces 4 to 8 fruits weighing 500 g to 1.5 kg, with a very hard skin. They keep for 3 to 6 months, and their orange, stringy flesh has a sweet, almond-like flavor.

name: Kono
desc: This very early variety, with its compact habit and ideal for pot cultivation, produces an abundance of mini dark purple eggplants, 6 to 10 cm in diameter. They contain delicious, tender, bitter-free white flesh.

name: Kral Russian
desc: This rare variety of round parsnip, originally from Russia, is adapted to all soils and climates, even the coldest. Easy to grow, its uniquely shaped roots provide tender, sweet flesh. Thought you knew parsnips? This one will surprise even the most s

name: Krasnoarmejscaja
desc: This purple-flowered variety, which reaches 50cm in height, produces small pods. Each pod contains 2 to 3 small gray-green seeds, averaging 47% protein and 17% oil.

name: Kurakake Daizu
desc: This variety, with its violet flowers, grows to 60 to 90 cm tall and produces medium-sized, yellow-green seeds, sometimes speckled with black and fairly flattened.

name: Kweik
desc: This very old and fairly rare variety produces dark green leaves forming a medium-sized, slow-rising apple.

name: Welsh onion Kyoto Kujo Negi
desc: This traditional Japanese variety, originally from the Kyoto region, offers tender green foliage and sweet-tasting white stems on plants 60 cm tall.

name: Cowpea Lablab purpureus
desc:
This highly decorative variety offers climbing plants with purple stems and veins. The pink flowers give way to short, broad, flat dark purple pods, with small black seeds and a white hilum.

Often grown as an ornamental plant, the young pods are n

name: / Kales Lacinato Rainbow
desc:
It's an evolving variety that adds a bit of color to "Palmetto". Its straight stem ends in long, blistered leaves with magnificent shades of green and violet on the veins.
Flowering often begins in the third year.

name: Trachymene Lacy Mix
desc: This species, from the Apiaceae family, produces architecturally impressive umbels in a mixture of blue, pink and white, on plants with delicately chiselled foliage. It is one of the most interesting flower species for dried flower bouquets.

name: Ladybird
desc: Also known as Cocinnamon Poppy, this poppy native to Turkey, Iran and the Caucasus has very large, bright red flowers, 8 cm in diameter, with a black spot at the bottom of each petal.

name: Sorrel Large de Belleville
desc: This variety - perennial, hardy and easy to grow - is very productive, with large, light-green leaves. It stays in place for several years. In cooking, its raw or cooked leaves add a tangy note.

name: of Milan Large Drumhead Savoy
desc:
This old variety produces a wide, thick, tight apple that is flattened at the top. The green outer leaves are broad, spreading and moderately blistered.
This cabbage is eaten in late autumn and early winter.

name: Large Leaf Thai
desc: This ancient variety produces purplish flowers and stems. The green leaves are slender and offer a flavor of anise and clove.

name: Perillas Large Leaved Korean
desc:
This variety produces light-green leaves on both sides, with a mild basil and mint flavor. It is very popular in Asian cuisine.

Find out more on the Kokopelli blog: "Qualités Médicinales des Perillas".

name: Laura
desc: This variety, adapted to cold climates, produces beautiful lettuces with a diameter of 30 to 35 cm and a good shelf life. The leaves are blond-green, slightly curled at the edges and crisp.

name: Laureen's Grape
desc: This dazzling variety produces lacy foliage and long, upright stems, around 1 m long, offering unique flowers with delicate dark purple petals and white centers.

name: Lee
desc:
This compact, productive, heat-demanding variety produces an abundance of dark-green fruit. Delicious raw or cooked, they can be harvested and eaten before they reach 8 cm in length.

 

Okra fruits contain a mucilaginous substance, useful for th

name: Legend
desc: This compact variety is particularly resistant to disease, especially mildew, and produces beautiful, round, bright-red fruits weighing 150 to 300 g. The flesh is sweet and mild.

name: Lemon Bush
desc: This compact variety produces clusters of 3 to 4 round, pale-yellow, almost white fruits, turning lemon-yellow at full ripeness, weighing 80 to 150g. Their juicy flesh reveals a sweet, slightly lemony flavor.

name: Lesya
desc:
