This extremely sweet bell pepper attracts with its original heart shape ending in a small point. Lesya offers an abundance of fruit, bright red when ripe, with thick, juicy flesh. Perfect in salads or grilled!

 

This bushy, compact variety can

name: Liatris Liatris aspera
desc: This perennial species grows up to 1.20 m tall and produces an abundance of dense spikes of mauve flowers from June to October.

name: Liatris Liatris cylindracea
desc: This perennial species grows to 60 cm in height and, from June to September, offers an abundance of pink-fuchsia flower spikes at the top of the stems.

name: Liatris Liatris ligulistylis
desc: This perennial species can grow up to 150 cm tall and, from June to September, produces an abundance of dense spikes of pink-fuchsia flowers at the top of the stems.

name: Liatris Liatris mucronata
desc: It is a perennial species reaching 80 cm in height. In late summer and autumn, it produces long, dense spikes of pink to violet flowers.

name: Liatris Liatris punctata
desc:
This perennial species can reach 1 m in height. In summer and early autumn, it produces dense spikes of mauve flowers.
Its bulbous root was sometimes eaten by Amerindians in times of food shortage.

name: Liatris Liatris pycnostachya
desc: This perennial species can reach 2 m in height. In summer and early autumn, it produces dense spikes of fuchsia-pink flowers.

name: Liatris Liatris scariosa
desc: This perennial species can reach 1.20 m in height. From August to September, it produces very long flower spikes with purple flowers 1 to 3 cm in diameter.

name: Liatris Liatris spicata
desc: This beautiful perennial species, otherwise known as "Kansas Feather", can grow up to 2 m tall. In its second year, summer and early autumn, it produces dense spikes of fuchsia-pink flowers. It is used as a medicinal plant by Native Americans.

name: Millets Lime Spray
desc: This highly ornamental variety produces long, cylindrical, compact, apple-green inflorescences. They can be cut before maturity to make bouquets. When fully ripe, the seeds are easy to harvest.

name: Quinoas Linares
desc:
This very old variety, ideal for growing in the mountains or on the plains, produces panicles of various colors that can reach 1.50 m in height. They contain medium-sized yellow seeds.

This very old variety, originally from Chile, is at the origin

name: Common Bean Lingot Suisse Blanc
desc: This old, hardy and productive variety has 15 to 16 cm-long pods containing 5 to 6 almost cylindrical white seeds. They can be eaten fresh, semi-dry or dry, and are used in the preparation of cassoulet.

name: Lioba
desc: This variety has voluminous foliage, green with bronze shading, with a slight peel and good eating qualities. It is particularly resistant to bolting and hibernation.

name: Lisa King
desc: This vigorous variety with well-developed foliage grows to no more than 1 m tall. It produces bunches of 6 to 8 red, round, slightly heart-shaped fruits, with smooth, thin skin and little tendency to burst. Firm flesh.

name: Little Finger
desc: This variety produces fine, 1 cm-thick roots with crunchy, sweet flesh. This variety is suited to heavy soils.

name: Livingston's Beauty
desc: This old variety, with very dense foliage, produces an abundance of clusters of 4 to 5 round fruits with few seeds and very good keeping qualities.

name: Livingston's Favorite
desc: This ancient variety produces an abundance of clusters of 3 to 4 round, smooth-skinned fruits that keep well once picked. Its dense flesh offers a well-balanced flavor that is much appreciated.

name: Lobelia Lobelia cardinalis
desc:
This hardy perennial can reach 1.20 m in height. It has slender, upright stems bearing clusters of tubular, red flowers. This decorative plant is often found in water gardens.

What are the characteristics of the Lobelia cardinalis plant?

Lobeli

name: Lobelia Lobelia inflata
desc: This species grows to 30-60 cm tall and produces slender, upright stems bearing clusters of elongated lavender flowers.

name: Lobelia Lobelia spicata
desc: This hardy perennial grows up to 75 cm tall and produces upright stems bearing elongated clusters of blue or white tubular flowers.

name: Lollo Di Vino
desc: This bremia-resistant variety produces "Lollo" type lettuces. Its round, reddish-purple leaves, with extremely curly edges, offer a crisp texture and good flavor.

name: Moschata squash Long Island Cheese
desc: The flattened, ribbed fruits of this old variety resemble a wheel of cheese. They weigh up to 5 kg and have an excellent shelf life. Their firm, sweet flesh is highly prized in pastries.

name: Long Keeper
desc: Picked just before frost, the small, round fruits of this variety can be stored for over three months at 15-20°C in the orchard. Their relatively acidic flesh makes them ideal for preserving.

name: Pepo squash Long Pie
desc: This very old variety offers fruits reaching 3 kg and 40 cm in length. Their green skin turns orange after several weeks of storage, and they contain sweet flesh, ideal for pastries.

name: Long Siphon
desc:
This variety produces club-shaped gourds with a very elongated neck. They can reach 90 cm in length.
The gourds dry in a dry, ventilated place and can be painted, carved or made into various objects.

name: Welsh onion Long White Tokyo
desc: This variety, which is not very sensitive to bolting, produces a single stem 40 to 70 cm high, with the appearance of a slender, mild-tasting leek.

name: Longue de Belgique
desc: This very old variety, originally from Belgium, produces numerous pods 20 to 30 cm long. They contain delicious light-green seeds that turn brown when ripe.

name: Sweet Pea Lord Nelson
desc: This climbing annual variety has highly fragrant navy-blue flowers.

name: Lower Salmon River
desc: This magnificent variety produces turban-shaped, salmon-pink fruits. They weigh 4 to 5 kg and have sweet flesh.

name: Lucid Gem
desc:
This vigorous, heat-adapted variety produces a large number of "beef-flesh" fruits in a beautiful shade of yellow to indigo-tinged red. Their thick flesh offers a sweet, fruity flavor.

The fruits contain few seeds and keep very well.

name: Lucullus
desc: This variety produces firm-textured white ribs topped with highly embossed blond foliage offering excellent flavor.

name: Lumina
desc: This variety offers 3 to 6 smooth, white, slightly ribbed fruits, up to 25 cm in diameter and weighing 3 to 8 kg. The skin is easy to peel, and the bright orange flesh is pleasant to carve. Fruits keep for 4 to 8 months.

name: Lumina
desc: This variety produces an abundance of elongated fruit, perfect for preserves and sauces.

name: Lutz / Winter Keeper
desc: This old variety, appreciated for its completely green foliage, has rounded, reddish-purple roots, about 15 cm wide and 25 cm long. They keep very well, with tender, sweet pink flesh veined with white.

name: seeds Lyng's California Greystripe
desc:
This variety reaches 2 m in height. It produces an upright, thick stem bearing a single flower 25 cm in diameter with long, golden-yellow petals and a domed, brown heart, offering an abundance of striped seeds.

The flower petals enhance salads, an

name: Ma-Zu Purple
desc: This Taiwanese variety produces an abundance of very elongated purple-purple fruits, 20 to 30 cm long and 4 to 5 cm in diameter. Their flesh has a tender texture.

name: Rudbeckia Macau
desc: This bushy perennial can reach 2 m in height. From June to September, it produces an abundance of yellow flowers with 6 to 12 petals surrounding a conical, rounded brown heart.

name: / Kales Madeley
desc:
This old variety produces beautiful, smooth, rounded leaves 1 m high. They have a tender texture and good flavor.
It's an old variety from England that seems close to the Chou Branchu du Poitou described in the Vilmorin-Andrieux book.

name: Magellan Burgess Purple
desc: This vigorous variety from the United States produces numerous large, dark pink, "beef-flesh" type fruits, flattened and ribbed with very firm flesh.

name: Lamb's Quarters and Company Magenta Spreen
desc: Giant Chenopod Magenta Spreen produces pretty, soft-green leaves with a bright pink center, offering a tender texture and good flavor. They have a high mineral content and can be eaten like spinach, both raw and cooked.

name: Quinoas Magenta Sunset
desc:
This variety, the fruit of Frank Morton's breeding work aimed at developing strong stems, good productivity and resistance to panicle sprouting during wet autumn periods, is descended from the Brightest Brilliant Rainbow variety.

It has also been se

name: Magic Lineup
desc:
This variety offers clusters of 6 to 10 red fruits weighing 30 to 50 g.
It's a brand-new obtention from Tom Wagner.

name: Magnat
desc: This variety, originally from Russia and very well adapted to the cold, was introduced to Creuse around 1760. Its huge apples can weigh up to 15 kg and can be prepared as sauerkraut or stored in silos.

name: Magnus Superior
desc: This variety, selected for its hardiness, offers very large purple-pink flowers perched on sturdy stems almost 1m high!

name: Mallow Malva sylvestris
desc: This perennial has often spreading stems, from 30 to 60 cm long, and dark green, cut, cordate foliage. It produces magnificent pink-purple flowers with darker veining on the petals.

name: Mallow Malva verticillata
desc: This East Asian species has large, dark-green, tender, wavy leaves with a lamb's lettuce taste. Shoots and young leaves can be eaten fresh, in salads, or cooked, like spinach.

name: Mammoth
desc: This variety produces very large leaves, up to the size of a hand, lightly embossed and green to yellow in color. They give off a very strong fragrance.

name: seeds Mammoth
desc:
This ancient Russian variety produces a thick, straight stem 3 to 4 m high. It bears a single inflorescence, 40 to 55 cm in diameter, with golden petals. It produces a multitude of delicious seeds.

The flower petals enhance salads, and the seeds a

name: Mango
desc: This rare French variety offers, until late in the season, bunches of 3 round, slightly ribbed fruits, a beautiful intense orange when ripe, weighing from 100 to 180 g. Their juicy, sweet flesh is reminiscent of mango.

name: Manitoba
desc: This variety, adapted to cool climates, produces smooth, bright-red fruit weighing around 150g. It needs no staking.

name: Marconi Purple
desc:
This rare purple Marconi variety offers elongated, conical peppers, about 15 cm long, with thin, deliciously flavorful flesh. Perfect fresh in salads, fried or sautéed.
These fruits ripen from green to dark purple or red, passing through shades of ye

name: Marenka
desc:
This variety produces highly tormented green gourds with prominent scale or fin shapes.
The gourds dry in a dry, ventilated place and can be painted, sculpted or transformed into various objects.

name: Common Bean Marfax
desc: This ancient variety produces an abundance of pods containing small, round, brown seeds. They are ideal for soups and salads.

name: Maria Nagy's Transylvanian Red
desc: This old Transylvanian variety produces beautiful, round, red onions 7 to 10 cm in diameter. They keep for up to 8 months.

name: Maria Nagy's Transylvanian Yellow
desc: This Transylvanian variety produces large, round, yellow bulbs, 8 to 10 cm in diameter, similar to their red counterparts. Appreciated for their slightly spicy flavor, they can be stored for several months.

name: Marina Di Chioggia
desc:
Marina Di Chioggia squash is an ancient variety with round, slightly flattened, embroidered to warty fruits. They can reach 35 cm in diameter and weigh from 4 to 8 kg. The dark green or bronze-green skin encloses firm, sweet, orange-red flesh.

Des

name: Marmande Garnier
desc: This wide-growing variety produces very large, fleshy, juicy red "beef-flesh" fruits with a balanced, fruity, slightly acidic flavor.

name: Marona
desc: This early, productive variety produces an abundance of green, threadless pods, 17 to 18 cm long, with excellent eating qualities. At the end of the season, they contain beige seeds.

name: Chrysanthemum Marsh Chrysanthemum
desc: This compact mini-daisy, around 30 cm tall, offers a profusion of pretty flowers with white petals surrounding a yellow heart. This undemanding plant is ideal for pots and window boxes.

name: Mascara
desc: This lettuce produces oak-leaf-type leaves, purplish-red in color and very wavy and curly. Excellent flavor. The variety is not very sensitive to bolting and is highly resistant to cold and heat.

name: Matchless
desc: This old variety, with its thick, hardy foliage, produces slightly flattened, orange-red fruits. They weigh around 200 g and have dense flesh.

name: Mayan Jaguar
desc: This variety produces a compact apple with a pale pink core and dark green leaves dotted with red. They have a very crunchy texture and good flavor.

name: Mayo Warty Bule
desc:
This beautiful variety produces large pear-shaped fruits with light-green, warty skin only on the rounder part.
The gourds dry in a dry, ventilated place and can be painted, sculpted or made into various objects.

name: Medford
desc: This vigorous variety with abundant foliage produces "beef-flesh" type fruit, with a bright red color and fairly thin skin.

name: Mixes Mediterranean turtles
desc:
The result of a collaboration with the Station d'observation et de protection des tortues, this incredible blend offers no less than thirty species and varieties ideal for providing a healthy and varied diet for all land tortoises.

It includes :

name: Mennonite German Gold
desc: This old, productive variety produces pineapple-like fruits weighing over 450 g, with a bright-red, orange-yellow skin. They contain few seeds and the multi-colored flesh is juicy and flavorful.

name: Meronda
desc: This early, productive variety has been selected by Sativa. It produces pear-shaped, deep-purple fruits with glossy skin, reaching 16 to 18 cm in length and 10 to 12 cm in diameter.

name: Mescher
desc:
This very old variety, adapted to cold climates, produces thick, broadly blistered green leaves with red and brown shading, forming a dense, crunchy apple.

Originally from Austria, it dates from the 1700s.

name: Mescluns Kokopelli Mesclun Late Summer and Autumn
desc:
A Rainbow of Colors and Flavors for late summer and fall raw salads:
- Mixture of 5 winter lettuces: Passion Blonde, Chicon de Vendée, Rougette de Montpellier, Triolaise and Hyper Red Rumpled Waved.
- Mixture of 4 chicories: Rouge de Vérone, Catalogn

name: Mexican
desc: This very old, vigorous variety produces many dense, chili-tomato-like fruits with few seeds and a well-balanced flavor. It tolerates heat and drought very well.

name: Common Bean Midnight Black Turtle
desc: This ancient variety, adapted to regions with rainy autumns, offers an abundance of small black beans to be eaten semi-dry or dry. Very popular in Latin America, they are perfect for making soup.

name: Midway
desc: This early variety produces large, thick-fleshed fruits with 3 or 4 lobes. They are 11 cm long by 11 cm wide and ripen from bright green to red. It is adapted to cool climates and resistant to tobacco mosaic virus.

name: California poppies Mikado Red
desc: Set against bluish-gray foliage, this variety boasts a myriad of silky-petaled, deep-orange-red flowers.

name: Asclepias Milkmaid
desc: This variety, with its elongated, gray-green foliage, produces numerous rounded umbels all summer long, with a hundred or so pure-white flowers that are particularly melliferous.

name: Mimulus Mimulus guttatus
desc:
This low-growing species has an abundance of tubular yellow flowers spotted with red, reminiscent in some quarters of a monkey's head.
Mimule is also known as Monkey Flower.

name: Mini Bottle
desc: This variety produces light-green, bottle-shaped fruit. The gourds dry in a dry, ventilated place and can be painted, sculpted or turned into various objects.

name: Miniature Hubbard Vert
desc: This ancient variety produces rounded, "Hubbard"-type fruits up to 25 cm long and weighing up to 2 kg. The smooth, bronze-green skin encloses thick, dry, yellow-orange flesh with a fine, delicious texture.

name: Miniature Red Bell
desc: This variety has a compact habit and produces up to 75 fruits forming 2 or 3 lobes and ripening from green to red when fully ripe. They have thick flesh and are ideal for stuffing.

name: Sweet Pea Miss Wilmott
desc: This climbing annual variety offers magnificent pink flowers with orange hues and a delicate fragrance along trellises.

name: Cotton Mississippi Brown
desc: This variety, ideal for a family garden and very productive in fiber, offers brilliant brown cotton bales. Very hardy and drought-resistant, Mississippi Brown will adapt to a wide range of growing conditions.

name: Mix
desc:
Mixed Lettuces for cutting:
"Merlot", "Radichetta/Cressonnette du Maroc", "Really Red Deer Tongue", "Salad Red Bowl Green" and "Oreilles du Diable".

name: Mix of Colours
desc:
A blend of two mild peppers:
"Corno di Toro Rouge" and "Corno di Toro Jaune".
These varieties produce an abundance of pointed, horn-shaped fruits. They offer juicy flesh and are perfect for stuffing.

name: Mix of Colours
desc: This mix of nasturtium varieties will bring a great diversity of colors to your vegetable garden! Nasturtium flowers can be eaten and offer a pleasant peppery flavor in mixed salads.

name: Cotton Mix of Colours
desc:
Cotton blend with white, green, dark red or brown fibers and white or pink flowers. This blend, produced in France by Kokopelli growers, is the result of field and greenhouse tests. We cannot guarantee the presence of every variety in the bag.

Erlen

name: Mix with large fruits
desc:
This blend offers an incredible wealth of colors and flavors to delight the eyes and taste buds.

"Marmande", "Des Andes", "Caro Rich", "Cœur de Boeuf Orange", "Brandywine Pink", "Rose de Berne", "Noire de Crimée", "Cherokee Purple", "Ananas Vert",

name: Mixed colors
desc:
This dazzling blend of chard ribs offers an explosion of color with a selection of 7 varieties with white, green, yellow, gold, pink, red and orange chards.

It contains the following varieties:

- Chard rib "Vertes à Larges Cardes" ;

- Côte d

name: Mixed Colours
desc: This climbing variety has wide-petaled orange to red flowers and characteristic, rounded leaves with broad lobes. Leaves, flower buds, flowers and seeds are edible.

name: Gazania Mixed Colours
desc: This mix offers 25 cm-high plants bearing flowers in various colors of bronze, orange, pink and yellow.

name: Honey Plant Blends Mixture of Annual Flowers Melliferous Varieties
desc:
This magnificent, multi-colored mixture - created on a base of phacelia, red clover and sweet clover - offers long, varied flowering to the delight of pollinating insects.

It covers an area of around 40 m2 and is made up of 27 melliferous species

name: Miyashige
desc: This variety has white cylindrical roots with green highlights at the collar. It is highly prized in Japan for its beautiful proportions, while remaining tender and crunchy.

name: Miyashige Daïkon
desc: This variety produces stubby, cylindrical roots up to 35 cm long, white underground and green at the collar. Their dense, crunchy flesh has a pungent flavor.

name: Moldovenesti Buffalo
desc: This variety from Romania produces white beans on plants up to 2.50 m tall. The flowers are white.

name: seeds Mongolian Giant
desc:
This variety produces an upright, thick stem bearing a single yellow flower 40 to 50 cm in diameter with an abundance of black and white seeds 2 to 3 cm in size.

The flower petals enhance salads, and the seeds are a favorite with birds.

name: Monstrueuse de New York
desc: This vigorous, late-ripening, compact old variety produces very large, rounded, dark violet-glazed fruits. They can reach up to 2 kg, with delicious, firm flesh and few seeds.

name: Common Bean Montezuma Red
desc:
This ancient variety is adapted to very poor soils and produces small red grains, perfect for making chili.
This variety originated in the Mayan culture of Chiapas, Mexico.

name: Moonlight
desc: This variety of nasturtium has creamy-yellow flowers. Leaves, flower buds, flowers and seeds are edible.

name: Mooregold
desc: This variety produces a large number of very tasty, drum-shaped fruits weighing from 1 to 2.5 kg, like those of the "Buttercup" squash. They can be eaten before they are fully ripe and have a very long shelf life.

name: Morton's Secret Mix 16
desc: The 2016 Mixture of the Year is a selection of lettuces resembling blood-red lotuses. Features include "Falstaff", "Hawaiian" and "Deer Tongue". Hawaiian" and "Deer Tongue". If it's hard to imagine, it's because the composition is kept secret for add

name: Moscatello
desc: This old Italian variety produces beautiful, spherical, green to straw-yellow, very slightly ribbed fruits weighing 2 to 3 kg. Their pale yellow to light green flesh reveals an excellent, highly aromatic sweetness.

name: Mountain Princess
desc: This extremely vigorous variety, perfectly adapted to cool climates and short seasons, produces an abundance of round, smooth fruit, weighing 200 to 250 g. Their dense flesh reveals a well-balanced flavor. Their dense flesh reveals a well-balanced fl

name: Mountain Savory
desc:
This perennial species prefers chalky, well-drained soil that's not too rich. Its leaves are more aromatic than those of common savory, and are used as a condiment in many dishes to aid digestion.

Its scent repels aphids and mosquitoes.

name: Mountain Sweet Yellow
desc: This very old variety produces long fruits weighing from 7 to 10 kg. Their dark yellow flesh has a juicy texture and a sweet flavor. Seeds are black.

name: Cotton Mrc 270
desc: This fairly productive variety produces bales of pure white cotton with medium-length fibers weighing 4 to 5 grams each. The flowers are yellow, turning pink when pollinated by insects!

name: Mrihani
desc: This 30 cm-high variety produces purple flowers and green, waffled, sharply-cut leaves with a delicious aniseed fragrance similar to that of Thai basil.

name: Sweet Pea Mrs. Collier
desc: This variety, appreciated for its beautiful clusters of highly fragrant white flowers, can climb up to 3 m in height.

name: Cotton Msb Brown
desc: This productive variety produces brown cotton bales weighing 5 to 6 grams each. The flowers are yellow, then turn pink when pollinated by insects!

name: Mulato Isleno
desc: This vigorous variety offers heart-shaped "Poblano" fruit that ripen from dark green to chocolate brown, with a smoky, aniseed flavour.

name: Lentil Multicolored
desc: Lentil is an annual legume with slender, highly branched stems, forming an upright clump 20 to 40 cm high. The small white or pale-blue flowers bloom from May to August. This variety comes in a range of colors.

name: Mushroom Red
desc: This variety is adapted to difficult growing conditions and produces an abundance of mushroom-shaped fruit hanging upside down, ripening from green to red and offering a fruity flavor.

name: Mustard Habanero
desc: This "Habanero" variety has ribbed fruits that ripen from green to mustard yellow - hence the name - to bright orange when fully ripe. Very ornamental, it bears all three colors at the same time.

name: Nambe Supreme
desc: This ancient variety produces conical fruit with a thin skin that ripens from green to red.

name: Napoletano
desc:
On fairly tall plants, this ancient variety produces very large green leaves, 10 to 15 cm long, relatively puckered with deeply marked veins, revealing an aniseed flavor.

It is a traditional variety in southern Italy.

name: Napoli Ischia
desc: This old variety has very large, flattened, beef-fleshed fruits, pinkish-red in color and weighing from 500 to 900 g.

name: / Kales Nash Red Kale
desc: This variety is characterized by 1.20-metre-high plants producing an abundance of reddish-purple leaves throughout the winter. It is an extremely cold-hardy variety, with a certain level of resistance to cabbage mildew.

name: Negro de Valle
desc: This variety produces an abundance of elongated fruits that ripen from green to chocolate-brown and offer thick flesh.

name: New Mama Super Sweet
desc:
This recent variety, one of the first open-pollinated super-sweet corn, produces multiple yellow ears of extremely sweet kernels early in the season. They retain their flavor after harvest.

 

It was selected from crosses between Tim Peters' sup

name: New-Mex Conquistador
desc: This variety produces an abundance of elongated fruits that ripen, and can be picked, from green to red. Their thick flesh is particularly tasty.

name: New-Mex Mirasol
desc: Plants of this variety are compact, no taller than 60 cm, and produce clusters of pointed, upright fruit that ripen from green to red.

name: New-Mexico Joe Parker
desc: This vigorous variety offers an abundance of elongated, pointed, smooth fruits that ripen from green to red, with thick, crunchy flesh that delivers a sweet flavor.

name: Newburg
desc:
This variety produces bronze-colored bulbs with excellent keeping qualities. Their juicy flesh offers a rich, medium-strong flavor.
Bulbs keep for up to 8 months.

name: Snapdragon Night And Dawn
desc: This variety, grown as an annual in our latitudes, is a variation of "Night and Day". The wavy-lipped flowers, contrasting red and white, form long spikes. The heart of the throat is yellow.

name: Snapdragon Night And Day
desc:
From June to October, this variety produces dense spikes 20 to 25 cm long, made up of lion's snout-shaped, bicolored flowers whose deep red and white colors contrast magnificently.

 

The snapdragon is a gelatinous perennial of Mediterranean ori

name: Noon Ta Klong Pea
desc: This variety, essential in Thai cuisine, offers a myriad of clusters of small, round, pea-sized fruits. When bright green and immature, they enhance curries and sauces.

name: / Kales North Star Polaris
desc: This vigorous variety can be harvested all winter long. Cold-hardy, it has large, gray-green leaves with white veins and a tender texture. Some variations remain within the variety.

name: Nosegay
desc: This highly ornamental variety with laurel foliage is suitable for pot cultivation and produces plants 15 to 20 cm high. The small, round, rainbow-colored fruits appear in clusters and resemble berries.

name: Numex Barkers Hot
desc: This vigorous variety produces abundant clusters of elongated fruit.

name: Numex Centennial
desc: This highly ornamental variety has violet foliage and flowers. It produces clusters of upright, pointed fruits, 2 to 3 cm long, which ripen from violet to creamy-yellow to light red.

name: Numex Cinco de Mayo
desc: This highly ornamental variety, created by the University of New Mexico, produces elongated fruits that ripen from yellow to red.

name: Numex Espagnola
desc: This variety is adapted to cool climates and short seasons, and produces up to 20 narrow, pointed fruits that ripen from green to red. They have a thin skin and firm flesh.

name: Numex Halloween
desc: This highly ornamental dwarf variety produces small, upright fruits that turn from black to orange when fully ripe.

name: Numex Jalmundo
desc:
This variety produces "Jalapeno"-type fruits, red when ripe, 9.5 cm long and 3.5 cm wide. It is twice as productive as the "Early Jalapeno" variety.
This is a recent variety introduced in 2010 by the Chile Pepper Institute in New Mexico. It is the re

name: Numex Sunset
desc: This highly productive variety offers up to 12 elongated, smooth, pointed fruits. They ripen from green to orange and are excellent for grilling. Very good flavor.

name: Numex Thanksgiving
desc: A very ornamental dwarf variety, it produces small, upright fruits. They turn from ivory-white to orange when fully ripe.

name: Oak Leaf
desc: This very old variety, slow to bolt, produces deeply cut, light-green leaves. They have a tender texture and mild flavor.

name: Oaky Red Splash
desc: This "oak-leaf" variety, close to the "Antarès" variety, has bronze leaves irregularly spotted with red. The ribs are often pink. Erect, it can also be very voluminous when the soil is fertile.

name: cockle Ocean Pearl
desc: This annual variety with slender, branched stems bears 5-petaled flowers of superb pure white.

name: Oenothera Oenothera elata
desc: This California native has very large, fragrant yellow flowers at the top of stems up to 1.80 m tall.

name: Oenothera Oenothera macrocarpa
desc: This hardy perennial reaches 30 cm in height and produces an abundance of stems bearing a solitary, cup-shaped flower, lemon-yellow punctuated with red, blooming during the day.

name: Oenothera Oenothera pilosella
desc: This perennial species, 15 to 20 cm high, spreads out to form a dense carpet with shallow roots. In summer, the stems bear an abundance of highly fragrant yellow flowers. Ideal for flowerbeds!

name: Comfrey Officinalis
desc: This species, common in undergrowth, produces small clusters of white, pink or purplish flowers in spring. Known since antiquity for its medicinal properties and garden applications, it is particularly popular with gardeners who love fermented extrac

name: / Kales Old Growth Palm
desc: This is a Lacinato/Palmier selection designed to delay flowering during the summer and promote resistance to drought and aphids - for more abundant leaf production.

name: Sweet Pea Old Spice Mix
desc: This very heat-insensitive variety climbs to 1.20 m in height and produces an abundance of small, fragrant flowers in creamy-white, purple, pink and violet.

name: Omalee
desc: This variety produces plum-shaped, sometimes slightly zebra-shaped fruits with fragrant flesh. They come in a wide variety of colors, shapes and flavors.

name: Onavas Red
desc:
Plants of this ancient Mexican variety can grow up to 3 m tall, with an abundance of panicles rich in purple-red seeds.

The anthocyanin-rich sheaths of Sorghum bicolor leaves are used as a natural red dye. This use is widespread in Africa.

name: Peas Opal Creek
desc:
This highly productive, hardy, early variety produces elegant, attractive lemon-yellow pods. Tender and sweet, they can be eaten raw or cooked. The plant's leaves and young shoots (gourmands) are also edible!

 

Developed by Alan Kapuler of Peac

name: Opalescent
desc:
This variety, a cross between "Mrihani" and "Opal", produces extraordinary plants with large, deeply serrated, dark purple leaves and lavender-pink inflorescences. It reveals a rare fruity flavor.

 

Developed by Frank Morton, he says: "Similar

name: Arroch Opera
desc: This ancient variety, adapted to a wide range of growing conditions, has tender, very broad, intensely red leaves. They remain dark when cooked, and can be eaten raw or cooked like spinach.

name: Orange
desc: This variety produces bright orange ribs that contrast beautifully with its intense green leaves. They offer a tender texture and good flavor.

name: Orange Bell
desc: This variety offers fruits that ripen from green to orange when fully ripe, with thick flesh and excellent flavor.

name: Orange Gleam
desc: This climbing variety offers delicately scented, bright-orange, semi-double flowers. Leaves, flower buds, flowers and seeds are edible.

name: Orange Grape Tress
desc:
This variety produces clusters of 20 to 30 tiny orange fruits, 1 to 1.5 cm in diameter.

It is a creation of Mushroom in Oregon. It is related to the "Centiflor Hypertress" variety.

name: Orange Walk
desc: This variety produces large, elongated fruit with an orange skin. This is a recent variety - developed by Tom Wagner - which can display a wide range of colors, shapes and flavors.

name: Orchid Cream
desc: This compact nasturtium grows to no more than 30 cm tall and offers a profusion of edible, nectar-bearing flowers with creamy yellow petals splashed with deep red spots.

name: Peas Oregon Hyper Tendril
desc:
The plants are 1 meter high. This is a butter pea. The flowers are purple and the tendrils are highly developed. The pods are entirely purple or marbled with purple.
This is a rare variety developed by Mushroom in Oregon. We advise gardeners to repro

name: Quinoas Oro de Valle
desc: This compact variety produces fairly large, golden-yellow panicles, reducing the risk of mold during the wet harvest season. It offers an abundance of golden and brown grains.

name: Ortega
desc: This vigorous variety produces 10 to 15 "New-Mexico" peppers, 15 cm long, pointed and slightly flattened, perfect for grilling. They ripen from green to red.

name: Outredgeous Selections
desc: This variety, developed by Franck Morton from his selections of the most pigmented lettuces, offers slightly wavy, crisp leaves with extremely red hues.

name: Oxheart
desc: This old variety is remarkable for its rapid development and the size it reaches. It produces orange-red roots, often as wide as they are long.

name: Oxheart. Sweet Heart
desc:
This heart of beef variety offers beautiful, fleshy, heart-shaped fruits weighing 200 to 500 g. They have a well-balanced, sweet, mild flavor.

 

Created by Brad Gates at Wild Boar Farms, USA.

name: Sweet Pea Painted Lady
desc: This variety offers highly fragrant white and pink flowers in clusters from summer to early autumn. It can climb to heights of up to 3 m!

name: Salpiglossis Painted Tongue Royal Mix
desc: This variety, native to the Andes, has a multitude of large, flared trumpet-shaped flowers. Their silky, velvety petals display a wide range of vibrant colors.

name: Paiute
desc: This old American variety, with its red-colored stems, sometimes exceeds 3 m in height and produces multicolored spikes with a predominance of white and blue berries streaked with red.

name: Chinese cabbage Pak Choi
desc: This variety, around 50 cm tall, produces smooth green leaves with a long, fleshy white stalk reminiscent of a condensed chard rib.

name: Pale Purple Echinacea
desc:
This highly medicinal perennial has slender, upright stems from June to July, bearing a flower with pale pink to purple petals 4 to 8 cm long, curving towards the ground.

 

This species is a major player in Native American pharmacopoeia.

name: Curly endive Palla Rossa
desc:
This variety produces a beautiful, compact "Chiogga"-type apple 10 to 12 cm in diameter. The round, red leaves with white veins offer a crunchy texture and good, rather bitter flavor.

This variety originates from Italy.

name: Pansy Ap
desc:
This variety offers medium-sized, yellow fruits that turn indigo in the sun thanks to their anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants. The flesh is yellow. Fruits can vary widely in color, shape and flavor.
This recent variety - developed by Tom Wagner - w

name: Pantano Romanesco
desc: This old, very vigorous variety, comparable to Marmande, produces deep red, beefsteak-like fruit with dense, juicy flesh. They have a mild, sweet flavor with a hint of acidity.

name: Papaver miyabeanum
desc: The solitary flowers of this variety are pale yellow and 2 to 3 cm in diameter. Plants do not exceed 20 cm in height, and the gray-green foliage forms a compact clump of basal leaves.

name: Papaver pseudocanescens
desc: This very drought-resistant variety produces slender, upright stems 20 cm high bearing a single sulphur-yellow flower.

name: Papaver triniifolium
desc: This perennial species, native to the Caucasus, has magnificent gray-green foliage and single, pale orange flowers. Plants are 40 cm high.

name: Paris Market
desc:
This ancient variety produces small, round roots with sparse foliage. Their orange flesh offers a tender texture and sweet flavor.

Characteristics of the Paris Round Carrot

The Paris Market Round Carrot, Daucus carota, is an early variety of ro

name: Parma gilt
desc: This late variety, originally from Italy, has large, copper-colored bulbs with soft, white flesh. They can be eaten both raw and cooked, and have an excellent shelf life.

name: Patagonia
desc: This variety produces upright, conical fruits about 3 cm long, which ripen from yellow mottled with purple to orange to red.

name: Sorrel Patience
desc: This perennial plant offers a generous tuft of long, oval leaves. Less acidic than sorrel, their flavor is similar to that of spinach, and they can be eaten raw or cooked.

name: Pelandron
desc: This very old, early variety produces large, green pods marbled with purple, very tender, fleshy and threadless. Originally from Italy, it was once widely cultivated in the south of France.

name: Penis Shield
desc: This variety produces very elongated gourds used as penis cases in New Guinea.The gourds dry in a dry, ventilated place and can be painted, carved or made into various objects.

name: Moschata squash Pennsylvania Dutch
desc: This very old variety produces butternut-like fruits weighing 5 to 10 kg with long, often curved necks. Their dark orange flesh has a very pleasant flavor. They keep for up to 10 months.

name: Peppermint
desc:
This variety has large, dark-green leaves with white veins, appreciated for their sweet taste. The crisp, bright-pink ribs retain their color when cooked.

This chard can be eaten at any stage of growth, with a more pronounced taste at maturity.

name: Pepperoncini
desc: This vigorous, compact variety offers an abundance of slightly conical "Cuban"-type fruits, which ripen from green-yellow to red. They are suitable for drying and have a sweet flavor.

name: Cutting Celery PerCel
desc: This particularly fragrant biennial variety produces fine, brittle stems and curly, parsley-like foliage. It can be used in a thousand and one ways to enhance any dish.

name: Persuasion
desc:
This variety offers fruits weighing from 60 to 120 g, indigo in color with shades of pink, green and gold. They have very good shelf life on the plants and after harvest.

Developed in 2016 by Brad Gates of Wild Boar Farms in California, "Persuasio

name: Peter Pepper
desc: This unique pepper, with its compact habit, produces a multitude of 5 to 10 cm-long fruits in the bold shape of a penis. They ripen from green to red and are perfect for making a tasty dried chili powder.

name: Petits Becs
desc: This rare variety produces very small, spinning top-shaped fruits. They ripen from pale green to red and their flesh offers a fruity flavor with a slightly spicy first note.

name: Mixes Phacelia/Melilot Mixed
desc:
Phacelia is one of the most honey-producing plants. It blooms for several months with magnificent lavender-blue flowers.
Sweet clover also attracts insects with its fragrant, drooping yellow flowers.

name: Philosopher's Mix
desc:
This mix offers a selection of romaine lettuces: "Outredgeous", "Plato", "Olga", "Really Red Deer Tongue", "Flashy Romainesque"...
These are the varietal creations of Frank Morton of Wild Garden Seeds in Oregon.

name: Pico de Gallo
desc: This ancient variety has very narrow leaves and produces elongated fruits, 6 to 8 cm long, which ripen from green to red and have thick flesh.

name: Pigeon heart
desc: This variety, native to France, produces numerous clusters of small, golden-yellow, egg-shaped fruits with juicy, sweet flesh. Very good productivity until frost.

name: Ping Tung
desc:
This old-fashioned variety produces fifteen or so slender, delicately curved purple fruits, up to 30 cm long.

This vigorous-growing variety adapts to a wide range of growing conditions.

name: Nepeta Pink Cat
desc:
This herbaceous perennial forms compact clumps of dark-green foliage and, in midsummer, clusters of stems with large, deep-pink flowers.
It adapts well to drought.

name: Pink Lady
desc: This vigorous, high-yielding variety regularly produces magnificent, slender, dark-pink fruit, up to 30 cm long, right through to autumn. Their very tender flesh reveals an exquisite flavor.

name: Pink Lipstick / Pink Passion
desc: This variety produces small, flamboyant pink ribs with dark green leaves.

name: Oenothera Pink Petticoats
desc: This highly ornamental variety can reach 50 cm in height and bears pink-mauve flowers with yellow centers.

name: Pink Ping Pong
desc: This vigorous variety from the USA produces an abundance of ping-pong ball-sized, pink fruit with a yellow collar, and juicy flesh with a subtle, very sweet flavor.

name: Plantains Plantain
desc: This herbaceous plant produces slender leaves divided like deer antlers. They become more tender after the first frosts and offer a fine, pleasant flavor. They are eaten raw in salads.

name: Moschata squash Pleine de Naples
desc: The Pleine de Naples squash is an ancient variety with fruits reminiscent of a violin case. They can reach 80 cm in length and weigh over 10 kg. The dark-green, striped skin turns ochre when ripe and encloses excellent, musky, sweet orange flesh.

name: Polar Beauty
desc: This dwarf variety from Alaska is adapted to cool climates and pot culture. It produces small, seed-rich red fruits with juicy, sweet flesh.

name: Polish
desc: This very old variety, resistant to bolting, produces a large, tender green apple with crisp, waffle-shaped leaves.

name: Ponderosa Pink
desc: This highly productive old variety is adapted to humid regions. It produces large, irregularly-shaped fruit with soft, firm, dense flesh containing few seeds. They have little resistance to shattering.

name: Prairie Fire
desc: This dwarf, vigorous variety, native to Montana in the United States, produces orange-red fruit with dense, juicy flesh and a pleasant fruity flavor.

name: Precheur's toe
desc:
This ancient bean, sometimes called perpetual bean because of its hardiness, produces very large white beans. Its young pods can be prepared like green beans, and its beans can be eaten dry or semi-dry.
The Orteil du Prêcheur runner bean is a hardy v

name: Précoce de Quimper
desc: Precoce de Quimper, adapted to cool regions or those with a short season, offers small, round, red fruits weighing 40 to 80 g. Their flesh is juicy and slightly acidic.

name: Prevoskodnyj
desc: This Russian variety, adapted to cool climates and short seasons, produces an abundance of clusters of 4 to 5 round, red fruits. They have a very tough skin and a tangy flavor.

name: Sweet Pea Prima Donna
desc: This old variety, bred by Henry Eckford in 1896, gave rise to the "Spencer" line of sweet peas in the 1900s. It can reach heights of over 2 m, with three pink, highly fragrant flowers per stem.

name: Primadonna Deep Rose
desc: This variety has been selected for its robustness, its tall height (around 1 metre) and its very large, purple-pink flowers, 12 to 15 cm in diameter.

name: Princesse Noire de Belgique
desc:
This old variety produces stringless, green pods with a firm texture and black seeds.

This variety adapts to a wide range of growing conditions, particularly autumn crops.

name: Principe Borghese
desc: This variety grows to less than 1 m tall and produces hundreds of clusters of firm, plum-shaped fruit. They contain little juice or seeds and are ideal for drying or preserving.

name: Proboscidea Proboscidea parviflora
desc:
This rather low-growing species with sticky foliage has beautiful, fragrant yellow or pink flowers followed by highly decorative claw-shaped capsules 10 to 15 cm long.

When young, they can be eaten raw, cooked or candied in vinegar.

Also known

name: Provence Green
desc: The Green Artichoke of Provence, an old French variety, has many small, elongated green heads that are particularly prized when eaten raw, as a pepper dish, when immature.

name: Provence Violet
desc: The artichoke Violet de Provence is a traditional variety from the south of France, early and very productive. Its heads are small, elongated and pointed, of a magnificent violet color, fleshy and tender. When young, these artichokes weigh between 80

name: Pruden's Purple
desc: This very old, vigorous variety, with potato-like foliage, produces flattened, ribbed, shatter-resistant fruits weighing 450 to 600 g. Perfect for salads, their firm, very fragrant flesh contains few seeds.

name: Mixed grain crop Pulses Meslin Spelt
desc:
Mixture of large spelt, peasant wheat, field peas, hairy vetch and several messicolous plants such as Nielle des blés and blueberry.

Light green, sparse and no taller than a metre, it leaves plenty of room for diversity. It's the ideal plant to le

name: Arroch Purple
desc: This old, hardy variety is vigorous and heat-resistant, with magnificent mauve, pointed leaves. They can be eaten raw, in salads, or cooked like spinach.

name: Purple Dark Opal
desc:
This particularly decorative variety features large, pointed leaves in changing colors from brilliant purple to dark green, as well as mauve blossoms.

It was developed by John Scarchuk and Joseph Lent at the University of Connecticut in the 1950s

name: Purple Dragon
desc:
This dark-green variety produces heartless, stubby, conical roots with purple skin. Their firm, light yellow to dark orange flesh is particularly rich in vitamins and antioxidants, and has a sweet taste.

This variety is recommended for cool-climat

name: Millets Purple Majesty
desc:
This magnificent purple variety produces long, 30 cm spikes. They can be used before maturity to make bouquets. When fully mature, the seeds are easy to harvest.

It is an excellent soil restorer, with a root system up to 45 cm deep. It can produce

name: Purple Peacock
desc: This variety produces dark green sprays, purplish stems and red-veined leaves. The sprays are followed by a fine crop of sweet-tasting stems. Each part can be eaten raw or lightly cooked.

name: Purple Perfect
desc: A variety with potato-like foliage. It produces an abundance of smooth, round, deep-pink fruits. They weigh 250 to 400 g and have a sweet-tart flavor.

name: Purple Plum
desc:
This vigorous variety produces plum-shaped purple roots. Their white flesh has a crisp, juicy texture and a mild flavor that improves with light frosts.
This variety is sensitive to heat and heavy frosts.

name: Purple Ruffles
desc: This improved variety offers magnificent foliage with a strong clove fragrance. Selected for its deep purple color and highly serrated leaves, it won the All America Selections award.

name: Purple Russian
desc: This variety produces clusters of 5 to 7 pointed plum-shaped fruits, pale pink tinged with green, suitable for making sauces and coulis. The dense flesh offers a sweet, appreciated flavor.

name: Purple Sprouts
desc:
This very old variety produces a central head and then numerous shoots throughout the winter. They are picked as they lengthen, before flowering.

This variety adapts to a wide range of growing conditions.

name: Purple Top Milan
desc:
This variety, which is not very sensitive to bolting, produces flattened white roots with pink collars. Foliage is upright, stiff and not lobed at the base. Their white flesh has a fine, firm texture and good flavor.

This variety is one of the ear

name: Purple Valley
desc:
This variety of barley has six rows of purple naked kernels. They require no hulling and can be used in soups, risottos, etc. Lightly toasted, they can be cooked in the same way as brown rice.
This variety, native to the Willamette Valley in the Unit

name: seeds Purple-seeded Tarahumara
desc:
This 3 m tall variety produces an upright, robust stem bearing a single flower 25 to 30 cm in diameter with yellow petals. Its heart contains an abundance of seeds.

The flower petals enhance salads, and the seeds are a favorite with birds.

name: Pycnanthemum Pycnanthemum verticillatum
desc: This species reaches up to 1 m in height and from July to September produces an abundance of white flowers with a powerful mint fragrance, much appreciated by pollinators.

name: Quadrato d'Asti Giallo
desc:
This old, vigorously-growing variety produces 15 cm fruits with 3 or 4 lobes, golden-yellow in color when ripe, with thick, sweet flesh.

It offers a certain resistance to harsh conditions. It prefers warm climates.

name: Quadrato d'Asti Rosso
desc:
This old variety produces cubic fruits with 3 or 4 lobes that ripen from green to red and offer thick, fleshy flesh with a sweet, mild flavor.
This variety has a vigorous growth habit. It prefers regions with a relatively warm climate.

name: Quatro Milpas
desc:
This old, productive variety offers elongated, fleshy, dark reddish-brown fruit when fully ripe.
This old variety originates from Quatro Milpas, Sonora, Mexico.

name: Sweet Pea Queen Alexandria
desc: This old variety, introduced by Henry Eckford in 1905 and named in honor of Queen Alexandria, wife of King Edward VII, can grow up to 2.50 m tall, with beautiful, highly fragrant, bright-red flowers.

name: Queen Of Sheba
desc: This highly ornamental variety, with its compact habit, has magnificent violet flowers contrasting with dark green foliage. It has an exquisite fragrance and intense flavor.

name: Cotton Rajhans
desc: This variety, ideal for allotments, yields beautiful pure white cotton and is particularly easy to grow, thanks to its adaptability to a wide range of growing conditions. Rajhans is also relatively resistant to cotton "pests".

name: Rave d'Auvergne
desc: This old variety produces white roots with a dark purple collar. They are round and flattened, and can reach 10 cm in diameter.

name: Red Acre
desc:
This old variety produces compact, red apples up to 15 cm in diameter and weighing up to 1.5 kg. They are not susceptible to shattering and have a firm texture.
This variety adapts to a wide range of growing conditions.

name: Red Ball Jets
desc: This variety, which is highly resistant to bremia and necrosis and slow to bolt, produces a red apple surrounded by round, waffle-shaped leaves with wavy edges. They have a firm texture and pleasant flavor.

name: Red Baron
desc: This well-preserved variety has rounded, slightly flattened, dark-red bulbs with a mild, sweet flavor. It won the Royal Horticultural Society's Award of Garden Merit.

name: Welsh onion Red Beard Scallion
desc: This traditional Asian variety, with its mild flavor, produces astonishing chives with red stems and green foliage, about 30 cm long. Expose plants to the cold of winter to obtain a more intense red hue.

name: Cotton Red Beauty
desc: This variety, with its highly ornamental foliage, produces white cotton and blooms bright pink, quite early for the species.

name: Lima beans Red Calico
desc: This highly decorative variety has many flat green pods, beige when ripe. They contain magnificent large berries, red mottled with black.

name: Red Cluster Pear Hypertress
desc: This variety, developed by Alan Kapuler, offers huge, impressive bunches topped with hundreds of small, pear-shaped red fruits, 15 to 30 g in size, very tasty and sweet.

name: Red Earred Butterheart
desc: This is a new Tribe Selection "'Really Red Deer Tongue" with pointed, dark-red leaves that are shorter, wider, more wrinkled and melt in the mouth than its relative.

name: Red Express
desc:
This variety produces a compact, purplish-red apple weighing up to 2 kg with a crunchy texture.
This variety has a vigorous growth habit.

name: Celosia Red Flame
desc: This variety with burgundy foliage reaches 60 to 90 cm in height and offers bright red flowers 5 to 10 cm wide, which retain their color when dried.

name: Red Habanero
desc: This ancient variety can reach 1 m in height and produces an abundance of wrinkled, lantern-shaped fruit. They ripen from green to red and offer a fiery flesh with an incomparably fruity flavor.

name: Baselles Red Leaf
desc:
This ancient variety, from tropical Asia, produces a red stem, climbing up to 3 m high, and green leaves with red highlights. Also known as "Malabar spinach", it is eaten like spinach and has a light sorrel taste.
Although this variety adapts to a wi

name: Red Marble
desc: This disease-resistant variety produces numerous small bulbs, 4 to 5cm in diameter, with dark red skin and excellent keeping qualities.

name: Asclepias Red milkweed
desc:
This perennial species is highly resistant to cold. It can reach 1.50 m in height and produces elongated foliage and around a hundred pink umbellate flowers. Its seed pods measure 12 cm.

Highly nectariferous, it is appreciated by hummingbirds, but

name: Red Plum
desc: This old, highly productive French variety produces an abundance of delicious, soft, oval berries, weighing 20 to 40 g. They contain few seeds and are ideal for salads and preserves.

name: / Kales Red Ruffled
desc: Red Ruffled" Kale is very cold-hardy and produces large, gray-green leaves with reddish-purple veins. They become serrated at the end of winter, offer a fine texture and good flavor, and are harvested with their stalks in March-April.

name: Red Sails
desc: This slow-growing variety has deeply lobed, waffle-shaped, bronze-red leaves with a crunchy texture and mild flavor. It is particularly spectacular in the cold season, and contains 6 times more vitamin A and 3 times more vitamin C than commercial let

name: Red Strawberry
desc: This 1 m-high variety produces 2 to 3 small, strawberry-like ears with dark-red kernels. This is one of the most beautiful popcorn varieties.

name: Red Tabasco
desc:
This ancient variety produces small, elongated, upright fruits that are red when fully ripe, with juicy, fruity, smoky flesh.

This pepper is the main ingredient in the famous "Tabasco" sauce.

name: Red Velvet
desc: This ancient variety, one of the reddest, has reddish-brown leaf tips and brown-tinged green undersides. They have a crunchy texture and pleasant flavor.

name: Red Seeded Citron melon
desc:
Green watermelon with red seeds for jam is a very old variety of watermelon, traditionally used for jams.

Characteristics of green watermelon for jam with red seeds

Citrullus lanatus is a very old variety, traditionally used for jam, particular

name: Reine des Hâtives
desc: This variety offers flattened, sometimes slightly ribbed fruits weighing 60 to 110 g and 5 to 8 cm in diameter. They are red with a yellowish collar.

name: Reinette Fruitée
desc: This variety produces beautiful medium-sized, round, red fruits. They have a sweet flavor.

name: Retato Degli Ortolani
desc: This ancient Italian variety produces rounded, cantaloupe-like fruits weighing 1 to 1.5 kg, with highly embroidered skin. Their salmon-orange flesh is highly fragrant and offers excellent sweetness.

name: Zucchinis Rheinau Gold
desc:
This variety produces a large number of elongated fruits with a thin, yellow skin that need not be peeled. Eaten raw or cooked, they offer a highly appreciated flavor.

WHAT ARE THE 

characteristics of rheinau gold zucchini?

Rheinau Gold" zuc

name: Rhine Gold
desc: This variety produces an abundance of flat, yellow pods up to 20 cm long, with no threads. They have a tender texture and contain white seeds.

name: Rio Grande
desc: This vigorous variety produces an abundance of smooth, pointed fruits, 8 to 15 cm long, with delicious, thick flesh. They ripen from green to red and are ideal for processing.

name: Robert's Royality
desc: This compact, vigorous variety, adapted to cool climates, produces an abundance of magnificent deep purple pods, 10 to 12 cm long, turning dark green after cooking. Suitable for pot cultivation.

name: Robinhood Mix
desc: This selection offers 3 lettuces that are slow to bolt and not very susceptible to bremia, sclerotinia and botrytis: a round, compact batavia, "Little John", an upright romaine lettuce, "Robinhood", and a wide-open leaf lettuce, "Lincoln Greenleaf".

name: Rocbrun
desc:
This very hardy, upright variety is an earlier improvement on Rocquencourt. It produces butter pods up to 17 cm long, straight, fleshy and stringless, as well as brown beans.

It is not very susceptible to classic bean diseases such as anthracnose.

name: Rodynda
desc: This vigorous variety, adapted to cool regions, produces a dense, oval, dark-red apple of excellent eating quality. Enjoy cooked, or raw in salads!

name: Common Bean Rognon de Pont l'Abbé
desc: This old French variety is particularly prized for its abundance of pods containing creamy-white, slightly elongated seeds with very thin skins. They can be eaten either semi-dry or dry.

name: Cardoons Romagne Giant
desc: This vigorous variety, closely related to the artichoke, produces large, spiny leaves with broad, thick, fleshy ribs and good flavor.

name: Pepo squash Rondini
desc: This very vigorous variety produces an abundance of small, round fruits, 8 cm in diameter and weighing 100 to 200 g. The skin is dark green with light green flecks, and the flesh is yellow with a sweet flavor.

name: Rosalind Purple
desc: This variety produces very compact, dark purple heads up to 15 cm in diameter. They turn emerald-green when cooked.

name: Rose
desc:
This magnificent variety can reach 1.20 m in height. It has airy, finely cut foliage and a multitude of flowers with candy-pink petals, tinted dark at the base, surrounding a yellow heart.

 

Cosmos are a favorite with birds. The flowers can be

name: Iberis Rose Cardinal
desc:
Iberis umbellata is a fast-growing, very long-blooming, bushy, evergreen plant.

This variety produces magnificent beds of pink, fragrant flowers.

name: Rosé d'Armorique
desc: This old variety, which is not very susceptible to mildew and keeps very well, has rounded bulbs with coppery-pink skins. Their juicy, sweet flesh with pinkish highlights can be enjoyed both raw and cooked.

name: Rosencrantz
desc: This bremia-resistant variety produces a very compact apple with dark red leaves that turn pink at the core. They have a very crunchy texture and superb flavor.

name: Rosie
desc: This variety, an improvement on "Rubin", offers more compact plants with a more uniform purple color, leaves with a deliciously sweet flavor and beautiful mauve-purple flowers.

name: seeds Rostov
desc:
This ancient variety reaches 2 m in height. It produces a thin, straight stem bearing a single yellow flower whose black seeds are very interesting for making oil.

The flower petals can be used to garnish salads, and the seeds are very popular wit

name: Rostova Oxheart
desc:
This variety offers "beef heart" red fruits weighing 350 to 500 g. Their flavor is highly appreciated.

This variety originated in southern Russia, in the Rostov region, and was introduced in 1999 by Nik Peplenov. It was renamed "Sunset's Red Horiz

name: Rotunda Gogoshari
desc: This old, very vigorous variety produces very fruity, sweet, juicy, crunchy, heart-shaped fruits that are red when ripe.

name: Rouge Grenobloise
desc:
Grenobloise Red Batavia lettuce is a very old variety. It produces large, medium-dense heads of very blistered green leaves, colored bright red and with a crunchy texture.

Characteristics of Batavia Grenobloise lettuce

Grenobloise Red Batavia l

name: Round Pink with White Tip
desc:
This extremely early variety produces round, pink roots with white tips, and firm, crunchy flesh, ideal for crudités. It is the only garden radish that is truly pink.

Characteristics of the Round Pink Radish with White Tip

Raphanus sativus is a

name: Rousse d’Hiver
desc:
This old-fashioned, hardy variety produces large, firm apples with slightly corrugated green leaves tinged with blond and red. They have a lovely crunchy texture and excellent flavor.
It can also be grown in spring and summer.

name: Rowpack
desc: This vigorous variety from the United States has a compact habit. Not very susceptible to disease, it produces clusters of 4 to 5 red fruits, round and curved at the top, suitable for canning.

name: Sweet Pea Royal Family
desc:
This variety produces an abundance of large, fragrant flowers in all colors: pink, salmon, red, white, purple, lavender...
This variety has a vigorous growth habit.

name: Rubens Rouge
desc: This variety, adapted to cool climates, offers a mild flavor and good productivity. Beautiful red leaves surround an emerald-green heart with semi-embossed leaves.

name: Ruby Giant
desc: This ancient variety, stabilized in 1912, is the result of a cross between "Ruby King" and "Chinese Giant" and produces beautiful cubic peppers, red when ripe, with thick, flavorful flesh.

name: Rudbeckia Rudbeckia laciniata
desc: This perennial species can reach 2.40 m in height. From June to September, it produces an abundance of 7 cm-diameter yellow flowers with drooping petals and a prominent green heart.

name: Rudbeckia Rudbeckia maxima
desc: This perennial species grows up to 2 m tall. It produces sparsely branched stems, broad, bluish foliage and flowers with yellow petals and a conical heart, first green then brown.

name: Rudbeckia Rudbeckia subtomentosa
desc: This very bushy perennial can reach 2 m in height, and from June to September produces an abundance of yellow flowers with 6 to 20 petals and a conical, prominent brown heart.

name: Russian Oxheart
desc:
This old variety produces an abundance of fleshy, firm, ox-heart-shaped fruit. They contain few seeds.

This variety has a vigorous growth habit.

name: Sabre
desc: The saber bean, 2 to 3 m high, produces impressive flat, saber-shaped pods up to 50 cm long. Fleshy and low in fiber, they offer excellent flavor and are eaten like green beans.

name: Saffron Crocus Saffron bulb - Crocus sativus (calibre 9-11)
desc:
Crocus sativus bulbs will be available for sale again at the end of June 2024.

Crocus sativus, the only species capable of producing saffron, is a 15 cm-tall bulbous plant whose violet flowers provide 3 yellow stamens and 3 red stigmas. Saffron bu

name: Saint Fiacre
desc: This hardy, productive old French variety produces an abundance of white flowers, followed by long, straight, stringless, tender green pods. When fully ripe, the seeds are icy brown.

name: Saint Jean l’Agneau
desc: This old French variety, resistant to bolting, forms very quickly and produces a dark green, bronze-tinted apple. Its large, blistered leaves offer a firm texture and excellent flavor.

name: Saint Vincent
desc:
Saint Vincent leaf lettuce is a very old variety, with broad, blond-green leaves tinged with red. They have a tender texture and mild flavor.

Characteristics of Saint Vincent leaf lettuce

Saint Vincent Cutting Lettuce, Lactuca sativa, is a mid-

name: Sainte Lucie
desc: This ancient variety produces beautiful, thin-skinned, strongly ribbed, "beefsteak"-type fruits weighing up to 500 g. Their thick, dense flesh reveals an exquisite old-fashioned flavor, sweet and very fragrant.

name: Sakurajima Mammoth
desc: This ancient, vigorous Japanese variety produces giant radishes with excellent keeping qualities and pure white, tender, sweet flesh. While the average radish weighs 5-6 kg, the largest can reach 30 kg!

name: Salad Bowl Green
desc: This highly decorative variety has very lobed, green leaves. It is a lettuce with good keeping qualities.

name: Salad Bowl Red
desc: This highly decorative variety produces "oak-leaf" lettuces with bronze foliage that is very blistered, crunchy and delicious.

name: Salad Master
desc: This vigorous variety from the United States has bushy foliage. It has low susceptibility to fusarium and verticillium, and produces round, slightly flattened, medium-sized fruits. Mild flavor.

name: San Felipe
desc: This old-fashioned variety with its compact habit is well suited to both cold and dry seasons, and produces conical fruits that ripen from green to red, delicious in spicy sauces.

name: San Juan Tsile
desc: This vigorous variety produces an abundance of red conical fruits with a fragrant flavor.Pepper strength varies according to growing conditions (sunshine, soil type, etc.).

name: of Milan San Michele / Violaceo Di Verona
desc: This short-growing winter variety is easy to cultivate. Its flattened head is made up of very tender, purplish-pink leaves.

name: Brussels sprouts Sanda
desc: Late-blooming and very hardy, the Sanda variety produces half-dwarf plants around 50 cm tall. Throughout the winter, they produce a myriad of exquisite, firm, round and smooth apples, 2 to 3 cm in diameter.

name: Sandia Hot
desc: This variety, selected by the New-Mexico Chile Pepper Institute, offers some 20 conical fruits, 15 to 20 cm long. They ripen from green to red and reveal a delicious fruity flavor.

name: Cowpea Sant'Anna
desc: This productive, drought-tolerant variety produces incredibly long, spaghetti-like green pods. They can reach 80 cm in length, but are generally eaten when they are 40 cm long.

name: Santaka
desc: This variety has dense vegetation and produces up to 150 pointed fruits, ripening from green to red when fully ripe. They have fine flesh and are suitable for drying.

name: Santo
desc: This variety of coriander has been selected to delay the setting of seed, thus increasing the harvesting period for stems and leaves. The first harvest takes place 45 days after sowing.

name: Saxa
desc:
This low-vegetation variety produces round roots with red skin and white flesh with a crunchy texture.

This hardy variety adapts to a wide range of growing conditions.

name: Sayamusume
desc: This highly productive variety produces an abundance of 7 to 9 cm green pods containing 2 to 4 green seeds.

name: Scarlet Ohno Revival
desc: This splendid variety of mustard turnip has a rounded, scarlet root, topped with delicious, smooth, green or purple-red leaves. Reintroduced and selected by Franck Morton, it is not yet fully stabilized.

name: Scarlet Runner
desc: This old, highly decorative variety has magnificent scarlet-red flowers and can climb up to 5 m high. The slender pods, around 20 cm long, contain large black and purple seeds.

name: Schoon's Hard Shell
desc: This old, vigorous variety produces fruits weighing 2.5 to 3.5 kg. Their hard, ochre skin encloses dense, apricot-colored flesh with a sweet, fragrant flavor that lasts for several days after harvesting.

name: Cotton Sea Island Brown
desc: This variety offers slightly shiny fibers when spun, longer than other cottons, and its naked seeds are easy to extract.

name: Sea Shells
desc:
This variety, with its cut and wispy vegetation, has large flowers with piped petals, evoking the shape of a seashell. White, pale pink and red, they bloom all summer long.

 

Cosmos are a favorite with birds. The flowers can be left to dry in a

name: Nepeta Select Blue
desc: This selection of a perennial species offers plants 15 cm high and 30 cm wide with lavender flowers. The heart-shaped, gray-green leaves are highly fragrant.

name: Self Blanching
desc:
This early snowball variety produces a very white apple with a firm texture and excellent flavor.
The leaves naturally envelop and whiten the apple.

name: Moschata squash Seminole
desc:
This sweet, orange-fleshed variety is excellent for pies, soups and baked dishes. The fruit is teardrop-shaped. Thanks to their very tough skin, they can be kept for almost a year.
In cool climates, this variety sometimes fails to ripen and can there

name: Peas Serpette d’Auvergne
desc:
This very old and hardy variety has pods around 8 cm long, containing 9 to 11 delicious, smooth, green and tender seeds, remarkable for their finesse.

 

Mentioned in the first editions of Vilmorin-Andrieux's "Les plantes potagères" and original

name: Serrano
desc: This variety, which can grow to 1.50 m, produces an abundance of flame-shaped fruit. Ideal for drying and preserving, as well as for spicy recipes, they ripen from bright green to orange to bright red.

name: Wax gourd Seven Star
desc: This variety, popular in Asian cuisine, produces green fruits 20 to 30 cm long. When ripe, they can be eaten like squash, and are covered with a waxy coating that allows them to keep for up to 2 years.

name: Seven Top/Southern Prize
desc: This ancient variety of turnip is grown for its vitamin- and calcium-rich leaves. They can be eaten as young shoots or at full maturity, when they reach 45 to 60 cm in height.

name: Sheepnose
desc: This "pepper-tomato" variety produces fruits 10 cm in diameter and 7 cm high. They ripen from green to red and have very thick, juicy, flavorful flesh.

name: Shiraz
desc:
This variety with green leaves and red stems produces round, red roots with a firm texture.

This variety is particularly insensitive to brown rhizoctonia ("Rhizoctonia solani").

name: Shogoin
desc:
This variety offers pure white roots, 5 to 20 cm in diameter, with soft, tender flesh. Traditionally, in Japan, they are eaten marinated. This turnip is also grown for its foliage.
This variety thrives in dry, warm conditions.

name: Shogoin Globe
desc: This variety produces round radishes up to 17 cm in diameter, with white skin and a green collar, and firm, crunchy flesh. The roots develop largely above ground. They can be cooked.

name: Siam Queen Thai
desc: This highly ornamental variety has large, elongated green leaves with a delicious licorice flavor, much appreciated in Southeast Asian cuisine. The flowers and upper leaves are tinged with purple.

name: / Kales Siberian
desc: This variety can grow up to 1 m tall and produces curly, blue-green leaves with white veins. They have a very tender texture when picked young.

name: Sibirische Orange
desc: This variety produces an abundance of drop-shaped fruit. They contain few seeds and little juice. Their firm flesh has a very mild flavor.

name: Sibley
desc: This old "Hubbard" variety can grow up to 5 m tall. Its 5 kg teardrop-shaped fruits have a slate-blue skin that turns pink when fully ripe. Their orange flesh has a pleasant flavor that improves with storage.

name: Sicilian Giant
desc: All-month radish. Late variety. Red root, 4-5 cm in diameter, resistant to bursting, does not dig. Flesh firm, not pungent, crisp and mild-tasting. Hairy foliage.

name: AIDS Sida cordifolia
desc: This species, also known as "khareti" or "bala", is a herbaceous plant with yellow flowers in summer. It is used in traditional Indian medicine as a stimulant and to burn fat.

name: Silene Silene coronaria
desc: This easy-to-grow, hardy perennial produces beautiful, compact plants that flower abundantly from June to September. Its single, bright magenta-red flowers, 2 to 3 cm in diameter, bloom on silvery-gray foliage and stems.

name: Silphium Silphium perfoliatum
desc:
This perennial species, 2.5 to 3 m high, produces large leaves grouped in twos and an abundance of pretty yellow flowers with 18 to 40 petals. Ideal for restoring biodiversity!

 

The abundant seeds appeal to many garden birds.

Its English na

name: Silphium Silphium trifoliatum
desc:
This perennial species grows up to 1.50 m tall. It produces pointed leaves grouped in 3s along the stem and an abundance of yellow flowers with 12 to 15 petals.
The abundant seeds appeal to many garden birds. They are self-sowing.

name: Silt
desc: This variety produces an abundance of fruit that are pointed at both ends, wrinkled and bright yellow when fully ripe. They are 3 to 4 cm long and have a delicious lemony, smoky flavor.

name: Silver Queen
desc: This vigorous, branching variety produces pale-green, rounded fruits 15 cm long. They contain a mucilaginous substance that thickens sauces and stews, and can be eaten both raw and cooked.

name: Sirloin Of Zebra
desc: This variety offers variegated red and green fruits, weighing from 40 to 80 g, with equally reddish-brown and green flesh and a highly appreciated flavor. Fruits can vary widely in color, shape and flavor.

name: Slow Bolt
desc: This easy-to-grow variety of coriander is one of the most heat-resistant and slow to set seed. It offers abundant foliage, with an inimitable fragrance, and is ideal for repeated cutting.

name: Snake gourd Snake Gourd
desc:
This species, also known as patole, produces slender fruits up to 1.5 m long, orange-red when ripe. Consumed immature and green, when the fruits reach 30 to 40 cm in length, they offer a taste somewhere between green beans and asparagus.

This spec

name: Snow Prince
desc:
This variety offers beautifully textured, medium-sized white or creamy-yellow fruit with a bronze tint at the collar. It is the result of a cross between a "Vincent's White" white tomato and a "Bosque Blue" indigo tomato.

This is a new variety pro

name: Soi de Buzau
desc: This ancient variety produces an apple up to 20 cm in diameter, light green in color, whose fine, excellent leaves are often used to make rolls.

name: Common Bean Soissons Gros Blanc
desc:
Haricot de Soissons Gros Blanc - String beans for shelling

The Gros Blanc Soissons bean, an old variety, can climb more than 3 m high and has large, light-green pods that turn yellow when ripe. They contain 4 to 6 very large white beans. Well-suit

name: Morelle Solanum sisybrifolium
desc: This highly ornamental vegetable plant, though riddled with hundreds of thorns, produces delicious, cherry-sized vermilion fruit.When the fruit reaches maturity, it opens to let it finish growing and ripening.

name: Rudbeckia Solar Eclipse
desc:
This magnificent variety, around 60 cm tall, has large flowers with mahogany centers surrounded by bright yellow petals. It's perfect for any flower bed and ideal for bouquets!

 

Rudbeckia hirta and Rudbeckia serotina were used by Native Americ

name: Solstice
desc: This variety has a central head, 10 cm in diameter, followed by numerous offshoots - due to the plant's ability to grow from the stem. It was selected by Lupine Knoll Farm in Oregon.

name: Sonata Mix
desc: This compact cosmos, ideal for borders and pots, offers an abundance of elegant flowers in harmonious shades of white to deep pink.

name: Sorrel Sorrel
desc:
This ancient variety produces clumps of oblong green leaves with a tangy flavor that can be eaten raw or cooked, but in moderation, as sorrel is rich in oxalic acid.

The variety can reach 60 cm in height.

name: Asclepias Soulmate
desc: This variety has bright pink, vanilla-scented flowers which, when fertilized, produce huge seed pods, each adorned with a silky parachute.

name: Spanish Black
desc: This variety produces elongated roots with an almost black-purple skin and white-yellow flesh. They have a fine texture and excellent flavor.

name: Spanish Piquillo
desc: This very old variety, with its characteristic triangular shape, has little flesh and a fairly tough, bright red skin. It is traditionally cooked stuffed or roasted.

name: Speckled
desc:
This very old variety, poorly adapted to the heat of summer, has a rosette of rounded pale-green leaves, strongly speckled with burgundy-red, with a highly appreciated flavor.

The peripheral leaves can be cut off, one by one, as needed. New ones d

name: Cardoons Spineless White
desc: This ancient, highly decorative variety resembles the artichoke. It produces thick, dissected leaves and fleshy, thornless ribs with a dense texture. They are excellent cooked or blanched.

name: Cardoons Spiny Tunisian
desc: This old, highly decorative variety produces fleshy, spiny ribs with a thick texture. Very tender, they can be eaten cooked or simply blanched.

name: Luffas Sponge Gourd
desc:
This creeping or climbing species produces cylindrical fruits 20 to 40 cm long. Much appreciated in Asian cuisine, the young fruits are eaten like zucchini. The fibers of the ripe, dried fruit serve as a vegetable sponge.

This plant, native to Ind

name: Peas Spring Blush
desc: This variety with mauve and white flowers produces green pods with pink undertones. The tendrils are highly developed.

name: Spring flavors
desc: This blend is composed of 5 varieties, a veritable festival of flavors, colors and textures, adapted to early-season cultivation: Reine de Mai, Cressonnette du Maroc, Blonde Maraichère, Grosse Blonde Paresseuse and Rouge Grenobloise.

name: Spring Gold
desc: This old variety has golden-blond, wavy foliage forming a crisp apple. It has good vigor and excellent eating qualities.

name: Squirting cucumber Squirting Cucumber
desc:
This unusual plant, also known as donkey gherkin, is unique in the way it spreads. A simple touch of the mature fruit is all it takes for them to violently detach and expel their seeds several meters away.
This is one of the few cucurbits native to E

name: St. John's wort St John's Wort
desc: This perennial species grows up to 60 cm tall and produces oval leaves and flowers with 5 yellow petals dotted with brown spots.

name: Standby
desc: This variety developed by Tom Peters, selected for its hardiness and late flowering, produces highly aromatic, green, serrated foliage. The seeds have a lime-like flavor.

name: Common Bean Starazagorsky
desc: This Greek variety has magnificent oval seeds colored black and white, rather like the yin-yang symbol. They can be eaten in any form, green, semi-dry or dry.

name: Strawberry
desc: This variety produces "Hubbard"-type fruits weighing 6 to 8 kg, with orange-red, warty skin. Their dense, compact flesh has a pleasant flavor.

name: Gomphrena Strawberry Fields
desc: From July to the first frosts, this variety produces numerous small, bright red pompons. Ideal for fresh or dry bouquets, it adds a touch of lively color to flowerbeds and vegetable gardens!

name: Lamb's Quarters and Company Strawberry Spinach
desc:
This species forms a rosette of triangular, toothed, dark-green leaves. It then unfurls a stem 1 m high, bearing fleshy, glomerular fruits that look like small strawberries and taste sweet, reminiscent of beet.

The leaves can be eaten raw when you

name: Streamline
desc: This red-flowered variety, adapted to heavy soils, produces long, straight, slightly flattened pods about 30 to 45 cm long, containing purple grains with black variegation.

name: Zucchinis Striato Pugliese
desc:
This variety offers slightly ribbed, "Cocozelle"-type fruits up to 18 cm long. Their dark green skin is streaked with lighter shades.

WHAT ARE THE 

CHARACTERISTICS OF THE STRIATO PUGLIESE ZUCCHINI?

The "Striato Pugliese" zucchini, a member o

name: Striped Cavern
desc:
This variety, similar to Schimmeig Striped Hollow, produces pepper-shaped tomatoes with well-defined lobes. The fruits are hollow, with few seeds, as if ready for stuffing. They are red with orange stripes and weigh over 200 g.

The fruit keeps for

name: Stylophorum Stylophorum sutchuenense
desc:
This Papaveraceae species has plants 30 to 60 cm tall bearing single yellow flowers. The green leaves are heart-shaped. Flowering takes place in May/June.
This species is native to China, where it grows at altitudes of 1100 to 1700 meters.

name: Sub Arctic Plenty
desc: This compact variety is suited to cool climates. Even at low temperatures, it produces small, round fruits with juicy flesh and a slightly acidic flavor. The variety was developed in Canada.

name: Sugar Drop Cherry
desc:
This variety offers small, very sweet fruits, about 1.5 cm in diameter. According to Lee Goodwin, even the foliage has a very sweet fragrance. They contain few seeds. The plants grow to a height of 2m50.
This is a new variety of yellow cherry tomato

name: Peas Sugar Magnolia
desc: This magnificent variety is the fruit of over 15 years' breeding by our friend Alan Kapuler in the USA. It produces beautiful purple pods throughout the season, with a tender, sweet taste that should be harvested young.

name: Sugar Rush Stripey
desc:
This splendid variety produces fruit with contorted shapes and atypical colors. They ripen from yellow-green to peach-orange, with fine red stripes, and offer a deliciously sweet, fruity flavor, similar to Aji peppers.

 

Not yet totally stable,

name: Asclepias Sullivant Asclepiade
desc:
This perennial species is highly resistant to cold. It reaches heights of up to 1.20 m and produces elongated foliage, around forty pink umbels and 12 cm-long seed pods.

Highly nectariferous, it is appreciated by hummingbirds, butterflies and othe

name: Oenothera Sundrops
