desc: This species offers magnificent bright yellow flowers 3 to 5 cm in diameter all summer long. A hardy perennial, it can withstand light frosts and is very easy to grow thanks to its hardiness.

name: Lupin Sunrise
desc: This annual lupine, renowned for its long flowering season, offers generous foliage topped with beautiful flower spikes whose colors change over time. They change from blue, white and yellow to violet, orange and copper.

name: Sunrise Orange
desc: This vigorous variety has a compact habit. It produces numerous cubic fruits about 10 cm wide, with thick, reddish-yellow flesh when fully ripe.

name: Super Sioux
desc: This variety is vigorous and produces until frost. The fruit is oval, pinkish-red with yellow tints, and not very sensitive to bursting. Their flesh is thick and tangy.

name: Surprise Siberia
desc: This very early variety produces an abundance of red fruit weighing from 90 to 120 grams.

name: / Kales Sutherland
desc: This variety, native to Sutherland in northern Scotland, offers an abundance of green leaves on plants up to a meter tall.

name: Sweet Cherry
desc: This cherry-pepper variety offers an abundance of small, dark-red fruits when ripe, suitable for canning. Their flavor is very fruity.

name: Sweet Jemison
desc: This variety, created by Dough Jones, produces elongated peppers that ripen from green to yellow, renowned for their very sweet flavor.

name: Sweet Keeper
desc: This old, very runny variety produces gray-green fruits weighing 5 to 7 kg, slightly flattened and ribbed, with a thick epidermis. Their bright orange flesh has a very mild flavor and a high beta-carotene content. They keep for almost 1 year.

name: Pepo squash Table Queen
desc:
This ancient variety of acorn squash produces 1 kg fruits with a very hard, dark-green skin. Their dry, bright-orange flesh has a delicious nutty flavor that improves with storage.

This variety keeps for 4 to 8 months.

name: Tango
desc:
This magnificent species can reach 1 m in height. It has an abundance of peachy-orange flower spikes and aniseed-scented foliage.
It is native to the southwestern USA. The name Agastache comes from the Greek aga, meaning "abundance", and stachys, mea

name: seeds Tarahumara
desc:
This old variety produces an upright stem over 3 m high, bearing a single golden-yellow flower up to 30 cm in diameter with white, domed seeds.
The flower petals enhance salads, and the seeds are a favorite with birds.

name: Tarahumara Popping
desc:
This ancient, vigorous variety, native to Mexico, can grow up to 3 m tall and produces beautiful, abundant panicles of white seeds used like popcorn.

 

The anthocyanin-rich sheaths of Sorghum bicolor leaves are used as a natural red dye. This u

name: Tarahumara Warty
desc:
This variety produces small, warty, oval, green gourds.
The gourds dry in a dry, ventilated place and can be painted, sculpted or made into various objects.

name: Tasty Evergreen
desc: This very old variety offers numerous round fruits with green epidermis, then pale yellow when fully ripe. Their emerald-green flesh is sweet and very popular with children.

name: Tavor
desc: This variety, perennial in temperate regions, produces an almost thornless stem. It has 7 to 8 large, green, purple-tinged flower heads, about 12 cm in diameter, appreciated for their tender texture.

name: Tendersweet
desc: This ancient variety produces elongated, heartless roots 15 to 20 cm long. Their fine flesh offers a superb sweet flavor.

name: Tengamine
desc: This white-flowered variety, which reaches 50 cm in height, produces small pods. They each contain 2 green seeds, stained with a buff eye, which average 41% protein and 20% oil.

name: Tequila Sunrise
desc: This highly ornamental variety produces pointed, upright carrot-shaped fruits, ripening from green to orange. Their firm, crunchy flesh has a lively, lemony flavor.

name: Thai Chao Praya
desc: This vigorous, prolific Thai variety produces small, egg-shaped fruits weighing 50-80 g, creamy white with light green stripes. They are used in many Asian dishes.

name: Moschata squash Thai Kang Kob
desc:
This variety produces very ribbed, warty fruit that turns from green to bronze when ripe. Their rich orange flesh is much appreciated. They keep for 5 to 8 months.
This variety has a vigorous growth habit.

name: Thai Small Leaf
desc:
Thai Small Leaf is a small basil with purple flowers and stems. The pointed green leaves offer a flavor of anise and clove.

This variety is called Horapha (โหระพา) in Thailand and Húng Quế in Vietnam.

name: Sweet Pea The Major
desc: This old variety has very fragrant mauve flowers with white marbling. It was selected by Major Turral and is also known as Marbled Purple.

name: Pepo squash Thelma Sanders Sweet Potato
desc: The gourd-shaped fruits are 15 cm long and deeply ribbed. The skin is cream-colored, turning golden yellow when fully ripe. The flesh, also golden yellow, is thick and excellent.

name: Theron
desc:
This variety produces an average of 3 huge fruits reaching 25 kg. The pink skin encloses firm orange flesh. They are very popular cooked and are also often used to feed farm animals.
This highly elongated variety grows up to 10 m.

name: Thompson
desc: Suitable for autumn cultivation, this variety produces medium to large dark green apples. For a long time, it produces an abundance of shoots, which should be picked as they lengthen, before the flowers bloom.

name: Tip Top (Improved Nantaise)
desc:
This very early, high-yielding variety has beautiful, cylindrical, regular roots 18 to 22 cm long, with no core. An improved Nantaise variety, its orange flesh is both soft and sweet.

Enjoy it raw or cooked!

name: seeds Titan
desc:
This variety produces an upright, thick stem, around 3.5 m high, followed by a magnificent single inflorescence, with yellow petals and a chocolate center, 45 to 60 cm in diameter. It offers an abundance of seeds.

The flower petals enhance salads,

name: To cut
desc: This variety, also known as Swiss chard, produces fine chard about 60 cm high, with tender green leaves. They can be harvested continuously, as long as the heart is preserved.

name: Tolli Sweet
desc: This productive variety offers long, bright-red fruits, 12 to 13 cm long. They have a sweet, fruity flavor.

name: Tom Thumb Mix
desc: This dwarf variety, with its compact habit, is perfect for growing in pots or window boxes. From July to October, it produces an abundance of flowers in shades ranging from yellow to dark red.

name: Mix with large fruits
Tondino Di Manduria
desc: This old variety, 50 to 80 cm tall, produces an abundance of clusters of 4 to 6 slightly oval "Roma"-type fruits. Their very juicy flesh is particularly appreciated in salads.

name: Tonnelet
desc: This highly original variety offers an abundance of small, elongated fruits weighing 60-90 g, with red and yellow stripes and firm flesh.

name: Toraziroh
desc:
This vigorous, fast-growing variety produces generous harvests of dark-green, rather prickly leaves. The stems, also edible, are somewhat reminiscent of "Pak Choy" cabbage.
This variety is relatively slow to bolt.

name: Touchon
desc: This old, traditional variety, perfectly suited to early crops, has straight roots around 20 cm long. They don't form a heart and reveal firm, high-quality flesh.

name: Touchstone Gold
desc: This variety has bright green leaves and rounded, orange-colored roots. The golden-yellow flesh does not color other foods. It has a fine texture and mild flavor.

name: Travel
desc: Also known as the traveler's tomato, this early, productive variety offers incredible fruits weighing around 100 g, with multiple lobes welded together. They can be eaten as and when required, and reveal thick, juicy flesh.

name: Tribulus Tribulus terrestris
desc: Tribule terrestre is an annual plant with many medicinal properties, particularly in the sphere of sexual vigor.

name: Tropea Red
desc: This early Italian variety from Calabria produces round, bright red onions with soft, sweet flesh. Unsuitable for preserving, they can be eaten at any stage of ripeness.

name: / Kales True Siberian
desc: This variety is very well adapted to cold, damp climates and has an abundance of green leaves.

name: Tsakoniki
desc:
This beautiful old Greek variety, adapted to warm climates, produces beautiful, shiny, elongated purple fruit with white stripes, about 20-25 cm long.
The flesh is white with excellent flavor and no bitterness.

name: Tulsis Tulsi Mtule
desc:
This basil can grow up to 2 m tall and produces beautiful, serrated green leaves covered with a fine down that exude a very strong scent of camphor and clove.

Discover it on Kokopelli's blog: "Tulsis and other Basilico-Molecular Truths to free you

name: Tunisian
desc:
This ancient, highly productive variety, 2 to 3 m tall, has panicles filled with an abundance of gray seeds. They are traditionally used to make porridge.

 

The sheaths of Sorghum bicolor leaves, rich in anthocyanin, are used as a natural red d

name: / Kales Tuscan black
desc: This old, hardy variety, not very sensitive to cold, has a tall, straight stem ending in a bunch of long, dark green-blue, almost black leaves. They are eaten in soup or toast, after cooking in water.

name: Tuscan red
desc: This early Italian variety produces large, round, slightly flattened, dark-red bulbs with soft, crunchy flesh. They can be eaten raw or cooked, and keep for several months.

name: Tuxhorn
desc: This variety produces pineapple-like ribbed fruit, dark red with orange marbling. Their dense flesh offers a mild flavor, richer and sweeter when picked when fully ripe.

name: Moschata squash U.n. Plaza Calabaza
desc: This ancient variety produces flat, deeply ribbed fruit weighing 1 to 2 kg, with a highly original skin pattern of green and white lines and spots.

name: Udmalbet
desc: The egg-shaped fruits are light green with purple stripes. They turn yellow when fully ripe. Flowers are mauve.

name: Umpqua
desc: This variety produces dark-green buds around 12 to 15 cm in diameter. It also produces an abundance of shoots over a long period.

name: Valencia
desc: This sweet onion of Spanish origin, adapted to regions south of the Loire, has large, round, coppery-brown bulbs with sweet, white flesh. They can be eaten young or when fully ripe, and don't keep very long.

name: Valenciano
desc:
This variety produces flattened, slightly ribbed white fruits 30 cm in diameter. Their thick, bright-orange flesh offers a sweet flavor ideal for pastries.

A beautiful variety for painted decorations.

name: Valeriana Valeriana officinalis
desc: This hardy perennial can reach 2 m in height. It produces umbels of white or pink flowers. The plant is renowned for its medicinal properties, notably its sedative and calming properties.

name: Curly endive Variegata Di Lusia Tardiva
desc: This late variety produces medium-sized apples with broad leaves enveloping green cores with red spots. Tasty, crisp and resistant to low temperatures.

name: / Kales Vates
desc:
This compact variety produces blue-gray leaves 50 to 60 cm long. Finely chopped, they offer a tender texture and excellent flavor.
This cold-hardy variety can be grown in spring and autumn.

name: Velarde
desc:
This variety produces elongated, red fruit with relatively early ripening and firm flesh.
The variety originated in Velarde, New Mexico.

name: Velvet Red
desc: This hardy, productive variety produces bunches of small, red cherry tomatoes, 2 to 3 cm in diameter and extremely fragrant. Fruit and foliage are downy. Excellent flavor.

name: Verde Claro
desc:
This vigorous variety, derived from "Raisin Vert", produces small, oval tomatoes that are particularly tasty, with thin, light-colored skin that remains green even when fully ripe. The fruit falls naturally when fully ripe.

 

One of the lines d

name: Verde Di Macerata
desc: This ancient variety, originally from Italy, produces beautiful apples with a brilliant light-green color. They are protected by abundant dark-green foliage and retain their color after cooking.

name: Vernonia Vernonia missurica
desc: This hardy perennial can grow up to 2 m tall. On upright, branched stems, it produces dozens of highly decorative, purple, disheveled inflorescences.

name: Vernonia Vernonia noveboracensis
desc: This perennial species grows to over 2 m tall and produces upright, branched stems bearing several reddish-purple inflorescences.

name: Versailles
desc:
This early, vigorous variety produces elegant flowers in a variety of colors, from pure white to carmine red and pink. It is particularly well-suited to bouquets.

This species is very popular with birds. The flowers can be left to dry in autumn, s

name: Vert d'Autan
desc: This old French variety, of the "Fin de Bagnols" type, produces a multitude of very fine, stringless green beans, even late in the season. When ripe, they contain elongated, pale-green beans.

name: Verte Maraichère
desc:
This old, very hardy variety has an attractive rosette of upright, bright-green leaves that are fairly thick and crisp. Less voluminous than Romaine "Blonde Maraichère", it is however a little earlier.

This variety is suitable for spring and summe

name: Vesuvius Naine
desc: This dwarf variety has beautiful salmon-pink flowers all season long, with a dark spot at the base of the upper petals. Like the leaves, the flowers are edible and add a remarkable touch of color and flavor to summer salads.

name: turnip Vienna white
desc:
This very old, early variety, 25 to 30 cm tall, produces a large root with white flesh and a tender texture.
It is a fast-growing variety.

name: Vinton 81
desc: This variety of tofu soybean can also be eaten fresh, and produces green pods containing large yellow seeds rich in protein - up to 45% - and lipids - up to 21%.

name: / Kales Violet Moss Curled
desc: This is a fairly uniform variety with very mossy leaves, purple in the middle.

name: Viroflay Giant
desc:
This variety is extremely vigorous and suitable for autumn sowing. The large, smooth leaves are up to 25 cm long and 20 cm wide at the base. They are very fleshy, thick and dark green in color. Plants can reach 60 cm in height. Growth: 50 days.

 F

name: Vulcan
desc: This very early, heat-resistant variety is a cross between "Red Fire" and a Korean cutting lettuce. Its crisp, slightly blistered leaves are bright green and red.

name: Waimanalo Long
desc: This Thai variety produces an abundance of long, sinuous, purple-black fruits. Their flesh has a soft, tender texture and good flavor.

name: Waldmann Dark Green
desc: This "Grand Rapids" variety has dark-green, waffle-shaped leaves. It is best grown in late spring or late autumn, and can be harvested in about 50 days.

name: Walla Walla
desc: This old, very hardy variety produces large, light-brown bulbs that can reach 1 kg when fully ripe, and are unsuitable for storing. Their juicy white flesh has an extremely sweet flavor.

name: Waltham
desc: This compact variety produces a green central head, 10 to 20 cm in diameter, which, once cut, is followed by an abundance of lateral shoots for 6 to 8 weeks. It is highly adapted to cold weather.

name: Wasatch Beauty Improved
desc: This variety, originally from the United States, is sensitive to hot weather. It produces clusters of 2 to 3 round, flattened fruits with firm, red flesh.

name: Watermelon
desc:
Red Meat radish is a variety that produces round, green and white roots 5 to 10 cm in diameter. The purple-pink flesh is crunchy, juicy, mildly pungent and particularly sweet.

Characteristics of the Red Meat winter radish

Red Meat radish, Rapha

name: Wavy Red Cos
desc: This variety has very red, wavy leaves with excellent flavor. It is highly resistant to cold and heat.

name: Wayahead
desc: This old, vigorous variety produces an abundance of bright red, slightly flattened fruit with dense flesh and a sweet, somewhat fragrant taste.

name: Wenk's Yellow Hot
desc: This ancient variety produces conical fruit that ripen from yellow to orange to red, with thick flesh.

name: Whangaparoa Crown
desc: This old variety offers up to 6 slightly ribbed fruits weighing from 1 to 3 kg. The blue-gray skin encloses very sweet orange flesh. The fruit keeps well.

name: White Beauty
desc:
White Beauty round white radish is a variety with perfectly round, bright-white roots, around 4 cm in diameter, that don't dig in. The flesh is firm, juicy and crunchy.

Characteristics of the White Beauty round white radish

The White Beauty rou

name: Iberis White Candytuft
desc:
Iberis umbellata is a fast-growing, long-blooming, bushy, evergreen plant.

This variety produces magnificent clusters of fragrant white flowers.

name: Ammi White Dill
desc: This improvement on the high ammi offers, on plants 1.5 to 2 m high, very large umbels of white flowers around 13 cm in diameter. They will add a touch of lightness to any garden bed.

name: Garlic bulbs White Garlic Bulbs "Messidor" AB - 250 g
desc:

name: Garlic bulbs White Garlic Bulbs "Precosem" AB - 250 g

name: Welsh onion White Nebuko
desc: The stems of this variety are long with a slight bulge. Eaten raw or cooked, they are very tender with a mild flavor. The bulb, 20-25 mm in diameter, is eaten raw or cooked and has a strong onion-like flavor. The flowers are eaten raw.

name: White Peach
desc: This old variety offers round, peach-sized, creamy-white fruits weighing 60 to 90 g. Their slightly fluffy skin encloses juicy flesh with a mildly sweet, delicately tart flavor.

name: Iberis White Pinnacle
desc:
Iberis umbellata is a fast-growing, long-blooming, bushy, evergreen plant.

This variety produces magnificent clusters of small, fragrant white flowers.

name: White Pumpkin
desc: This variety produces round, slightly ribbed, white-skinned fruits weighing 3 kg. Their tender, slightly greenish orange flesh is not very sweet.

name: Quinoas White Spike
desc: This variety, bred by Frank Morton, has white panicles with white seeds. It has good resistance to panicle sprouting during very wet autumn periods.

name: White Wonder
desc: This variety produces flat, ribbed, ivory-white fruit. The very firm flesh has a mild, very sweet flavor.

name: Curly endive Wild Garden Mix
desc: A genetic rainbow of chicories with a wide range of colors, apples and leaves... This genetic reservoir was obtained by mixing traditional Italian varieties to select them for autumn sowing in cool regions.

name: Reseda Wild Reseda
desc:
This tinctorial species has cream-colored flower spikes. The yellow dye is extracted from the flowers, leaves and seeds. It is a highly melliferous species.

This species is also known as "Gaude", "Herbe à jaunir" or "Mignonette jaunâtre".

It ha

name: Winter Red
desc: This very old, vigorous and extremely hardy variety has a large, open rosette with thick, elongated leaves that are reddish-brown with a firm texture and good flavor.

name: / Kales Winter Red
desc: This variety produces "Russian Red" type red leaves whose growth and color intensify with cold. They have a tender texture.

name: Curly endive Witloof
desc: After forcing the roots, this ancient variety produces dense, firm endives with delicious, crunchy leaves and a slightly bitter taste. They can be eaten raw, cooked or braised.

name: Wolverine
desc:
This variety produces beautifully rounded, indigo-tinged yellow fruits weighing 200-350 g. The flesh is dense and juicy, with an excellent fruity flavor, sweet with no acidity.

It comes from a selection made by Dean Slater from Reinhard Kraft's cros

name: Wonderlight
desc: This is a very vigorous, productive variety. It produces an abundance of lemon-yellow, oval-shaped, slightly pointed fruit. They have firm flesh and few seeds.

name: Xeno's Density
desc: This compact, uniform variety is highly resistant to mildew. It produces a dense apple with wavy green leaves speckled with red, a crisp texture and good flavor.

name: Yellow
desc: This old variety is of ornamental interest, producing yellow, narrow stems and green, elongated, waffle-shaped foliage.

name: Sweet clover Yellow
desc: This highly melliferous biennial to perennial species has 30 to 80 cm-high stems and, from May to August, fragrant, drooping yellow flowers. It is used as a green manure because it fixes nitrogen and roots vigorously.

name: Yellow Banana Giant
desc: This old, vigorous variety produces an abundance of very sweet, banana-shaped fruit, 15 to 20 cm long. They ripen from bright yellow to red and offer thick, juicy flesh with a much-appreciated sweet flavor.

name: Yellow Mangel
desc: This variety, traditionally used as a fodder plant, can also be eaten by humans. It has an elongated, yellow-orange root that can weigh up to 4 kg and keeps very well. The light green foliage is also appreciated.

name: Yellow Monster
desc: This compact variety produces gigantic, lobed, cubic fruits, 20 cm long and 10 cm wide. They turn from green to golden yellow when fully ripe. The flesh is thick and juicy, with a sweet, pleasant flavor.

name: Yellow Plum
desc: This ancient variety produces an abundance of plum-shaped, lemon-yellow clusters of 7 to 10 fruits right up to the frosts. The flesh is fleshy and juicy, with a mild, sweet flavor and no acidity.

name: Ratibida Yellow Prairie
desc:
This perennial species can reach 1 m in height and produces branched stems bearing, from June to September, an abundance of yellow flowers surrounding an upright, cylindrical black heart.
They make excellent cut flowers.

name: Zucchinis Yellow Straightneck
desc:
This variety offers an abundance of yellow, elongated, straight-necked fruit. They are best eaten when they reach 10 cm in length, when they are crunchy. Their white flesh is sweet and mild.

CHARACTERISTICS OF THE STRAIGHTNECK YEL

name: Yeti
desc: This climbing variety can reach 3 m in height. It has white flowers. Leaves, flower buds, flowers and seeds are edible.

name: Tulsis Zambian Tulsi Gene Pool
desc:
This basil gene pool is a mixture of five populations originating from Zambia and produced from seed, in the same area, using open pollination. It's a gene pool of basil with diverse flavors, fragrances and medicinal properties.

To be discovered o

name: Zapalo Plomo
desc:
This variety, which is more of a population, has ribbed fruits of various shapes and a blue-gray skin.
It is a very old Amerindian population found in the USDA seed bank.

name: Zapotec Pink Ribbed
desc: This ancient Mexican variety, not very sensitive to drought, produces clusters of 6 to 8 flattened, extremely ribbed fruits. Their flesh is thin and relatively floury, making them ideal for stuffing recipes.

name: Zuckertraube
desc: This vigorous-growing variety produces numerous clusters of 10 to 14 fruits weighing around 30 g, with juicy, soft flesh and a sweet, very fragrant taste. Each plant can produce around 3 kg of fruit.
